learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The present continuous (am/is/are + -ing) describes an action in progress now or around now — unfinished and often temporary.

Please don't make so much noise. I'm working. · The population of the world is rising very fast.

learn.title: Form: statement · negative · question
learn.html:
+I'm working · she's working · they're working
−I'm not working · he isn't working
?Are you working? · What are you doing?

learn.title: Now vs around now
learn.html:
▶ At this moment
"Where's Kate?" "She's having a shower."
📆 Around this period
I'm reading a good book at the moment. (not necessarily right now)
📈 Changing / developing
Your English is getting better.

learn.title: Temporary & repeated actions
learn.html:
Use it for something happening for a limited period, even if not at this exact second.

I'm living with friends until I find a flat. (temporary)

With always it can show a repeated action that annoys us: You're always losing your keys!

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Hi! What are you doing these days?
I'm studying for my driving test, and I'm working part-time at a café.
Busy! And Sam? Is he still here?
No, he's travelling around Asia this month.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I working now.  ✓ I'm working now. (don't drop am/is/are)
✗
Your English is getting more better.  ✓ …is getting better.
✗
She is makeing dinner.  ✓ She is making dinner. (drop the e)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• am/is/are + -ing = in progress now / around now / temporary / changing.
  • Signal words: now, at the moment, these days, currently.
  • always + continuous can express annoyance at a repeated action.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Present continuous (am/is/are + -ing) описывает действие в процессе сейчас или в этот период — незавершённое, часто временное: I'm working. · The population is rising fast.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Present continuous (am/is/are + -ing) — қазір немесе осы кезеңде болып жатқан, аяқталмаған, көбіне уақытша әрекетті білдіреді: I'm working. · The population is rising fast.

ru.title: Форма: утверждение · отрицание · вопрос
ru.html: + I'm working; − he isn't working; ? Are you working? / What are you doing?

kk.title: Тұлғасы: болымды · болымсыз · сұраулы
kk.html: + I'm working; − he isn't working; ? Are you working? / What are you doing?

ru.title: Прямо сейчас и в этот период
ru.html: В этот момент (She's having a shower), в этот период (I'm reading a good book at the moment), меняющийся процесс (Your English is getting better).

kk.title: Дәл қазір мен осы кезең
kk.html: Дәл осы сәтте (She's having a shower), осы кезеңде (I'm reading a good book at the moment), өзгеріп бара жатқан үдеріс (Your English is getting better).

ru.title: Временные и повторяющиеся действия
ru.html: На ограниченный срок: I'm living with friends until I find a flat. С always — повторяющееся, раздражающее действие: You're always losing your keys!

kk.title: Уақытша және қайталанатын әрекет
kk.html: Шектеулі уақытқа: I'm living with friends until I find a flat. always-пен қайталанып, ашуlandыратын әрекет: You're always losing your keys!

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «What are you doing these days?» — «I'm studying for my driving test, and I'm working part-time.» — «Sam?» — «He's travelling around Asia this month.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «What are you doing these days?» — «I'm studying for my driving test, and I'm working part-time.» — «Sam?» — «He's travelling around Asia this month.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I working now → I'm working now. getting more better → getting better. makeing → making.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I working now → I'm working now. getting more better → getting better. makeing → making.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: am/is/are + -ing = сейчас/в этот период/временно/меняется. Слова-сигналы: now, at the moment, these days. always + continuous — оттенок раздражения.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: am/is/are + -ing = қазір/осы кезеңде/уақытша/өзгеріп жатқан. Сигнал сөздер: now, at the moment, these days. always + continuous — ашулану реңкі.

activities.q: “Look — that man ___ to open your car door!”
why: Present simple is for habits, not this live moment.

items.s: Your English is getting more better.
I'm living with friends this month.

activities.why: 'better' not 'more better'; don't drop is before -ing.

alts: 're sleeping

activities.instruction: Ask about the plan (use the continuous):
activities.prompt: You / do / anything / this weekend?
activities.answer: are you doing anything this weekend
alts: are you doing anything this weekend?

activities.why: a changing situation → “The weather is getting warmer.”

activities.before: I usually go to work by car, but this week I
activities.after: by train — my car's at the garage. (go)
alts: am going
activities.why: 'this week' (temporary) → present continuous 'm going, not the usual habit.

activities.q: Your brother keeps borrowing money and never gives it back. Annoyed, you say:
why: Correct — always + continuous shows the habit annoys you.

activities.why: a developing trend right now → is increasing (present continuous).

activities.prompt: These days, more and more people work from home. (a rising trend)
activities.answer: more and more people are working from home
alts: these days more and more people are working from home
more and more people are working from home.

activities.scene: Answer naturally about right now vs these days.

steps.npc: Hi! Are you busy? What are you doing?

options.t: I'm cooking dinner, actually. Can I call you back?
options.why: an action in progress now → present continuous.

options.t: I cook dinner. I call you back.
options.why: Right now → “I'm cooking… I'll call you back.”

options.t: Good — I'm still learning the system, but it's getting easier.
options.why: temporary period + a changing situation → continuous.

options.t: Good — I still learn the system.
options.why: an ongoing temporary process → “I'm still learning.”

activities.why: Use the present continuous for actions in progress now, temporary situations and changing trends.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The present simple describes things that are generally or always true — facts, habits, routines and permanent situations. Not just now, but in general.

Nurses look after patients in hospitals. · The earth goes round the sun. · I get up at 7.

learn.title: Form & the -s rule
learn.html:
+I/you/we/they work · he/she/it works
−I don't work · she doesn't work
?Do you work? · Does she work?
⭐
Add -s for he/she/it in positives; after does/doesn't the verb loses the -s: Does she work? (not works)

learn.title: When we use it
learn.html:
🔁 Habits / routines
I check my email every morning.
🌍 Permanent facts
Water boils at 100°C.
⏰ Timetables
The train leaves at 9.30.

learn.title: Frequency & 'how often'
learn.html:
The present simple often appears with frequency adverbs (always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never) and answers how often?

How often do you go to the gym? — I usually go twice a week.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
What do you do?
I'm a software developer. I work from home and travel once a month for meetings.
Nice. Do you enjoy it?
Yes — it gives me a lot of freedom.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
He work in a bank.  ✓ He works in a bank.
✗
Does he works?  ✓ Does he work? (no -s after does)
✗
What means this word?  ✓ What does this word mean?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Facts, habits, routines, timetables → present simple.
  • Add -s for he/she/it; use do/does for − and ?.
  • Signal words: always, usually, often, every day, twice a week.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Present simple описывает то, что вообще или всегда верно: факты, привычки, постоянные ситуации: Nurses look after patients. · The earth goes round the sun.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Present simple — жалпы немесе әрдайым шындықты білдіреді: фактілер, әдеттер, тұрақты жайлар: Nurses look after patients. · The earth goes round the sun.

ru.title: Форма и правило -s
ru.html: + he/she/it works (−s). − don't/doesn't. ? Do/Does…? После does/doesn't глагол теряет −s: Does she work?

kk.title: Тұлғасы және -s ережесі
kk.html: + he/she/it works (−s). − don't/doesn't. ? Do/Does…? does/doesn't-тан кейін етістік −s жоғалтады: Does she work?

ru.title: Когда используем
ru.html: Привычки/распорядок (I check my email every morning), постоянные факты (Water boils at 100°C), расписание (The train leaves at 9.30).

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз
kk.html: Әдет/күнделік (I check my email every morning), тұрақты факт (Water boils at 100°C), кесте (The train leaves at 9.30).

ru.title: Частотность и 'how often'
ru.html: С наречиями частотности (always, usually, often, never) и отвечает на how often?: How often do you go to the gym? — I usually go twice a week.

kk.title: Жиілік және 'how often'
kk.html: Жиілік үстеулерімен (always, usually, often, never) және how often? сұрағына жауап береді: How often do you go to the gym? — I usually go twice a week.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «What do you do?» — «I'm a software developer. I work from home and travel once a month.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «What do you do?» — «I'm a software developer. I work from home and travel once a month.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: He work → He works. Does he works? → Does he work? What means this word? → What does this word mean?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: He work → He works. Does he works? → Does he work? What means this word? → What does this word mean?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Факты, привычки, расписания → present simple. he/she/it → -s; для − и ? — do/does. Слова-сигналы: always, usually, every day.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Фактілер, әдеттер, кестелер → present simple. he/she/it → -s; − мен ? үшін do/does. Сигнал сөздер: always, usually, every day.

why: A fixed daily fact isn't a live action.

alts: what does this word mean?
activities.why: “What does this word mean?” (base verb after does).

activities.q: “Light ___ faster than sound.” (a scientific fact)
why: Correct — a permanent fact → present simple travels.
Continuous is for now, not a general truth.

activities.why: no -s after does → “Does your brother work abroad?”

activities.instruction: Rewrite adding 'usually' in the correct place:
activities.prompt: I have lunch at one.
activities.answer: i usually have lunch at one
alts: i usually have lunch at one.
activities.why: frequency adverb before the main verb → “I usually have lunch at one.”

options.t: I manage a small team and I write reports every week.
I am managing a small team and I am writing reports every week.

activities.why: Present simple for facts, habits and timetables; add -s for he/she/it, do/does for negatives and questions.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The key contrast: present continuous = happening now / around now (temporary); present simple = in general (permanent, repeated).

The water is boiling. Can you turn it off? · Water boils at 100°C.

learn.title: Now vs in general
learn.html:
Continuous (now / temporary)Simple (general / permanent)
She's learning to drive this month.She lives in London.
I'm staying at a hotel this week.I usually stay with friends.

learn.title: Signal words help you choose
learn.html:
continuousnow, at the moment, today, this week, currently, Look!, Listen!
simplealways, usually, often, every day, normally, in general

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
You look tired.
I am. I usually sleep well, but this week I'm not sleeping much — I'm preparing for exams.
I always feel stressed before exams too.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Look! It rains.  ✓ Look! It's raining. (now)
✗
I'm going to the gym every day. (habit)  ✓ I go to the gym every day.
✗
She works late tonight. (this evening)  ✓ She's working late tonight.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Now / temporary → continuous; general / permanent → simple.
  • Look!/Listen!/at the moment → continuous.
  • always/usually/every day → simple.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Главный контраст: continuous = сейчас/в этот период (временно); simple = вообще (постоянно, регулярно): The water is boiling. · Water boils at 100°C.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Негізгі қарама-қайшылық: continuous = қазір/осы кезеңде (уақытша); simple = жалпы (тұрақты, қайталанатын): The water is boiling. · Water boils at 100°C.

ru.title: Сейчас и вообще
ru.html: Continuous: She's learning to drive this month; I'm staying at a hotel this week. Simple: She lives in London; I usually stay with friends.

kk.title: Қазір мен жалпы
kk.html: Continuous: She's learning to drive this month; I'm staying at a hotel this week. Simple: She lives in London; I usually stay with friends.

ru.title: Слова-сигналы помогают выбрать
ru.html: continuous: now, at the moment, today, this week, Look!, Listen! simple: always, usually, often, every day, normally.

kk.title: Сигнал сөздер таңдауға көмектеседі
kk.html: continuous: now, at the moment, today, this week, Look!, Listen! simple: always, usually, often, every day, normally.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «You look tired.» — «I usually sleep well, but this week I'm not sleeping much — I'm preparing for exams.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «You look tired.» — «I usually sleep well, but this week I'm not sleeping much — I'm preparing for exams.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Look! It rains → It's raining. I'm going to the gym every day → I go… every day. She works late tonight → She's working late tonight.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Look! It rains → It's raining. I'm going to the gym every day → I go… every day. She works late tonight → She's working late tonight.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Сейчас/временно → continuous; вообще/постоянно → simple. Look!/Listen!/at the moment → continuous. always/every day → simple.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Қазір/уақытша → continuous; жалпы/тұрақты → simple. Look!/Listen!/at the moment → continuous. always/every day → simple.

activities.why: 'Look!' → is snowing; a scientific fact → boils.

activities.why: a permanent fact about the road → bends.

activities.why: her permanent job → present simple works (continues: '…she's working nights').

activities.q: “I can't talk long — I ___ dinner. I ___ at about six every evening.”
why: Correct — now → am cooking; habit → cook.
The first is a live action → continuous.

activities.why: happening now/temporarily → “I'm studying for a test.”
a regular habit → present simple → “He goes to church every Sunday.”

activities.prompt: I / not eat out / this week (saving money)
activities.answer: i'm not eating out this week
alts: im not eating out this week
i am not eating out this week
i'm not eating out this week.
activities.why: a temporary situation → “I'm not eating out this week.”

activities.scene: Choose simple vs continuous by meaning.

steps.npc: Do you still play in the band?

options.t: Yes, we practise every Friday, but this month we're recording an album.
Yes, we're practising every Friday, but this month we record an album.
options.why: regular habit → practise; temporary project → are recording.

activities.why: Match the form to the meaning: temporary/now → continuous; permanent/repeated → simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some verbs describe a state, not an action. We normally use them in the present simple, even for 'now' — not the continuous.

I want to go home now. (not I'm wanting) · Do you know the answer?

learn.title: Common state (non-action) verbs
learn.html:
thinkingknow, understand, remember, believe, mean, think (=opinion)
feelingslike, love, hate, want, prefer, need
senses / havingsee, hear, seem, belong, own, have (=possess)

learn.title: Some verbs have two meanings
learn.html:
state → simpleaction → continuous
I think you're right. (opinion)I'm thinking about my plans. (mental activity)
He has a car. (possession)He's having lunch. (=eating)
I see what you mean.I'm seeing the doctor tomorrow.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Do you like the new manager?
I'm not sure yet — I'm still getting to know her. But she seems fair.
I think she's a good choice.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'm wanting a coffee.  ✓ I want a coffee.
✗
She's knowing the answer.  ✓ She knows the answer.
✗
Are you understanding?  ✓ Do you understand?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• State verbs (know, want, like, seem, believe) → present simple.
  • Some verbs = state OR action: have, think, see, taste.
  • Test: an action can be 'in progress'; a state cannot.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы обозначают состояние, а не действие. Их ставим в present simple даже для 'сейчас': I want to go now (не I'm wanting). Do you know…?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктер әрекетті емес, күйді білдіреді. Оларды 'қазір' болса да present simple-де қолданамыз: I want to go now (не I'm wanting). Do you know…?

ru.title: Частые глаголы состояния
ru.html: Мышление: know, understand, remember, believe, mean, think (мнение). Чувства: like, love, hate, want, prefer, need. Восприятие/обладание: see, hear, seem, belong, own, have (иметь).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін күй етістіктері
kk.html: Ойлау: know, understand, remember, believe, mean, think (пікір). Сезім: like, love, hate, want, prefer, need. Сезіну/иелену: see, hear, seem, belong, own, have (иелену).

ru.title: У некоторых глаголов два значения
ru.html: Состояние → simple: I think you're right; He has a car; I see what you mean. Действие → continuous: I'm thinking about it; He's having lunch; I'm seeing the doctor.

kk.title: Кейбір етістіктің екі мағынасы бар
kk.html: Күй → simple: I think you're right; He has a car; I see what you mean. Әрекет → continuous: I'm thinking about it; He's having lunch; I'm seeing the doctor.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Do you like the new manager?» — «I'm still getting to know her. But she seems fair.» — «I think she's a good choice.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Do you like the new manager?» — «I'm still getting to know her. But she seems fair.» — «I think she's a good choice.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I'm wanting a coffee → I want a coffee. She's knowing → She knows. Are you understanding? → Do you understand?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I'm wanting a coffee → I want a coffee. She's knowing → She knows. Are you understanding? → Do you understand?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Глаголы состояния (know, want, like, seem) → present simple. Некоторые = состояние ИЛИ действие: have, think, see. Тест: действие может «идти», состояние — нет.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Күй етістіктері (know, want, like, seem) → present simple. Кейбіреуі күй НЕ әрекет: have, think, see. Тест: әрекет 'болып жатады', күй 'болып жатпайды'.

activities.q: “I ___ this song. Turn it up!”

activities.why: understand and want are state verbs → present simple.
'taste' meaning 'have a flavour' is a state → tastes.

activities.prompt: I am not believing you.
alts: i do not believe you
i don't believe you.
activities.why: believe is a state verb → “I don't believe you.”

activities.before: Can I call you back? We
alts: are having

activities.q: “You look serious. What ___?” (mental activity, right now)
why: Correct — mental activity in progress → are you thinking about.

activities.why: know is a state verb → “I know the answer.”
belong is a state verb → “This bag belongs to me.”

activities.prompt: I see the dentist. (tomorrow — an arrangement)
alts: im seeing the dentist tomorrow
i am seeing the dentist tomorrow
i'm seeing the dentist tomorrow.
activities.why: see = have an appointment (action/arrangement) → “I'm seeing the dentist tomorrow.”

options.t: Lovely — but I think I need some water.
options.why: think (opinion) and need are states → simple.

options.t: Lovely — but I'm thinking I'm needing some water.
options.why: opinion + need are states → “I think I need some water.”

activities.why: State verbs (know, want, like, seem, believe) stay in the present simple; a few (have, think, see) switch to continuous when they mean an action.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The past simple describes finished actions and situations at a definite time in the past. Regular verbs add -ed; many common verbs are irregular.

I enjoyed the party a lot. · Mozart wrote more than 600 pieces of music.

learn.title: Form: same for all subjects
learn.html:
+I/you/he/we/they worked · went · saw
−I didn't work · she didn't go
?Did you work? · What did you do?
⭐
After did / didn't use the base verb — the past form appears only in positives: I went → I didn't go → Did you go?

learn.title: Spelling & pronunciation of -ed
learn.html:
Spelling: stop → stopped, study → studied, like → liked.

Three sounds: /t/ worked, /d/ played, /ɪd/ wanted, needed (an extra syllable after t/d).

learn.title: Time expressions
learn.html:
Used with finished-time words: yesterday, last week, in 2019, two hours ago, when I was a child.

I met her three years ago. · We lived abroad when we were young.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
How was your holiday?
Great! We flew to Portugal, rented a car and drove along the coast.
Sounds amazing. Did you try the local food?
Of course — we ate so much seafood!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I didn't went.  ✓ I didn't go.
✗
Did you saw it?  ✓ Did you see it?
✗
She writed a letter.  ✓ She wrote a letter.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Finished past + definite time → past simple.
  • Regular: -ed; learn the common irregulars (went, saw, wrote, took).
  • did/didn't + base verb for − and ?.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Past simple — завершённые действия в определённое время в прошлом. Правильные глаголы +-ed; многие частые — неправильные: I enjoyed the party. · Mozart wrote 600+ pieces.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Past simple — өткендегі белгілі уақыттағы аяқталған әрекеттер. Дұрыс етістіктер -ed қосады; көп жиі етістік бұрыс: I enjoyed the party. · Mozart wrote 600+ pieces.

ru.title: Форма: одинаково для всех
ru.html: + worked/went/saw. − didn't. ? Did…? После did/didn't — базовый глагол; особая форма только в утверждении.

kk.title: Тұлғасы: барлық жаққа бірдей
kk.html: + worked/went/saw. − didn't. ? Did…? did/didn't-тан кейін бастапқы етістік; бұрыс тұлға тек болымдыда.

ru.title: Написание и произношение -ed
ru.html: Написание: stopped, studied, liked. Три звука: /t/ worked, /d/ played, /ɪd/ wanted/needed (доп. слог после t/d).

kk.title: -ed емлесі мен айтылуы
kk.html: Емле: stopped, studied, liked. Үш дыбыс: /t/ worked, /d/ played, /ɪd/ wanted/needed (t/d-дан кейін қосымша буын).

ru.title: Слова времени
ru.html: Завершённое время: yesterday, last week, in 2019, two hours ago, when I was a child. I met her three years ago.

kk.title: Уақыт сөздері
kk.html: Аяқталған уақыт: yesterday, last week, in 2019, two hours ago, when I was a child. I met her three years ago.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «How was your holiday?» — «We flew to Portugal, rented a car and drove along the coast.» — «Did you try the food?» — «We ate so much seafood!»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «How was your holiday?» — «We flew to Portugal, rented a car and drove along the coast.» — «Did you try the food?» — «We ate so much seafood!»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I didn't went → didn't go. Did you saw it? → Did you see it? She writed → She wrote.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I didn't went → didn't go. Did you saw it? → Did you see it? She writed → She wrote.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Завершённое прошлое + точное время → past simple. Правильные: -ed; учите неправильные. Для − и ? — did/didn't + базовый глагол.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Аяқталған өткен + нақты уақыт → past simple. Дұрыс: -ed; бұрыстарды жаттаңыз. − мен ? үшін did/didn't + бастапқы етістік.

activities.q: “Where ___ you ___ last summer?”
why: Correct — did + base form → did you go.

activities.prompt: They understood the instructions.
alts: they did not understand the instructions
they didn't understand the instructions.

activities.prompt: Sort by the sound of the -ed ending.
activities.why: /t/ after voiceless; /d/ after voiced; /ɪd/ only after t/d.

activities.after: a child, we lived by the sea. (be)

activities.q: “I ___ my keys yesterday, so I ___ to call a locksmith.”

activities.why: base form after didn't → “didn't have enough time.”
base after did → “Did she go to university?”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a question about the past:
activities.prompt: You paid the bill.
alts: did you pay the bill?

options.t: It went well. They asked a lot of questions and I felt confident.
It goed well. They asked and I feeled confident.

activities.why: Past simple for finished actions at a definite time; learn the irregular forms and use the base verb after did/didn't.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The past continuous (was/were + -ing) describes an action in progress at a moment in the past — often interrupted by a shorter action.

This time last year I was living in Brazil. · I was walking home when I met Dan.

learn.title: Form
learn.html:
+I/he/she/it was working · you/we/they were working
−He wasn't listening.
?Were you working? · What were you doing at 8?

learn.title: Continuous (background) vs simple (interruption)
learn.html:
past continuous = the longer actionpast simple = the shorter action
I was cooking dinnerwhen the phone rang.
While she was studying,the lights went out.
⭐
Two past continuous verbs = two things happening at the same time: While I was cooking, he was watching TV.

learn.title: State verbs stay simple
learn.html:
As in the present, state verbs aren't normally used in the continuous — even in the past.

I was liking ✗ → I liked the film. · She was knowing ✗ → She knew the answer.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Sorry I missed your call.
No problem. What were you doing?
I was driving — I couldn't answer. Then when I stopped, I saw your message.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
When I arrived, she cooked. (already in progress)  ✓ When I arrived, she was cooking.
✗
They was playing.  ✓ They were playing.
✗
I was wanting to leave.  ✓ I wanted to leave.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• was/were + -ing = in progress at a past moment.
  • Background action (continuous) + interruption (simple).
  • while + past continuous; when + past simple. State verbs → past simple.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Past continuous (was/were + -ing) — действие, шедшее в момент прошлого, часто прерванное коротким: I was walking home when I met Dan.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Past continuous (was/were + -ing) — өткендегі бір сәтте болып жатқан әрекет, жиі қысқа әрекетпен бөлінеді: I was walking home when I met Dan.

ru.title: Форма
ru.html: + I/he/she was working; you/we/they were working. − wasn't/weren't. ? Were you…? / What were you doing?

kk.title: Тұлғасы
kk.html: + I/he/she was working; you/we/they were working. − wasn't/weren't. ? Were you…? / What were you doing?

ru.title: Continuous (фон) и simple (прерывание)
ru.html: Длительное — past continuous (I was cooking); короткое — past simple (when the phone rang). Два continuous = два одновременных действия.

kk.title: Continuous (фон) мен simple (бөлу)
kk.html: Ұзақ әрекет — past continuous (I was cooking); қысқасы — past simple (when the phone rang). Екі continuous = қатар болған екі әрекет.

ru.title: Глаголы состояния — в simple
ru.html: Как и в настоящем, глаголы состояния не ставятся в continuous: was liking → liked; was knowing → knew.

kk.title: Күй етістіктері simple-де қалады
kk.html: Осы шақтағыдай, күй етістіктері continuous-та қолданылмайды: was liking → liked; was knowing → knew.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Sorry I missed your call.» — «What were you doing?» — «I was driving… Then when I stopped, I saw your message.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Sorry I missed your call.» — «What were you doing?» — «I was driving… Then when I stopped, I saw your message.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: When I arrived, she cooked → she was cooking. They was playing → They were playing. I was wanting → I wanted.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: When I arrived, she cooked → she was cooking. They was playing → They were playing. I was wanting → I wanted.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: was/were + -ing = шло в момент прошлого. Фон (continuous) + прерывание (simple). while + continuous; when + simple. Глаголы состояния → past simple.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: was/were + -ing = өткен сәтте болып жатқан. Фон (continuous) + бөлу (simple). while + continuous; when + simple. Күй етістіктері → past simple.

activities.q: “I ___ a shower when the doorbell rang.”
why: The shower was already in progress.
Correct — the longer background action → was having.

items.s: We were watching TV at nine.

activities.why: they → were; know is a state verb → knew.
in progress at a past moment → was having.

activities.after: for the bus, it started to rain. (wait)
TV when I suddenly heard a loud noise outside. (watch)

activities.q: “When the teacher ___, the students ___.”
why: The entrance is a short action → simple.
'were talking' shows what was already happening.

activities.why: 'while' marks the background → “While I was cooking, the phone rang.”
want is a state verb → “They wanted to go home.”

activities.prompt: I was reading. Someone knocked on the door.
activities.answer: i was reading when someone knocked on the door
alts: i was reading when someone knocked on the door.
when someone knocked on the door i was reading
activities.why: “I was reading when someone knocked on the door.”

steps.npc: What did you see at the time of the accident?

options.t: A man was crossing the road when a car came round the corner.
A man crossed the road when a car was coming round the corner.
options.why: The crossing was in progress: “A man was crossing… when a car came.”

activities.why: Past continuous for the longer background action; past simple for the shorter interrupting action. State verbs stay simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The present perfect (have/has + past participle) connects the past to now: a finished action with a result or importance in the present, at an unspecified time.

I've lost my key. (= I can't get in now) · There's been an accident. (it affects things now)

learn.title: Form
learn.html:
+I/you/we/they have ('ve) done · he/she/it has ('s) done
−I haven't done · she hasn't done
?Have you done? · Has she done?
⭐
Learn the past participles: go→gone, see→seen, do→done, break→broken, write→written, take→taken.

learn.title: been vs gone
learn.html:
has gone (to)has been (to)
He's gone to Italy. (he's there now)She's been to Italy. (she went and came back)

learn.title: just / already / yet
learn.html:
justa short time ago: I've just had lunch.
alreadysooner than expected: I've already paid.
yetuntil now, in − and ?: Has it stopped raining yet? · It hasn't finished yet.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Is the report ready?
Almost — I've finished the first part, but I haven't checked the numbers yet.
OK. Has Mark seen it?
Yes, he's already sent me some comments.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I have finished it yesterday.  ✓ I finished it yesterday. (exact past time → past simple)
✗
She has went home.  ✓ She has gone home.
✗
Have you finished already? (neutral question)  ✓ Have you finished yet?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• have/has + past participle: a past action with a present result, no exact time.
  • gone (to) = still there; been (to) = went and back.
  • just / already / yet; no specific past-time words.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Present perfect (have/has + причастие) связывает прошлое с настоящим: завершённое действие с результатом сейчас, без точного времени: I've lost my key. · There's been an accident.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Present perfect (have/has + өткен есімше) өткенді қазірмен байланыстырады: қазіргі нәтижесі бар, нақты уақытсыз аяқталған әрекет: I've lost my key. · There's been an accident.

ru.title: Форма
ru.html: + have/has + participle. − haven't/hasn't. ? Have/Has…? Учите причастия: go→gone, see→seen, do→done, write→written.

kk.title: Тұлғасы
kk.html: + have/has + participle. − haven't/hasn't. ? Have/Has…? Есімшелерді жаттаңыз: go→gone, see→seen, do→done, write→written.

ru.title: been vs gone
ru.html: has gone (to) — всё ещё там (He's gone to Italy). has been (to) — съездил и вернулся (She's been to Italy).

kk.title: been vs gone
kk.html: has gone (to) — сонда әлі (He's gone to Italy). has been (to) — барып қайтқан (She's been to Italy).

ru.title: just / already / yet
ru.html: just — только что (I've just had lunch). already — раньше ожидаемого (I've already paid). yet — к настоящему, в − и ? (Has it stopped yet? · not yet).

kk.title: just / already / yet
kk.html: just — жаңа ғана (I've just had lunch). already — күткеннен ерте (I've already paid). yet — әзірге, − мен ?-та (Has it stopped yet? · not yet).

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Is the report ready?» — «I've finished the first part, but I haven't checked the numbers yet.» — «He's already sent me comments.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Is the report ready?» — «I've finished the first part, but I haven't checked the numbers yet.» — «He's already sent me comments.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: have finished it yesterday → finished it yesterday. has went → has gone. Нейтральный вопрос: yet, не already.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: have finished it yesterday → finished it yesterday. has went → has gone. Бейтарап сұрақ: yet, already емес.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: have/has + причастие: прошлое действие с результатом, без времени. gone = там; been = вернулся. just/already/yet; без слов точного прошлого.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: have/has + есімше: нәтижесі бар өткен әрекет, уақытсыз. gone = сонда; been = барып қайтқан. just/already/yet; нақты өткен уақыт сөзі жоқ.

activities.q: “Someone ___ the window!”

activities.before: My phone isn't working. I think it
alts: 's broken down

activities.prompt: (the film / finish)
alts: has the film finished yet?

activities.after: to the bank — she'll be back soon.'

activities.before: 'Do you want to see this film?' 'No, I've
activities.why: a positive statement, sooner than expected → already.

activities.q: “I can't find my glasses. I ___ them everywhere.”
why: No exact time; the result matters now.
Correct — search up to now with a present result → have looked.

activities.correct: (use past simple: He went to Japan last year)
activities.why: 'last year' is a finished time → past simple: “He went to Japan last year.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using the present perfect + just:
activities.prompt: He arrived a moment ago.
alts: he's just arrived
he has just arrived.
activities.why: a very recent action → “He's just arrived.”

activities.scene: Report what is done and not done.

options.t: We've done most of it, but we haven't tested it yet.
options.why: present result → have done; not yet → haven't tested yet.

options.t: We did most of it, but we don't test it yet.
options.why: present result up to now → “We've done… we haven't tested it yet.”

activities.why: Present perfect links a past action to a present result; use been/gone and just/already/yet, but a specific past time needs the past simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
We also use the present perfect for life experience (any time up to now) and for unfinished time periods (today, this week, this year).

Have you ever been to Japan? · I've seen that film three times.

learn.title: Experience: ever / never
learn.html:
experience (any time in your life)I've never eaten oysters. · Have you ever flown business class?
with numbers / how many timesShe's visited Paris twice.

learn.title: Unfinished time periods
learn.html:
When the time period includes now (today, this morning, this week, this year), the action can still continue or be repeated.

I've drunk four coffees today. (today isn't finished) · We've had a lot of problems this year.

learn.title: With superlatives & 'the first time'
learn.html:
Very common after a superlative or 'the first/second time…'.

It's the best film I've ever seen. · This is the first time I've ever ridden a horse.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Have you ever tried rock climbing?
No, I've never done it. But I've been hiking loads of times.
This is the most active I've ever felt!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Did you ever be to London?  ✓ Have you ever been to London?
✗
I never have seen snow.  ✓ I've never seen snow. (never after have)
✗
It's the best meal I ever ate.  ✓ …the best meal I've ever eaten.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Experience up to now → present perfect (ever, never, times).
  • Unfinished time (today, this week) → present perfect.
  • After superlatives and 'the first time' → present perfect + ever.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Present perfect также для жизненного опыта (когда-либо до сейчас) и для незавершённых периодов (today, this week): Have you ever been to Japan?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Present perfect-ті өмірлік тәжірибе (осы уақытқа дейін кез келген кезде) және аяқталмаған уақыт кезеңіне (today, this week) де қолданамыз: Have you ever been to Japan?

ru.title: Опыт: ever / never
ru.html: Когда-либо в жизни: I've never eaten oysters. Have you ever flown business class? С числами: She's visited Paris twice.

kk.title: Тәжірибе: ever / never
kk.html: Өмірдегі кез келген кез: I've never eaten oysters. Have you ever flown business class? Сандармен: She's visited Paris twice.

ru.title: Незавершённые периоды
ru.html: Когда период включает сейчас (today, this week, this year): I've drunk four coffees today. We've had problems this year.

kk.title: Аяқталмаған уақыт кезеңдері
kk.html: Кезең қазірді қамтыса (today, this week, this year): I've drunk four coffees today. We've had problems this year.

ru.title: Superlative & 'the first time'
ru.html: Часто после превосходной степени или 'the first time…': It's the best film I've ever seen. This is the first time I've ridden a horse.

kk.title: Superlative & 'the first time'
kk.html: Superlative-тен немесе 'the first time…'-тан кейін жиі: It's the best film I've ever seen. This is the first time I've ridden a horse.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Have you ever tried rock climbing?» — «No, I've never done it. But I've been hiking loads of times.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Have you ever tried rock climbing?» — «No, I've never done it. But I've been hiking loads of times.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Did you ever be to London? → Have you ever been. I never have seen → I've never seen. best meal I ever ate → I've ever eaten.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Did you ever be to London? → Have you ever been. I never have seen → I've never seen. best meal I ever ate → I've ever eaten.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Опыт до настоящего → present perfect (ever, never, times). Незавершённое время (today, this week) → present perfect. Superlative + ever.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Осы уақытқа дейінгі тәжірибе → present perfect (ever, never, times). Аяқталмаған уақыт (today, this week) → present perfect. Superlative + ever.

activities.q: “___ you ever ___ a famous person?”
why: Experience up to now → present perfect.

items.s: I never have broken a bone.

activities.why: never goes after have; 'Have you ever been?'.

alts: have ever

activities.prompt: (you / eat / sushi)
alts: have you ever eaten sushi?

activities.after: three cups of tea today, and it's only 10 a.m.! (have)
alts: have had

activities.q: “I ___ a lot of coffee this morning.” (said at 11 a.m.)
why: It would be past simple if the morning were over.
Correct — the morning still includes now → have drunk.

activities.why: 'the first time' + present perfect → “…the first time I've driven abroad.”

activities.prompt: I have never stayed in such a nice hotel.
activities.answer: it's the nicest hotel i've ever stayed in
alts: this is the nicest hotel i've ever stayed in
it's the nicest hotel i have ever stayed in
it's the nicest hotel i've ever stayed in.
activities.why: “It's the nicest hotel I've ever stayed in.”

steps.npc: Have you managed a team before?

options.t: Yes, I've led two projects, though I've never managed more than five people.
options.why: experience up to now → present perfect.

options.t: Yes, I led two projects, though I never managed more than five people.
options.why: life experience → “I've led… I've never managed…”

activities.why: Present perfect for life experience (ever/never/times), unfinished time periods, and after superlatives/'the first time'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The present perfect continuous (have/has been + -ing) focuses on an activity that started in the past and is still going on — or has just stopped and has visible results now.

I've been waiting for an hour. · You look tired. — Yes, I've been running.

learn.title: Form
learn.html:
+I have been ('ve been) working · she has been ('s been) working
−I haven't been working
?Have you been working? · How long have you been working?

learn.title: How long + for / since
learn.html:
Very common with how long, for and since for an activity continuing up to now.

How long have you been learning English? — I've been learning it for three years / since 2021.

learn.title: Recent activity with a present result
learn.html:
It also explains a present situation by a recent activity (finished or not).

My hands are dirty. I've been fixing the car. · Why are you out of breath? — I've been cycling.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
You look exhausted!
I've been studying all day. I've been trying to finish this essay since this morning.
How long have you been working on it?
About six hours!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I am waiting for two hours.  ✓ I've been waiting for two hours.
✗
I've been knowing her for years.  ✓ I've known her for years. (state verb → simple)
✗
She has been work here since May.  ✓ She has been working here since May.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• have/has been + -ing = an activity continuing up to now (or just stopped).
  • Common with how long, for, since; focus on the activity/duration.
  • State verbs (know, be, have=own) don't use the continuous.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Present perfect continuous (have/has been + -ing) — процесс, начавшийся в прошлом и идущий сейчас (или только что прекратившийся, с видимым результатом): I've been waiting for an hour.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Present perfect continuous (have/has been + -ing) өткенде басталып, әлі жалғасып жатқан (не жаңа тоқтап, нәтижесі көрінетін) әрекетке назар аударады: I've been waiting for an hour.

ru.title: Форма
ru.html: + have/has been + -ing. − haven't/hasn't been + -ing. ? Have you been…? / How long have you been…?

kk.title: Тұлғасы
kk.html: + have/has been + -ing. − haven't/hasn't been + -ing. ? Have you been…? / How long have you been…?

ru.title: How long + for / since
ru.html: Часто с how long, for, since: How long have you been learning English? — for three years / since 2021.

kk.title: How long + for / since
kk.html: how long, for, since-пен жиі: How long have you been learning English? — for three years / since 2021.

ru.title: Недавняя активность с результатом
ru.html: Объясняет ситуацию сейчас недавним действием: My hands are dirty. I've been fixing the car.

kk.title: Нәтижесі көрінетін жуырдағы әрекет
kk.html: Қазіргі жағдайды жуырдағы әрекетпен түсіндіреді: My hands are dirty. I've been fixing the car.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «You look exhausted!» — «I've been studying all day.» — «How long have you been working on it?» — «About six hours!»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «You look exhausted!» — «I've been studying all day.» — «How long have you been working on it?» — «About six hours!»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I am waiting for two hours → I've been waiting. I've been knowing → I've known (глагол состояния). been work → been working.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I am waiting for two hours → I've been waiting. I've been knowing → I've known (күй етістігі). been work → been working.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: have/has been + -ing = процесс до настоящего. С how long, for, since; фокус на процессе/длительности. Глаголы состояния — без continuous.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: have/has been + -ing = қазірге дейін жалғасқан әрекет. how long, for, since-пен; әрекет/ұзақтыққа назар. Күй етістіктері continuous-та емес.

Correct — recent activity explaining the result now → have been working.

Correct — recent continuous activity explaining now → been running.

activities.why: have = own is a state verb → “She has had that car since 2019.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite to focus on the ongoing activity:
activities.prompt: I started working here in March, and I still work here.

activities.scene: Explain what you've been up to.

options.why: an ongoing activity up to now → have been -ing.

options.t: I renovate my flat — it takes months.
options.why: an activity continuing over time → “I've been renovating…”

activities.why: Present perfect continuous focuses on an activity continuing up to now (how long / for / since) or explains a present result; state verbs stay simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Both connect past to now, but the focus differs. Continuous (been -ing) focuses on the activity / how long. Simple (have done) focuses on the result / how much / how many.

I've been reading a book. (activity) · I've read 200 pages. (result/amount)

learn.title: Activity vs completed result
learn.html:
continuous → activity, not finishedsimple → finished / quantity
I've been cleaning the house. (busy with it)I've cleaned the house. (it's done now)
She's been writing emails all morning.She's written ten emails.

learn.title: How long vs how much/many
learn.html:
how long? → continuousHow long have you been learning?
how much / many? → simpleHow many words have you learned?
⭐
Some verbs (live, work, study, teach) can use either for a long unchanged situation: I've lived = I've been living here for ten years.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Have you finished the design?
Not quite — I've been working on it all week. But I've done the hardest part.
How many versions have you made?
Three so far.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I've been reading three books this week. (a number)  ✓ I've read three books this week.
✗
How long have you painted the room?  ✓ How long have you been painting the room?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Continuous = activity / how long (maybe unfinished).
  • Simple = result / how much / how many (finished).
  • live/work/study: either form for a long situation.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Оба связывают прошлое с настоящим, но фокус разный. Continuous — процесс / как долго. Simple — результат / сколько: I've been reading a book. · I've read 200 pages.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Екеуі де өткенді қазірмен байланыстырады, бірақ назар басқа. Continuous — әрекет / қанша уақыт. Simple — нәтиже / қанша: I've been reading a book. · I've read 200 pages.

ru.title: Процесс и завершённый результат
ru.html: Continuous → процесс, не окончен (I've been cleaning). Simple → завершено/количество (I've cleaned; She's written ten emails).

kk.title: Әрекет пен аяқталған нәтиже
kk.html: Continuous → әрекет, аяқталмаған (I've been cleaning). Simple → аяқталған/сан (I've cleaned; She's written ten emails).

ru.title: How long vs how much/many
ru.html: how long? → continuous. how much/many? → simple. live/work/study — для долгой ситуации оба: I've lived = I've been living here for ten years.

kk.title: How long vs how much/many
kk.html: how long? → continuous. how much/many? → simple. live/work/study — ұзақ өзгермеген жайтқа екеуі де: I've lived = I've been living here for ten years.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Have you finished the design?» — «I've been working on it all week. But I've done the hardest part.» — «How many versions have you made?»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Have you finished the design?» — «I've been working on it all week. But I've done the hardest part.» — «How many versions have you made?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I've been reading three books (число) → I've read three books. How long have you painted…? → have you been painting.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I've been reading three books (сан) → I've read three books. How long have you painted…? → have you been painting.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Continuous = процесс / как долго (возможно не окончен). Simple = результат / сколько (завершено). live/work/study: для долгой ситуации оба.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Continuous = әрекет / қанша уақыт (аяқталмаса да). Simple = нәтиже / қанша (аяқталған). live/work/study: ұзақ жайтқа екеуі де.

activities.q: “I ___ six emails this morning.” (a number → result)
why: Continuous focuses on the activity, not a count.

items.s: How long have you been waiting?
I've read two books this week.
I've been reading two books this week.

activities.why: how long → continuous; a count (two books) → simple.

activities.why: the ongoing activity explaining the mess → 've been repairing.

alts: have repaired

activities.why: how much + result → present perfect simple: have you lost.

options: has been playing / has been winning

activities.why: how many times → completed count → “have you phoned him?”
activity over a period (all afternoon) → “I've been making cakes all afternoon.”

he has saved £2000

he has saved 2000 pounds.

We've raised donors all week, and we've been raising £5,000.

activities.why: Continuous = activity/how long; simple = result/how much/how many. Choose by focus.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
For a situation that started in the past and continues now, English uses the present perfect — not the present simple — to say how long.

Amy is in hospital. She's been there since Monday. (not She is there since Monday)

learn.title: Simple or continuous?
learn.html:
continuous (activity)simple (state verbs / result)
How long have you been learning English?How long have you known her? · How long have you had that car?
⭐
State verbs (know, have=own, like, be) use the simple, not the continuous.

learn.title: Present perfect, not present simple
learn.html:
The situation began in the past, so English uses a past-connected tense — even though it's still true now.

We've been married for 20 years. · How long have you lived here? (not How long do you live here?)

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
How long have you known each other?
We've been friends since university — about ten years.
And how long have you been living together?
Since last summer.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I know her for five years.  ✓ I've known her for five years.
✗
How long are you here? (already here)  ✓ How long have you been here?
✗
She's been having a headache all day.  ✓ She's had a headache all day.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Situation started in the past + continues now → present perfect (not present simple).
  • Activity → continuous; state verbs → simple.
  • How long have you …? / How long have you been …?

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Для ситуации, что началась в прошлом и длится сейчас, английский использует present perfect, а не present simple, чтобы сказать how long: She's been there since Monday.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Өткенде басталып, әлі жалғасатын жағдай үшін ағылшын how long-ты present perfect-пен айтады, present simple-мен емес: She's been there since Monday.

ru.title: Simple или continuous?
ru.html: Continuous (процесс): How long have you been learning? Simple (глаголы состояния): How long have you known her? / had that car?

kk.title: Simple әлде continuous?
kk.html: Continuous (әрекет): How long have you been learning? Simple (күй етістіктері): How long have you known her? / had that car?

ru.title: Present perfect, а не present simple
ru.html: Ситуация началась в прошлом, поэтому используется время, связанное с прошлым: We've been married for 20 years. How long have you lived here?

kk.title: Present perfect, present simple емес
kk.html: Жағдай өткенде басталған, сондықтан ағылшын өткенге қатысты шақ қолданады: We've been married for 20 years. How long have you lived here?

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «How long have you known each other?» — «We've been friends since university.» — «How long have you been living together?»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «How long have you known each other?» — «We've been friends since university.» — «How long have you been living together?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I know her for five years → I've known. How long are you here? → How long have you been here? been having a headache → 's had a headache.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I know her for five years → I've known. How long are you here? → How long have you been here? been having a headache → 's had a headache.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Началось в прошлом + длится сейчас → present perfect (не present simple). Процесс → continuous; глаголы состояния → simple.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Өткенде басталып, әлі жалғасса → present perfect (present simple емес). Әрекет → continuous; күй етістіктері → simple.

activities.q: “How long ___ you ___ your teacher?”
why: A situation continuing to now needs the present perfect.
know isn't used in the continuous.

activities.why: started in the past + continues → present perfect.

activities.after: a headache since he woke up. (have)

activities.why: a long unchanged situation → either has worked or has been working.

why: 'have this bike for years' must be present perfect.

activities.why: started in the past + continues → “We've been married since 2015.”
an activity continuing to now → “How long have you been waiting?”

activities.why: A situation that began in the past and continues now takes the present perfect (continuous for activities, simple for state verbs) — not the present simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
for + a period of time; since + a point in time (when the period started). Both answer how long? with the present perfect.

I've waited for two hours. · I've waited since 2 o'clock.

learn.title: for vs since
learn.html:
for + periodsince + starting point
for ten minutes, for a week, for years, for a long timesince Monday, since 2020, since lunchtime, since I arrived

learn.title: when …? vs how long …?
learn.html:
When …? → past simpleWhen did you start your job? — Three years ago.
How long …? → present perfectHow long have you had your job? — For three years.
⭐
"ago" goes with the past simple: I started three years ago = I've worked here for three years.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
When did you move to Berlin?
In 2019 — so I've lived here for about five years.
And how long have you been at your current job?
Since last March.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
since two hours  ✓ for two hours. (period)
✗
for Monday  ✓ since Monday. (point)
✗
When have you started?  ✓ When did you start?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• for + period; since + point in time.
  • When …? → past simple; How long …? → present perfect.
  • ago = past simple: five years ago = for five years.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: for + период; since + точка отсчёта. Оба отвечают на how long? в present perfect: for two hours · since 2 o'clock.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: for + мерзім; since + бастау нүктесі. Екеуі де how long?-қа present perfect-пен жауап береді: for two hours · since 2 o'clock.

ru.title: for vs since
ru.html: for + период: for ten minutes, for a week, for years. since + начало: since Monday, since 2020, since I arrived.

kk.title: for vs since
kk.html: for + мерзім: for ten minutes, for a week, for years. since + бастау: since Monday, since 2020, since I arrived.

ru.title: when …? vs how long …?
ru.html: When…? → past simple (When did you start? — Three years ago). How long…? → present perfect (How long have you had it? — For three years). ago → past simple.

kk.title: when …? vs how long …?
kk.html: When…? → past simple (When did you start? — Three years ago). How long…? → present perfect (How long have you had it? — For three years). ago → past simple.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «When did you move to Berlin?» — «In 2019 — so I've lived here for five years.» — «How long have you been at your job?» — «Since last March.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «When did you move to Berlin?» — «In 2019 — so I've lived here for five years.» — «How long have you been at your job?» — «Since last March.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: since two hours → for two hours. for Monday → since Monday. When have you started? → When did you start?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: since two hours → for two hours. for Monday → since Monday. When have you started? → When did you start?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: for + период; since + точка. When…? → past simple; How long…? → present perfect. ago = past simple.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: for + мерзім; since + нүкте. When…? → past simple; How long…? → present perfect. ago = past simple.

activities.q: “I've had this laptop ___ 2021.”
“___ have you had your driving licence?” (answer: For two years)
why: 'When' would take the past simple + a point.
Correct — a duration answer (For two years) → How long + present perfect.

activities.after: you buy your car?' 'Last year.' (when / you)
alts: when did
activities.why: When…? → past simple → When did you buy…?
still true now → present perfect → “How long have you lived abroad?”

activities.answer: i haven't seen her for three months
alts: i haven't seen her for three months.
i have not seen her for three months

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: getting to know someone

steps.npc: When did you take up photography?

options.why: When…? → ago (past simple); How long → for + present perfect.

options.t: Since five years, so I do it for five years.
options.why: “five years ago… I've done it for five years.”

activities.why: for + period, since + point; When…? takes the past simple, How long…? takes the present perfect.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Use the past simple for a finished time (we know or ask when). Use the present perfect for an unfinished time / no specific time / a present result.

I did a lot yesterday. · I've done a lot today. (today isn't over)

learn.title: Finished vs unfinished time
learn.html:
past simple (finished time)present perfect (up to now)
I saw Ann yesterday.I've seen Ann this week.
He lived in Rome from 2010 to 2015.He's lived in Rome since 2010. (still there)

learn.title: Time words that decide the tense
learn.html:
past simpleyesterday, last week, in 2019, ago, when …?, then
present perfecttoday, this week, ever, never, yet, so far, recently, already, just

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Have you seen the new café?
Yes! I went there on Saturday. The coffee was great.
I've heard good things but I haven't tried it yet.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I've seen him yesterday.  ✓ I saw him yesterday.
✗
When have you arrived?  ✓ When did you arrive?
✗
Did you ever eat sushi? (experience)  ✓ Have you ever eaten sushi?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Finished time (yesterday, ago, when?) → past simple.
  • Unfinished/unspecified time, present result → present perfect.
  • Let the time expression guide your choice.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Past simple — для завершённого времени (знаем/спрашиваем когда). Present perfect — незавершённое / без времени / результат сейчас: I did a lot yesterday. · I've done a lot today.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Past simple — аяқталған уақытқа (қашан екенін білеміз/сұраймыз). Present perfect — аяқталмаған уақыт / нақты уақытсыз / қазіргі нәтиже: I did a lot yesterday. · I've done a lot today.

ru.title: Завершённое и незавершённое время
ru.html: Past simple: I saw Ann yesterday; He lived in Rome from 2010 to 2015. Present perfect: I've seen Ann this week; He's lived in Rome since 2010.

kk.title: Аяқталған мен аяқталмаған уақыт
kk.html: Past simple: I saw Ann yesterday; He lived in Rome from 2010 to 2015. Present perfect: I've seen Ann this week; He's lived in Rome since 2010.

ru.title: Слова, определяющие время
ru.html: past simple: yesterday, last week, in 2019, ago, when…?, then. present perfect: today, this week, ever, never, yet, so far, recently, already, just.

kk.title: Шақты шешетін уақыт сөздері
kk.html: past simple: yesterday, last week, in 2019, ago, when…?, then. present perfect: today, this week, ever, never, yet, so far, recently, already, just.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Have you seen the new café?» — «I went there on Saturday. The coffee was great.» — «I've heard good things but I haven't tried it yet.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Have you seen the new café?» — «I went there on Saturday. The coffee was great.» — «I've heard good things but I haven't tried it yet.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I've seen him yesterday → I saw him yesterday. When have you arrived? → When did you arrive? Did you ever eat sushi? → Have you ever eaten sushi?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I've seen him yesterday → I saw him yesterday. When have you arrived? → When did you arrive? Did you ever eat sushi? → Have you ever eaten sushi?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Завершённое время (yesterday, ago, when?) → past simple. Незавершённое/без времени, результат сейчас → present perfect. Слово времени подсказывает выбор.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Аяқталған уақыт (yesterday, ago, when?) → past simple. Аяқталмаған/уақытсыз, қазіргі нәтиже → present perfect. Уақыт сөзі бағыт береді.

activities.q: “I ___ my homework. Can I go out now?”
why: No finished-time word; the result matters now.
Correct — present result, no time → 've done.

activities.after: many cures, and they're still working on more. (find)
alts: 've found
activities.why: up to now, ongoing → present perfect have found.

activities.after: my leg last week, so I can't play.' (break)

activities.q: “I ___ this city three times, and last year I ___ for a whole month.”
why: The count up to now → present perfect.
Correct — experience 've visited; last year → stayed (past).

activities.why: 'two days ago' → past simple → “I bought a new phone two days ago.”
experience → present perfect → “Have you ever been to Greece?”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using the present perfect (unfinished time):
activities.prompt: I didn't eat anything before now.
alts: i haven't eaten anything yet.
i have not eaten anything yet

activities.scene: Mix present perfect and past simple.

options.t: Yes, I had a sandwich at one. And I've just made coffee — want some?
Yes, I've had a sandwich at one. And I just made coffee.

activities.why: Finished time → past simple; unspecified/unfinished time or present result → present perfect.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A finished action often has a result now. British English usually uses the present perfect; but with a specific past time, we must switch to the past simple.

I've lost my passport. (result: I can't travel) · I lost it on Monday. (specific time)

learn.title: Same situation, two tenses
learn.html:
present perfect (result now)past simple (when it happened)
She's gone out. (she's not here)She went out an hour ago.
They've cancelled the match.They cancelled it because of the rain.

learn.title: News → details
learn.html:
We often announce news with the present perfect, then give details in the past simple.

The company has launched a new phone. It revealed the price yesterday and said it would ship in June.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Guess what — I've got a new job!
That's great! When did you find out?
They called me this morning. I start next month.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The train has left five minutes ago.  ✓ The train left five minutes ago.
✗
I've broke my arm last year.  ✓ I broke my arm last year.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Result now, no time stated → present perfect.
  • Add a specific past time → switch to past simple.
  • Headline/news → present perfect; details → past simple.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: У завершённого действия часто есть результат сейчас. Британский английский обычно использует present perfect; но с точным прошлым временем — past simple: I've lost my passport. · I lost it on Monday.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Аяқталған әрекеттің қазіргі нәтижесі болады. Британдық ағылшын жиі present perfect; бірақ нақты өткен уақыт болса, past simple-ке ауысамыз: I've lost my passport. · I lost it on Monday.

ru.title: Одна ситуация, два времени
ru.html: Present perfect (результат): She's gone out. Past simple (когда): She went out an hour ago.

kk.title: Бір жағдай, екі шақ
kk.html: Present perfect (нәтиже): She's gone out. Past simple (қашан): She went out an hour ago.

ru.title: Новость → детали
ru.html: Новость часто сообщаем в present perfect, затем детали в past simple: The company has launched… It revealed the price yesterday.

kk.title: Жаңалық → мәлімет
kk.html: Жаңалықты жиі present perfect-пен хабарлаймыз, содан кейін мәліметті past simple-мен: The company has launched… It revealed the price yesterday.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «I've got a new job!» — «When did you find out?» — «They called me this morning.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «I've got a new job!» — «When did you find out?» — «They called me this morning.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: has left five minutes ago → left five minutes ago. I've broke my arm last year → I broke my arm last year.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: has left five minutes ago → left five minutes ago. I've broke my arm last year → I broke my arm last year.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Результат, без времени → present perfect. Точное прошлое время → past simple. Новость → present perfect; детали → past simple.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Нәтиже, уақытсыз → present perfect. Нақты өткен уақыт → past simple. Жаңалық → present perfect; мәлімет → past simple.

activities.q: “I ___ my keys!” (you notice now, no time given)
why: A time would be needed for the past simple.
Correct — a present result, no time → 've lost.

items.s: He's just arrived ten minutes ago.

activities.why: a specific past time forces the past simple.

alts: 's spilt
has spilled
's spilled
activities.why: a present result, no time → has spilt.

activities.after: my wallet at the station this morning. (leave)
the cup! They beat City 2–0 last night. (win)
alts: 've won
have won
activities.why: the headline result → present perfect has won (details follow in the past).

activities.q: “Prices ___ sharply. They ___ by 10% last month alone.”
why: The trend statement uses present perfect.

activities.why: a specific clock time → past simple → “The meeting started at nine.”
When…? → past simple → “When did the parcel arrive?”

activities.instruction: Announce as news (present perfect), then add the detail:
activities.prompt: decide / to move abroad (we / last week)
activities.answer: we've decided to move abroad. we made the decision last week
alts: we have decided to move abroad. we made the decision last week
we've decided to move abroad; we made the decision last week
activities.why: news → “We've decided…”; detail → “We made the decision last week.”

options.t: I've passed my driving test! I took it this morning.
I passed my driving test! I've taken it this morning.
options.why: news → “I've passed”; detail → “I took it this morning.”

activities.why: A present result with no stated time → present perfect; add a specific past time and switch to the past simple (news → details).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The past perfect (had + past participle) is the "past before the past": an action that happened before another past action or time.

When I arrived at the party, Tom had already gone home. (he left first, then I arrived)

learn.title: Form
learn.html:
+I/you/he/we/they had ('d) done
−I hadn't done
?Had you done …?
⭐
'd can mean had or would — the following verb shows which: I'd finished (had); I'd go (would).

learn.title: Past perfect vs past simple
learn.html:
earlier action → past perfectlater action → past simple
The film had startedwhen we got to the cinema.
She had studied Frenchbefore she moved to Paris.

If the order is clear (e.g. with after/before), the past simple is often enough: After she finished, she went home.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Why were you so hungry?
Because I hadn't eaten all day — I'd been too busy.
And the meeting?
By the time I got there, they had already started.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
When I arrived, the train already left.  ✓ …the train had already left.
✗
I had went  ✓ I had gone. (past participle)
✗
Don't overuse it: for a simple sequence, past simple is fine — I got up, had a shower and left.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• had + past participle = an action before another past action.
  • Earlier event = past perfect; later event = past simple.
  • Use it to make the order of past events clear.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Past perfect (had + причастие) — «прошлое до прошлого»: действие раньше другого прошлого: When I arrived, Tom had already gone home.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Past perfect (had + өткен есімше) — «өткеннен бұрынғы өткен»: басқа өткен әрекеттен бұрын болған әрекет: When I arrived, Tom had already gone home.

ru.title: Форма
ru.html: + had ('d) done. − hadn't done. ? Had you done…? 'd = had или would; различает следующий глагол (I'd finished / I'd go).

kk.title: Тұлғасы
kk.html: + had ('d) done. − hadn't done. ? Had you done…? 'd = had немесе would; келесі етістік ажыратады (I'd finished / I'd go).

ru.title: Past perfect vs past simple
ru.html: Более раннее → past perfect (The film had started); более позднее → past simple (when we got there). С after/before, если порядок ясен, достаточно past simple.

kk.title: Past perfect vs past simple
kk.html: Ертерек әрекет → past perfect (The film had started); кейінгі → past simple (when we got there). after/before-пен рет анық болса, past simple жеткілікті.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Why were you so hungry?» — «Because I hadn't eaten all day.» — «By the time I got there, they had already started.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Why were you so hungry?» — «Because I hadn't eaten all day.» — «By the time I got there, they had already started.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the train already left → the train had already left. I had went → I had gone. Не злоупотребляйте — для простой последовательности хватает past simple.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the train already left → the train had already left. I had went → I had gone. Артық қолданбаңыз — қарапайым ретте past simple жеткілікті.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: had + причастие = действие до другого прошлого. Раньше = past perfect; позже = past simple. Чтобы показать порядок прошлых событий.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: had + есімше = басқа өткеннен бұрынғы әрекет. Ертерек = past perfect; кейінгі = past simple. Өткен оқиғалардың ретін анықтау үшін.

activities.q: “When we got home, the babysitter ___.”
why: That would be the same time.
Correct — she left before we got home → had left.

items.s: By 2010 he had written three novels.
By 2010 he had wrote three novels.
I'd never seen snow before that trip.
When I arrived, the show already finished.

activities.before: I couldn't get in because I
alts: 'd left

activities.prompt: She had met him before.
alts: she had not met him before
she hadn't met him before.

activities.after: , we tidied up the whole house. (leave)
alts: 'd left
left
activities.why: the earlier event → had left (or past simple with 'after', since order is clear).

activities.q: “The ground was wet because it ___ during the night.”
why: The wet ground implies an earlier cause.
Correct — rain came before we noticed the wet ground → had rained.
Not the focus on duration here.

activities.why: past participle → “she had already gone to bed.”
do → done: “I had done my homework before dinner.”

activities.instruction: Join with the past perfect for the earlier action:
activities.prompt: The concert started. Then we arrived.
activities.answer: the concert had started when we arrived
alts: the concert had already started when we arrived
when we arrived the concert had started
the concert had started when we arrived.

activities.scene: Use the past perfect for earlier causes.

steps.npc: Why didn't you come to the cinema?

options.t: By the time I finished work, the film had already begun.
options.why: earlier event → had begun; later → finished (past simple).

options.t: By the time I finished work, the film already began.

activities.why: Past perfect (had + participle) marks the earlier of two past events; the later event stays in the past simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The past perfect continuous (had been + -ing) describes an activity that had been in progress before a moment in the past — often explaining a past situation or its result.

Her eyes were red. She'd been crying. · We were tired because we'd been travelling all day.

learn.title: Form
learn.html:
+I/he/we had been ('d been) waiting
−I hadn't been waiting
?How long had you been waiting?

learn.title: vs past perfect simple
learn.html:
continuous → activity / durationsimple → completed / result
He was out of breath — he'd been running.He was pleased — he'd run a marathon.
I'd been reading for hours.By 9 p.m. I'd read 100 pages.
⭐
State verbs use the simple: I'd known her for years (not 'd been knowing).

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
You looked upset yesterday.
I was. I'd been waiting two hours and the train was cancelled.
How long had you been standing there?
Far too long!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I was tired because I worked all day. (to stress duration)  ✓ …because I'd been working all day.
✗
She had been knowing him for years.  ✓ She'd known him for years.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• had been + -ing = an activity in progress before a past moment.
  • Often explains a past situation/result (tired, wet, red eyes).
  • Continuous = activity/duration; simple = completed. State verbs → simple.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Past perfect continuous (had been + -ing) — процесс до момента в прошлом, часто объясняет прошлую ситуацию/результат: She'd been crying. · We'd been travelling all day.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Past perfect continuous (had been + -ing) — өткендегі бір сәтке дейін болып жатқан әрекет, жиі өткен жағдайды/нәтижені түсіндіреді: She'd been crying. · We'd been travelling all day.

ru.title: Форма
ru.html: + had been ('d been) waiting. − hadn't been waiting. ? How long had you been waiting?

kk.title: Тұлғасы
kk.html: + had been ('d been) waiting. − hadn't been waiting. ? How long had you been waiting?

ru.title: Сравнение с simple
ru.html: Continuous → процесс/длительность (he'd been running). Simple → завершено/результат (he'd run a marathon). Глаголы состояния simple: I'd known her.

kk.title: Simple-мен салыстыру
kk.html: Continuous → әрекет/ұзақтық (he'd been running). Simple → аяқталған/нәтиже (he'd run a marathon). Күй етістіктері simple: I'd known her.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «You looked upset.» — «I'd been waiting two hours and the train was cancelled.» — «How long had you been standing there?»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «You looked upset.» — «I'd been waiting two hours and the train was cancelled.» — «How long had you been standing there?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: because I worked all day (акцент на длительности) → because I'd been working. had been knowing → 'd known.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: because I worked all day (ұзақтыққа назар) → because I'd been working. had been knowing → 'd known.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: had been + -ing = процесс до момента прошлого. Часто объясняет ситуацию. Continuous = процесс; simple = завершено. Глаголы состояния → simple.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: had been + -ing = өткен сәтке дейінгі әрекет. Жиі өткен жағдайды түсіндіреді. Continuous = әрекет; simple = аяқталған. Күй етістіктері → simple.

activities.q: “The players were exhausted. They ___ for 90 minutes.”
why: Simple stresses completion, not the tiring activity.
Correct — a long activity explaining 'exhausted' → had been playing.

items.s: My feet hurt because I'd been walking all day.
I'd been knowing her for years.
By noon she'd been driving for six hours.
He was tired because he been working.

alts: 'd been raining
activities.why: an activity before that past moment → had been raining.

activities.prompt: (they / wait) before the taxi came?
activities.answer: how long had they been waiting
alts: how long had they been waiting?

activities.before: When I met her, she was happy — she
alts: 'd just got
had just gotten
'd just gotten
activities.why: a completed achievement → past perfect simple had just got.

activities.q: “By the time the film ended, I ___ asleep twice, and I ___ the ending anyway.”
why: 'fall asleep' is completed, not a process.
'fall asleep' is a completed event → simple.
Correct — both completed events → past perfect simple.

activities.why: stress the duration up to that moment → “she'd been waiting for an hour.”
had been + -ing → “We had been living there for years.”

activities.prompt: His hands were covered in oil. (fix / the car)
alts: he had been fixing the car
he'd been fixing the car.
activities.why: a recent activity explaining the result → “He'd been fixing the car.”

activities.scene: Explain a past situation with its cause.

steps.npc: Why was the kitchen so messy when you got home?

options.t: The kids had been baking all afternoon and hadn't cleaned up.
options.why: activity before that moment → had been baking; completed → hadn't cleaned.

options.t: The kids baked all afternoon and don't clean up.
options.why: an activity up to that past moment → “had been baking… hadn't cleaned up.”

activities.why: Past perfect continuous (had been -ing) shows an activity in progress before a past moment and often explains a past result; state verbs and completed results use the simple.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
For possession, relationships, illnesses and features, have and have got mean the same. have got is more informal/spoken; have is neutral.

I've got a headache. = I have a headache. · She's got two brothers. = She has two brothers.

learn.title: Different question & negative forms
learn.html:
have (with do/does)have got (with have/has)
