kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: should to rest → should rest. He oughts to go → He ought to go. Do you should call? → Should you call?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: should / ought to = совет, мнение, 'вероятно'. Мягче must; + базовый глагол (ought to + глагол). Вопрос/отрицание без do.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: should / ought to = кеңес, пікір, 'ықтимал'. must-тан жұмсақ; + бастапқы етістік (ought to + етістік). do-сыз сұрақ/болымсыз.

activities.q: “You look exhausted. You ___ take a break.”
why: 'must' is too strong for friendly advice.

activities.why: no 'to' after should; ought to has no -s.

alts: should not

activities.prompt: You should call your parents more often.
activities.answer: you ought to call your parents more often
alts: you ought to call your parents more often.
activities.why: “You ought to call your parents more often.”

activities.before: The train left on time, so it

activities.q: “I've studied hard, so I ___ do well in the exam.”
why: Correct — a likely outcome → should do well.

activities.prompt: It's a bad idea to skip breakfast.
alts: you shouldn't skip breakfast.
you should not skip breakfast

steps.npc: I'm nervous about my presentation tomorrow.

options.t: You should practise tonight, and you shouldn't stay up too late.
You should to practise tonight, and you shouldn't staying up late.

activities.why: should / ought to give advice or opinions and can express expectation ('it should be ready'); softer than must, always with the base verb.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
should have + past participle = the right thing to do in the past, but it didn't happen (regret/criticism). shouldn't have = something happened that was wrong.

I should have studied harder. (I didn't) · You shouldn't have said that.

learn.title: Regret & criticism about the past
learn.html:
should have done (didn't, but should)shouldn't have done (did, but shouldn't)
We should have booked a table. (now it's full)You shouldn't have spent so much.

learn.title: should after certain verbs
learn.html:
should often follows verbs/adjectives of suggesting and importance: suggest, recommend, insist, it's important/essential that …

I suggest (that) she should apply. · It's important that everyone should be on time. (also: … everyone be on time)

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
I failed the interview.
Don't worry. Maybe you should have prepared more, but you'll do better next time.
Yeah — I shouldn't have stayed up so late the night before.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I should studied more.  ✓ I should have studied more.
✗
You should of told me.  ✓ You should have told me.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• should have done = the right past action that didn't happen (regret).
  • shouldn't have done = a wrong past action that did happen.
  • Write 'should have' (should've), never 'should of'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: should have + причастие = правильно было бы в прошлом, но не случилось (сожаление/упрёк). shouldn't have = случилось то, чего не следовало: I should have studied harder. · You shouldn't have said that.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: should have + есімше = өткенде дұрыс еді, бірақ болмады (өкіну/сын). shouldn't have = дұрыс емес нәрсе болды: I should have studied harder. · You shouldn't have said that.

ru.title: Сожаление и упрёк о прошлом
ru.html: should have done (не сделал, но надо было): We should have booked a table. shouldn't have done (сделал, но не следовало): You shouldn't have spent so much.

kk.title: Өткен туралы өкіну мен сын
kk.html: should have done (болмады, бірақ керек еді): We should have booked a table. shouldn't have done (болды, бірақ керек емес): You shouldn't have spent so much.

ru.title: should после некоторых глаголов
ru.html: Часто после suggest, recommend, insist, it's important/essential that…: I suggest she should apply. It's important that everyone should be on time.

kk.title: Кейбір етістіктерден кейін should
kk.html: suggest, recommend, insist, it's important/essential that…-дан кейін жиі: I suggest she should apply. It's important that everyone should be on time.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «I failed the interview.» — «Maybe you should have prepared more.» — «I shouldn't have stayed up so late.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «I failed the interview.» — «Maybe you should have prepared more.» — «I shouldn't have stayed up so late.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I should studied more → should have studied. You should of told me → should have told me.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I should studied more → should have studied. You should of told me → should have told me.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: should have done = правильное прошлое действие, что не случилось. shouldn't have done = случившееся неверное действие. Пишем 'should have', не 'should of'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: should have done = болмаған дұрыс өткен әрекет (өкіну). shouldn't have done = болған қате әрекет. 'should have', 'should of' емес.

activities.q: “I missed the train. I ___ earlier.”
why: Correct — a past regret → should have left.

items.s: I shouldn't have eaten so much.

alts: should've come

activities.prompt: He told everyone your secret.
activities.answer: he shouldn't have told everyone your secret
alts: he shouldn't have told everyone your secret.
he should not have told everyone your secret

activities.q: “Your plant died.” “You ___ it more often.” (advice about what didn't happen)
why: Correct — regret about the past → should have watered.

activities.why: 'should have', never 'should of' → “We should have booked earlier.”
past participle → “You shouldn't have lent him money.”

activities.prompt: I didn't save the file, and I lost my work.
activities.answer: i should have saved the file
alts: i should've saved the file
i should have saved the file.

steps.npc: I ignored the warning light and now the engine's broken.

options.t: You should have taken it to a garage — and you shouldn't have kept driving.
You should take it to a garage — and you shouldn't kept driving.

activities.why: should have + past participle = the right past action that didn't happen; shouldn't have + past participle = a wrong action that did happen (never 'should of').

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
had better ('d better) + base verb = strong advice for now/the near future, often with a warning. It's time (+ subject + past) = it should happen now, but hasn't.

You'd better hurry or you'll miss it. · It's time we left.

learn.title: had better
learn.html:
+I'd better go now.
−You'd better not be late.
⭐
'd better = had better (not 'would'), but it refers to now/the future, not the past. + base verb (no 'to').

learn.title: It's time …
learn.html:
It's time to + verbIt's time to go. (general)
It's time + subject + pastIt's time you went to bed. (= you should already)
It's (high/about) timeIt's high time they did something!

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
It's nearly midnight.
You're right — we'd better get going. It's time we went home.
You'd better not forget your keys this time!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
You'd better to go.  ✓ You'd better go.
✗
You'd better don't be late.  ✓ You'd better not be late.
✗
It's time you go.  ✓ It's time you went.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• had better + base verb = urgent advice now (often a warning).
  • Negative: 'd better not (+ base verb).
  • It's time + subject + past simple = it should have happened already.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: had better ('d better) + глагол = настойчивый совет на сейчас/ближайшее будущее, часто с предупреждением. It's time (+ subject + прош.) = пора, но ещё не произошло: You'd better hurry. · It's time we left.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: had better ('d better) + етістік = қазір/жақын болашаққа қатты кеңес, жиі ескертумен. It's time (+ subject + өткен) = болу керек, бірақ болмады: You'd better hurry. · It's time we left.

ru.title: had better
ru.html: + I'd better go. − You'd better not be late. 'd better = had better (не would), но о настоящем/будущем; + базовый глагол.

kk.title: had better
kk.html: + I'd better go. − You'd better not be late. 'd better = had better (would емес), бірақ қазір/болашаққа қатысты; + бастапқы етістік.

ru.title: It's time …
ru.html: It's time to go (общее). It's time you went to bed (давно пора). It's high time they did something!

kk.title: It's time …
kk.html: It's time to go (жалпы). It's time you went to bed (баяғыда болу керек). It's high time they did something!

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «It's nearly midnight.» — «We'd better get going. It's time we went home.» — «You'd better not forget your keys!»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «It's nearly midnight.» — «We'd better get going. It's time we went home.» — «You'd better not forget your keys!»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: You'd better to go → You'd better go. You'd better don't be late → You'd better not be late. It's time you go → It's time you went.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: You'd better to go → You'd better go. You'd better don't be late → You'd better not be late. It's time you go → It's time you went.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: had better + глагол = срочный совет сейчас (часто предупреждение). Отрицание: 'd better not. It's time + subject + past simple = давно пора.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: had better + етістік = қазір шұғыл кеңес (жиі ескерту). Болымсыз: 'd better not. It's time + subject + past simple = баяғыда болу керек еді.

activities.q: “The train leaves in five minutes. We ___ run!”
why: Correct — urgent advice now → 'd better run.

items.s: You'd better to take an umbrella.

activities.why: 'd better + base verb; It's time + subject + past.

activities.after: tell anyone — it's a surprise. ('d better not)
alts: had better not

activities.prompt: You should get a job. (you don't have one yet)
activities.answer: it's time you got a job
alts: it's time you got a job.

activities.before: This room is a mess. It's time you

activities.q: (a general recommendation, no urgency) “You ___ eat more vegetables.”

activities.why: It's time + subject + past → “It's time the children went to bed.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as urgent advice with 'had better':
activities.prompt: It's important for you to leave now.
alts: you had better leave now
you'd better leave now.

steps.npc: We're going to be late for the film.

options.t: You're right — it's time we left. We'd better take a taxi.
options.why: It's time + past (left); 'd better + base verb.

options.t: You're right — it's time we leave. We'd better to take a taxi.
options.why: “it's time we left… we'd better take a taxi.”

activities.why: had better + base verb = urgent advice now (often a warning); It's time + subject + past simple = it should already be happening.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
would ('d) has several uses: the result in conditionals, past habits, polite offers/requests, and reporting the future in the past.

If I had time, I would help. · As a child, I would spend hours reading. · Would you like a coffee?

learn.title: Main uses
learn.html:
imaginary resultI'd love to travel more (if I could).
past habit (like used to)Every summer we'd visit my grandparents.
willingness / refusal in the pastI asked him, but he wouldn't help.
future in the past (reported)She said she would call.

learn.title: Polite offers, requests & preferences
learn.html:
Would you like …? (offer) · Would you mind …? (request) · I'd rather / I'd prefer (preference).

Would you like to sit down? · I'd rather stay in tonight.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Would you like to join us for dinner?
I'd love to, thanks! Though I'd rather not eat too late.
No problem. My grandmother would always say, "Eat early, sleep well!"

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I would to help.  ✓ I would help. / I'd help.
✗
Past states → use 'used to', not 'would': We would have a dog.  ✓ We used to have a dog.
✗
I'd rather to stay.  ✓ I'd rather stay.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• would = imaginary result, past habit, polite offer/request, future-in-past.
  • Past habits only for actions (would visit); states → used to.
  • would + base verb; I'd rather + base verb.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: У would ('d) несколько употреблений: результат в условных, прошлая привычка, вежливое предложение/просьба, будущее в прошедшем: If I had time, I would help. · Would you like a coffee?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: would ('d)-тың бірнеше қолданысы бар: шартты сөйлемдегі нәтиже, өткен әдет, сыпайы ұсыныс/өтініш, өткендегі болашақ: If I had time, I would help. · Would you like a coffee?

ru.title: Основные употребления
ru.html: Воображаемый результат: I'd love to travel. Прошлая привычка (как used to): we'd visit my grandparents. Отказ: he wouldn't help. Будущее в прошедшем: She said she would call.

kk.title: Негізгі қолданыстары
kk.html: Қиялдағы нәтиже: I'd love to travel. Өткен әдет (used to сияқты): we'd visit my grandparents. Бас тарту: he wouldn't help. Өткендегі болашақ: She said she would call.

ru.title: Вежливое предложение, просьба, предпочтение
ru.html: Would you like…? (предложение) · Would you mind…? (просьба) · I'd rather / I'd prefer (предпочтение): I'd rather stay in tonight.

kk.title: Сыпайы ұсыныс, өтініш, артық көру
kk.html: Would you like…? (ұсыныс) · Would you mind…? (өтініш) · I'd rather / I'd prefer (артық көру): I'd rather stay in tonight.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Would you like to join us for dinner?» — «I'd love to! Though I'd rather not eat too late.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Would you like to join us for dinner?» — «I'd love to! Though I'd rather not eat too late.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I would to help → I would help. Прошлое состояние → used to: We would have a dog → We used to have a dog. I'd rather to stay → I'd rather stay.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I would to help → I would help. Өткен күй → used to: We would have a dog → We used to have a dog. I'd rather to stay → I'd rather stay.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: would = воображаемый результат, прошлая привычка, вежливое предложение/просьба, будущее в прошедшем. Прошлая привычка только для действий (would visit); состояния → used to.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: would = қиялдағы нәтиже, өткен әдет, сыпайы ұсыныс/өтініш, өткендегі болашақ. Өткен әдет тек әрекетке (would visit); күй → used to. would + бастапқы етістік.

activities.q: “When we were kids, we ___ play in the woods all day.” (a repeated past action)
why: Correct — a repeated past action → would (= used to).

items.s: We would have a big garden (past state).

activities.why: would + base verb; past states → used to.

alts: would

activities.instruction: Say you prefer the second option:
activities.prompt: Cinema or a walk? (a walk)
activities.answer: i'd rather go for a walk
alts: i would rather go for a walk
i'd rather go for a walk.
activities.why: “I'd rather go for a walk.” (base verb)

activities.before: I tried to warn him, but he
alts: would not

activities.why: Would you mind + -ing → “Would you mind opening the window?”

activities.instruction: Report the promise (future in the past):
activities.prompt: 'I will help you,' she said.
activities.answer: she said she would help me
alts: she said (that) she would help me
she said she would help me.
activities.why: will → would → “She said she would help me.”

steps.npc: Tell me about your childhood summers.

options.t: We'd go to the lake every day, and my dad would build a fire in the evening.
We would to go to the lake, and my dad will build a fire.

activities.why: would = conditional result, past habitual actions (like used to), polite offers/requests, and future-in-the-past; use used to for past states.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Modal verbs make polite requests, offers, permission and suggestions. More past-tense modals (could, would) usually sound more polite/tentative than present ones.

Could you help me? · Can I use your phone? · Shall we start?

learn.title: Requests & permission
learn.html:
ask others to do (request)Can/Could/Would you …? · Would you mind + -ing?
ask to do something (permission)Can/Could/May I …?
⭐
could/would = more polite than can; may = formal permission.

learn.title: Offers & suggestions
learn.html:
offer to helpCan/Shall I …? · Would you like me to …?
suggest togetherShall we …? · Why don't we …? · How about + -ing?

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Could you pass me the salt, please?
Sure. Would you like me to get you anything else?
No thanks. Shall we order dessert?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Would you mind to help?  ✓ Would you mind helping?
✗
Can you to open the door?  ✓ Can you open the door?
✗
"Would you mind opening it?" → agree with Yes  ✓ No, not at all. (= I don't mind)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Requests: Can/Could/Would you …? Would you mind + -ing?
  • Permission: Can/Could/May I …? Offers: Can/Shall I …? Suggestions: Shall we …?
  • could/would/may are more polite/formal.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Модальные делают вежливые просьбы, предложения, разрешения и предложения-советы. Прошедшие модальные (could, would) звучат вежливее: Could you help me? · Can I use your phone?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Модаль етістіктер сыпайы өтініш, ұсыныс, рұқсат және кеңес жасайды. Өткен тұлғалы модальдер (could, would) сыпайырақ естіледі: Could you help me? · Can I use your phone?

ru.title: Просьба и разрешение
ru.html: Просьба к другим: Can/Could/Would you…? / Would you mind + -ing? Разрешение: Can/Could/May I…? could/would вежливее can; may — формально.

kk.title: Өтініш пен рұқсат
kk.html: Басқаға өтініш: Can/Could/Would you…? / Would you mind + -ing? Рұқсат сұрау: Can/Could/May I…? could/would — can-нан сыпайы; may — ресми.

ru.title: Предложение и совет
ru.html: Предложить помощь: Can/Shall I…? / Would you like me to…? Предложить вместе: Shall we…? / Why don't we…? / How about + -ing?

kk.title: Ұсыныс пен кеңес
kk.html: Көмек ұсыну: Can/Shall I…? / Would you like me to…? Бірге ұсыну: Shall we…? / Why don't we…? / How about + -ing?

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Could you pass me the salt?» — «Sure. Would you like me to get you anything else?» — «Shall we order dessert?»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Could you pass me the salt?» — «Sure. Would you like me to get you anything else?» — «Shall we order dessert?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Would you mind to help? → Would you mind helping? Can you to open…? → Can you open? Согласие на «Would you mind…?»: No, not at all.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Would you mind to help? → Would you mind helping? Can you to open…? → Can you open? «Would you mind…?»-қа келісім: No, not at all.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Просьба: Can/Could/Would you…? Would you mind + -ing? Разрешение: Can/Could/May I…? Предложение: Shall I…? Вместе: Shall we…? could/would/may вежливее.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Өтініш: Can/Could/Would you…? Would you mind + -ing? Рұқсат: Can/Could/May I…? Ұсыныс: Shall I…? Бірге: Shall we…? could/would/may — сыпайырақ.

activities.q: You want to borrow someone's pen. You ask:
options: Could you lend me a pen?
why: That asks them to lend — also possible, but focuses on them.
Correct — asking to do something yourself → Could I borrow…?

items.s: Would you mind closing the door?
Would you mind to close the door?
Can I ask you a question?

activities.why: Would you mind + -ing → turning the music down.

activities.prompt: (we / take / a break)
alts: shall we take a break?

activities.q: “Would you mind if I opened the window?”
why: Correct — to agree, say No, not at all (= I don't mind).

activities.after: I help you find the way? (offer)
alts: Shall
can
shall

activities.why: mind + -ing → “Would you mind passing me the report?”

activities.prompt: Give me a hand.
activities.answer: could you give me a hand
alts: could you give me a hand?
could you give me a hand, please
could you give me a hand please

options.t: Could I have a room with a view, and would you mind calling a taxi later?
Can I to have a room with a view, and would you mind to call a taxi?
options.why: “Could I have… would you mind calling a taxi?”

activities.why: Use Can/Could/Would you …? and Would you mind + -ing for requests; Can/Could/May I …? for permission; Shall I/we …? for offers and suggestions — could/would/may sound more polite.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Two conditionals for present/future situations. if + present, will (real/likely). if + past, would (unreal/imaginary or unlikely).

If it rains, we'll stay in. (real possibility) · If I won the lottery, I'd travel. (imaginary)

learn.title: Real (1st) vs unreal (2nd)
learn.html:
first — real/possiblesecond — unreal/unlikely
If I have time, I'll help you.If I had time, I'd help you. (but I don't)
If you heat ice, it melts. (always true — zero)If I were you, I'd apologise.

learn.title: Form notes
learn.html:
No will/would in the if-clause. In the second conditional we often use were for all persons, especially If I were you … (advice). The result can also use could or might.

If she were here, she'd know. · If we left now, we might catch the train.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
What will you do if you get the job?
If they offer it, I'll take it. But if I had a real choice, I'd rather work abroad.
If I were you, I'd go for it either way.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
If I will see her, I'll tell her.  ✓ If I see her, I'll tell her.
✗
If I would have money, I'd travel.  ✓ If I had money, I'd travel.
✗
If I had time, I will help.  ✓ If I had time, I'd help.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Real/likely → if + present, will. Unreal/unlikely → if + past, would.
  • No will/would in the if-clause.
  • 2nd conditional: were for all persons; result can use could/might.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Два условных для настоящего/будущего. if + наст., will (реально/вероятно). if + past, would (нереально/маловероятно): If it rains, we'll stay in. · If I won the lottery, I'd travel.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Осы/болашақ үшін екі шартты. if + осы шақ, will (нақты/мүмкін). if + past, would (қиял/екіталай): If it rains, we'll stay in. · If I won the lottery, I'd travel.

ru.title: Реально (1) и нереально (2)
ru.html: first: If I have time, I'll help. second: If I had time, I'd help (но нет). Совет: If I were you, I'd apologise.

kk.title: Нақты (1) мен қиял (2)
kk.html: first: If I have time, I'll help. second: If I had time, I'd help (бірақ жоқ). Advice: If I were you, I'd apologise.

ru.title: О форме
ru.html: В if-части нет will/would. Во втором часто were для всех лиц (особ. If I were you). В результате возможны could/might.

kk.title: Тұлға туралы
kk.html: if-бөлігінде will/would жоқ. Second-те барлық жаққа жиі were (әсіресе If I were you). Нәтижеде could/might болуы мүмкін.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «What will you do if you get the job?» — «If they offer it, I'll take it. But if I had a real choice, I'd rather work abroad.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «What will you do if you get the job?» — «If they offer it, I'll take it. But if I had a real choice, I'd rather work abroad.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: If I will see her → If I see her. If I would have money → If I had money. If I had time, I will help → I'd help.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: If I will see her → If I see her. If I would have money → If I had money. If I had time, I will help → I'd help.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Реально → if + наст., will. Нереально → if + past, would. В if-части нет will/would. Во 2-м were; в результате could/might.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Нақты → if + осы, will. Қиял → if + past, would. if-бөлігінде will/would жоқ. 2-де were; нәтижеде could/might.

activities.q: “If I ___ rich, I'd buy an island.” (I'm not rich)

items.s: If it will rain, we'll cancel.
If I knew, I'd tell you.
If I would know, I'd tell you.

activities.after: that button, the machine will stop. (press)
a car, I'd drive to work. (have)

activities.q: “If we ___ now, we ___ the last bus.” (a real plan)

alts: was

activities.why: if-clause → past simple → “If I had more space…”
match halves: “If she studies, she will pass” or “If she studied, she would pass.”

activities.prompt: If I have time, I'll learn to paint.
activities.answer: if i had time, i'd learn to paint
alts: if i had time i'd learn to paint
if i had time, i would learn to paint
if i had time i would learn to paint
activities.why: have→had, 'll→'d → “If I had time, I'd learn to paint.”

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: real plans vs dreams

steps.npc: It might be sunny at the weekend.

options.t: If it's sunny, we'll go to the coast.
If it would be sunny, we'd go to the coast.
options.why: real plan → “If it's sunny, we'll go.”

options.t: If I could live anywhere, I'd choose Kyoto.
If I can live anywhere, I'll choose Kyoto.

activities.why: Real/likely → if + present, will; unreal/unlikely → if + past, would. Never put will/would in the if-clause.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To talk about an unreal present (imagining now differently), we use the past simple after if and after I wish.

If I knew her number, I'd call her. (I don't know it) · I wish I knew her number. (but I don't)

learn.title: I wish + past simple
learn.html:
I wish / If only + past simple = I want a present situation to be different (but it isn't).

I wish I had more time. · I wish I could help. · If only he were here.

learn.title: were for all persons
learn.html:
After wish/if, we often use were instead of 'was' for all persons (formal but common).

I wish I were taller. · If I were you, I'd rest.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
I'm stuck on this problem.
I wish I could help, but I don't understand it either.
If only we had the answer key!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I wish I have more money.  ✓ I wish I had more money.
✗
I wish I can fly.  ✓ I wish I could fly.
✗
If only she would be here now.  ✓ If only she were here now.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Unreal present → past simple after if and I wish.
  • I wish + past = I want now to be different.
  • were for all persons; wish I could = I'd like the ability now.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Для нереального настоящего (представить сейчас иначе) после if и I wish — past simple: If I knew her number, I'd call. · I wish I knew her number.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Шындыққа жанаспайтын осы шақ үшін (қазірді басқаша елестету) if пен I wish-тен кейін past simple: If I knew her number, I'd call. · I wish I knew her number.

ru.title: I wish + past simple
ru.html: I wish / If only + past simple = хочу, чтобы сейчас было иначе (но нет): I wish I had more time. I wish I could help.

kk.title: I wish + past simple
kk.html: I wish / If only + past simple = қазіргі жағдай басқаша болғанын қалаймын (бірақ олай емес): I wish I had more time. I wish I could help.

ru.title: were для всех лиц
ru.html: После wish/if часто were вместо 'was': I wish I were taller. If I were you, I'd rest.

kk.title: were — барлық жаққа
kk.html: wish/if-тен кейін жиі 'was' орнына were: I wish I were taller. If I were you, I'd rest.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «I'm stuck.» — «I wish I could help, but I don't understand it either.» — «If only we had the answer key!»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «I'm stuck.» — «I wish I could help, but I don't understand it either.» — «If only we had the answer key!»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I wish I have more money → I wish I had. I wish I can fly → I wish I could fly. If only she would be here → If only she were here.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I wish I have more money → I wish I had. I wish I can fly → I wish I could fly. If only she would be here → If only she were here.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Нереальное настоящее → past simple после if и I wish. I wish + past = хочу иначе сейчас. were для всех; wish I could = хочу способность сейчас.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Шындыққа жанаспайтын осы → if пен I wish-тен кейін past simple. I wish + past = қазірді басқаша қалаймын. were барлық жаққа; wish I could = қазір қабілет қалаймын.

activities.q: “I ___ I lived closer to work.” (I don't)
why: Correct — a regret about now → wish + past.

items.s: I wish I had a car.
I wish I have a car.

activities.why: wish + past simple; wish I could (not can).

activities.before: It's so noisy here. I wish it
alts: was

activities.prompt: I don't know how to drive.
activities.answer: i wish i knew how to drive
alts: i wish i knew how to drive.

activities.after: your address, I'd send you a card. (know)

activities.typeLabel: wish I could / wish I had
activities.q: “I can't play any instrument.” “I ___ play the piano.”
why: Correct — an ability you lack → wish I could play.

activities.why: wish + past simple → “I wish I had more free time.”
unreal present → “If only he were more patient.”

activities.instruction: Express the same idea with 'I wish':
activities.prompt: I'm sorry I'm not taller.
alts: i wish i was taller
i wish i were taller.

options.t: I know. I wish we had more support, and if only the deadlines weren't so tight.
I know. I wish we have more support, and if only the deadlines wouldn't be so tight.
options.why: “I wish we had… if only the deadlines weren't…”

activities.why: For an unreal present, use the past simple after if and after I wish / If only; use were for all persons and 'wish I could' for a missing ability.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To imagine the past differently (it's too late to change), use the third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle. And I wish + past perfect for past regrets.

If I had known, I would have come. · I wish I had studied harder.

learn.title: Third conditional form
learn.html:
if-clauseif + had + past participle: If she had left earlier …
resultwould have + past participle: … she would have caught the train.
⭐
The result can also use could have / might have: We might have won if we'd played better.

learn.title: I wish + past perfect (past regret)
learn.html:
I wish / If only + past perfect = I regret something in the past.

I wish I hadn't said that. · If only we had booked earlier.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
I missed the deadline.
If you had told me, I would have helped you finish it.
I know. I wish I had asked for help sooner.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
If I would have known …  ✓ If I had known …
✗
I wish I would have gone.  ✓ I wish I had gone.
✗
She would have came.  ✓ She would have come.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Unreal past → if + past perfect, would have + past participle.
  • Past regret → I wish / If only + past perfect.
  • No 'would' in the if-clause; result can use could/might have.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Чтобы представить прошлое иначе (уже поздно менять) — третий условный: if + past perfect, would have + причастие. Для сожаления — I wish + past perfect: If I had known, I would have come. · I wish I had studied harder.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Өткенді басқаша елестету үшін (өзгертуге кеш) үшінші шартты: if + past perfect, would have + есімше. Өткен өкініш үшін I wish + past perfect: If I had known, I would have come. · I wish I had studied harder.

ru.title: Форма третьего условного
ru.html: if-часть: if + had + причастие. Результат: would have + причастие. Возможны could have / might have: We might have won if we'd played better.

kk.title: Үшінші шартты тұлғасы
kk.html: if-бөлігі: if + had + есімше. Нәтиже: would have + есімше. could have / might have да болуы мүмкін: We might have won if we'd played better.

ru.title: I wish + past perfect (сожаление)
ru.html: I wish / If only + past perfect = сожалею о прошлом: I wish I hadn't said that. If only we had booked earlier.

kk.title: I wish + past perfect (өткен өкініш)
kk.html: I wish / If only + past perfect = өткендегі бір нәрсеге өкінемін: I wish I hadn't said that. If only we had booked earlier.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «I missed the deadline.» — «If you had told me, I would have helped you.» — «I wish I had asked for help sooner.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «I missed the deadline.» — «If you had told me, I would have helped you.» — «I wish I had asked for help sooner.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: If I would have known → If I had known. I wish I would have gone → I wish I had gone. would have came → would have come.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: If I would have known → If I had known. I wish I would have gone → I wish I had gone. would have came → would have come.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Нереальное прошлое → if + past perfect, would have + причастие. Сожаление → I wish / If only + past perfect. В if-части нет 'would'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Шындыққа жанаспайтын өткен → if + past perfect, would have + есімше. Өткен өкініш → I wish / If only + past perfect. if-бөлігінде 'would' жоқ.

activities.q: “If you had asked me, I ___ you.”

items.s: If I had seen it, I'd have told you.
If I would have seen it, I'd have told you.
I wish I hadn't eaten so much.
I wish I wouldn't have eaten so much.

activities.why: if + past perfect (not would have); wish + past perfect.

alts: 'd rung

activities.before: If I had studied medicine, I
alts: 'd be
activities.why: mixed conditional: past cause → present result → would be.

activities.after: I had taken that job — it sounded perfect. (wish)

activities.q: “We ___ the flight if the taxi ___ on time.”
why: Correct — unreal past → would have + past perfect.
This describes the past, not a real plan.

activities.why: if-clause → past perfect → “If I had known, I would have stayed.”
past participle → “I wish I had gone to university.”

activities.prompt: I didn't set an alarm, so I was late.
activities.answer: if i had set an alarm, i wouldn't have been late
alts: if i'd set an alarm, i wouldn't have been late
if i had set an alarm i wouldn't have been late
if i'd set an alarm i wouldn't have been late
activities.why: “If I had set an alarm, I wouldn't have been late.”

options.t: If we had prepared better, we might have won it. I wish we'd started earlier.
options.why: 3rd conditional (had prepared / might have won) + wish + past perfect.

options.t: If we would have prepared better, we might won it. I wish we started earlier.
options.why: “If we had prepared… we might have won… I wish we'd started…”

activities.why: Unreal past → if + past perfect, would have + past participle; past regret → I wish / If only + past perfect. Never use 'would' in the if-clause.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
wish expresses a desire for a different reality. The tense after 'wish' tells you which time: past simple (now), past perfect (past), would (annoyance / a change).

I wish I had a garden. · I wish I had listened. · I wish it would stop raining!

learn.title: Three patterns
learn.html:
wish + past simplean unreal present: I wish I spoke Italian.
wish + past perfecta past regret: I wish I had saved more.
wish + wouldannoyance / want someone to change: I wish you would stop interrupting.

learn.title: wish + would — important limits
learn.html:
wish … would is for other people's behaviour or things you can't control — not your own actions, and not for states.

I wish it would stop raining. · I wish I would be taller → I wish I were taller.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
My neighbours play loud music every night.
I wish they would turn it down! And I wish I lived somewhere quieter.
I wish I had chosen a different flat.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I wish I would have a car.  ✓ I wish I had a car.
✗
I wish I would be more organised.  ✓ I wish I were more organised.
✗
I wish I studied harder last year.  ✓ I wish I had studied harder last year.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• wish + past simple = unreal present; wish + past perfect = past regret.
  • wish + would = annoyance / want a change (not your own actions or states).
  • If only … works the same way and is more emphatic.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: wish выражает желание иной реальности. Время после 'wish' указывает момент: past simple (сейчас), past perfect (прошлое), would (раздражение/перемена): I wish I had a garden. · I wish it would stop raining!

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: wish басқа шындықты қалауды білдіреді. 'wish'-тен кейінгі шақ уақытты көрсетеді: past simple (қазір), past perfect (өткен), would (ашулану/өзгеріс): I wish I had a garden. · I wish it would stop raining!

ru.title: Три модели
ru.html: wish + past simple — нереальное настоящее (I wish I spoke Italian). wish + past perfect — сожаление (I wish I had saved more). wish + would — раздражение/желание перемены (I wish you would stop).

kk.title: Үш үлгі
kk.html: wish + past simple — қазіргі шындыққа жанаспау (I wish I spoke Italian). wish + past perfect — өткен өкініш (I wish I had saved more). wish + would — ашулану/өзгеріс тілеу (I wish you would stop).

ru.title: wish + would — важные ограничения
ru.html: wish … would — о поведении других или неподконтрольном; не о своих действиях и не о состояниях: I wish I would be taller → I wish I were taller.

kk.title: wish + would — маңызды шектеулер
kk.html: wish … would — басқа адамдардың мінез-құлқы немесе бақыламайтын нәрселерге; өз әрекеттеріңе де, күйге де емес: I wish I would be taller → I wish I were taller.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «My neighbours play loud music every night.» — «I wish they would turn it down! And I wish I lived somewhere quieter.» — «I wish I had chosen a different flat.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «My neighbours play loud music every night.» — «I wish they would turn it down! And I wish I lived somewhere quieter.» — «I wish I had chosen a different flat.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I wish I would have a car → I wish I had a car. I wish I would be more organised → I wish I were. I wish I studied harder last year → I wish I had studied.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I wish I would have a car → I wish I had a car. I wish I would be more organised → I wish I were. I wish I studied harder last year → I wish I had studied.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: wish + past simple = нереальное настоящее; wish + past perfect = сожаление. wish + would = раздражение/перемена (не свои действия/состояния). If only — так же, но эмоциональнее.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: wish + past simple = қазіргі қиял; wish + past perfect = өткен өкініш. wish + would = ашулану/өзгеріс (өз әрекетің/күй емес). If only — сол сияқты, күштірек.

activities.q: “I wish I ___ that email — it was a big mistake.” (a past regret)
why: 'would' can't refer to your own past action.

items.s: I wish I had a bigger house.
I wish I would have a bigger house.
I wish it would stop snowing.
I wish I studied harder last term.

activities.why: present → past simple; past → past perfect; would only for others/uncontrollable.

alts: 'd taken

activities.before: My computer keeps crashing. I wish it
activities.why: annoyance about something you can't control → would work.

activities.q: “I wish you ___ me the truth more often.” (their behaviour)
why: 'told' would describe an unreal present state.
Correct — you want them to change → would tell.

activities.why: your own situation now → “I wish I lived by the sea.”
a past regret → “If only I had listened to you.”

activities.prompt: I'm sorry I didn't apply for the course.
activities.answer: i wish i had applied for the course
alts: i wish i'd applied for the course
i wish i had applied for the course.
activities.why: a past regret → “I wish I had applied for the course.”

steps.npc: Your flatmate never does the dishes.

options.t: I know — I wish he would help, and I wish I'd never agreed to share.
options.why: wish + would (his behaviour) + wish + past perfect (regret).

options.t: I know — I wish he helped, and I wish I would never agree to share.
options.why: his behaviour → “I wish he would help”; regret → “I wish I'd never agreed.”

activities.why: wish + past simple = unreal present; wish + past perfect = past regret; wish + would = wanting someone/something (not yourself) to change.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
In the passive, the action and the thing affected become the focus; who does it is unknown, obvious or unimportant. Form: be + past participle.

Active: Somebody cleans this room every day. → Passive: This room is cleaned every day.

learn.title: Present & past simple passive
learn.html:
presentam/is/are + participle: English is spoken here. · These cars are made in Japan.
pastwas/were + participle: The bridge was built in 1990. · We were invited to the wedding.

learn.title: The agent: by …
learn.html:
Add by + agent only when it gives useful information (often a person, artist, cause).

The window was broken by a child. · Penicillin was discovered by Fleming. (but: My car was stolen. — agent unknown)

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
What happened to the office?
It 's being renovated. The old furniture was removed last week.
And the new desks?
They 're made in Italy — they 'll be delivered soon.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The house built in 1990.  ✓ The house was built in 1990.
✗
English is speak here.  ✓ English is spoken here.
✗
It was happened yesterday.  ✓ It happened yesterday. ('happen' has no passive)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Passive = be + past participle; change 'be' for the tense.
  • Use it when the doer is unknown, obvious or unimportant.
  • Add 'by + agent' only when it's useful.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: В пассиве в центре — действие и объект; исполнитель неизвестен/неважен. Форма: be + причастие: This room is cleaned every day.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ырықсыз етісте әрекет пен нысан басты назарда; кім істегені белгісіз/маңызсыз. Тұлғасы: be + есімше: This room is cleaned every day.

ru.title: Настоящий/прошедший пассив
ru.html: Наст.: am/is/are + причастие (English is spoken). Прош.: was/were + причастие (The bridge was built in 1990).

kk.title: Осы/өткен ырықсыз
kk.html: Осы: am/is/are + есімше (English is spoken). Өткен: was/were + есімше (The bridge was built in 1990).

ru.title: Агент: by …
ru.html: Деятеля через by + агент — только если важно: The window was broken by a child. Если неизвестен: My car was stolen.

kk.title: Агент: by …
kk.html: Істеушіні by + агент — тек пайдалы болса: The window was broken by a child. Белгісіз болса: My car was stolen.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «What happened to the office?» — «It's being renovated. The old furniture was removed last week.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «What happened to the office?» — «It's being renovated. The old furniture was removed last week.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: The house built in 1990 → was built. English is speak here → is spoken. It was happened → It happened (happen без пассива).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: The house built in 1990 → was built. English is speak here → is spoken. It was happened → It happened (happen ырықсыз болмайды).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Пассив = be + причастие; be меняется по времени. Когда исполнитель неизвестен/неважен. 'by + агент' только если полезно.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Ырықсыз = be + есімше; be шаққа қарай өзгереді. Кім істегені белгісіз/маңызсыз болғанда. 'by + агент' тек пайдалы болса.

activities.q: Which is passive?

activities.instruction: Rewrite in the passive (drop the obvious agent):
activities.prompt: Someone has stolen my bike.
alts: my bike has been stolen.
activities.why: present perfect passive → “My bike has been stolen.”

activities.q: “Look — the road ___ at the moment, so we'll have to go round.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite in the passive, keeping the agent:
activities.prompt: A dog bit the postman.
activities.answer: the postman was bitten by a dog
alts: the postman was bitten by a dog.

steps.npc: What's the latest on the museum?

options.t: A new wing is being built, and it will be opened next year.
A new wing is building, and it will open by someone next year.
options.why: “A new wing is being built… it will be opened next year.”

activities.why: Passive = be + past participle (changed for the tense); use it when the doer is unknown/obvious/unimportant, adding 'by + agent' only when useful.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The passive works across all tenses. Keep the tense in be, then add the past participle.

It is done · It is being done · It has been done · It will be done · It is going to be done.

learn.title: Passive forms by tense
learn.html:
present continuousam/is/are being + participle: The car is being repaired.
present perfecthave/has been + participle: The work has been finished.
past perfecthad been + participle: The room had been booked.
future / going towill be / be going to be + participle: It will be sent tomorrow.

learn.title: Passive with modals & infinitives
learn.html:
modal + be + participle; to be + participle; being + participle.

The report must be finished today. · It needs to be checked. · I don't like being told what to do.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Has the contract been signed?
Not yet — it 's still being reviewed. It should be finished by Friday.
Good. Everyone needs to be informed once it's done.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The house is build now.  ✓ The house is being built now.
✗
It has been finish.  ✓ It has been finished.
✗
It must be do today.  ✓ It must be done today.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Keep the tense in 'be'; add the past participle.
  • continuous → being + participle; perfect → been + participle.
  • modal + be + participle; to be + participle; being + participle.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Пассив работает во всех временах. Время держим в be, затем причастие: It is being done · It has been done · It will be done.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ырықсыз етіс барлық шақта жұмыс істейді. Шақты be-де сақтап, есімше қосасыз: It is being done · It has been done · It will be done.

ru.title: Пассив по временам
ru.html: continuous: being + причастие (is being repaired). perfect: been + причастие (has been finished). past perfect: had been + причастие. future: will be + причастие.

kk.title: Шақ бойынша ырықсыз тұлғалар
kk.html: continuous: being + есімше (is being repaired). perfect: been + есімше (has been finished). past perfect: had been + есімше. future: will be + есімше.

ru.title: С модальными и инфинитивом
ru.html: modal + be + причастие; to be + причастие; being + причастие: must be finished; needs to be checked; I don't like being told.

kk.title: Модаль мен инфинитивпен
kk.html: modal + be + есімше; to be + есімше; being + есімше: must be finished; needs to be checked; I don't like being told.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Has the contract been signed?» — «It's still being reviewed. It should be finished by Friday.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Has the contract been signed?» — «It's still being reviewed. It should be finished by Friday.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: is build now → is being built. has been finish → has been finished. must be do → must be done.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: is build now → is being built. has been finish → has been finished. must be do → must be done.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Время держим в 'be'; добавляем причастие. continuous → being + причастие; perfect → been + причастие. modal + be + причастие.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Шақты 'be'-де сақтаңыз; есімше қосыңыз. continuous → being + есімше; perfect → been + есімше. modal + be + есімше.

activities.q: “The bridge ___ at the moment.”

activities.why: use the past participle after been/be.

alts: 's been solved
had been prepared already
activities.why: past perfect passive → had already been prepared.

activities.q: “The results ___ next week.”
why: Active, but the doer is irrelevant.

activities.prompt: They have cancelled all the flights.
activities.answer: all the flights have been cancelled
alts: all the flights have been cancelled.

options.t: It's being tested now, and the design has already been approved.
options.why: present continuous passive + present perfect passive.

options.t: It's testing now, and the design has already approved.
options.why: “It's being tested… the design has been approved.”

activities.why: Keep the tense in 'be' and add the past participle; use being + participle for continuous, been + participle for perfect, and modal/to be + participle where needed.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some special passive patterns. be born is always passive. get can replace be in informal passives. Verbs with two objects can make two passives.

I was born in 1998. · My phone got broken. · She was given a prize.

learn.title: be born; get + participle
learn.html:
be bornalways passive: Where were you born? (not did you born)
get + participle (informal)often for changes/accidents: He got hurt. · They got married. · I got paid today.

learn.title: Verbs with two objects
learn.html:
give, send, offer, show, tell, pay, teach → two possible passive subjects. The person version is more common.

They gave Anna a bonus. → Anna was given a bonus. / A bonus was given to Anna.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Where were you born?
In Almaty. My family got moved to Astana when I was ten.
And your new job — were you offered a good salary?
Yes, I was given a great package.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I born in 2000.  ✓ I was born in 2000.
✗
She was explained the rules.  ✓ The rules were explained to her. (explain doesn't take a person-passive)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• be born is always passive (was/were born).
  • get + participle = an informal passive (often accidents/changes).
  • Two-object verbs → 'the person was given/told/offered …'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Особые пассивные модели. be born всегда пассив. get заменяет be в разговорном пассиве. Глаголы с двумя дополнениями дают два пассива: I was born in 1998. · My phone got broken. · She was given a prize.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір ерекше ырықсыз үлгілер. be born әрқашан ырықсыз. get бейресми ырықсызда be-ні алмастырады. Екі толықтауышты етістіктер екі ырықсыз жасайды: I was born in 1998. · My phone got broken. · She was given a prize.

ru.title: be born; get + причастие
ru.html: be born — всегда пассив: Where were you born? get + причастие (разг., часто перемены/случайности): He got hurt. They got married.

kk.title: be born; get + есімше
kk.html: be born — әрқашан ырықсыз: Where were you born? get + есімше (бейресми, жиі өзгеріс/жазатайым): He got hurt. They got married.

ru.title: Глаголы с двумя дополнениями
ru.html: give, send, offer, show, tell, pay, teach → два подлежащих для пассива. Вариант с лицом чаще: Anna was given a bonus.

kk.title: Екі толықтауышты етістіктер
kk.html: give, send, offer, show, tell, pay, teach → екі ырықсыз ие. Адам нұсқасы жиірек: Anna was given a bonus.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Where were you born?» — «In Almaty.» «Were you offered a good salary?» — «I was given a great package.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Where were you born?» — «In Almaty.» «Were you offered a good salary?» — «I was given a great package.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I born in 2000 → I was born. She was explained the rules → The rules were explained to her.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I born in 2000 → I was born. She was explained the rules → The rules were explained to her.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: be born всегда пассив. get + причастие = разговорный пассив. Двухобъектные глаголы → 'the person was given/told/offered…'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: be born әрқашан ырықсыз. get + есімше = бейресми ырықсыз. Екі толықтауышты етістіктер → 'the person was given/told/offered…'.

activities.q: “When ___ you ___?”

activities.why: was born (not 'born'); get + participle is fine informally.

activities.prompt: They offered Tom a new contract.
activities.answer: tom was offered a new contract
alts: tom was offered a new contract.

activities.before: Be careful with that glass — it might
activities.why: informal passive for an accident → get broken.

activities.q: “They told the students the news.” → most natural passive:
options: The news was told the students.
The students were told the news.
why: Correct — person first → The students were told the news.

activities.why: 'was born' doesn't take an agent → “Shakespeare was born in 1564.”

activities.prompt: Someone stole my wallet on the bus.
activities.answer: my wallet got stolen on the bus
alts: my wallet got stolen on the bus.
activities.why: informal passive → “My wallet got stolen on the bus.”

steps.npc: Tell me a bit about yourself.

options.t: I was born in Karaganda, and last year I was offered a scholarship abroad.
I born in Karaganda, and last year I was offered by a scholarship abroad.

activities.why: be born is always passive; get + participle is an informal passive; verbs with two objects usually make the person the passive subject.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To report what people in general say/believe, we use two passive structures with verbs like say, think, believe, know, report, expect.

It is said that he is very rich. = He is said to be very rich.

learn.title: Two patterns
learn.html:
It + is + participle + that …It is believed that the painting is genuine.
Subject + is + participle + to + verbThe painting is believed to be genuine.

learn.title: Reporting the past: to have done
learn.html:
For an earlier action, use to have + past participle.

It is thought that he left the country. = He is thought to have left the country.

learn.title: be supposed to
learn.html:
be supposed to = (1) people say/expect; (2) it's the plan/rule.

This restaurant is supposed to be excellent. (people say) · You 're supposed to wear a helmet. (it's the rule)

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
He is said that he is rich.  ✓ He is said to be rich. / It is said that he is rich.
✗
He is thought to left.  ✓ He is thought to have left.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• It is said/thought/believed that … (impersonal).
  • Subject + is said/thought to + verb (personal).
  • Earlier action → to have + past participle; be supposed to = people say / it's the rule.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Чтобы передать, что говорят/считают вообще, две пассивные конструкции с say, think, believe, know, report: It is said that he is rich. = He is said to be rich.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Жұрт жалпы айтатын/сенетінін жеткізу үшін say, think, believe, know, report сияқты етістіктермен екі ырықсыз құрылым: It is said that he is rich. = He is said to be rich.

ru.title: Две модели
ru.html: It + is + причастие + that…: It is believed that the painting is genuine. Subject + is + причастие + to + глагол: The painting is believed to be genuine.

kk.title: Екі үлгі
kk.html: It + is + есімше + that…: It is believed that the painting is genuine. Subject + is + есімше + to + етістік: The painting is believed to be genuine.

ru.title: Прошлое: to have done
ru.html: Для более раннего действия — to have + причастие: He is thought to have left the country.

kk.title: Өткенді жеткізу: to have done
kk.html: Ертерек әрекет үшін to have + есімше: He is thought to have left the country.

ru.title: be supposed to
ru.html: be supposed to = (1) говорят/ожидают; (2) план/правило: It is supposed to be excellent. You're supposed to wear a helmet.

kk.title: be supposed to
kk.html: be supposed to = (1) жұрт айтады/күтеді; (2) жоспар/ереже: It is supposed to be excellent. You're supposed to wear a helmet.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: He is said that he is rich → He is said to be rich. He is thought to left → to have left.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: He is said that he is rich → He is said to be rich. He is thought to left → to have left.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: It is said/thought/believed that… (безлично). Subject + is said/thought to + глагол. Раннее действие → to have + причастие; be supposed to = говорят / правило.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: It is said/thought/believed that… (жалпы). Subject + is said/thought to + етістік. Ертерек әрекет → to have + есімше; be supposed to = айтады / ереже.

activities.why: an earlier action → “He is known to have robbed a bank.”
“It is reported that the fire started in the kitchen.”

activities.prompt: People say this beach is beautiful.
activities.answer: this beach is supposed to be beautiful
alts: this beach is supposed to be beautiful.

steps.npc: Is the new CEO any good?

options.t: She's said to be very effective, and she's supposed to arrive next week.
options.why: is said to be + is supposed to (expectation).

options.t: She's said that very effective, and she's supposed arrive next week.
options.why: “She's said to be effective… she's supposed to arrive.”

activities.why: Report general beliefs with 'It is said/thought that …' or 'Subject is said/thought to …'; use 'to have done' for earlier actions and 'be supposed to' for what people say or the rule.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
have something done = arrange for someone else to do a service for you. Form: have + object + past participle.

I had my hair cut yesterday. (a hairdresser did it) · We're having the kitchen painted.

learn.title: Form across tenses
learn.html:
presentI have my car serviced every year.
pastShe had her photo taken.
future / going toWe're going to have a new bathroom fitted.
⭐
Compare: I cut my hair. (I did it myself) vs I had my hair cut. (someone did it for me)

learn.title: Also for unpleasant events
learn.html:
have/get something done can also describe something bad that happened to you (not arranged).

He had his wallet stolen. · We got our flight cancelled.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Your car looks great!
Thanks — I had it cleaned and I'm getting the brakes checked tomorrow.
Good idea. I need to have mine repaired too.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I had cut my hair. (meaning at the salon)  ✓ I had my hair cut. (object before the participle)
✗
We had painted the house (by builders)  ✓ We had the house painted.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• have + object + past participle = arrange a service.
  • Word order: have + THING + done (not have done + thing).
  • Also for bad things done to you: had his phone stolen.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: have something done = организовать, чтобы кто-то другой сделал услугу. Форма: have + object + причастие: I had my hair cut yesterday.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: have something done = басқа біреуге қызмет істетуді ұйымдастыру. Тұлғасы: have + object + есімше: I had my hair cut yesterday.

ru.title: По временам
ru.html: Наст.: I have my car serviced. Прош.: She had her photo taken. Буд.: We're going to have a bathroom fitted. Сравни: I cut my hair (сам) vs I had my hair cut.

kk.title: Шақтар бойынша
kk.html: Осы: I have my car serviced. Өткен: She had her photo taken. Болашақ: We're going to have a bathroom fitted. Салыстыр: I cut my hair (өзім) vs I had my hair cut.

ru.title: И для неприятностей
ru.html: have/get something done описывает и плохое, что случилось с вами: He had his wallet stolen. We got our flight cancelled.

kk.title: Жағымсыз оқиғаларға да
kk.html: have/get something done сізге болған жаман нәрсені де білдіреді: He had his wallet stolen. We got our flight cancelled.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Your car looks great!» — «I had it cleaned and I'm getting the brakes checked tomorrow.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Your car looks great!» — «I had it cleaned and I'm getting the brakes checked tomorrow.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I had cut my hair (в салоне) → I had my hair cut. We had painted the house (мастера) → We had the house painted.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I had cut my hair (салонда) → I had my hair cut. We had painted the house (шеберлер) → We had the house painted.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: have + object + причастие = организовать услугу. Порядок: have + ВЕЩЬ + done. И для плохого: had his phone stolen.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: have + object + есімше = қызмет ұйымдастыру. Сөз реті: have + ЗАТ + done. Жаман оқиғаға да: had his phone stolen.

activities.q: “I had my suit cleaned.” This means…
why: That would be 'I cleaned my suit.'
Correct — a service by someone else → had it cleaned.

items.s: She had repaired her car (at a garage).

activities.why: word order: have + object + past participle.

activities.prompt: A dentist checked my teeth.
alts: i had my teeth checked.
are going to have
activities.why: a planned service → “We're going to have new windows fitted.”

activities.q: “I painted the fence.” vs “I had the fence painted.” Which shows a professional did it?
why: Correct — a service → I had the fence painted.

activities.why: object before the participle → “I had my hair cut.”
a bad event → “He had his bike stolen.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a bad experience (have something done):
activities.prompt: Someone stole my passport.
alts: i had my passport stolen.
i got my passport stolen

options.t: Yes, we had the walls repainted and we're getting new carpets fitted.
options.why: had + object + participle; getting + object + participle.

options.t: Yes, we had repainted the walls and we're getting fitted new carpets.
options.why: “we had the walls repainted… getting new carpets fitted.”

activities.why: have + object + past participle = arrange a service (someone does it for you); the same structure describes bad things done to you (had his bike stolen).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
In reported (indirect) speech we tell someone what another person said. After a past reporting verb (said, told), the tense usually shifts back one step.

"I'm tired." → She said (that) she was tired.

learn.title: The backshift
learn.html:
present → pastam/is/are → was/were · do/does → did · have → had
past → past perfectdid → had done
will → would · can → couldmay → might · must → had to

learn.title: Pronouns, time & place words change too
learn.html:
now → thentoday → that day · tomorrow → the next day
here → there · this → that"I'll do it tomorrow." → He said he'd do it the next day.
⭐
If it's still true or the reporting verb is present, you needn't shift: She says she's tired.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
What did Mia say?
She said she was busy and that she'd call me later.
Did she say where she was?
She told me she was working from home.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
He said he is tired.  ✓ He said he was tired.
✗
She said she will come.  ✓ She said she would come.
✗
He said me that …  ✓ He told me that … / He said that …

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• After a past reporting verb, shift the tense back one step.
  • Change pronouns and time/place words (today→that day, here→there).
  • No shift needed if still true or the reporting verb is present.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: В косвенной речи передаём чужие слова. После прошедшего глагола (said, told) время обычно сдвигается назад: "I'm tired." → She said she was tired.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Төлеу сөзде басқаның сөзін жеткіземіз. Өткен реттеуші етістіктен (said, told) кейін шақ әдетте бір саты артқа жылжиды: "I'm tired." → She said she was tired.

ru.title: Backshift (сдвиг назад)
ru.html: present → past (is→was, do→did, have→had); past → past perfect; will→would, can→could, may→might, must→had to.

kk.title: Backshift (артқа жылжу)
kk.html: present → past (is→was, do→did, have→had); past → past perfect; will→would, can→could, may→might, must→had to.

ru.title: Местоимения, время, место тоже
ru.html: now→then, today→that day, tomorrow→the next day, here→there, this→that. Если всё ещё верно или глагол в настоящем — сдвиг не нужен.

kk.title: Есімдік, уақыт, орын сөздері де
kk.html: now→then, today→that day, tomorrow→the next day, here→there, this→that. Егер әлі шындық болса немесе етістік осы шақта болса — жылжыту қажет емес.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «What did Mia say?» — «She said she was busy and that she'd call me later.» — «She told me she was working from home.»

kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «What did Mia say?» — «She said she was busy and that she'd call me later.» — «She told me she was working from home.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: He said he is tired → he was tired. She said she will come → she would come. He said me → He told me / He said.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: He said he is tired → he was tired. She said she will come → she would come. He said me → He told me / He said.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: После прошедшего глагола время на шаг назад. Меняем местоимения и слова времени/места. Если всё ещё верно/настоящее — без сдвига.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Өткен реттеуші етістіктен кейін шақ бір саты артқа. Есімдік пен уақыт/орын сөздерін өзгертіңіз. Әлі шындық/осы шақ болса — жылжыту жоқ.

activities.q: "I will help you." → She said she ___ help me.

items.s: He said he is busy (reporting later).
She told me she had finished.
She said me she had finished.

activities.prompt: "I don't feel well."
activities.answer: she said she didn't feel well
alts: she said (that) she didn't feel well
she said she did not feel well
she said she didn't feel well.

activities.prompt: "I can't come to the meeting."
activities.answer: he told me he couldn't come to the meeting
alts: he told me (that) he couldn't come to the meeting
he told me he could not come to the meeting
he told me he couldn't come to the meeting.

activities.before: "I saw her yesterday." → He said he had seen her
alts: the previous day

activities.q: Reporting a general truth now: "Water boils at 100°C." → She said that water ___
why: Backshift isn't required for a permanent truth.
Correct — a general truth can stay → boils.

activities.why: will → would: “She said she would phone the next day.”
'tell' needs a person: “He told me that…” or “He said that…”

activities.prompt: Anna: "I have already finished my part."
activities.answer: anna said she had already finished her part
alts: anna said (that) she had already finished her part
anna said she had already finished her part.
activities.why: present perfect → past perfect: “She had already finished her part.”

steps.npc: Tom said: 'I'm going to be late and I'll text you.' What did he say?

options.t: He said he was going to be late and that he'd text me.
options.why: is going to → was going to; will → would.

options.t: He said he is going to be late and that he will text me.
options.why: shift back: “he was going to be late… he'd text me.”

activities.why: After a past reporting verb, shift the tense back one step and change pronouns and time/place words; use 'tell someone' but 'say (that)'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Beyond statements, we report questions, requests and commands — each with its own structure. And say vs tell follow clear rules.

She asked if I was ready. · He told me to wait. · They asked me where I lived.

learn.title: say vs tell
learn.html:
tell + personsay (+ to person)
She told me the news. · told him to goShe said (that)… · She said to me…
⭐
Fixed: tell the truth/a lie/a story/the time; say hello/sorry/a word.

learn.title: Reported questions (normal word order, no do)
learn.html:
yes/no question"Are you tired?" → She asked if/whether I was tired.
wh- question"Where do you live?" → She asked where I lived. (not where did I live)

learn.title: Reported commands & requests
learn.html:
tell/ask + object + (not) to + verb.

"Sit down." → He told me to sit down. · "Please don't smoke." → She asked us not to smoke.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She asked where did I live.  ✓ She asked where I lived. (statement order)
✗
He said me to wait.  ✓ He told me to wait.
✗
She told to close the door.  ✓ She told me/us to close the door.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• tell + person; say (+ to + person). Reported questions: normal order, no do; if/whether for yes/no.
  • Commands/requests: tell/ask + object + (not) to + verb.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Кроме утверждений, передаём вопросы, просьбы, команды — у каждого своя структура. say и tell по правилам: She asked if I was ready. · He told me to wait.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Хабардан басқа, сұрақ, өтініш, бұйрықты жеткіземіз — әрқайсысының құрылымы бар. say мен tell нақты ережелермен: She asked if I was ready. · He told me to wait.

ru.title: say vs tell
ru.html: tell + человек (told me the news; told him to go). say (+ to person): said (that)…; said to me… Устойчиво: tell the truth/a lie/the time; say hello/sorry.

kk.title: say vs tell
kk.html: tell + адам (told me the news; told him to go). say (+ to person): said (that)…; said to me… Тұрақты: tell the truth/a lie/the time; say hello/sorry.

ru.title: Косвенные вопросы (обычный порядок, без do)
ru.html: yes/no: "Are you tired?" → She asked if/whether I was tired. wh-: "Where do you live?" → She asked where I lived (без do).

kk.title: Төлеу сұрақтары (қалыпты рет, do жоқ)
kk.html: yes/no: "Are you tired?" → She asked if/whether I was tired. wh-: "Where do you live?" → She asked where I lived (do жоқ).

ru.title: Косвенные команды/просьбы
ru.html: tell/ask + object + (not) to + глагол: "Sit down." → He told me to sit down. "Don't smoke." → She asked us not to smoke.

kk.title: Төлеу бұйрық/өтініш
kk.html: tell/ask + object + (not) to + етістік: "Sit down." → He told me to sit down. "Don't smoke." → She asked us not to smoke.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: where did I live → where I lived. He said me to wait → He told me to wait. told to close → told me/us to close.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: where did I live → where I lived. He said me to wait → He told me to wait. told to close → told me/us to close.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: tell + человек; say (+ to + человек). Косв. вопрос: обычный порядок, без do; yes/no → if/whether. Команда/просьба: tell/ask + object + (not) to + глагол.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: tell + адам; say (+ to + адам). Төлеу сұрақ: қалыпты рет, do жоқ; yes/no → if/whether. Бұйрық/өтініш: tell/ask + object + (not) to + етістік.

activities.q: "She ___ me she was leaving."

items.s: He asked where did I live.
She asked if I was ready.

activities.why: reported questions use statement order, no do, if/whether for yes/no.

activities.prompt: "Do you speak French?"
activities.answer: she asked if i spoke french
alts: she asked whether i spoke french
she asked if i spoke french.
she asked whether i spoke french.

activities.prompt: "Where are you going?"
activities.answer: he asked where i was going
alts: he asked where i was going.
activities.why: “He asked where I was going.” (no do, statement order)

activities.prompt: "Open your books."
activities.answer: the teacher told us to open our books
alts: the teacher told us to open our books.
the teacher told the students to open their books
activities.why: tell + object + to + verb → “told us to open our books.”

activities.before: "Please don't be late." → She asked me

activities.why: statement order → “He asked me what time it was.”

activities.prompt: "Could you help me, please?" she said to me.
activities.answer: she asked me to help her
alts: she asked me to help her.
activities.why: a request → “She asked me to help her.”

steps.npc: The manager asked: 'Have you sent the report?' and said: 'Do it now.'

options.t: She asked if I'd sent the report and told me to do it right away.
options.why: reported question (if) + reported command (told me to).

options.t: She asked have I sent the report and said me to do it.
options.why: “She asked if I'd sent… and told me to do it.”

activities.why: Report questions with statement word order (if/whether for yes/no) and no do; report commands/requests with tell/ask + object + (not) to + verb; use tell + person, say (+ to + person).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Most questions invert the subject and auxiliary. With no auxiliary, add do/does/did. Special cases: subject questions (who/what as subject) and questions with prepositions.

Where do you work? · Who broke it? · What are you looking at?

learn.title: Word order
learn.html:
(wh-) + aux + subject + verbWhat time did the film start?
be as main verb → invert beIs she a doctor? · Why were they late?
subject question (no do)Who wants tea? · What happened?

learn.title: Prepositions in questions
learn.html:
In everyday English the preposition goes to the end.

Who did you go with? · What is she talking about? · Where are you from?

learn.title: Negative questions
learn.html: Don't you …? / Isn't it …? often express surprise or check something we expect to be true. Careful with the answer: "Don't you like it?" → "No" = I don't like it.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Where you are going?  ✓ Where are you going?
✗
Who did break the window?  ✓ Who broke the window? (subject question)
✗
About what are you talking?  ✓ What are you talking about?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Invert aux + subject; add do/does/did if there's no auxiliary.
  • Subject who/what questions → no do (Who called?).
  • Prepositions go to the end (Who with? What about?).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Большинство вопросов инвертируют подлежащее и вспомогательный. Нет вспомогательного — добавляем do/does/did. Особые: вопросы к подлежащему и с предлогами: Where do you work? · Who broke it?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп сұрақ бастауыш пен көмекшіні орын алмастырады. Көмекші жоқ болса — do/does/did. Ерекше: бастауыш сұрақтары және предлогты сұрақтар: Where do you work? · Who broke it?

ru.title: Порядок слов
ru.html: (wh-) + вспомог. + подлежащее + глагол: What time did the film start? be главный: Is she…? Вопрос к подлежащему (без do): Who wants tea?

kk.title: Сөз реті
kk.html: (wh-) + көмекші + бастауыш + етістік: What time did the film start? be негізгі: Is she…? Бастауыш сұрағы (do жоқ): Who wants tea?

ru.title: Предлоги в вопросах
ru.html: В речи предлог в конце: Who did you go with? What is she talking about? Where are you from?

kk.title: Сұрақтағы предлогтар
kk.html: Күнделікте предлог соңында: Who did you go with? What is she talking about? Where are you from?

ru.title: Отрицательные вопросы
ru.html: Don't you…? / Isn't it…? часто выражают удивление. Осторожно с ответом: «Don't you like it?» → «No» = не нравится.

kk.title: Болымсыз сұрақтар
kk.html: Don't you…? / Isn't it…? жиі таңдану білдіреді. Жауапқа абай: «Don't you like it?» → «No» = ұнатпаймын.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Where you are going? → Where are you going? Who did break…? → Who broke…? About what are you talking? → talking about?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Where you are going? → Where are you going? Who did break…? → Who broke…? About what are you talking? → talking about?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Инверсия вспомог. + подлежащее; нет вспомог. — do/does/did. Вопрос к подлежащему who/what → без do. Предлоги в конце.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Көмекші + бастауышты алмастыр; көмекші жоқ болса do/does/did. Бастауыш who/what → do жоқ. Предлогтар соңында.

activities.q: Which is correct?
why: Correct — aux before subject → Where has she gone?

activities.why: invert aux + subject; subject-who takes no do.

activities.instruction: Make a question about the underlined part — She's waiting for <u>someone</u>.
alts: who is she waiting for?
who's she waiting for
who's she waiting for?

activities.q: "___ invented the telephone?" (asking about the doer)
why: Correct — subject question → no do → Who invented?
No do in a subject question.

alts: what
activities.why: object + preposition at the end → “What are you worried about?”
preposition to the end → “Who did you travel with?”

activities.prompt: You expected them to remember your birthday, but maybe they didn't.
alts: didn't they remember your birthday?

options.t: Where did you grow up, and who did you work with before?
options.why: aux inversion + preposition at the end.

options.t: Where you grew up, and with who you worked before?
options.why: “Where did you grow up… who did you work with?”

activities.why: Invert the auxiliary and subject (add do/does/did if needed); subject who/what questions take no do; put prepositions at the end.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Indirect questions (Do you know …? Could you tell me …?) are more polite. After the introduction, use statement word order — no inversion, no do.

Where is the station? → Do you know where the station is?

learn.title: Word order changes
learn.html:
direct questionindirect question
What time is it?Do you know what time it is?
Where does she live?Could you tell me where she lives?

learn.title: Yes/no → if / whether
learn.html:
For yes/no questions inside, use if or whether.

Is it open? → Do you know if it's open? · I wonder whether she'll come.

learn.title: Real-life conversation
learn.html:
Excuse me, do you know where the nearest bank is?
Sorry, I'm not sure. Could you ask that shop assistant?
Good idea. I wonder if they have a cash machine inside.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Do you know where is the station?  ✓ Do you know where the station is?
✗
Can you tell me what time does it start?  ✓ …what time it starts?
✗
I wonder is it true.  ✓ I wonder if it's true.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Indirect questions use statement word order (no inversion, no do).
  • Yes/no questions → if / whether.
  • Common openers: Do you know …? Could you tell me …? I wonder …

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Косвенные вопросы (Do you know…? Could you tell me…?) вежливее. После введения — порядок как в утверждении — без инверсии и do: Where is the station? → Do you know where the station is?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Жанама сұрақтар (Do you know…? Could you tell me…?) сыпайырақ. Кіріспеден кейін хабарлы сөз реті — инверсия, do жоқ: Where is the station? → Do you know where the station is?

ru.title: Меняется порядок слов
ru.html: Прямой: What time is it? Косвенный: Do you know what time it is? Прямой: Where does she live? Косвенный: Could you tell me where she lives?

kk.title: Сөз реті өзгереді
kk.html: Тура: What time is it? Жанама: Do you know what time it is? Тура: Where does she live? Жанама: Could you tell me where she lives?

ru.title: Yes/no → if / whether
ru.html: Для внутреннего yes/no — if или whether: Is it open? → Do you know if it's open? I wonder whether she'll come.

kk.title: Yes/no → if / whether
kk.html: Ішкі yes/no сұраққа if не whether: Is it open? → Do you know if it's open? I wonder whether she'll come.

ru.title: Разговор из жизни
ru.html: «Do you know where the nearest bank is?» — «Could you ask that shop assistant?» — «I wonder if they have a cash machine.»
