kk.title: Нақты өмірден сұхбат
kk.html: «Do you know where the nearest bank is?» — «Could you ask that shop assistant?» — «I wonder if they have a cash machine.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: where is the station → where the station is. what time does it start → what time it starts. I wonder is it true → I wonder if it's true.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: where is the station → where the station is. what time does it start → what time it starts. I wonder is it true → I wonder if it's true.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Косвенные вопросы — порядок как в утверждении (без инверсии/do). yes/no → if/whether. Зачины: Do you know…? Could you tell me…? I wonder…

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Жанама сұрақтар хабарлы сөз ретін алады (инверсия/do жоқ). yes/no → if/whether. Ашқыштар: Do you know…? Could you tell me…? I wonder…

activities.q: Which is correct?
options: Do you know where is she?
Do you know where she is?
why: No inversion inside an indirect question.

items.s: Could you tell me what time it opens?
Could you tell me what time does it open?
Do you know if he's coming?
Do you know is he coming?

activities.prompt: Where does Anna live?
activities.answer: do you know where anna lives
alts: do you know where anna lives?

activities.prompt: Is the shop open on Sundays?
activities.answer: do you know if the shop is open on sundays
alts: do you know if the shop is open on sundays?
do you know whether the shop is open on sundays
do you know whether the shop is open on sundays?

activities.before: Could you tell me how much this

activities.q: You want to ask a stranger the time politely. Best:
options: Could you tell me what time it is?
why: Direct, less polite to a stranger.
Correct — polite indirect question → Could you tell me what time it is?

activities.why: no do → “Do you know when the train leaves?”
statement order → “I wonder where he has gone.”

activities.instruction: Make the direct question polite and indirect:
activities.prompt: Where can I buy tickets?
activities.answer: do you know where i can buy tickets
alts: could you tell me where i can buy tickets
do you know where i can buy tickets?
could you tell me where i can buy tickets?
activities.why: “Do you know where I can buy tickets?”

options.t: Could you tell me where the museum is and if it's open today?
options.why: indirect wh- (where … is) + yes/no (if it's open).

options.t: Could you tell me where is the museum and is it open today?

activities.why: Indirect questions use statement word order (no inversion, no do); use if/whether for yes/no questions inside.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Auxiliary verbs (be, do, have, will, can…) help us avoid repeating a whole verb phrase — in short answers, agreeing, and so/neither patterns.

"Do you like it?" "Yes, I do." · "I'm tired." "So am I."

learn.title: Avoiding repetition
learn.html:
short answerHave you finished? — Yes, I have.
She won't agree, but I will.I don't smoke, but he does.

learn.title: so / neither (nor) + auxiliary
learn.html:
agreeing with positive → soagreeing with negative → neither/nor
"I'm hungry." "So am I.""I can't swim." "Neither can I."
"I love pizza." "So do I.""I didn't go." "Nor did I."
⭐
Note the inversion: So am I / Neither do I. (not So I am)

learn.title: I think so / I hope so / I'm afraid so
learn.html: Use so to avoid repeating a clause: "Is it going to rain?" "I think so." Negatives: "I don't think so." / "I hope not."

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
"I'm tired." "So I am."  ✓ "So am I."
✗
"I can't." "Neither I can."  ✓ "Neither can I."
✗
I think yes.  ✓ I think so.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Auxiliaries replace a repeated verb (Yes, I do / So am I).
  • so + aux + subject (positive); neither/nor + aux + subject (negative) — with inversion.
  • I think/hope so; I don't think so / I hope not.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Вспомогательные глаголы (be, do, have, will, can…) помогают не повторять всю группу — в кратких ответах, согласии, so/neither: «Do you like it?» «Yes, I do.» «I'm tired.» «So am I.»

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көмекші етістіктер (be, do, have, will, can…) бүкіл етістік тобын қайталамауға көмектеседі — қысқа жауап, келісу, so/neither-де: «Do you like it?» «Yes, I do.» «I'm tired.» «So am I.»

ru.title: Избегаем повтора
ru.html: Краткий ответ: Have you finished? — Yes, I have. I don't smoke, but he does.

kk.title: Қайталаудан құтылу
kk.html: Қысқа жауап: Have you finished? — Yes, I have. I don't smoke, but he does.

ru.title: so / neither (nor) + вспомог.
ru.html: Согласие с утверждением → so: «So am I / So do I». С отрицанием → neither/nor: «Neither can I / Nor did I». Инверсия: So am I (не So I am).

kk.title: so / neither (nor) + көмекші
kk.html: Болымдымен келісу → so: «So am I / So do I». Болымсызбен → neither/nor: «Neither can I / Nor did I». Инверсия: So am I (So I am емес).

ru.title: I think so / I hope so
ru.html: Чтобы не повторять предложение — so: «Is it going to rain?» «I think so.» Отрицание: «I don't think so.» / «I hope not.»

kk.title: I think so / I hope so
kk.html: Тұтас сөйлемді қайталамау үшін so: «Is it going to rain?» «I think so.» Болымсыз: «I don't think so.» / «I hope not.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: So I am → So am I. Neither I can → Neither can I. I think yes → I think so.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: So I am → So am I. Neither I can → Neither can I. I think yes → I think so.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Вспомогательные заменяют повторяемый глагол. so + вспомог. + подлежащее (утвержд.); neither/nor + вспомог. + подлежащее (отриц.) — с инверсией. I think/hope so; I don't think so / I hope not.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Көмекшілер қайталанатын етістікті алмастырады. so + көмекші + бастауыш (болымды); neither/nor + көмекші + бастауыш (болымсыз) — инверсиямен. I think/hope so; I don't think so / I hope not.

activities.q: "I love this song." "So ___ I."

activities.why: inversion: So am I / Neither can I.

activities.instruction: Agree with: 'I didn't enjoy it.' (start with Neither):
alts: neither did i.
nor did i
Nor
neither
nor
activities.why: agreeing with a negative → Neither have I.

activities.q: "Will the shop be open?" (you're fairly sure it will)
why: 'I think not' means you doubt it.

activities.prompt: My brother enjoys football. I don't enjoy football.
activities.answer: my brother enjoys football, but i don't
alts: my brother enjoys football but i don't
my brother enjoys football, but i don't.
my brother enjoys football but i don't.

steps.npc: I'm really into hiking these days.

options.t: So am I! But I've never tried climbing." "Neither have I.
options.why: So am I (positive) + Neither have I (negative).

options.t: So I am! But I've never tried climbing." "Neither I have.

activities.why: Auxiliaries avoid repeating a verb (Yes, I do / So am I / Neither did I — with inversion); use 'so' after think/hope/be afraid to avoid repeating a whole clause.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A question tag is a short question added to a statement to check something or invite agreement. Rule: positive statement → negative tag, and vice versa, using the same auxiliary.

It's cold, isn't it? · You don't smoke, do you?

learn.title: The system
learn.html:
beShe's a teacher, isn't she?
present/past simpleYou like it, don't you? · He came, didn't he?
modals / will / haveYou can drive, can't you? · They'll come, won't they?

learn.title: Special tags
learn.html:
I am … → aren't I?I'm late, aren't I?
Let's … → shall we?Let's go, shall we?
imperative → will/won't you?Close the door, will you?
negative words (nobody/never)Nobody phoned, did they? (treated as negative)

learn.title: Intonation
learn.html:
Falling tone = you expect agreement (checking). Rising tone = a real question (you're not sure).

It's a lovely day, isn't it? ↘ (agree) · You locked the door, didn't you? ↗ (unsure)

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
You're coming, isn't it?  ✓ You're coming, aren't you? (match the subject)
✗
I'm right, amn't I?  ✓ I'm right, aren't I?
✗
Let's go, will we?  ✓ Let's go, shall we?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Positive → negative tag; negative → positive tag; same auxiliary + subject pronoun.
  • Specials: I'm … aren't I? / Let's … shall we? / imperative … will you?
  • Negative words (never, nobody) make the statement negative → positive tag.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Разделительный вопрос — короткий вопрос в конце для проверки/согласия. Правило: утверждение → отрицательный tag (и наоборот), тем же вспомогательным: It's cold, isn't it? · You don't smoke, do you?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Question tag — хабарлы сөйлемге қосылатын қысқа сұрақ; тексеру немесе келісім сұрау. Ереже: болымды → болымсыз tag (керісінше), сол көмекшімен: It's cold, isn't it? · You don't smoke, do you?

ru.title: Система
ru.html: be: She's a teacher, isn't she? present/past: You like it, don't you? He came, didn't he? модаль/will/have: You can drive, can't you?

kk.title: Жүйесі
kk.html: be: She's a teacher, isn't she? present/past: You like it, don't you? He came, didn't he? модаль/will/have: You can drive, can't you?

ru.title: Особые tag
ru.html: I am… → aren't I? Let's… → shall we? повелит. → will/won't you? Отрицательные слова (nobody/never): Nobody phoned, did they?

kk.title: Ерекше tag-тар
kk.html: I am… → aren't I? Let's… → shall we? бұйрық → will/won't you? Болымсыз сөздер (nobody/never): Nobody phoned, did they?

ru.title: Интонация
ru.html: Нисходящий тон = ждёте согласия (проверка). Восходящий = настоящий вопрос (не уверены).

kk.title: Интонация
kk.html: Түсетін тон = келісім күтесіз (тексеру). Көтерілетін = нағыз сұрақ (сенімсіз).

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: You're coming, isn't it? → aren't you? amn't I? → aren't I? Let's go, will we? → shall we?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: You're coming, isn't it? → aren't you? amn't I? → aren't I? Let's go, will we? → shall we?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Утверждение → отрицательный tag (и наоборот); тот же вспомог. + местоимение. Особые: I'm… aren't I? / Let's… shall we? / повелит… will you? never/nobody → положительный tag.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Болымды → болымсыз tag (керісінше); сол көмекші + есімдік. Ерекше: I'm… aren't I? / Let's… shall we? / бұйрық… will you? never/nobody → болымды tag.

activities.q: "You've met her before, ___?"
why: Positive statement needs a negative tag.

activities.why: match the subject; I'm → aren't I; Let's → shall we.

activities.q: "Nobody knows the answer, ___?"
why: Correct — 'nobody' is negative → positive tag → do they?
The statement is already negative in meaning.

activities.why: negative statement → positive tag → “I'm not late, am I?”

activities.prompt: He hasn't finished
alts: he hasn't finished, has he?
he hasn't finished has he

steps.npc: (You want to agree it's a great view.)

options.t: It's a beautiful view, isn't it?
It's a beautiful view, doesn't it?

activities.why: Positive → negative tag and vice versa, using the same auxiliary and matching pronoun; watch the specials (aren't I, shall we) and negative words.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some verbs are followed by another verb in the -ing form, not the infinitive. This is a fixed pattern you must memorise.

I enjoy cooking. · She stopped smoking. · Do you mind waiting?

learn.title: Common verbs + -ing
learn.html:
enjoy · finish · stopI finished reading the book.
mind · suggest · avoidHe avoids driving at night.
admit · deny · imagineShe denied taking the money.
keep (= continue) · considerHe kept talking. · I'm considering moving.

learn.title: Negative & past forms
learn.html:
Negative: not + -ing → I enjoy not working at weekends.

For a finished earlier action you can use having + past participle: She admitted having taken it (= she took it before).

After give up, put off, carry on, go on (phrasal verbs) → also -ing: I've given up smoking.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Do you mind opening the window?  — Not at all.

— How's the diet?  — I've stopped eating sugar and I keep feeling hungry!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I enjoy to cook.  ✓ I enjoy cooking.
✗
She suggested to go home.  ✓ She suggested going home.
✗
He avoided to answer.  ✓ He avoided answering.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• enjoy, finish, stop, mind, suggest, avoid, admit, deny, keep, consider + -ing.
  • Negative = not + -ing; earlier action = having + done.
  • Never “enjoy to do” — the -ing form is fixed after these verbs.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После некоторых глаголов второй глагол стоит в форме -ing (не инфинитив). Это фиксированная модель, её нужно запомнить: I enjoy cooking. She stopped smoking. Do you mind waiting?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктерден кейін екінші етістік -ing түрінде тұрады (инфинитив емес). Бұл — жаттап алатын тұрақты үлгі: I enjoy cooking. She stopped smoking. Do you mind waiting?

ru.title: Глаголы + -ing
ru.html: enjoy, finish, stop, mind, suggest, avoid, admit, deny, keep (=продолжать), consider + -ing: I finished reading. She denied taking the money.

kk.title: -ing қажет ететін етістіктер
kk.html: enjoy, finish, stop, mind, suggest, avoid, admit, deny, keep (=жалғастыру), consider + -ing: I finished reading. She denied taking the money.

ru.title: Отрицание и прошедшая форма
ru.html: Отрицание: not + -ing → I enjoy not working at weekends. Ранее произошедшее: having + done → She admitted having taken it. give up/put off/carry on + -ing.

kk.title: Болымсыз және өткен түрі
kk.html: Болымсыз: not + -ing → I enjoy not working at weekends. Бұрын болған іс: having + done → She admitted having taken it. give up/put off/carry on + -ing.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Do you mind opening the window? — Not at all. — I've stopped eating sugar and I keep feeling hungry!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Do you mind opening the window? — Not at all. — I've stopped eating sugar and I keep feeling hungry!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I enjoy to cook. → enjoy cooking. suggested to go → suggested going. avoided to answer → avoided answering.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I enjoy to cook. → enjoy cooking. suggested to go → suggested going. avoided to answer → avoided answering.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: enjoy, finish, stop, mind, suggest, avoid, admit, deny, keep, consider + -ing. Отрицание = not + -ing; раннее действие = having + done. Никогда «enjoy to do».

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: enjoy, finish, stop, mind, suggest, avoid, admit, deny, keep, consider + -ing. Болымсыз = not + -ing; бұрынғы іс = having + done. “enjoy to do” деп ешқашан айтпаймыз.

activities.q: "I really enjoy ___ to music."

activities.answer: I don't mind waiting a few minutes

activities.q: "It started raining, but we ___ walking anyway."

activities.prompt: 'Yes, I took your pen,' she said.
alts: she admitted taking the pen
she admitted having taken my pen

activities.prompt: I don't smoke any more.
alts: i've given up smoking
i gave up smoking

steps.npc: Have you managed to change any habits?

options.t: Yes, I've stopped eating late.
Yes, I've stopped to eat late.
options.why: 'stop to eat' = pause in order to eat — wrong meaning here.

options.t: I keep forgetting to go!
I keep to forget to go!

activities.why: After enjoy, finish, stop, mind, suggest, avoid, admit, deny, keep, consider, use the -ing form — never the to-infinitive.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many verbs are followed by the to-infinitive, not the -ing form. Again, this is a fixed pattern.

She decided to leave. · I forgot to call. · We managed to finish.

learn.title: Common verbs + to
learn.html:
decide · plan · hope · offerThey offered to help.
promise · agree · refuseHe refused to answer.
manage · fail · learn · affordWe can't afford to buy it.
seem · appear · tend · pretendShe seems to know everyone.

learn.title: Negative & perfect infinitive
learn.html:
Negative: not to + verb → I decided not to go.

Perfect infinitive to have + done for an earlier action: She seems to have left already.

After some verbs you can use a question word + to: I don't know what to do / how to start.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Did you fix it?  — No, I failed to open the file, so I decided to wait for you.

— He promised to call, but he forgot to.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I decided going.  ✓ I decided to go.
✗
He promised helping.  ✓ He promised to help.
✗
I decided to not go.  ✓ I decided not to go.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• decide, plan, hope, offer, promise, agree, refuse, manage, fail, learn, afford, seem, pretend + to + verb.
  • Negative = not to + verb; earlier action = to have + done.
  • Question word + to: what/how/where to do.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После многих глаголов идёт to-инфинитив (не -ing). Тоже фиксированная модель: She decided to leave. I forgot to call. We managed to finish.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп етістіктен кейін to-infinitive тұрады (-ing емес). Бұл да тұрақты үлгі: She decided to leave. I forgot to call. We managed to finish.

ru.title: Глаголы + to
ru.html: decide, plan, hope, offer, promise, agree, refuse, manage, fail, learn, afford, seem, pretend + to: They offered to help. He refused to answer.

kk.title: to қажет ететін етістіктер
kk.html: decide, plan, hope, offer, promise, agree, refuse, manage, fail, learn, afford, seem, pretend + to: They offered to help. He refused to answer.

ru.title: Отрицание и perfect infinitive
ru.html: Отрицание: not to + verb → I decided not to go. Раннее действие: to have + done → She seems to have left. Вопрос. слово + to: what/how to do.

kk.title: Болымсыз және perfect infinitive
kk.html: Болымсыз: not to + verb → I decided not to go. Бұрынғы іс: to have + done → She seems to have left. Сұрау сөз + to: what/how to do.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I failed to open the file, so I decided to wait for you. — He promised to call, but he forgot to.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I failed to open the file, so I decided to wait for you. — He promised to call, but he forgot to.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I decided going. → decided to go. promised helping → promised to help. to not go → not to go.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I decided going. → decided to go. promised helping → promised to help. to not go → not to go.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: decide, plan, hope, offer, promise, agree, refuse, manage, fail, learn, afford, seem, pretend + to + verb. Отрицание = not to; раннее = to have done.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: decide, plan, hope, offer, promise, agree, refuse, manage, fail, learn, afford, seem, pretend + to + verb. Болымсыз = not to; бұрынғы = to have done.

activities.q: "They decided ___ a taxi."

items.s: I can't afford to buy it.

activities.answer: We agreed to meet after work

activities.q: "The keys weren't there. He ___ to have lost them."

activities.prompt: 'No, I won't apologise,' he said.
alts: he refused to apologize

activities.prompt: The exam was hard, but I passed.
alts: i managed to pass the exam

steps.npc: What are you doing this summer?

options.t: I'm planning to travel around Spain.
I'm planning travelling around Spain.
No, Sam agreed to come too.
No, Sam agreed coming too.

activities.why: After decide, plan, hope, offer, promise, agree, refuse, manage, fail, seem, pretend, use to + verb — never the -ing form.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many verbs take an object + to-infinitive: the person is different from the subject.

I want you to help. · She asked me to wait. · They told him to leave.

learn.title: verb + object + to
learn.html:
want · would like · askI'd like you to come.
tell · advise · encourageShe advised me to rest.
expect · invite · warn · remindRemind him to lock the door.
allow · force · teach · orderThey allowed us to stay.

learn.title: make & let (no 'to')
learn.html:
After make and let the infinitive has no 'to' (bare infinitive):

They made me wait. · She let him go. · help allows both: help me (to) carry.

Passive of make uses 'to': I was made to wait.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— The teacher told us to be quiet and asked me to close the door.

— My boss wants me to work Saturday, but Mum won't let me go!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I want that you help me.  ✓ I want you to help me.
✗
She made me to wait.  ✓ She made me wait.
✗
They let us to stay.  ✓ They let us stay.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• want/ask/tell/advise/expect/remind/allow + object + to + verb.
  • make and let take a bare infinitive (no 'to'): make me wait, let him go.
  • Not “want that…” — English uses object + to.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Многие глаголы берут дополнение + to-инфинитив (действующее лицо — другой человек): I want you to help. She asked me to wait. They told him to leave.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп етістік object + to-infinitive қабылдайды (әрекет иесі басқа адам): I want you to help. She asked me to wait. They told him to leave.

ru.title: verb + object + to
ru.html: want, would like, ask, tell, advise, encourage, expect, invite, warn, remind, allow, force, teach, order + дополнение + to: Remind him to lock the door.

kk.title: verb + object + to
kk.html: want, would like, ask, tell, advise, encourage, expect, invite, warn, remind, allow, force, teach, order + object + to: Remind him to lock the door.

ru.title: make & let (без ‘to’)
ru.html: После make и let инфинитив без ‘to’: They made me wait. She let him go. help — оба варианта. Пассив: I was made to wait.

kk.title: make & let (‘to’ жоқ)
kk.html: make және let-тен кейін инфинитив ‘to’-сыз: They made me wait. She let him go. help — екеуі де: help me (to) carry. Passive: I was made to wait.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — The teacher told us to be quiet and asked me to close the door. — My boss wants me to work Saturday, but Mum won't let me go!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — The teacher told us to be quiet and asked me to close the door. — My boss wants me to work Saturday, but Mum won't let me go!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I want that you help me. → want you to help. made me to wait → made me wait. let us to stay → let us stay.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I want that you help me. → want you to help. made me to wait → made me wait. let us to stay → let us stay.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: want/ask/tell/advise/expect/remind/allow + дополнение + to. make, let — без ‘to’. «want that…» неверно.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: want/ask/tell/advise/expect/remind/allow + object + to. make, let — ‘to’-сыз инфинитив. “want that…” дұрыс емес.

activities.q: "I want ___ me the truth."

activities.why: let/make = bare infinitive; ask = object + to; no 'want that'.

activities.answer: She reminded me to bring my passport

activities.q: "The film was so sad it ___ me cry."
why: 'allowed' would need 'to': allowed me to cry.

activities.why: make/let → bare; the rest → object + to.

activities.prompt: 'Sit down, please,' the officer said to us.
activities.answer: the officer told us to sit down
alts: he told us to sit down
activities.why: tell + object + to → “told us to sit down”.

activities.prompt: My boss said I could leave early.
activities.answer: my boss let me leave early
alts: my boss let me leave

activities.scene: Report instructions using object + to / make / let.

options.t: She asked us to finish the report today.
She asked us finishing the report.
No, she won't let us leave until it's done.
No, she won't let us to leave.

activities.why: Most verbs take object + to (ask/tell/want/advise); make and let take a bare infinitive (no 'to').

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A few verbs take either -ing or to, but the meaning changes. You must choose by meaning.

I remember locking it (memory of the past). · Remember to lock it (a future duty).

learn.title: remember / forget / regret
learn.html:
-ing = look backI remember meeting her. (I met her, and I recall it)
to = the task aheadRemember to meet her. (don't forget this duty)
regret + -ingI regret saying that. (I'm sorry I said it)
regret + toWe regret to inform you… (bad news now)

learn.title: stop / go on / try (preview)
learn.html:
stop + -ing = end the activity: I stopped smoking. stop + to = pause in order to: I stopped to smoke.

go on + -ing = continue the same thing; go on + to = move to something new: She went on to become a doctor.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Did you post the letter?  — Sorry, I forgot to post it.  — But I remember giving it to you!

— We were driving and stopped to have lunch.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Remember locking the door (as a reminder).  ✓ Remember to lock the door.
✗
I stopped to smoke (= I quit).  ✓ I stopped smoking.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• remember/forget/regret + -ing = look back; + to = the task ahead / news now.
  • stop + -ing = quit; stop + to = pause in order to.
  • go on + -ing = continue; go on + to = move to a new thing.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Несколько глаголов берут и -ing, и to, но меняется значение. I remember locking it (воспоминание). Remember to lock it (будущая обязанность).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бірнеше етістік -ing те, to да қабылдайды, бірақ мағынасы өзгереді. I remember locking it (өткенді еске алу). Remember to lock it (алдағы міндет).

ru.title: remember / forget / regret
ru.html: -ing = взгляд назад: I remember meeting her. to = предстоящее дело: Remember to meet her. regret + -ing = сожалеть; regret + to = плохая новость (сейчас).

kk.title: remember / forget / regret
kk.html: -ing = өткенге қарау: I remember meeting her. to = алдағы міндет: Remember to meet her. regret + -ing = өкіну; regret + to = жаман хабар (қазір).

ru.title: stop / go on / try
ru.html: stop + -ing = прекратить: stopped smoking. stop + to = остановиться, чтобы: stopped to smoke. go on + -ing = продолжать; go on + to = перейти к новому.

kk.title: stop / go on / try
kk.html: stop + -ing = істі тоқтату: stopped smoking. stop + to = сол үшін тоқтау: stopped to smoke. go on + -ing = жалғастыру; go on + to = жаңа іске көшу.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I forgot to post it. — But I remember giving it to you! — We stopped to have lunch.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I forgot to post it. — But I remember giving it to you! — We stopped to have lunch.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Как напоминание: Remember to lock the door. В значении «бросил»: I stopped smoking (не stopped to smoke).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Ескерту ретінде: Remember to lock the door. Тастадым мағынасында: I stopped smoking (stopped to smoke емес).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: remember/forget/regret + -ing = прошлое; + to = предстоящее. stop + -ing = бросить; stop + to = остановиться, чтобы. go on + -ing/to.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: remember/forget/regret + -ing = өткен; + to = алдағы. stop + -ing = тастау; stop + to = сол үшін тоқтау. go on + -ing/to.

activities.q: "Please remember ___ the report before you leave. (it's a duty)"
why: -ing = a memory; wrong for a duty.
Correct — a future task → remember to send.

items.s: I stopped to smoke = I quit.
I remember meeting him = I recall it.

activities.why: stop+to = pause in order to; remember+to = a duty.

activities.before: On the way home we stopped

activities.q: "She finished university and went on ___ her own company."
why: Correct — a new step → go on to start.

activities.q: "I distinctly remember ___ the door — so why is it open?"
why: Correct — a memory of a past action → remember locking.
'to lock' = a future duty — wrong here.

activities.why: -ing recalls the past; to points to a duty or present news.
a reminder of a duty → forget to post.

activities.prompt: I'm sorry I shouted at you.
alts: i regret shouting

activities.instruction: Rewrite so 'stop' means 'pause in order to':
activities.prompt: We were tired, so we had a rest.
alts: we stopped to have a rest

options.t: Oh no, I forgot to take them!
Oh no, I forgot taking them!
options.why: 'forgot taking' = no memory of doing it — wrong here.

steps.npc: Were you late because of traffic?

options.t: Yes, and I stopped to help someone.
options.why: stop + to = paused in order to help.

options.t: Yes, and I stopped helping someone.
options.why: that means you quit helping — wrong meaning.

activities.why: remember/forget/regret + -ing look back; + to point ahead. stop + -ing = quit; stop + to = pause in order to.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
More verbs where -ing and to give different meanings — this time try, need and help.

try to = make an effort · try + -ing = experiment to see what happens.

learn.title: try / need
learn.html:
try to doI tried to open the jar. (made an effort — hard)
try -ingTry knocking louder. (a suggestion to test)
need to do (active)You need to clean the car.
need -ing (= passive)The car needs cleaning. (= needs to be cleaned)

learn.title: help & dare
learn.html:
help takes to or a bare infinitive — both correct: He helped me (to) carry the bags.

can't help + -ing = can't stop yourself: I can't help laughing.

dare: I didn't dare (to) say anything.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— My laptop's frozen.  — Try restarting it. If that fails, you'll need to take it in.

— This shirt needs ironing. Can you help me do it?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The car needs to clean.  ✓ The car needs cleaning / needs to be cleaned.
✗
I can't help to laugh.  ✓ I can't help laughing.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• try to = make an effort; try + -ing = experiment/test.
  • need to (active) vs need + -ing (= passive: it needs doing).
  • help + (to) do; can't help + -ing = can't stop yourself.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Ещё глаголы, где -ing и to дают разные значения: try, need, help. try to = стараться; try + -ing = попробовать (тест).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: -ing пен to әр түрлі мағына беретін тағы етістіктер: try, need, help. try to = тырысу; try + -ing = сынап көру.

ru.title: try / need
ru.html: try to do = приложить усилие (трудно); try -ing = попробовать. need to do (актив); need -ing = пассив: The car needs cleaning (= to be cleaned).

kk.title: try / need
kk.html: try to do = күш салу (қиын); try -ing = сынап көру ұсынысы. need to do (актив); need -ing = пассив: The car needs cleaning (= to be cleaned).

ru.title: help & dare
ru.html: help: с to или без — оба верны: helped me (to) carry. can't help + -ing = не удержаться: can't help laughing. dare: didn't dare (to) say.

kk.title: help & dare
kk.html: help: to немесе ‘to’-сыз — екеуі де: helped me (to) carry. can't help + -ing = өзіңді тоқтата алмау: can't help laughing. dare: didn't dare (to) say.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Try restarting it. If that fails, you'll need to take it in. — This shirt needs ironing. Can you help me do it?

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Try restarting it. If that fails, you'll need to take it in. — This shirt needs ironing. Can you help me do it?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: The car needs to clean. → needs cleaning / to be cleaned. can't help to laugh → can't help laughing.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: The car needs to clean. → needs cleaning / to be cleaned. can't help to laugh → can't help laughing.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: try to = стараться; try + -ing = попробовать. need to (актив) vs need + -ing (пассив). help + (to) do; can't help + -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: try to = тырысу; try + -ing = сынау. need to (актив) vs need + -ing (пассив). help + (to) do; can't help + -ing.

activities.q: "The heating's broken. It ___ repairing."
why: subject 'it' → needs, and this is the passive pattern.
'needs to repair' would mean the heating repairs something.

items.s: I tried to lift it but it was too heavy.

activities.before: Your hair's a mess — it needs
alts: to be cut

activities.why: a suggestion to test → try putting on.

activities.q: "He ___ to warn them, but nobody answered the phone."
why: Correct — made an effort → tried to warn.
'needed to warn' doesn't fit 'but nobody answered'.

activities.q: "The soup's a bit bland. Try ___ some pepper."
why: 'try to add' = make an effort — odd here.

activities.why: to = effort/active; -ing = experiment or passive need.

activities.prompt: Someone has to water these plants.
alts: the plants need watering
these plants need to be watered

activities.prompt: I carried the boxes and she assisted me.
activities.answer: she helped me carry the boxes
alts: she helped me to carry the boxes

options.t: Try updating the software first.
Try to update — no wait, it needs to update itself.
options.why: the suggestion pattern here is try + -ing.

options.t: Then it needs looking at by a technician.
options.why: need + -ing = passive (needs to be looked at).

options.t: Then it needs to look at by a technician.
options.why: passive need = needs looking at / needs to be looked at.

activities.why: try to = effort; try + -ing = experiment. need to = active; need + -ing = passive. help + (to) do; can't help + -ing.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
like, love, hate, prefer can take -ing or to with little change. But would like/love/prefer (offers, specific occasions) take to.

I like swimming. · I'd like to swim now.

learn.title: like vs would like
learn.html:
like + -ing / to (general)I like getting up early. / I like to get up early.
would like + to (specific)I'd like to get up early tomorrow.
would love / hate / prefer + toI'd love to come. · I'd prefer to wait.

learn.title: like = enjoy vs like = think it's good
learn.html:
-ing often = enjoy: I like cooking (I enjoy it). to often = it's a good idea/habit: I like to check my emails first (a routine I choose).

Note would like is a polite request: Would you like to sit down?

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Do you like dancing?  — I love it! But right now I'd prefer to sit and talk.

— Would you like to order?  — Yes, I'd like to try the soup.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I would like swimming now.  ✓ I'd like to swim now.
✗
Would you like drinking something?  ✓ Would you like to drink / something to drink?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• like/love/hate/prefer + -ing or to (general — small difference).
  • would like/love/hate/prefer + to (a specific occasion; offers & requests).
  • Would you like to…? is the polite way to offer or invite.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: like, love, hate, prefer берут -ing или to (разница мала). Но would like/love/prefer (предложение, конкретный случай) — to: I like swimming. I'd like to swim now.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: like, love, hate, prefer -ing те, to да қабылдайды (айырма аз). Бірақ would like/love/prefer (ұсыныс, нақты жағдай) — to: I like swimming. I'd like to swim now.

ru.title: like vs would like
ru.html: like + -ing/to (в целом): I like getting/to get up early. would like + to (конкретно): I'd like to get up early tomorrow. would love/prefer + to.

kk.title: like vs would like
kk.html: like + -ing/to (жалпы): I like getting/to get up early. would like + to (нақты): I'd like to get up early tomorrow. would love/prefer + to.

ru.title: like = нравиться vs like = считать правильным
ru.html: -ing = получать удовольствие: I like cooking. to = привычка/выбор: I like to check my emails first. would like = вежливая просьба: Would you like to sit down?

kk.title: like = ұнату vs like = дұрыс деп есептеу
kk.html: -ing = ұнату: I like cooking. to = дұрыс әдет/шешім: I like to check my emails first. would like = сыпайы өтініш: Would you like to sit down?

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Do you like dancing? — I love it! But right now I'd prefer to sit and talk. — Would you like to order? — I'd like to try the soup.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Do you like dancing? — I love it! But right now I'd prefer to sit and talk. — Would you like to order? — I'd like to try the soup.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I would like swimming now. → I'd like to swim now. Would you like drinking? → Would you like to drink / something to drink?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I would like swimming now. → I'd like to swim now. Would you like drinking? → Would you like to drink / something to drink?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: like/love/hate/prefer + -ing/to (в целом). would like/love/prefer + to (конкретно; предложения, просьбы). Would you like to…? — вежливое предложение.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: like/love/hate/prefer + -ing/to (жалпы). would like/love/prefer + to (нақты жағдай; ұсыныс, өтініш). Would you like to…? — сыпайы ұсыныс.

activities.q: "I'd like ___ a coffee, please."

items.s: Would you like to sit down?

alts: finishing

activities.q: "Thanks for the invitation — we'd ___ to come!"
"Normally I hate flying, but I'd ___ to fly this once to save time."
why: Correct — specific occasion → would prefer to fly.
after 'would' use the base form + to.

items.t: I'd like to read that book

activities.why: 'would' + to signals a specific occasion or offer.
would like + to → “Would you like to have dessert?”

activities.prompt: Do you want a drink?
alts: would you like something to drink
would you like to have a drink

activities.prompt: I don't want to go out; staying in is better for me.
alts: i would prefer to stay in
id prefer to stay in
activities.why: would prefer + to → “I'd prefer to stay in”.

activities.scene: Respond politely with like / would like.

steps.npc: Would you like to join us for dinner?

options.t: I'd love to! Thank you.
I'd love joining! Thank you.
Yes, I really like eating pasta.
Yes, I really would like eating pasta.

activities.why: like/love/prefer + -ing or to for general tastes; would like/love/prefer + to for a specific occasion, offer or request.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Two ways to say what you like more. prefer works with -ing/to and nouns; would rather takes a bare infinitive.

I prefer tea to coffee. · I'd rather stay home.

learn.title: prefer — the patterns
learn.html:
prefer + noun/-ing + toI prefer walking to driving.
prefer + to … rather than …I prefer to walk rather than drive.
would prefer + to (specific)I'd prefer to walk today.

learn.title: would rather (+ bare infinitive)
learn.html:
would rather + verbI'd rather stay in. (not 'to stay')
would rather … than …I'd rather walk than wait.
would rather somebody + pastI'd rather you didn't smoke here.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Cinema or a walk?  — I'd rather go for a walk, honestly. I prefer being outside.

— Can I open the window?  — I'd rather you didn't — it's cold.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'd rather to stay.  ✓ I'd rather stay.
✗
I prefer tea than coffee.  ✓ I prefer tea to coffee.
✗
I'd rather you don't smoke.  ✓ I'd rather you didn't smoke.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• prefer A to B; prefer to do rather than do.
  • would rather + bare infinitive; would rather … than …
  • would rather somebody + past tense: I'd rather you didn't.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Два способа сказать, что нравится больше. prefer — с -ing/to и существительными; would rather — с инфинитивом без ‘to’: I prefer tea to coffee. I'd rather stay home.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: “Көбірек ұнатамын” дегеннің екі жолы. prefer — -ing/to және зат есіммен; would rather — ‘to’-сыз инфинитивпен: I prefer tea to coffee. I'd rather stay home.

ru.title: prefer — модели
ru.html: prefer + noun/-ing + to: prefer walking to driving. prefer + to … rather than …: prefer to walk rather than drive. would prefer + to (конкретно).

kk.title: prefer — үлгілер
kk.html: prefer + noun/-ing + to: prefer walking to driving. prefer + to … rather than …: prefer to walk rather than drive. would prefer + to (нақты).

ru.title: would rather
ru.html: would rather + инфинитив без ‘to’: I'd rather stay in. would rather … than …: rather walk than wait. would rather somebody + past: I'd rather you didn't smoke.

kk.title: would rather
kk.html: would rather + ‘to’-сыз етістік: I'd rather stay in. would rather … than …: rather walk than wait. would rather somebody + past: I'd rather you didn't smoke.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I'd rather go for a walk. I prefer being outside. — Can I open the window? — I'd rather you didn't.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I'd rather go for a walk. I prefer being outside. — Can I open the window? — I'd rather you didn't.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I'd rather to stay. → rather stay. prefer tea than coffee → prefer tea to coffee. rather you don't → rather you didn't.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I'd rather to stay. → rather stay. prefer tea than coffee → prefer tea to coffee. rather you don't → rather you didn't.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: prefer A to B; prefer to do rather than do. would rather + инфинитив без ‘to’; … than … would rather somebody + past.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: prefer A to B; prefer to do rather than do. would rather + ‘to’-сыз инфинитив; … than … would rather somebody + past.

activities.q: "I'd rather ___ at home tonight."
why: not the -ing form after would rather.

items.s: I'd rather you didn't call late.
I prefer reading than watching TV.

activities.why: prefer … to …; would rather + bare infinitive.

activities.answer: I'd rather you didn't tell anyone

activities.q: "I don't want to fly. I'd rather ___ by train."
"This is a library. I'd rather you ___ so loudly."

activities.prompt: I don't want to go out tonight.
alts: i would rather stay in
id rather not go out
i'd rather not go out

activities.prompt: I'd choose to email instead of phoning.
activities.answer: i prefer to email rather than phone
alts: i prefer to email rather than to phone

activities.scene: State preferences with prefer / would rather.

steps.npc: Shall we eat out or cook?

options.t: I'd rather cook tonight, if that's OK.
I'd rather to cook tonight.
I prefer water to wine, thanks.
I prefer water than wine, thanks.

activities.why: prefer A to B / prefer to do rather than do; would rather + bare infinitive, and would rather + somebody + past tense.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
After a preposition (in, on, at, of, for, about, without, before, after…), a verb is always in the -ing form — never the infinitive.

She's good at drawing. · Thanks for helping. · He left without saying goodbye.

learn.title: The rule in action
learn.html:
preposition + -inginterested in learning · afraid of failing
after time wordsbefore leaving, after finishing, since arriving
by + -ing (= method)She passed by studying hard.
without + -ingHe fixed it without asking for help.

learn.title: Watch out for 'to'
learn.html:
Sometimes to is a preposition, not part of an infinitive — then it takes -ing:

look forward to seeing you · be used to living here · object to working late.

Test: could a noun follow? “look forward to the trip” ✓ → so “to seeing”.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Are you good at cooking?  — Not really, but I'm interested in learning.

— Thanks for coming.  — I look forward to seeing you again.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
interested in to learn  ✓ interested in learning.
✗
look forward to see you  ✓ look forward to seeing you.
✗
He left without to say bye.  ✓ without saying bye.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• preposition + -ing, always (in, of, for, about, without, before, after…).
  • Some expressions end in to the preposition → still -ing: look forward to doing, be used to doing.
  • by + -ing = the method you use.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После предлога (in, on, of, for, about, without, before, after…) глагол всегда в форме -ing: good at drawing. Thanks for helping. without saying goodbye.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Предлогтан кейін (in, on, of, for, about, without, before, after…) етістік әрқашан -ing: good at drawing. Thanks for helping. without saying goodbye.

ru.title: Правило на практике
ru.html: preposition + -ing: interested in learning, afraid of failing. Слова времени: before leaving, after finishing. by + -ing = способ. without + -ing.

kk.title: Ереже іс жүзінде
kk.html: preposition + -ing: interested in learning, afraid of failing. Уақыт сөздері: before leaving, after finishing. by + -ing = тәсіл. without + -ing.

ru.title: Осторожно с ‘to’
ru.html: Иногда to — предлог (не инфинитив) → -ing: look forward to seeing, be used to living, object to working. Тест: можно ли после поставить существительное? (to the trip ✓).

kk.title: ‘to’-ға абай болыңыз
kk.html: Кейде to — предлог (инфинитив емес) → -ing: look forward to seeing, be used to living, object to working. Тест: артынан зат есім қоюға бола ма? (to the trip ✓).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Are you good at cooking? — I'm interested in learning. — Thanks for coming. — I look forward to seeing you again.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Are you good at cooking? — I'm interested in learning. — Thanks for coming. — I look forward to seeing you again.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: interested in to learn → in learning. look forward to see → to seeing. without to say → without saying.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: interested in to learn → in learning. look forward to see → to seeing. without to say → without saying.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Предлог + -ing всегда. Некоторые выражения кончаются на предлог to → -ing: look forward to doing, be used to doing. by + -ing = способ.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Предлог + -ing әрқашан. Кейбір тіркестер to предлогымен бітеді → -ing: look forward to doing, be used to doing. by + -ing = тәсіл.

activities.q: "She's interested in ___ Chinese."

alts: practicing

activities.q: "They objected to ___ on Sunday."
why: Correct — object to + working (a noun could follow: object to the plan).

activities.why: After want/decide, 'to' is infinitive; after look forward/be used, it's a preposition.

activities.prompt: He entered. He didn't knock.
alts: he came in without knocking

activities.prompt: She got fit. She went running.
alts: she got fit by going running

options.t: Great — I'm thinking of taking the next level.
Great — I'm thinking of to take the next level.
Of course. I look forward to hearing from you.
Of course. I look forward to hear from you.
options.why: look forward to + -ing, not the infinitive.

activities.why: A verb after any preposition takes -ing — including expressions ending in the preposition 'to' (look forward to, be/get used to, object to).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
be used to + -ing = something is familiar/normal for you now. get used to + -ing = the process of becoming familiar.

I'm used to getting up early. · I'm slowly getting used to the cold.

learn.title: The three forms
learn.html:
be used to + -ing / nounShe's used to working nights. / used to the noise.
get used to + -ing / nounHe's getting used to driving on the left.
NOT used to it yetI'm not used to living alone.

learn.title: used to do ≠ be used to doing
learn.html:
used to + infinitive = a past habit (no longer true): I used to smoke.

be used to + -ing = familiar now: I am used to smoking = it's normal for me. Very different — don't confuse them!

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Is the night shift hard?  — It was, but I'm used to it now.

— New city — how are you coping?  — I'm getting used to finding my way around.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'm used to get up early.  ✓ I'm used to getting up early.
✗
I used to live here (meaning: it's normal now).  ✓ I'm used to living here.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• be used to + -ing/noun = it's familiar/normal now.
  • get used to + -ing = becoming familiar (a process).
  • used to + infinitive = a PAST habit — a different structure.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: be used to + -ing = это привычно/нормально для тебя сейчас. get used to + -ing = процесс привыкания. I'm used to getting up early. I'm getting used to the cold.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: be used to + -ing = бұл саған қазір таныс/қалыпты. get used to + -ing = үйрену үдерісі. I'm used to getting up early. I'm getting used to the cold.

ru.title: Три формы
ru.html: be used to + -ing/noun: used to working nights / the noise. get used to + -ing: getting used to driving on the left. Отриц.: I'm not used to living alone.

kk.title: Үш түрі
kk.html: be used to + -ing/noun: used to working nights / the noise. get used to + -ing: getting used to driving on the left. Болымсыз: I'm not used to living alone.

ru.title: used to do ≠ be used to doing
ru.html: used to + инфинитив = прошлая привычка (сейчас нет): I used to smoke. be used to + -ing = привычно сейчас: I am used to smoking. Совершенно разное!

kk.title: used to do ≠ be used to doing
kk.html: used to + инфинитив = өткен әдет (қазір жоқ): I used to smoke. be used to + -ing = қазір таныс: I am used to smoking. Екеуі мүлде бөлек!

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Is the night shift hard? — I'm used to it now. — New city? — I'm getting used to finding my way around.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Is the night shift hard? — I'm used to it now. — New city? — I'm getting used to finding my way around.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I'm used to get up early. → used to getting. В значении «привычно сейчас»: I'm used to living here (не used to live).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I'm used to get up early. → used to getting. Қазір қалыпты мағынасында: I'm used to living here (used to live емес).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: be used to + -ing/noun = привычно сейчас. get used to + -ing = процесс привыкания. used to + инфинитив = прошлая привычка (другая структура).

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: be used to + -ing/noun = қазір таныс. get used to + -ing = үйрену үдерісі. used to + инфинитив = өткен әдет (бөлек құрылым).

activities.q: "After a month, she's used to ___ in English."

items.s: I'm used to living here = it's normal now.
I used to live here = a past situation.
I'm used to get up early.
He's getting used to the weather.

activities.why: be used to + -ing; used to + infinitive for past habits.

alts: the sight of blood
activities.why: be used to + noun is also possible.

activities.typeLabel: used to do or be used to doing?
activities.q: "When I was a child I ___ walk to school. Now I take the bus."
why: Correct — a past habit → used to walk.
'was used to walking' would mean it felt normal, not simply 'a past habit'.
present doesn't fit 'when I was a child'.

activities.q: "It felt strange at first, but I'm slowly ___ used to the silence."
why: 'being used to' isn't the process meaning.
Correct — process of adapting → getting used to.

activities.why: Same words, opposite structures: past habit vs current familiarity.
be used to + -ing → “not used to eating spicy food”.

activities.prompt: Working at night is normal for me now.
activities.answer: i'm used to working at night
alts: i am used to working at night
activities.why: be used to + -ing = it's familiar now.

activities.prompt: At first the noise bothered me, but not any more.
activities.answer: i got used to the noise
alts: i've got used to the noise
i got used to it
activities.why: get used to = became familiar with.

steps.npc: How's life in the new country?

options.t: I'm still getting used to the food.
I'm still getting used to eat the food.
options.why: get used to + -ing/noun, not the infinitive.

options.t: Better — I'm used to speaking it every day now.
Better — I used to speaking it every day.
options.why: 'used to' + -ing needs 'be': I'm used to speaking.

activities.why: be used to + -ing = familiar now; get used to + -ing = becoming familiar. Don't confuse with used to + infinitive (a past habit).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many verbs are followed by a fixed preposition + -ing. Learn the verb and its preposition together, as a unit.

She succeeded in passing. · They accused him of cheating.

learn.title: Common combinations
learn.html:
succeed in · believe inHe succeeded in finding a job.
dream of · think of/aboutI dream of travelling the world.
apologise for · insist onShe insisted on paying.
accuse sb of · stop sb fromThey stopped him from leaving.

learn.title: Verb + object + preposition + -ing
learn.html:
Some take an object before the preposition:

accuse somebody of doing · congratulate somebody on passing · thank somebody for helping · prevent/stop somebody from going · blame somebody for breaking.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Congratulations on getting the job!  — Thanks. It took ages, but I finally succeeded in convincing them.

— Sorry for keeping you waiting.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She insisted to pay.  ✓ She insisted on paying.
✗
They accused him to steal.  ✓ accused him of stealing.
✗
Thank you to help.  ✓ Thank you for helping.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Learn verb + preposition + -ing as a set: succeed in, dream of, insist on, apologise for.
  • Some need an object: accuse sb of, congratulate sb on, stop sb from.
  • The preposition always forces the -ing form.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После многих глаголов идёт фиксированный предлог + -ing. Учите глагол с предлогом вместе: succeed in passing; accuse him of cheating.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп етістіктен кейін тұрақты предлог + -ing тұрады. Етістік пен предлогты бірге жаттаңыз: succeed in passing; accuse him of cheating.

ru.title: Частые сочетания
ru.html: succeed in, believe in, dream of, think of/about, apologise for, insist on, accuse sb of, stop sb from + -ing.

kk.title: Жиі тіркестер
kk.html: succeed in, believe in, dream of, think of/about, apologise for, insist on, accuse sb of, stop sb from + -ing.

ru.title: Verb + object + preposition + -ing
ru.html: Некоторые берут дополнение перед предлогом: accuse somebody of, congratulate somebody on, thank somebody for, prevent/stop somebody from, blame somebody for.

kk.title: Verb + object + preposition + -ing
kk.html: Кейбіреуі предлогтың алдында object алады: accuse somebody of, congratulate somebody on, thank somebody for, prevent/stop somebody from, blame somebody for.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Congratulations on getting the job! — I finally succeeded in convincing them. — Sorry for keeping you waiting.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Congratulations on getting the job! — I finally succeeded in convincing them. — Sorry for keeping you waiting.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: insisted to pay → insisted on paying. accused him to steal → accused him of stealing. Thank you to help → for helping.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: insisted to pay → insisted on paying. accused him to steal → accused him of stealing. Thank you to help → for helping.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Глагол + предлог + -ing учат как набор. Некоторые берут дополнение: accuse sb of, congratulate sb on, stop sb from. Предлог всегда требует -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Етістік + предлог + -ing жиынтық болып жатталады. Кейбіреуі object алады: accuse sb of, congratulate sb on, stop sb from. Предлог әрқашан -ing тудырады.

activities.q: "She apologised ___ being late."

activities.answer: She insisted on paying for dinner

activities.q: "He was accused ___ stealing the car."

activities.prompt: They said he had cheated.
alts: they accused him of having cheated

activities.prompt: The alarm meant the thief couldn't get in.
activities.answer: the alarm prevented the thief from getting in
alts: the alarm prevented him from getting in

options.t: Someone accused him of taking the money.
Someone accused him for taking the money.
Yes, and he insisted on showing us the receipts.
Yes, and he insisted to show us the receipts.

activities.why: Verbs like succeed in, dream of, insist on, apologise for, accuse sb of, stop sb from all take a fixed preposition + the -ing form.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Several common expressions are always followed by -ing. Learn them as fixed phrases.

It's worth seeing. · There's no point in waiting. · I spent hours cleaning.

learn.title: Key expressions
learn.html:
(be) worth + -ingThis film is worth watching.
there's no point (in) + -ingThere's no point (in) arguing.
it's no use / no good + -ingIt's no use crying.
have difficulty / trouble + -ingI have difficulty sleeping.

learn.title: spend / waste time + -ing
learn.html:
spend / waste + time / money + -ing: I spent £50 repairing it. · Don't waste time worrying.

Also: go + -ing for activities — go shopping, go swimming, go running.

And busy + -ing: She's busy studying for exams.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Shall I call again?  — There's no point calling — the shop's closed.

— Fancy going swimming?  — I'd love to, but I'm busy packing.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's worth to see.  ✓ It's worth seeing.
✗
There's no point to wait.  ✓ no point (in) waiting.
✗
I spent an hour to clean.  ✓ spent an hour cleaning.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• worth, no point (in), no use, no good, have difficulty + -ing.
  • spend/waste + time/money + -ing; busy + -ing.
  • go + -ing for sports & activities (go shopping/swimming).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Несколько частых выражений всегда идут с -ing. Учите как устойчивые фразы: It's worth seeing. There's no point in waiting. I spent hours cleaning.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бірнеше жиі тіркес әрқашан -ing алады. Тұрақты тіркес ретінде жаттаңыз: It's worth seeing. There's no point in waiting. I spent hours cleaning.

ru.title: Ключевые выражения
ru.html: (be) worth + -ing; there's no point (in) + -ing; it's no use/no good + -ing; have difficulty/trouble + -ing: I have difficulty sleeping.

kk.title: Негізгі тіркестер
kk.html: (be) worth + -ing; there's no point (in) + -ing; it's no use/no good + -ing; have difficulty/trouble + -ing: I have difficulty sleeping.

ru.title: spend / waste time + -ing
ru.html: spend/waste + time/money + -ing: spent £50 repairing it; waste time worrying. go + -ing: go shopping/swimming. busy + -ing: busy studying.

kk.title: spend / waste time + -ing
kk.html: spend/waste + time/money + -ing: spent £50 repairing it; waste time worrying. go + -ing: go shopping/swimming. busy + -ing: busy studying.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — There's no point calling — the shop's closed. — Fancy going swimming? — I'm busy packing.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — There's no point calling — the shop's closed. — Fancy going swimming? — I'm busy packing.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: worth to see → worth seeing. no point to wait → no point (in) waiting. spent an hour to clean → cleaning.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: worth to see → worth seeing. no point to wait → no point (in) waiting. spent an hour to clean → cleaning.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: worth, no point (in), no use, no good, have difficulty + -ing. spend/waste + time/money + -ing; busy + -ing; go + -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: worth, no point (in), no use, no good, have difficulty + -ing. spend/waste + time/money + -ing; busy + -ing; go + -ing.

activities.q: "This book is worth ___."
why: worth is followed by -ing, not to.

items.s: I spent an hour to wait.

activities.why: no use + -ing; spent an hour waiting.

alts: practicing

activities.answer: There's no point in getting angry

activities.q: "The museum's free and fascinating — it's definitely ___ visiting."

activities.why: worth/no point/spend time → -ing; easy/decide → to (next units).

activities.prompt: You should see the castle — it's a great trip.
alts: the castle is worth visiting
it's worth seeing the castle

activities.prompt: It's useless to argue with him.
activities.answer: there's no point in arguing with him
alts: there's no point arguing with him

steps.npc: Shall we drive to the beach?

options.t: There's no point going — it's pouring.
There's no point to go — it's pouring.
The new café is worth trying.
The new café is worth to try.

activities.why: Fixed expressions — worth, no point (in), no use/good, have difficulty, spend/waste time, busy, go — are all followed by the -ing form.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To explain why we do something (purpose), we use to + verb, for + noun/-ing, or so that + clause.

I went out to buy milk. · It's a knife for cutting bread. · I left early so that I could catch the train.

learn.title: The three patterns
learn.html:
to + verb (purpose of an action)She phoned to apologise.
for + noun (purpose/reason)We stopped for lunch.
for + -ing (what something is used for)A corkscrew is for opening bottles.
so that + subject + verbSpeak up so that everyone can hear.

learn.title: in order to / so as to
learn.html:
More formal versions of 'to': in order to / so as to: I got up early in order to finish. Negative purpose: so as not to / in order not to: I whispered so as not to wake the baby.

so that often uses can/could/will/would: … so that I wouldn't forget.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Why did you go to town?  — To see the doctor and for some shopping.

— I wrote it down so that I wouldn't forget.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I came here for see you.  ✓ I came here to see you.
✗
a machine to make coffee  ✓ a machine for making coffee.
✗
so that catch the bus  ✓ so that I could catch the bus.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• to + verb = purpose of an action (why you do it).
  • for + noun or for + -ing (what a thing is used for).
  • so that + subject + verb when there's a new subject or a modal (can/could/would).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Чтобы объяснить зачем (цель): to + verb, for + noun/-ing или so that + предложение. I went out to buy milk. A knife for cutting bread. … so that I could catch the train.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Мақсатты (неге?) білдіру үшін: to + verb, for + noun/-ing немесе so that + сөйлем. I went out to buy milk. A knife for cutting bread. … so that I could catch the train.

ru.title: Три модели
ru.html: to + verb (цель действия): phoned to apologise. for + noun: stopped for lunch. for + -ing (для чего предмет): for opening bottles. so that + subject + verb.

kk.title: Үш үлгі
kk.html: to + verb (әрекет мақсаты): phoned to apologise. for + noun: stopped for lunch. for + -ing (неге арналған): for opening bottles. so that + subject + verb.

ru.title: in order to / so as to
ru.html: Более формально: in order to / so as to. Отрицательная цель: so as not to / in order not to: whispered so as not to wake the baby. so that + can/could/would.

kk.title: in order to / so as to
kk.html: Ресмиірек: in order to / so as to. Болымсыз мақсат: so as not to / in order not to: whispered so as not to wake the baby. so that + can/could/would.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Why did you go to town? — To see the doctor and for some shopping. — I wrote it down so that I wouldn't forget.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Why did you go to town? — To see the doctor and for some shopping. — I wrote it down so that I wouldn't forget.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: for see you → to see you. a machine to make coffee → for making coffee. so that catch the bus → so that I could catch.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: for see you → to see you. a machine to make coffee → for making coffee. so that catch the bus → so that I could catch.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: to + verb = цель действия. for + noun/-ing = для чего предмет. so that + subject + verb — когда есть новый субъект или модальный глагол.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: to + verb = әрекет мақсаты. for + noun/-ing = неге арналған. so that + subject + verb — жаңа субъект немесе модаль болғанда.

activities.q: "I went to the shop ___ some bread."
why: Correct — purpose of an action → to buy.
'for buying' fits a thing's use, not this action.

items.s: This tool is for cutting metal.
I phoned for tell you the news.
I left early so that I could rest.

activities.why: what a thing is used for → for + -ing.

activities.before: I'll write the address down so that you

activities.answer: She whispered so as not to wake him
activities.why: negative purpose → so as not to + verb.

activities.q: "I gave Tom my keys ___ he could get in."
why: 'to' needs the same subject; here the subject changes (Tom).
Correct — new subject → so that he could get in.

activities.why: to + verb; for + noun/-ing; so that + subject + verb.
purpose of an action → “came to ask you”.

activities.prompt: I set an alarm. I didn't want to oversleep.
activities.answer: i set an alarm so that i wouldn't oversleep
alts: i set an alarm so that i would not oversleep

activities.prompt: She went to Paris. She wanted to study art.
activities.answer: she went to paris to study art
alts: she went to paris in order to study art

activities.scene: Answer 'why?' with to / for / so that.

options.t: To get a better job.
options.why: purpose of an action → to + verb.

options.t: For get a better job.
options.why: 'for + verb' is wrong; use to + verb.

options.t: So that I can beat the traffic.
So that beat the traffic.
options.why: so that needs a subject + verb.

activities.why: to + verb for the purpose of an action; for + noun/-ing for what something is used for; so that + subject + verb when the subject changes or a modal is needed.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many adjectives are followed by the to-infinitive: to describe feelings, ease/difficulty, or a judgement.

I'm glad to help. · It's hard to explain. · She was the first to arrive.

learn.title: adjective + to
learn.html:
feelingshappy/glad/sad/sorry/afraid to hear that
ease/difficultyeasy/hard/difficult/impossible to do
likely/sure/certain/readyThey're likely to win. · I'm ready to go.
first/next/last/only + tothe last person to leave

learn.title: It's easy to … / … is easy to do
learn.html:
Two patterns mean the same:

It's easy to use this app. = This app is easy to use. (no object after 'use' — it moves to the front).

Also It's + adjective + of + person + to for judging behaviour: It was kind of you to help. · It's silly of him to lie.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— I'm sorry to hear you're ill.  — Thanks — it's nice of you to call.

— Is the exam tough?  — The questions are hard to understand, but you're sure to pass.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's easy to use it (this app is easy to use it).  ✓ This app is easy to use. (no 'it')
✗
It was kind from you to help.  ✓ kind of you to help.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• adjective + to + verb: glad to help, hard to explain, ready to go.
  • It's easy to do X = X is easy to do (drop the repeated object).
  • It's + adjective + of + person + to (judging behaviour): kind of you to…

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Многие прилагательные берут to-инфинитив (чувства, лёгкость/трудность, оценка): I'm glad to help. It's hard to explain. the first to arrive.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп сын есім артынан to-infinitive алады (сезім, жеңіл/қиын, баға): I'm glad to help. It's hard to explain. the first to arrive.

ru.title: adjective + to
ru.html: Чувства: happy/glad/sorry/afraid to hear. Легко/трудно: easy/hard/impossible to do. likely/sure/ready to. first/next/last/only + to.

kk.title: adjective + to
kk.html: Сезім: happy/glad/sorry/afraid to hear. Жеңіл/қиын: easy/hard/impossible to do. likely/sure/ready to. first/next/last/only + to.

ru.title: It's easy to … / … is easy to do
ru.html: Две модели — одно значение: It's easy to use this app = This app is easy to use (после ‘use’ нет дополнения). Также: of + person + to — оценка поведения: kind of you to help.

kk.title: It's easy to … / … is easy to do
kk.html: Екі үлгі бірдей: It's easy to use this app = This app is easy to use (‘use’-тан кейін объект жоқ). Тағы: of + person + to — мінезге баға: kind of you to help.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I'm sorry to hear you're ill. — It's nice of you to call. — The questions are hard to understand, but you're sure to pass.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I'm sorry to hear you're ill. — It's nice of you to call. — The questions are hard to understand, but you're sure to pass.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: This app is easy to use it. → easy to use (без ‘it’). kind from you → kind of you to help.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: This app is easy to use it. → easy to use (‘it’ жоқ). kind from you → kind of you to help.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: прил. + to + verb. It's easy to do X = X is easy to do (убираем повторное дополнение). It's + adj + of + person + to.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: сын есім + to + verb. It's easy to do X = X is easy to do (қайталанған объектіні алып тастаймыз). It's + adj + of + person + to.

activities.q: "I'm very glad ___ you."

items.s: She was the first to finish.
This box is easy to carry it.
It was kind from you to wait.

activities.answer: He was the last one to leave

activities.q: "This software ___ to install — it takes two minutes."
why: Correct — X is easy to install (object dropped).

activities.q: "It was silly ___ me to forget the tickets."
why: not 'for' with this adjective of judgement.

activities.prompt: It's impossible to please everyone.
alts: it is impossible to please everyone
activities.why: X is impossible to please (object to the front, no repeat).

activities.prompt: You helped me, and that was nice.
activities.answer: it was nice of you to help me
alts: it was nice of you to help

options.t: I'm so happy to hear that!
I'm so happy for hear that!
Of course. It's the least I could do.
Of course. It was kind from me.

activities.why: Adjectives take to + verb (glad to, hard to, ready to). Drop a repeated object (easy to use, not 'use it'), and judge behaviour with 'of + person + to'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A few adjectives work with both to and of/about + -ing — but the meaning differs.

afraid to do = you don't do it (choice). afraid of doing = you fear it might happen.

learn.title: afraid / frightened / scared
learn.html:
afraid to doShe was afraid to say no. (so she didn't)
afraid of -ingHe's afraid of losing his job. (worried it may happen)
afraid of -ing (accident)I didn't move, afraid of waking the baby.

learn.title: interested / sorry — two uses
learn.html:
interested to = react to news: I was interested to hear the results. interested in + -ing = want to do: I'm interested in studying law.

sorry to = react now: Sorry to bother you. sorry for/about + -ing = apologise for the past: Sorry for shouting.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Why didn't you complain?  — I was afraid to.  — Afraid of losing the contract?

— Sorry to interrupt, but are you interested in joining us?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'm interested to learn languages (= I want to).  ✓ interested in learning languages.
✗
Sorry to shout earlier.  ✓ Sorry for shouting earlier.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• afraid to do = you avoid doing it; afraid of doing = you fear it happening.
  • interested to hear (react) vs interested in doing (want to).
  • sorry to bother (now) vs sorry for doing (apologise for the past).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые прилагательные идут и с to, и с of/about + -ing, но значение разное. afraid to do = не делаешь (выбор). afraid of doing = боишься, что случится.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір сын есім to-мен де, of/about + -ing-пен де келеді, бірақ мағынасы бөлек. afraid to do = істемейсің (таңдау). afraid of doing = болып қалуынан қорқасың.

ru.title: afraid / frightened / scared
ru.html: afraid to do: afraid to say no (не сказал). afraid of -ing: afraid of losing his job (боится, что случится). afraid of waking the baby (случайно).

kk.title: afraid / frightened / scared
kk.html: afraid to do: afraid to say no (айтпады). afraid of -ing: afraid of losing his job (болуынан қорқады). afraid of waking the baby (кездейсоқ).

ru.title: interested / sorry — два значения
ru.html: interested to = реакция на новость: interested to hear. interested in + -ing = хочет делать: interested in studying. sorry to = сейчас: sorry to bother. sorry for + -ing = извинение за прошлое.

kk.title: interested / sorry — екі мағына
kk.html: interested to = хабарға реакция: interested to hear. interested in + -ing = істегісі келеді: interested in studying. sorry to = қазіргі: sorry to bother. sorry for + -ing = өткен үшін кешірім.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I was afraid to complain. — Afraid of losing the contract? — Sorry to interrupt, but are you interested in joining us?

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I was afraid to complain. — Afraid of losing the contract? — Sorry to interrupt, but are you interested in joining us?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: interested to learn languages (=want) → interested in learning. Sorry to shout earlier → Sorry for shouting earlier.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: interested to learn languages (=want) → interested in learning. Sorry to shout earlier → Sorry for shouting earlier.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: afraid to do = избегаешь; afraid of doing = боишься, что случится. interested to hear vs interested in doing. sorry to bother vs sorry for doing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: afraid to do = істеуден қашу; afraid of doing = болуынан қорқу. interested to hear vs interested in doing. sorry to bother vs sorry for doing.

activities.q: "He's afraid ___ his money, so he keeps it in a safe."
why: 'afraid to lose' would mean he avoids losing on purpose — odd.
Correct — he fears it happening → afraid of losing.

items.s: She was afraid to ask = she didn't ask.
I'm interested in studying medicine = I want to.
Afraid of waking him, I tiptoed.

activities.why: apologise for the past → sorry for shouting.

activities.after: you so late at night. (for / call)
activities.why: apologise for the past → sorry for calling.

activities.q: "I'd be interested ___ how the story ends."
why: Correct — reacting to news/curiosity → interested to hear.
'in hearing' would stress an activity you want to do.

activities.q: "The ice looked thin, so we were afraid ___ walk on it."
why: Correct — we chose not to → afraid to walk.
'afraid of walking' = fear of the activity in general — less precise here.

activities.why: to = choice/reaction now; preposition + -ing = fear/apology for something.
want to do it → interested in learning (drop 'to').

activities.prompt: He worries that he might fall.
alts: he is afraid of falling
activities.why: fear it may happen → afraid of falling.

activities.prompt: I forgot your birthday, and I feel bad.
activities.answer: i'm sorry for forgetting your birthday
alts: sorry for forgetting your birthday
i am sorry for forgetting your birthday
activities.why: apologise for the past → sorry for forgetting.

steps.npc: Did you tell the boss about the problem?

options.t: No, I was afraid to.
No, I was afraid of to.
options.why: either 'afraid to' or 'afraid of doing' — not 'of to'.

options.t: I'm afraid of making things worse.
I'm afraid to making things worse.

activities.why: afraid to do = you avoid it; afraid of doing = you fear it happening. interested to hear (react) vs interested in doing (want); sorry to bother (now) vs sorry for doing (past).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
After verbs of perception (see, hear, watch, feel, notice, listen to) we use either somebody + bare infinitive or somebody + -ing — with a small difference in meaning.

I saw her cross the road. · I saw her crossing the road.

learn.title: do vs doing
learn.html:
+ bare infinitive = whole actionI saw him fall. (I saw the complete event)
+ -ing = action in progressI saw him falling. (in the middle of it)
hearI heard the phone ring. / heard it ringing.
watch / feel / noticeShe felt the ground shake / shaking.

learn.title: No 'to' — and no -ing after 'made'
learn.html:
Never use to here: I saw her to cross ✗.

Compare: make/let also take a bare infinitive (Unit 55): They made me wait. In the passive, though, 'to' appears: He was seen to leave.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— What happened?  — I heard someone shout, then saw two men running away.

— Did you notice him leave?  — No, but I felt the door close.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I saw her to fall.  ✓ I saw her fall / falling.
✗
I heard him to sing.  ✓ I heard him sing / singing.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• see/hear/watch/feel/notice + object + bare infinitive (whole action).
  • … + object + -ing (action in progress, part of it).
  • No 'to' — except in the passive (was seen to leave).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После глаголов восприятия (see, hear, watch, feel, notice) — somebody + инфинитив без ‘to’ или somebody + -ing: I saw her cross / crossing the road.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Қабылдау етістіктерінен (see, hear, watch, feel, notice) кейін somebody + ‘to’-сыз инфинитив немесе somebody + -ing: I saw her cross / crossing the road.

ru.title: do vs doing
ru.html: + инфинитив без ‘to’ = полное действие: saw him fall. + -ing = в процессе: saw him falling. hear: heard it ring/ringing. feel: felt it shake/shaking.

kk.title: do vs doing
kk.html: + ‘to’-сыз инфинитив = толық әрекет: saw him fall. + -ing = үдерісте: saw him falling. hear: heard it ring/ringing. feel: felt it shake/shaking.

ru.title: ‘to’ нет — как и после made
ru.html: Здесь to никогда: saw her to cross ✗. make/let тоже без ‘to’ (раздел 55). Но в пассиве ‘to’ появляется: He was seen to leave.

kk.title: ‘to’ жоқ — made-тан кейін де
kk.html: Мұнда to ешқашан: saw her to cross ✗. make/let те ‘to’-сыз (55-бөлім). Бірақ passive-те ‘to’ шығады: He was seen to leave.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I heard someone shout, then saw two men running away. — Did you notice him leave? — I felt the door close.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I heard someone shout, then saw two men running away. — Did you notice him leave? — I felt the door close.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: saw her to fall → saw her fall/falling. heard him to sing → heard him sing/singing.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: saw her to fall → saw her fall/falling. heard him to sing → heard him sing/singing.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: see/hear/watch/feel/notice + object + инфинитив без ‘to’ (полное) или -ing (в процессе). ‘to’ нет — кроме пассива (was seen to leave).

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: see/hear/watch/feel/notice + object + ‘to’-сыз инфинитив (толық) не -ing (үдерісте). ‘to’ жоқ — passive-тен басқа (was seen to leave).

activities.q: "I saw the thief ___ into the car and drive off."

items.s: We felt the house to shake.

activities.before: When I looked out, I saw it

activities.answer: We watched the sun go down

activities.q: "As I passed the studio, I heard someone ___ the piano beautifully."
why: bare infinitive suggests the whole piece from start to end.

items.t: I saw him score the goal
