I saw him running for the bus

activities.prompt: The plane took off. I watched it.
activities.answer: i watched the plane take off
alts: i watched the plane taking off

activities.prompt: She was waiting at the bus stop. I saw her.
activities.answer: i saw her waiting at the bus stop
alts: i saw her waiting

options.t: I saw a man run out of the bank.
I saw a man to run out of the bank.
I heard an alarm ringing inside.
I heard an alarm to ring inside.

activities.why: see/hear/watch/feel/notice + object + bare infinitive (whole action) or + -ing (in progress). Never use 'to' (except in the passive: was seen to leave).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
An -ing clause lets you combine two ideas about the same subject into one smoother sentence.

Feeling tired, I went to bed. (= Because I felt tired, I went to bed.)

learn.title: What -ing clauses express
learn.html:
reason (= because)Being ill, she stayed home.
at the same timeHe sat by the fire reading a book.
one action then anotherOpening the door, she went in.
resultThe bomb exploded, killing two people.

learn.title: Having done … (earlier action)
learn.html:
For an action clearly before the main one, use having + past participle:

Having finished lunch, we left. (= After we had finished lunch…)

The subject of both parts must be the same person/thing.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How did you hurt your arm?  — Running for the bus, I slipped and fell.

— Not knowing the way, we asked a local for directions.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Walking home, the rain started.  ✓ Walking home, I got wet. (same subject = I)
✗
Having ate lunch, we left.  ✓ Having eaten lunch, we left.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• -ing clause = reason, same-time action, sequence, or result — same subject.
  • Earlier action → having + past participle.
  • Negative → Not + -ing (Not knowing…). Keep the subject consistent.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: -ing-оборот объединяет две мысли об одном субъекте в одно плавное предложение: Feeling tired, I went to bed (= Because I felt tired…).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: -ing clause бір субъект туралы екі ойды бір тегіс сөйлемге біріктіреді: Feeling tired, I went to bed (= Because I felt tired…).

ru.title: Что выражает
ru.html: Причина (=because): Being ill, she stayed home. Одновременность: sat reading. Последовательность: Opening the door, she went in. Результат: exploded, killing two.

kk.title: Нені білдіреді
kk.html: Себеп (=because): Being ill, she stayed home. Бір мезгіл: sat reading. Бірінен соң бірі: Opening the door, she went in. Нәтиже: exploded, killing two.

ru.title: Having done … (более раннее)
ru.html: Для действия явно раньше главного: having + past participle: Having finished lunch, we left. Субъект обеих частей должен быть одинаковым.

kk.title: Having done … (бұрынғы іс)
kk.html: Негізгіден анық бұрын болған іске: having + past participle: Having finished lunch, we left. Екі бөліктің субъектісі бірдей болуы керек.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How did you hurt your arm? — Running for the bus, I slipped. — Not knowing the way, we asked a local.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How did you hurt your arm? — Running for the bus, I slipped. — Not knowing the way, we asked a local.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Walking home, the rain started. → Walking home, I got wet (субъект один = I). Having ate → Having eaten.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Walking home, the rain started. → Walking home, I got wet (субъект бірдей = I). Having ate → Having eaten.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: -ing-оборот = причина, одновременность, последовательность, результат — субъект один. Раннее действие → having + done. Отрицание → Not + -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: -ing clause = себеп, қатар әрекет, реттілік, нәтиже — субъект бірдей. Бұрынғы іс → having + done. Болымсыз → Not + -ing.

activities.q: "___ the film boring, we left early."
why: Correct — reason clause → Finding the film boring.
past form can't start an -ing clause.

items.s: Feeling cold, I closed the window.
Walking home, the sky went dark on me.
Not knowing what to say, he stayed silent.

activities.why: the subject must match — 'the sky' can't be the one walking home.

alts: having
activities.why: reason → Having no money (= Because I had no money).

alts: having finished

activities.answer: Opening the letter, she began to read

activities.q: "___ many times before, she wasn't nervous on stage."
why: Correct — an earlier repeated action → Having performed.

activities.q: "The storm hit the coast, ___ hundreds of homes."
why: need the -ing form for the result clause.

activities.prompt: She didn't feel well. She went home.
activities.answer: not feeling well, she went home
alts: feeling unwell, she went home

activities.prompt: They had locked the door. Then they left.
activities.answer: having locked the door, they left
alts: having locked the door they left
activities.why: earlier action → Having locked the door, they left.

steps.npc: So how did the trip start?

options.t: Having packed the night before, we left at dawn.
Having packing the night before, we left at dawn.
Not knowing the road, we took a wrong turn.
Not know the road, we took a wrong turn.

activities.why: -ing clauses combine two ideas with the same subject (reason, simultaneous action, sequence, result); use 'having + past participle' for an earlier action, and keep the subject consistent.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A countable noun has a plural and can take a/an (a book, two books). An uncountable noun has no plural and no a/an (water, music, information).

I bought a book. · I need information (not an information).

learn.title: How they behave
learn.html:
countableuncountable
a/ana chair ✓an advice ✗
pluralchairs ✓advices ✗
some/anysome chairssome advice
much/manymany chairsmuch advice

learn.title: Tricky uncountables
learn.html:
These are uncountable in English (often countable in other languages): advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, work, bread, money, weather, traffic, progress, research, accommodation.

News looks plural but is singular: The news is good.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Do you need any help?  — Yes, I need some information about trains.

— How was the trip?  — Tiring — there was so much traffic and my luggage was heavy.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She gave me a good advice.  ✓ some good advice.
✗
I have many works to do.  ✓ a lot of work.
✗
The news are bad.  ✓ The news is bad.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Countable: a/an, plural, many. Uncountable: no a/an, no plural, much.
  • Learn the tricky ones: advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, work.
  • News / information / advice are singular: the news is…

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Исчисляемое имеет множ. число и a/an (a book, two books). Неисчисляемое — нет множ. числа и a/an (water, information): I need information (не an information).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Саналатын зат есімнің көпшесі бар, a/an алады (a book, two books). Саналмайтын — көпше жоқ, a/an жоқ (water, information): I need information (an information емес).

ru.title: Как ведут себя
ru.html: a/an: a chair ✓ / an advice ✗. Множ.: chairs ✓ / advices ✗. some/any: у обоих. much/many: many chairs / much advice.

kk.title: Қалай қолданылады
kk.html: a/an: a chair ✓ / an advice ✗. Көпше: chairs ✓ / advices ✗. some/any: екеуінде де. much/many: many chairs / much advice.

ru.title: Сложные неисчисляемые
ru.html: В английском неисчисляемы: advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, work, bread, money, weather, traffic, progress, research. News выглядит множ., но единств.: The news is good.

kk.title: Қиын саналмайтындар
kk.html: Ағылшынша саналмайды: advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, work, bread, money, weather, traffic, progress, research. News — көпше сияқты, бірақ жекеше: The news is good.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Do you need any help? — I need some information about trains. — There was so much traffic and my luggage was heavy.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Do you need any help? — I need some information about trains. — There was so much traffic and my luggage was heavy.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a good advice → some good advice. many works → a lot of work. The news are bad → The news is bad.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a good advice → some good advice. many works → a lot of work. The news are bad → The news is bad.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Исчисляемое: a/an, множ., many. Неисчисляемое: без a/an и множ., much. Учите сложные. news/information/advice — единственное число.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Саналатын: a/an, көпше, many. Саналмайтын: a/an жоқ, көпше жоқ, much. Қиындарды жаттаңыз. news/information/advice — жекеше.

activities.q: "Can you give me some ___ about hotels?"

activities.why: furniture/information are uncountable — no plural, no a/an.
Each uncountable (luggage/work/money) has a countable partner (suitcase/job/coin).

activities.q: "We made good ___ on the project this week."

activities.before: Uncountable 'bread' → a countable piece is a
alts: slice
roll
activities.why: a loaf/slice of bread makes it countable.
advice is uncountable → “two useful pieces of advice”.

activities.prompt: I have a lot of works to finish.
activities.answer: i have a lot of work to finish
alts: i have lots of work to finish

activities.prompt: The furnitures in the office are new.
activities.answer: the furniture in the office is new
alts: the furniture in the office is new.

options.t: Just one suitcase and some hand luggage.
Just one luggage and some suitcases.
options.why: 'one luggage' is wrong; luggage is uncountable.

options.t: The news is that it's delayed an hour.
The news are that it's delayed.

activities.why: Countable nouns take a/an, plurals and many; uncountable nouns (advice, information, news, furniture, luggage, work) have no plural, no a/an, and use much — and news/information are singular.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some nouns are both — countable or uncountable — with a different meaning. And 'a/an' vs 'some' depends on which one you mean.

a coffee (a cup) vs coffee (the drink in general) · a paper (newspaper) vs paper (material).

learn.title: Two meanings
learn.html:
noununcountablecountable
glassmade of glassa glass (of water)
hairhair on your heada hair (single one)
experiencework experiencean experience (an event)
timeI have no timethree times (occasions)

learn.title: a / an vs some
learn.html:
Use a/an with a singular countable noun; use some with plurals and uncountables.

I'd like a sandwich and some water. · There are some apples and some milk in the fridge.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Two coffees, please. (= two cups)  — And some water?

— Nice hair!  — Thanks — there's a hair in my soup, though!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'd like a water.  ✓ some water / a glass of water.
✗
I had many experiences of Italy (= I know it).  ✓ a lot of experience of Italy.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Some nouns are countable OR uncountable with different meanings (a coffee / coffee).
  • a/an + singular countable; some + plurals and uncountables.
  • experience (knowledge) = uncountable; an experience (event) = countable.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые существительные и то и другое — с разным значением: a coffee (чашка) vs coffee (напиток); a paper (газета) vs paper (материал).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір зат есім екеуі де — мағынасы бөлек: a coffee (кесе) vs coffee (сусын); a paper (газет) vs paper (материал).

ru.title: Два значения
ru.html: glass: стекло / a glass (стакан). hair: волосы / a hair (волосок). experience: опыт / an experience (событие). time: время / three times (раза).

kk.title: Екі мағына
kk.html: glass: шыны (материал) / a glass (стакан). hair: шаш / a hair (бір тал). experience: тәжірибе / an experience (оқиға). time: уақыт / three times (рет).

ru.title: a / an vs some
ru.html: a/an — с ед. исчисляемым; some — с множ. и неисчисляемым: a sandwich and some water; some apples and some milk.

kk.title: a / an vs some
kk.html: a/an — жекеше саналатынмен; some — көпше және саналмайтынмен: a sandwich and some water; some apples and some milk.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Two coffees, please. — And some water? — Nice hair! — There's a hair in my soup!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Two coffees, please. — And some water? — Nice hair! — There's a hair in my soup!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a water → some water / a glass of water. many experiences of Italy (=знать) → a lot of experience of Italy.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a water → some water / a glass of water. many experiences of Italy (=білу) → a lot of experience of Italy.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Некоторые слова двойные (a coffee / coffee). a/an + ед. исчисляемое; some + множ./неисчисляемое. experience (знание) — неисч.; an experience (событие) — исч.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Кейбір зат есім екі түрлі (a coffee / coffee). a/an + жекеше саналатын; some + көпше/саналмайтын. experience (білім) — саналмайтын; an experience (оқиға) — саналатын.

activities.q: "Would you like ___ tea?"

items.s: There's a hair in my soup.

activities.why: 'a coffee' = a cup (OK); 'a water' → some water / a glass of water.
'a glass/three coffees' count units; 'glass/coffee' name the material/drink.

activities.q: "Have you read today's ___? There's a big story."
"For breakfast I had ___ egg and ___ toast."
why: egg here is countable → an egg.

activities.why: water is uncountable → “some water” (or a glass of water).

activities.prompt: You want two cups of coffee.
alts: two coffees please
can i have two coffees

activities.prompt: He hasn't got many experiences with computers.
activities.answer: he hasn't got much experience with computers
alts: he doesn't have much experience with computers

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: ordering in a café

options.t: A sandwich and some orange juice, please.
A sandwich and an orange juice, please.
options.why: juice is uncountable → some juice / a glass of juice.

options.t: Two coffees to take away.
Two coffee to take away.

activities.why: Many nouns are countable or uncountable depending on meaning; use a/an with singular countables and some with plurals and uncountables.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
some and any mean an indefinite quantity. Rough rule: some in positive sentences, any in negatives and questions.

I have some money. · I don't have any money. · Have you got any money?

learn.title: The basic pattern
learn.html:
positiveThere's some milk. · I bought some apples.
negativeThere isn't any milk. · I didn't buy any apples.
questionIs there any milk? · Did you buy any apples?

learn.title: some in questions; any = 'it doesn't matter'
learn.html:
Use some in questions that are offers or requests (you expect 'yes'): Would you like some tea? · Can I have some water?

any in a positive sentence = 'it doesn't matter which': Take any seat. · Anyone can do it.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Is there any coffee left?  — There's some in the pot. Would you like some?

— Which bus?  — Any bus — they all go to the centre.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I don't have some time.  ✓ I don't have any time.
✗
Would you like any cake?  ✓ Would you like some cake? (offer)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• some = positives; any = negatives & questions.
  • Offers/requests use some: Would you like some…? Can I have some…?
  • any in a positive = 'it doesn't matter which' (any seat, anyone).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: some и any — неопределённое количество. Правило: some в утверждениях, any в отрицаниях и вопросах: I have some money. I don't have any. Have you got any?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: some мен any — белгісіз мөлшер. Ереже: some болымдыда, any болымсыз бен сұрақта: I have some money. I don't have any money. Have you got any money?

ru.title: Базовая модель
ru.html: Утверждение: There's some milk. Отрицание: isn't any milk. Вопрос: Is there any milk?

kk.title: Негізгі үлгі
kk.html: Болымды: There's some milk. Болымсыз: isn't any milk. Сұрақ: Is there any milk?

ru.title: some в вопросах; any = неважно
ru.html: some — в предложениях/просьбах (ждёте 'да'): Would you like some tea? any в утверждении = 'любой': Take any seat. Anyone can do it.

kk.title: Сұрақтағы some; any = маңызды емес
kk.html: some — ұсыныс/өтініш сұрағында ('иә' күтесіз): Would you like some tea? Болымдыдағы any = 'қайсы болса да': Take any seat. Anyone can do it.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Is there any coffee left? — There's some. Would you like some? — Which bus? — Any bus.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Is there any coffee left? — There's some. Would you like some? — Which bus? — Any bus.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: don't have some time → any time. Would you like any cake? → some cake (предложение).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: don't have some time → any time. Would you like any cake? → some cake (ұсыныс).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: some = утверждения; any = отрицания/вопросы. Предложения/просьбы — some. any в утверждении = 'любой'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: some = болымды; any = болымсыз/сұрақ. Ұсыныс/өтініш — some. Болымдыдағы any = 'қайсы болса да'.

activities.q: "There aren't ___ eggs left."
"You can sit anywhere — take ___ seat you like."
why: Correct — positive 'any' = it doesn't matter which.

activities.q: "Excuse me, can I have ___ information about tours?"
why: 'any' sounds less natural in a polite request.

activities.prompt: There's some milk in the fridge.
activities.answer: there isn't any milk in the fridge
alts: there is not any milk in the fridge
there's no milk in the fridge

activities.prompt: (You want to offer your guest a biscuit.)
alts: would you like a biscuit
would you like some biscuits?

options.t: Yes, there's some in the cupboard.
Yes, there's any in the cupboard.

steps.npc: Would you like some salad too?

options.t: Yes please, but we don't have any tomatoes.
Yes please, but we don't have some tomatoes.

activities.why: some in positives and offers/requests; any in negatives and neutral questions; any in a positive sentence means 'it doesn't matter which'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Use a/an when the listener doesn't know which one yet (new, one of many). Use the when it's clear which one (already mentioned, or the only one).

I saw a dog. The dog was barking. (a → new; the → the one just mentioned)

learn.title: When to use each
learn.html:
a/an — first mention / one of manyShe's a doctor. · I need a pen.
the — second mentionI bought a shirt. The shirt was cheap.
the — only one / clear from contextthe sun, the door (of this room)
the — superlatives / of-phrasesthe best day · the capital of Peru

learn.title: the when it's obvious
learn.html:
We use the when there's only one in the situation, even at first mention: Can you close the window? (the one here) · I'm going to the bank. (our usual/local one)

Also the same, and ordinals: the first, the next one.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— I've bought a car!  — Nice — what colour's the car?

— Where's Sam?  — In the kitchen. (the one in this house)

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She is doctor.  ✓ She is a doctor.
✗
Sun is hot today.  ✓ The sun is hot today.
✗
I saw the dog in the street (first mention, one of many).  ✓ I saw a dog.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• a/an = new to the listener / one of many; jobs (she's a nurse).
  • the = already known, only one, or clear from context.
  • the with superlatives, of-phrases, ordinals (the best, the end of…, the first).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: a/an — когда слушатель ещё не знает, какой (новый, один из многих). the — когда ясно, какой: I saw a dog. The dog was barking.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: a/an — тыңдаушы қайсысы екенін білмегенде (жаңа, көптің бірі). the — қайсысы екені белгілі: I saw a dog. The dog was barking.

ru.title: Когда какой
ru.html: a/an — первое упоминание/один из многих: a doctor, a pen. the — второе упоминание: The shirt. the — единственный/ясный из контекста: the sun, the door. the — superlative/of: the best, the capital of.

kk.title: Қашан қайсысы
kk.html: a/an — бірінші рет/көптің бірі: a doctor, a pen. the — екінші рет: The shirt. the — жалғыз/контекстен анық: the sun, the door. the — superlative/of: the best, the capital of.

ru.title: the — когда очевидно
ru.html: Если в ситуации только один, даже при первом упоминании the: close the window; go to the bank. Также the same и порядковые: the first, the next.

kk.title: the — анық болғанда
kk.html: Жағдайда біреу ғана болса, бірінші реттен-ақ the: close the window; go to the bank. Тағы the same және реттік: the first, the next.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I've bought a car! — What colour's the car? — Where's Sam? — In the kitchen.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I've bought a car! — What colour's the car? — Where's Sam? — In the kitchen.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: She is doctor. → a doctor. Sun is hot. → The sun. Первое упоминание, один из многих: a dog (не the dog).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: She is doctor. → a doctor. Sun is hot. → The sun. Бірінші рет, көптің бірі: a dog (the dog емес).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: a/an = новое для слушателя/один из многих; профессия (a nurse). the = известное, единственное, ясное из контекста. the + superlative/of/порядковое.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: a/an = тыңдаушыға жаңа/көптің бірі; кәсіп (a nurse). the = белгілі, жалғыз, контекстен анық. the + superlative/of/реттік.

activities.q: "I read ___ interesting book last week."
why: Correct — first mention, vowel sound → an interesting book.

items.s: He's the best in the class.

activities.before: I saw a man and a woman.
alts: the

activities.why: of-phrase, only one → the capital of France.

activities.answer: She is a nurse in a hospital

activities.q: "We had dinner at a restaurant. ___ food was excellent."
why: the food is now specific (of that restaurant).

activities.prompt: I bought _ jacket. _ jacket was on sale.
activities.answer: i bought a jacket. the jacket was on sale
alts: i bought a jacket the jacket was on sale

activities.prompt: Moon was very bright last night.
activities.answer: the moon was very bright last night
alts: the moon was very bright

options.t: I got a new job at a bank!
options.why: first mention, one of many → a.

options.t: I got the new job at the bank!
options.why: if it's news to the listener, use a (first mention).

options.t: The one on the High Street.
A one on a High Street.

activities.why: a/an introduces something new or one of many (and jobs); the marks something already known, unique, or clear from context, and follows superlatives and of-phrases.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
We use the for things there is only one of, for systems and services, and in some fixed place expressions.

the sun, the sky, the internet, the radio, the cinema, the same.

learn.title: Common uses of the
learn.html:
unique thingsthe sun, the moon, the world, the sea, the sky
systems / servicesthe internet, the radio, the bus, the phone
public places (activity)go to the cinema / the theatre / the bank
with same / onlythe same colour · the only problem

learn.title: No 'the': TV, meals, most abstract nouns
learn.html: Contrast: the radio but on TV / watch television (no 'the'). the cinema but breakfast/lunch/dinner (no 'the'). No 'the' with meals, languages, or general abstract nouns: Life is short. · I love music.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— What did you do last night?  — We went to the cinema, then listened to the radio at home.

— Is it on TV?  — No, it's on the internet.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I saw it on the TV.  ✓ on TV (but on the radio).
✗
The life is hard.  ✓ Life is hard. (general)
✗
We had the dinner at eight.  ✓ had dinner.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• the: unique things (the sun), systems/services (the internet, the radio), the cinema/theatre.
  • No 'the': TV, meals (breakfast/lunch), languages, and general abstract nouns (life, music).
  • the same, the only + noun.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: the — для единственных в своём роде, для систем и услуг, в устойчивых выражениях: the sun, the sky, the internet, the radio, the cinema, the same.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: the — біреу ғана болатын нәрсеге, жүйелер мен қызметтерге, кейбір тұрақты тіркестерге: the sun, the sky, the internet, the radio, the cinema, the same.

ru.title: Частые случаи the
ru.html: Единственные: the sun, the moon, the sea, the sky. Системы/услуги: the internet, the radio, the bus. Обществ. места (действие): the cinema/theatre/bank. same/only: the same, the only.

kk.title: the-нің жиі қолданысы
kk.html: Жалғыз: the sun, the moon, the sea, the sky. Жүйе/қызмет: the internet, the radio, the bus. Қоғамдық орын (әрекет): go to the cinema/theatre/bank. same/only: the same, the only.

ru.title: Без ‘the’: TV, еда, абстрактные
ru.html: Сравни: the radio, но on TV (без 'the'). the cinema, но breakfast/lunch/dinner (без 'the'). Языки, общие абстрактные: Life is short. I love music.

kk.title: ‘the’ жоқ: TV, тамақ, дерексіз
kk.html: Салыстыр: the radio, бірақ on TV ('the' жоқ). the cinema, бірақ breakfast/lunch/dinner ('the' жоқ). Тіл, жалпы дерексіз: Life is short. I love music.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — We went to the cinema, then listened to the radio. — Is it on TV? — No, it's on the internet.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — We went to the cinema, then listened to the radio. — Is it on TV? — No, it's on the internet.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: on the TV → on TV (но on the radio). The life is hard → Life is hard. had the dinner → had dinner.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: on the TV → on TV (бірақ on the radio). The life is hard → Life is hard. had the dinner → had dinner.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: the: единственное, системы/услуги, the cinema/theatre. Без ‘the’: TV, еда, языки, общие абстрактные. the same, the only.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: the: жалғыз нәрсе, жүйе/қызмет, the cinema/theatre. ‘the’ жоқ: TV, тамақ, тіл, жалпы дерексіз. the same, the only.

activities.q: "We watched a documentary on ___ last night."
why: 'the TV' refers to the physical set, not broadcasting.

items.s: The sun rises in the east.
I heard it on the radio.
We had the lunch at noon.

alts: nothing

activities.q: "I read about it on ___, not in a newspaper."
"We were born in ___ year."

activities.prompt: The breakfast is the most important meal of day.
activities.answer: breakfast is the most important meal of the day
alts: breakfast is the most important meal of the day.

activities.prompt: I saw the interesting programme on the TV.
activities.answer: i saw an interesting programme on tv
alts: i saw an interesting programme on tv.
activities.why: first mention → an; broadcast → on TV (no 'the').

options.t: Sure — shall we go to the theatre?
Sure — shall we go to theatre?
Let's just have breakfast and relax.
Let's just have the breakfast and relax.

activities.why: Use the for unique things, systems/services (the internet, the radio) and the cinema/theatre; use no article for TV (broadcast), meals, languages and general abstract nouns.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
With some places we use no article when we mean the normal purpose/activity, and the when we mean the building itself.

He's in hospital (= a patient). · I went to the hospital to visit him (= the building).

learn.title: No article — the purpose
learn.html:
go to school / be at school= study there (a pupil)
in hospital / in prison= as a patient / a prisoner
at university, at church= to study / to worship
go to bed, be in bed= to sleep

learn.title: the = the building; and 'work' / 'home'
learn.html:
Use the for the building/other reasons: The parents visited the school. · There's a car park behind the church.

Special: work and home take no article and no 'to' with home: go home, be at work, at home. (Not to home, the work.)

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where are the kids?  — At school. I'm picking them up from the school at three.

— Are you still at work?  — No, I'm going home now.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'm going to home.  ✓ I'm going home.
✗
She's at the work.  ✓ at work.
✗
He was sent to the prison (= as a prisoner).  ✓ sent to prison.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• No article for the purpose: go to school/bed, in hospital/prison, at church.
  • the for the building or another reason (visit the school).
  • work/home: no article; home takes no 'to' (go home).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: С некоторыми местами нет артикля — когда речь о цели/деятельности; the — когда о здании: in hospital (пациент) vs the hospital (здание).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір орында артикль жоқ — қалыпты мақсат/әрекет болғанда; the — ғимаратты білдіргенде: in hospital (науқас) vs the hospital (ғимарат).

ru.title: Без артикля — цель
ru.html: go to school = учиться; in hospital/prison = пациент/заключённый; at university/church; go to bed = спать.

kk.title: Артикльсіз — мақсат
kk.html: go to school = оқу; in hospital/prison = науқас/тұтқын; at university/church; go to bed = ұйықтау.

ru.title: the = здание; work / home
ru.html: Здание/другая причина: visited the school; behind the church. work, home — без артикля; с home нет ‘to’: go home, at work (не to home, the work).

kk.title: the = ғимарат; work / home
kk.html: Ғимарат/басқа себеп: visited the school; behind the church. work, home — артикльсіз; home-мен ‘to’ жоқ: go home, at work (to home, the work емес).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Where are the kids? — At school. I'm picking them up from the school at three. — Still at work? — No, going home.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Where are the kids? — At school. I'm picking them up from the school at three. — Still at work? — No, going home.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: going to home → going home. at the work → at work. sent to the prison (заключённый) → to prison.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: going to home → going home. at the work → at work. sent to the prison (тұтқын) → to prison.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Цель — без артикля (go to school/bed, in hospital). the — здание. work/home — без артикля; с home нет ‘to’.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Мақсат — артикльсіз (go to school/bed, in hospital). the — ғимарат. work/home — артикльсіз; home-мен ‘to’ жоқ.

activities.q: "My son goes to ___ every morning to study."

items.s: He's in hospital after the accident.
We visited the school on open day.

activities.q: "After the trial, he was sent to ___ for five years."
why: 'the prison' = a specific building, not the purpose.

activities.q: "I usually get ___ from work around six."
why: 'at home' = a state, not the movement.

activities.prompt: My father is in the prison; I visit the prison on Sundays.
activities.answer: my father is in prison; i visit the prison on sundays
alts: my father is in prison, i visit the prison on sundays
activities.why: in prison (prisoner) vs the prison (the building you visit).

activities.prompt: After school, we went to the home.
alts: after school we went home
activities.why: no article for school (purpose) or home; no 'to' with home.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: a busy family day
activities.scene: Use the / no article with places.

options.t: At university — she has lectures.
At the university — she has lectures.
Just finished work, heading home.
options.why: work/home take no article; home no 'to'.

options.t: Just finished the work, heading to home.

activities.why: Use no article for the normal purpose (school, hospital, prison, bed) and for work/home; use the for the building itself or another reason — and never 'to home'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To talk about things in general, use a plural or uncountable noun with no article.

Cars are expensive. · Music is powerful. (NOT The cars / The music in general)

learn.title: General vs specific
learn.html:
general (no the)Children learn fast. · Life is short.
specific (the)The children next door are noisy.
general uncountableI love coffee / art / history.
specific of-phrasethe history of Rome

learn.title: the + singular = a whole species/instrument
learn.html:
In formal/scientific style, the + singular = the whole class: The tiger is an endangered animal. · The telephone was invented in 1876.

With musical instruments: play the piano, the guitar.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Do you like dogs?  — I love them, but the dogs upstairs bark all night.

— Can you play the guitar?  — A bit. I prefer listening to music.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The dogs are loyal animals (in general).  ✓ Dogs are loyal animals.
✗
I study the history.  ✓ I study history.
✗
She plays piano.  ✓ plays the piano.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• General idea → plural/uncountable with no article (Cars are…, Music is…).
  • Specific → the (the cars we saw). of-phrases → the (the history of…).
  • the + singular = a whole species (the tiger); play the + instrument.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: О вещах в общем — множественное или неисчисляемое без артикля: Cars are expensive. Music is powerful (не The cars).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Жалпы туралы айтқанда көпше немесе саналмайтын зат есім артикльсіз: Cars are expensive. Music is powerful (The cars емес).

ru.title: Общее vs конкретное
ru.html: Общее (без the): Children learn fast. Life is short. Конкретное (the): The children next door. Общее неисч.: love coffee/art. of: the history of Rome.

kk.title: Жалпы vs нақты
kk.html: Жалпы (the жоқ): Children learn fast. Life is short. Нақты (the): The children next door. Жалпы саналмайтын: love coffee/art. of: the history of Rome.

ru.title: the + ед.ч. = весь вид/инструмент
ru.html: В формальном стиле the + ед.ч. = весь класс: The tiger is endangered. The telephone was invented in 1876. Инструменты: play the piano, the guitar.

kk.title: the + жекеше = бүкіл түр/аспап
kk.html: Ресми стильде the + жекеше = бүкіл сынып: The tiger is endangered. The telephone was invented in 1876. Аспаптар: play the piano, the guitar.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Do you like dogs? — I love them, but the dogs upstairs bark all night. — Play the guitar? — I prefer music.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Do you like dogs? — I love them, but the dogs upstairs bark all night. — Play the guitar? — I prefer music.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: The dogs are loyal (в общем) → Dogs are loyal. study the history → study history. plays piano → plays the piano.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: The dogs are loyal (жалпы) → Dogs are loyal. study the history → study history. plays piano → plays the piano.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Общее → множ./неисч., без артикля. Конкретное → the; of → the. the + ед.ч. = весь вид; play the + инструмент.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Жалпы → көпше/саналмайтын, артикльсіз. Нақты → the; of → the. the + жекеше = бүкіл түр; play the + аспап.

activities.q: "___ are good for your health."

activities.after: is my favourite subject at school. (History)
alts: history

activities.why: general → no article; a specific set → the.

activities.q: "___ children in this class are very bright."
why: without 'the' it would mean children in general.

activities.q: "___ was invented before the car."

activities.why: general statement → “Computers have changed the world.”

activities.prompt: The books are a great source of knowledge.
activities.answer: books are a great source of knowledge
alts: books are a great source of knowledge.

activities.prompt: I love the nature and I study the biology.
activities.answer: i love nature and i study biology
alts: i love nature and study biology
activities.why: general abstract nouns take no article.

steps.npc: What do you think about social media?

options.t: Honestly, I think phones control our lives too much.
Honestly, I think the phones control our lives.
Yes, I play the piano and love jazz.
options.why: play the piano; jazz (general) no article.

options.t: Yes, I play piano and love the jazz.
options.why: play the piano; general 'jazz' takes no 'the'.

activities.why: Talk about things in general with a plural or uncountable noun and no article; use the for a specific group, of-phrases, whole species (the tiger) and instruments (the piano).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
the + adjective can mean a group of people in general (all of them). It is plural in meaning.

the rich = rich people · the young = young people · the unemployed = people without a job.

learn.title: the + adjective (groups)
learn.html:
the rich / the poorThe rich get richer. (plural verb)
the young / the old / the elderlyThe old deserve respect.
the sick / the injured / the homelessThe injured were taken to hospital.
the blind / the deaf / the disabledfacilities for the disabled

learn.title: Nationalities: the + -sh/-ch/-ese
learn.html:
the French, the English, the Spanish, the Chinese, the Japanese = the people of that country (plural): The French are proud of their food.

Others use a normal plural: Germans, Italians, Brazilians. For one person: a Frenchman / a German.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— The city helps the homeless in winter.  — Good — the vulnerable need support.

— The Japanese are famous for their politeness.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The rich is getting richer.  ✓ The rich are getting richer.
✗
the riches (= rich people)  ✓ the rich (no -s).
✗
The Frenchs love cheese.  ✓ The French love cheese.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• the + adjective = a whole group of people (plural verb, no -s): the rich are…
  • Nationalities in -sh/-ch/-ese: the French/Chinese are …
  • Other nationalities use a plural: Germans, Italians.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: the + прилагательное = группа людей в целом. Значение множественное: the rich = богатые; the young = молодёжь; the unemployed = безработные.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: the + сын есім — жалпы адамдар тобы (бәрі). Мағынасы көпше: the rich = бай адамдар; the young = жастар; the unemployed = жұмыссыздар.

ru.title: the + прил. (группы)
ru.html: the rich/poor: The rich get richer (множ. глагол). the young/old/elderly. the sick/injured/homeless: The injured were taken. the blind/deaf/disabled.

kk.title: the + сын есім (топтар)
kk.html: the rich/poor: The rich get richer (көпше етістік). the young/old/elderly. the sick/injured/homeless: The injured were taken. the blind/deaf/disabled.

ru.title: Национальности: the + -sh/-ch/-ese
ru.html: the French, the English, the Chinese, the Japanese = народ страны (множ.): The French are proud. Другие — обычное множ.: Germans, Italians. Один человек: a Frenchman.

kk.title: Ұлттар: the + -sh/-ch/-ese
kk.html: the French, the English, the Chinese, the Japanese = сол елдің халқы (көпше): The French are proud. Басқалары қалыпты көпше: Germans, Italians. Бір адам: a Frenchman.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — The city helps the homeless in winter. — The vulnerable need support. — The Japanese are famous for their politeness.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — The city helps the homeless in winter. — The vulnerable need support. — The Japanese are famous for their politeness.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: The rich is getting richer. → The rich are. the riches (=богатые) → the rich. The Frenchs → The French.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: The rich is getting richer. → The rich are. the riches (=байлар) → the rich. The Frenchs → The French.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: the + прил. = группа людей (множ. глагол, без -s). -sh/-ch/-ese: the French are… Другие: Germans, Italians.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: the + сын есім = адамдар тобы (көпше етістік, -s жоқ). -sh/-ch/-ese ұлттар: the French are… Басқалары: Germans, Italians.

activities.q: "The rich ___ not always happy."

alts: the old
activities.why: a group of people → the elderly/the old.

activities.after: are known for their sense of humour. (English people → the + …)
alts: the English

activities.q: "After the earthquake, ___ were rescued first."
"He's ___, and generally the French value their cuisine."
why: 'a French' isn't used for a person; use French or a Frenchman.
'the French' = the whole nation, not one person.

activities.prompt: Unemployed people receive support from the state.
activities.answer: the unemployed receive support from the state
alts: the unemployed receive state support

activities.prompt: People from Japan are very polite.
alts: the japanese are very polite.

options.t: Helping the poor and the sick, in my view.
Helping the poors and the sicks.
The Swedish are often praised for it.
The Swedishs are often praised.

activities.why: the + adjective names a whole group of people and takes a plural verb with no -s; nationalities ending in -sh/-ch/-ese use the + word (the French are…), others take a plural -s (Germans).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Whether a geographical name takes the follows patterns worth learning: mostly no the for single places, the for groups and 'of' names.

France, Mount Everest, Lake Como — no the. · The Alps, The Nile, The USA — with the.

learn.title: No 'the'
learn.html:
continents / countriesAfrica, Japan, Brazil
cities / single mountainsTokyo, (Mount) Fuji
single lakes / islandsLake Victoria, Sicily

learn.title: With 'the'
learn.html:
rivers / seas / oceansthe Thames, the Pacific, the Red Sea
mountain ranges / island groupsthe Alps, the Maldives
plural or 'of' countriesthe Netherlands, the UK, the USA
deserts / canals / gulfsthe Sahara, the Suez Canal

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where are you travelling?  — To Italy — a boat trip on the Mediterranean, then up into the Alps.

— Have you seen the Nile?  — No, but I've visited Egypt.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the France, the Africa  ✓ France, Africa (no the).
✗
Nile, Pacific Ocean  ✓ the Nile, the Pacific.
✗
USA is big.  ✓ The USA is big.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• No the: continents, countries, cities, single mountains/lakes/islands.
  • the: rivers, seas, oceans, ranges, island groups, deserts, canals.
  • the with plural/‘of’ countries: the Netherlands, the UK, the USA.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Артикль the у географических названий подчиняется схеме: одиночные — чаще без the; группы и названия с ‘of’ — the. France, Everest — без the. The Alps, The Nile, The USA — с the.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Географиялық атаудың the алуы үлгіге бағынады: жалғыз орындар — көбіне the жоқ; топтар мен ‘of’ атаулары — the. France, Everest — the жоқ. The Alps, The Nile, The USA — the.

ru.title: Без ‘the’
ru.html: Континенты/страны: Africa, Japan, Brazil. Города/отдельные горы: Tokyo, (Mount) Fuji. Отдельные озёра/острова: Lake Victoria, Sicily.

kk.title: ‘the’ жоқ
kk.html: Құрлықтар/елдер: Africa, Japan, Brazil. Қалалар/жеке таулар: Tokyo, (Mount) Fuji. Жеке көлдер/аралдар: Lake Victoria, Sicily.

ru.title: С ‘the’
ru.html: Реки/моря/океаны: the Thames, the Pacific, the Red Sea. Хребты/группы островов: the Alps, the Maldives. Множ./‘of’ страны: the Netherlands, the UK, the USA. Пустыни/каналы: the Sahara, the Suez Canal.

kk.title: ‘the’-мен
kk.html: Өзен/теңіз/мұхит: the Thames, the Pacific, the Red Sea. Тау жоталары/арал топтары: the Alps, the Maldives. Көпше/‘of’ елдер: the Netherlands, the UK, the USA. Шөл/канал: the Sahara, the Suez Canal.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — To Italy — a boat trip on the Mediterranean, then into the Alps. — Seen the Nile? — I've visited Egypt.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — To Italy — a boat trip on the Mediterranean, then into the Alps. — Seen the Nile? — I've visited Egypt.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the France, the Africa → France, Africa. Nile, Pacific Ocean → the Nile, the Pacific. USA is big → The USA is big.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the France, the Africa → France, Africa. Nile, Pacific Ocean → the Nile, the Pacific. USA is big → The USA is big.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Без the: континент, страна, город, отдельная гора/озеро/остров. the: река, море, океан, хребет, группа островов, пустыня, канал. the + множ./‘of’ страна.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: the жоқ: құрлық, ел, қала, жеке тау/көл/арал. the: өзен, теңіз, мұхит, жота, арал тобы, шөл, канал. the + көпше/‘of’ ел.

activities.q: "They sailed across ___ Atlantic."

activities.why: single mountains take no 'the' → Mount Everest.

activities.after: Alps stretch across several countries. (article or —)
alts: the
nothing

activities.why: country/single lake → no the; river/island group → the.

activities.q: "___ Netherlands is famous for its tulips."
"We took a boat down ___ Danube through ___ Hungary."

activities.prompt: _ United Kingdom includes _ Scotland.
alts: the uk includes scotland
activities.why: 'of'-type country → the UK; single country → Scotland (no article).

activities.prompt: _ Everest is the highest peak in _ Himalayas.
activities.answer: everest is the highest peak in the himalayas
alts: mount everest is the highest peak in the himalayas
activities.why: single mountain → no article; range → the Himalayas.

activities.scene: Use the / no article with places.

options.t: A cruise on the Nile, then a few days in Egypt.
options.why: river → the Nile; country → Egypt (no article).

options.t: A cruise on Nile, then a few days in the Egypt.
Maybe the Maldives — I love island groups.
Maybe Maldives — I love island groups.

activities.why: No 'the' for continents, countries, cities and single mountains/lakes/islands; 'the' for rivers, seas, oceans, ranges, island groups, deserts, canals and plural/‘of’ countries.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Names of streets, buildings and institutions also follow patterns: mostly no the, but the for many public buildings and 'of' names.

Oxford Street, Buckingham Palace, Cambridge University — no the. · The White House, The British Museum — with the.

learn.title: No 'the'
learn.html:
streets / squares / parksBond Street, Times Square, Hyde Park
airports / stationsHeathrow Airport, Victoria Station
Name + University / CastleOxford University, Edinburgh Castle
shops / restaurants (person's name)Harrods, McDonald's, Joe's Café

learn.title: With 'the'
learn.html:
hotels / theatres / cinemasthe Hilton, the Odeon, the Ritz
museums / galleriesthe British Museum, the Tate
'…of…' namesthe University of Oxford, the Tower of London
newspapersthe Times, the Guardian

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where shall we meet?  — Outside the Odeon on Baker Street, then dinner at Luigi's.

— Have you been to the British Museum?  — Yes, and to Buckingham Palace.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
on the Oxford Street  ✓ on Oxford Street.
✗
We stayed at Hilton.  ✓ at the Hilton.
✗
the Cambridge University  ✓ Cambridge University (but the University of Cambridge).

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• No the: streets, parks, stations, airports, Name+University/Castle, shops named after people.
  • the: hotels, theatres, cinemas, museums, galleries, newspapers.
  • ‘…of…’ names take the: the Tower of London, the University of Oxford.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Названия улиц, зданий, учреждений тоже по схеме: чаще без the, но многие общественные здания и ‘of’-названия — the. Oxford Street, Buckingham Palace — без the. The White House, The British Museum — с the.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көше, ғимарат, мекеме атаулары да үлгіге бағынады: көбіне the жоқ, бірақ көп қоғамдық ғимарат пен ‘of’ атаулары — the. Oxford Street, Buckingham Palace — the жоқ. The White House, The British Museum — the.

ru.title: Без ‘the’
ru.html: Улица/площадь/парк: Bond Street, Hyde Park. Аэропорт/вокзал: Heathrow Airport, Victoria Station. Name + University/Castle: Oxford University. Магазины/кафе (имя): Harrods, McDonald's.

kk.title: ‘the’ жоқ
kk.html: Көше/алаң/парк: Bond Street, Hyde Park. Әуежай/вокзал: Heathrow Airport, Victoria Station. Name + University/Castle: Oxford University. Дүкен/мейрамхана (адам аты): Harrods, McDonald's.

ru.title: С ‘the’
ru.html: Отель/театр/кинотеатр: the Hilton, the Odeon. Музей/галерея: the British Museum, the Tate. ‘…of…’: the Tower of London. Газеты: the Times, the Guardian.

kk.title: ‘the’-мен
kk.html: Қонақ үй/театр/кинотеатр: the Hilton, the Odeon. Мұражай/галерея: the British Museum, the Tate. ‘…of…’: the Tower of London. Газеттер: the Times, the Guardian.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Outside the Odeon on Baker Street, then dinner at Luigi's. — Been to the British Museum? — Yes, and to Buckingham Palace.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Outside the Odeon on Baker Street, then dinner at Luigi's. — Been to the British Museum? — Yes, and to Buckingham Palace.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: on the Oxford Street → on Oxford Street. at Hilton → at the Hilton. the Cambridge University → Cambridge University (но the University of Cambridge).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: on the Oxford Street → on Oxford Street. at Hilton → at the Hilton. the Cambridge University → Cambridge University (бірақ the University of Cambridge).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Без the: улица, парк, вокзал, аэропорт, Name+University/Castle, магазин по имени. the: отель, театр, музей, газета. ‘…of…’ → the.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: the жоқ: көше, парк, вокзал, әуежай, Name+University/Castle, адам атындағы дүкен. the: қонақ үй, театр, мұражай, газет. ‘…of…’ → the.

activities.q: "The shop is on ___ Regent Street."

activities.why: shops named after people take no 'the' → Harrods.

alts: nothing

activities.why: parks/stations → no the; galleries/‘of’ names → the.

activities.q: "The painting is in ___ National Gallery."
"She got her degree from ___ University of Edinburgh."
why: Correct — the University of Edinburgh (but 'Edinburgh University' without).

activities.why: shops named after a person → no article → “at McDonald's”.

activities.prompt: We visited _ Buckingham Palace and _ Tower of London.
activities.answer: we visited buckingham palace and the tower of london
alts: we visited buckingham palace and the tower of london.
activities.why: Palace name → no article; ‘…of…’ name → the.

activities.prompt: They stayed at _ Hilton on _ Park Lane.
activities.answer: they stayed at the hilton on park lane
alts: they stayed at the hilton on park lane.
activities.why: hotel → the Hilton; street → Park Lane (no article).

activities.scene: Use the / no article with names.

options.t: At the Royal Albert Hall, near Hyde Park.
At Royal Albert Hall, near the Hyde Park.
options.why: the Royal Albert Hall; Hyde Park (no article).

options.t: At Nando's on Oxford Street.
options.why: restaurant by name + street → no article.

options.t: At the Nando's on the Oxford Street.

activities.why: No 'the' for streets, parks, stations, airports, Name+University/Castle and shops named after people; 'the' for hotels, theatres, cinemas, museums, galleries, newspapers and ‘…of…’ names.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some nouns don't follow the usual rules: a few are always plural, some plural in form but singular, and some collective nouns can take either verb.

My trousers are new. · The police have arrived. · Physics is hard.

learn.title: Always plural (no singular)
learn.html:
two-part thingstrousers, jeans, glasses, scissors → are
use 'a pair of'a pair of scissors is …
plural-only nounsclothes, goods, thanks → are

learn.title: Tricky agreement
learn.html:
police, people, staff, cattle take a plural verb: The police are… The people were…

Plural-looking but singular: news, physics, mathematics, athletics, economics → is. Also 'a series/means/species' is singular.

Collective nouns (team, family, government) can take is (the unit) or are (the members).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where are my glasses?  — They're on the table.

— Is the news on?  — Yes — the police are giving a statement.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
The police is coming.  ✓ The police are coming.
✗
My jeans is dirty.  ✓ My jeans are dirty.
✗
Mathematics are difficult.  ✓ Mathematics is difficult.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Two-part things (trousers, scissors) and clothes/goods → plural (are).
  • police, people, staff → plural verb; news, physics, maths → singular verb.
  • Collective nouns → is (the group) or are (the members).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые существительные — исключения: одни всегда множ., другие по форме множ., по смыслу ед.: My trousers are new. The police have arrived. Physics is hard.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір зат есім ережеден тыс: біреулері әрдайым көпше, кейбірі пішіні көпше, мағынасы жекеше: My trousers are new. The police have arrived. Physics is hard.

ru.title: Всегда множ.
ru.html: Из двух частей: trousers, jeans, glasses, scissors → are. Через 'a pair of': a pair of scissors is. Только множ.: clothes, goods, thanks → are.

kk.title: Әрдайым көпше
kk.html: Екі бөлікті заттар: trousers, jeans, glasses, scissors → are. 'a pair of' арқылы: a pair of scissors is. Тек көпше: clothes, goods, thanks → are.

ru.title: Сложное согласование
ru.html: police, people, staff, cattle — множ. глагол. По форме множ., по смыслу ед.: news, physics, mathematics, economics → is. a series/means/species — ед. Собирательные (team, family): is или are.

kk.title: Қиын үйлесім
kk.html: police, people, staff, cattle — көпше етістік. Пішіні көпше, мағынасы жекеше: news, physics, mathematics, economics → is. a series/means/species — жекеше. Ұжымдық (team, family): is не are.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Where are my glasses? — They're on the table. — Is the news on? — The police are giving a statement.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Where are my glasses? — They're on the table. — Is the news on? — The police are giving a statement.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: The police is coming. → are. My jeans is dirty. → are. Mathematics are difficult. → is.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: The police is coming. → are. My jeans is dirty. → are. Mathematics are difficult. → is.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Из двух частей/clothes/goods → множ. police/people/staff → множ.; news/physics/maths → ед. Собирательные → is или are.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Екі бөлікті/clothes/goods → көпше. police/people/staff → көпше; news/physics/maths → жекеше. Ұжымдық → is не are.

activities.q: "Your trousers ___ on the chair."

alts: is

activities.q: "A pair of scissors ___ on the desk."
why: Correct — 'a pair' is the singular subject → is.
the verb agrees with 'a pair', not 'scissors'.

activities.q: "The team ___ celebrating their win in the changing room."
why: Correct — the members acting individually → are celebrating.

activities.prompt: My jeans is too tight.
alts: my jeans are too tight.

activities.prompt: The news about the floods are worrying.
activities.answer: the news about the floods is worrying
alts: the news about the floods is worrying.

options.t: The police are talking to some people.
The police is talking to some peoples.
The news is that everyone is safe.
The news are that everyone are safe.

activities.why: Two-part things (trousers) and police/people/staff take plural verbs; news/physics/maths and 'a pair/series/means' take singular verbs; collective nouns can take either.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
English often puts two nouns together: the first noun acts like an adjective and describes the second. The second noun is the main one.

a bus stop = a stop for buses · a car key = a key for the car.

learn.title: Key rules
learn.html:
first noun usually singulara shoe shop (not shoes shop)
plural on the second noun onlythree bus stops, two toothbrushes
meaning: noun2 is the thinga garden chair = a chair (for the garden)
stress on the first nouna WEEKend, a POST office

learn.title: noun+noun vs 's
learn.html:
Use noun + noun for types/purpose (a coffee cup = a cup for coffee). Use 's or of for possession by people (my brother's car).

Measurements: a five-star hotel, a ten-minute walk — the first part stays singular and often hyphenated.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where's the train station?  — Past the shopping centre, next to the car park.

— It's a twenty-minute walk, or a short bus ride.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a shoes shop  ✓ a shoe shop.
✗
a five-stars hotel  ✓ a five-star hotel.
✗
the car of my father (usual possession)  ✓ my father's car.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• noun + noun: the first describes the second (bus stop, coffee cup).
  • First noun stays singular; only the second takes the plural (bus stops).
  • Use 's/of for people's possessions; measurements use singular + hyphen (a ten-minute walk).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: В английском часто два существительных подряд: первое как прилагательное описывает второе. Главное — второе: a bus stop = остановка; a car key = ключ.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ағылшын жиі екі зат есімді қатар қояды: біріншісі сын есім сияқты, екіншісін сипаттайды. Басты — екінші: a bus stop = аялдама; a car key = кілт.

ru.title: Ключевые правила
ru.html: Первое существительное обычно ед.ч.: a shoe shop. Множ. только у второго: three bus stops. Смысл: noun2 — предмет. Ударение на первом слове: a WEEKend.

kk.title: Негізгі ережелер
kk.html: Бірінші зат есім әдетте жекеше: a shoe shop. Көпше тек екіншісінде: three bus stops. Мағына: noun2 — зат. Екпін бірінші сөзде: a WEEKend.

ru.title: noun+noun vs 's
ru.html: Тип/назначение — noun + noun: a coffee cup. Принадлежность людям — 's/of: my brother's car. Измерения: a five-star hotel, a ten-minute walk (ед.ч., дефис).

kk.title: noun+noun vs 's
kk.html: Түр/мақсат — noun + noun: a coffee cup. Адамның иелігі — 's/of: my brother's car. Өлшем: a five-star hotel, a ten-minute walk (жекеше, дефис).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Where's the train station? — Past the shopping centre, next to the car park. — It's a twenty-minute walk.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Where's the train station? — Past the shopping centre, next to the car park. — It's a twenty-minute walk.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a shoes shop → a shoe shop. a five-stars hotel → a five-star hotel. the car of my father → my father's car.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a shoes shop → a shoe shop. a five-stars hotel → a five-star hotel. the car of my father → my father's car.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: noun + noun: первое описывает второе. Первое — ед.ч.; множ. у второго. Принадлежность — 's/of; измерения — ед.ч. + дефис.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: noun + noun: біріншісі екіншісін сипаттайды. Бірінші — жекеше; көпше екіншісінде. Адам иелігі — 's/of; өлшем — жекеше + дефис.

It was a journey of three hours — a

activities.answer: I lost my house keys yesterday

"A cup used for tea is a ___."

alts: train ticket

activities.prompt: a break of twenty minutes
alts: a twenty minute break
activities.why: measurement → singular + hyphen → a twenty-minute break.

steps.npc: Tell me about your new flat.

options.t: It's near a shopping centre and a bus stop.
It's near a shoppings centre and a buses stop.
Yes, with a huge kitchen table.
options.why: kitchen table = table for the kitchen.

options.t: Yes, with a huge kitchen's table.

activities.why: In noun + noun, the first noun describes the second and stays singular; the plural goes on the second noun. Use 's/of for people's possessions, and singular + hyphen for measurements.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To show possession, use 's mainly with people and animals, and of mainly with things.

my sister's room · the roof of the house (not usually 'the house's roof').

learn.title: 's or of?
learn.html:
people / animals → 'sTom's bike · the dog's tail
plural ending in -s → s'my parents' house · the girls' team
irregular plural → 'sthe children's toys · women's rights
things → ofthe top of the page · the end of the film

learn.title: 's with time & places; double possessive
learn.html:
Use 's also with time and organisations/places: today's news, a week's holiday, the government's plan, London's parks.

a friend of mine / a friend of Tom's = one of my/Tom's friends (double possessive).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Whose phone is this?  — It's Sarah's. Hers is over there — that's the end of the table.

— Is she a friend of yours?  — Yes, an old friend of mine.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the room of my sister  ✓ my sister's room.
✗
the childrens' toys  ✓ the children's toys.
✗
a friend of me  ✓ a friend of mine.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• People/animals → 's; things → of.
  • Plural -s → just apostrophe (parents'); irregular plural → 's (children's).
  • Time/places → 's (today's, London's); double possessive: a friend of mine.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Принадлежность: 's — в основном люди/животные; of — в основном вещи: my sister's room; the roof of the house.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Иелікті білдіру: 's — негізінен адам/жануар; of — негізінен зат: my sister's room; the roof of the house.

ru.title: 's или of?
ru.html: Люди/животные → 's: Tom's bike. Множ. на -s → s': my parents' house. Неправ. множ. → 's: children's. Вещи → of: the top of the page.

kk.title: 's па, of па?
kk.html: Адам/жануар → 's: Tom's bike. -s-ке біткен көпше → s': my parents' house. Бұрыс көпше → 's: children's. Зат → of: the top of the page.

ru.title: 's — время/место; двойное
ru.html: 's — также со временем и организациями/местами: today's news, a week's holiday, London's parks. a friend of mine / of Tom's = один из моих/Тома друзей (двойное).

kk.title: 's — уақыт/орын; қос иелік
kk.html: 's — уақыт пен ұйым/орында да: today's news, a week's holiday, London's parks. a friend of mine / of Tom's = менің/Том достарының бірі (қос иелік).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Whose phone is this? — It's Sarah's. — Is she a friend of yours? — An old friend of mine.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Whose phone is this? — It's Sarah's. — Is she a friend of yours? — An old friend of mine.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the room of my sister → my sister's room. the childrens' toys → the children's toys. a friend of me → a friend of mine.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the room of my sister → my sister's room. the childrens' toys → the children's toys. a friend of me → a friend of mine.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Люди/животные → 's; вещи → of. Множ. -s → только апостроф (parents'); неправ. множ. → 's (children's). Время/место → 's; двойное: a friend of mine.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Адам/жануар → 's; зат → of. Көпше -s → апостроф қана (parents'); бұрыс көпше → 's (children's). Уақыт/орын → 's; қос иелік: a friend of mine.

activities.why: a thing → the top of the page.

activities.answer: I stayed at my friend's house

"He's a colleague ___."
why: 'of my' needs a noun after it.

alts: todays news

options.t: It's my daughter's — pink, with a broken zip.
It's the bag of my daughter — pink.
Yes, the keys to the house and a week's worth of notes.
options.why: 'of' for a thing; 's for a time expression.

options.t: Yes, the house's keys and a week worth of notes.
options.why: the keys of/to the house; a week's worth.

activities.why: Use 's for people, animals, time and places; use of for things. Plural -s takes just an apostrophe (parents'), irregular plurals take 's (children's), and 'a friend of mine' is the double possessive.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Quantity words split by noun type: many/few with countable plurals, much/little with uncountables, and a lot of with both.

many books, much time, a lot of both.

learn.title: Which word with which noun
learn.html:
countableuncountable
large amountmany / a lot ofmuch / a lot of
small amount(a) few(a) little
question / negativeHow many? / not manyHow much? / not much

learn.title: a few / few — a little / little
learn.html:
a few / a little = some (a positive, small amount): I have a few friends here (= some).

few / little (no 'a') = almost none (negative feel): I have few friends here (= not many, lonely). She has little patience.

much/many sound formal in positives; a lot of is more natural: a lot of people came.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How much money have we got?  — Not much, but we have a few coins.

— Busy day?  — Yes, so many emails and so little time!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
much friends  ✓ many friends / a lot of friends.
✗
I have a few money.  ✓ a little money.
✗
She has a little friends.  ✓ a few friends.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Countable: many, (a) few. Uncountable: much, (a) little. Both: a lot of.
  • a few/a little = some (positive); few/little = almost none (negative).
  • much/many are common in questions & negatives; a lot of in positives.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Слова количества по типу существительного: many/few — исчисляемые множ.; much/little — неисчисляемые; a lot of — оба. many books, much time, a lot of both.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Мөлшер сөздері зат түріне қарай: many/few — саналатын көпше; much/little — саналмайтын; a lot of — екеуі де. many books, much time, a lot of both.

ru.title: Какое слово с каким
ru.html: Много: many / a lot of (исч.), much / a lot of (неисч.). Мало: (a) few / (a) little. Вопрос/отрицание: How many/much?, not many/much.

kk.title: Қай сөз қай зат есіммен
kk.html: Көп: many / a lot of (саналатын), much / a lot of (саналмайтын). Аз: (a) few / (a) little. Сұрақ/болымсыз: How many/much?, not many/much.

ru.title: a few/few — a little/little
ru.html: a few / a little = немного (позитив): a few friends (=несколько). few / little (без 'a') = почти нет (негатив): few friends (=мало). much/many в утверждениях формальны; a lot of естественнее.

kk.title: a few/few — a little/little
kk.html: a few / a little = біраз (оң): a few friends (=біраз). few / little ('a' жоқ) = дерлік жоқ (теріс): few friends (=аз, жалғыз). much/many оң сөйлемде ресми; a lot of табиғи.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How much money? — Not much, but we have a few coins. — So many emails and so little time!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How much money? — Not much, but we have a few coins. — So many emails and so little time!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: much friends → many friends. a few money → a little money. a little friends → a few friends.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: much friends → many friends. a few money → a little money. a little friends → a few friends.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Исч.: many, (a) few. Неисч.: much, (a) little. Оба: a lot of. a few/a little = немного; few/little = почти нет.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Саналатын: many, (a) few. Саналмайтын: much, (a) little. Екеуі: a lot of. a few/a little = біраз; few/little = дерлік жоқ.

"Cheer up — there's still ___ hope."
why: 'little hope' would mean almost none — the wrong tone.

activities.answer: she has a lot of books and a lot of free time

activities.why: a lot of works with both countable and uncountable.

alts: few students understood the lecture

steps.npc: How much money do we have left?

options.t: Not much, but we still have a few days.
Not many, but we still have a little days.
There are a few near the station.
There are a little near the station.

activities.why: Use many/(a) few with countable plurals, much/(a) little with uncountables, and a lot of with both; a few/a little mean 'some', while few/little (no 'a') mean 'almost none'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
all, most, some, any, none, no talk about quantity out of a whole. The tricky part is when to add of.

most people (in general) vs most of the people (a specific group).

learn.title: With or without 'of'
learn.html:
+ noun (general)most children, some water, all cars
+ of + the/this/my…most of the children, some of my friends
+ of + pronounall of us, none of them
no vs noneno money (+noun) · I have none (alone)

learn.title: none & agreement; all of / all
learn.html:
no goes before a noun; none stands alone: How much is left? None.

none of + plural can take a singular or plural verb: None of them is/are ready.

Before us/you/them, 'of' is required: all of us (not all us). Before 'the/my' + noun, 'of' is optional with all/both/half: all (of) the class.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Did everyone pass?  — Most of them did, but none of the exchange students came.

— Any milk?  — None left, sorry. There's no bread either.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Most of children like sweets.  ✓ Most children (general) / most of the children.
✗
all us  ✓ all of us.
✗
I have no.  ✓ I have none.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• + noun for general (most people); + of the/my/this for a specific group.
  • Before us/you/them → always 'of' (all of us, none of them).
  • no + noun; none alone. None of + plural → is/are.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: all, most, some, any, none, no — количество из целого. Сложность — когда добавлять of: most people (в целом) vs most of the people (конкретная группа).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: all, most, some, any, none, no — бүтіннен мөлшер. Қиыны — of қашан қосылады: most people (жалпы) vs most of the people (нақты топ).

ru.title: С ‘of’ или без
ru.html: + существительное (в целом): most children, some water. + of + the/this/my: most of the children. + of + местоимение: all of us, none of them. no vs none: no money / I have none.

kk.title: ‘of’ бар/жоқ
kk.html: + зат есім (жалпы): most children, some water. + of + the/this/my: most of the children. + of + есімдік: all of us, none of them. no vs none: no money / I have none.

ru.title: none & согласование; all of / all
ru.html: no — перед существительным; none — самостоятельно: None. none of + множ. — is/are. Перед us/you/them 'of' обязателен: all of us. Перед the/my с all/both/half 'of' необязателен: all (of) the class.

kk.title: none & үйлесім; all of / all
kk.html: no — зат есім алдында; none — жеке тұрады: None. none of + көпше — is/are. us/you/them алдында 'of' міндетті: all of us. the/my + зат алдында all/both/half-пен 'of' міндетті емес: all (of) the class.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Most of them passed, but none of the exchange students came. — Any milk? — None left. There's no bread either.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Most of them passed, but none of the exchange students came. — Any milk? — None left. There's no bread either.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Most of children → Most children / most of the children. all us → all of us. I have no. → I have none.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Most of children → Most children / most of the children. all us → all of us. I have no. → I have none.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: + существительное — общее; + of the/my/this — конкретная группа. Перед us/you/them — всегда 'of'. no + сущ.; none — отдельно. None of + множ. → is/are.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: + зат есім — жалпы; + of the/my/this — нақты топ. us/you/them алдында — әрдайым 'of'. no + зат; none — жеке. None of + көпше → is/are.

activities.q: "___ people enjoy music."

activities.why: 'most of' needs 'the': most of the children (or 'most children').

activities.after: of the students passed the test. (Most)
alts: most
none

activities.why: of + the/my marks a specific set.

activities.q: "There's ___ sugar left, so I'll buy some."
why: 'none' stands alone, not before a noun.
'not' can't be used this way here.

activities.q: "___ them knew the answer."
why: Correct — before a pronoun → None of them.

activities.prompt: Most students work hard.
activities.answer: most of the students work hard
alts: most of this class work hard
most of the students in this class work hard
activities.why: specific group → most of the students.

activities.prompt: Not one of the shops was open.
activities.answer: none of the shops was open
alts: none of the shops were open

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: after a group trip

options.t: Most of us loved it, though none of the kids liked the museum.
Most us loved it, though none the kids liked it.
No serious ones — most people were happy.
None serious ones — most of people were happy.

activities.why: Use all/most/some/none + noun for general statements and + of the/my/this (or a pronoun) for a specific group; 'no' goes before a noun, 'none' stands alone, and before us/you/them 'of' is always needed.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
These words talk about two things. both = the two together; either = one or the other; neither = not one and not the other.

Both restaurants are good. · Either day is fine. · Neither answer is right.

learn.title: Forms and verbs
learn.html:
both + pluralBoth hotels are full.
either / neither + singularEither bus goes there. Neither one works.
+ of + the/us…both of us · either of them · neither of the plans
paired: both…and / either…or / neither…norboth tea and coffee · either now or later · neither he nor she

learn.title: neither = negative; agreement
learn.html:
neither already means 'not', so the verb is positive: Neither answer is correct (not isn't).

neither/either of + plural takes a singular verb in careful English: Neither of them is here (informally 'are' is common).

With neither…nor / either…or, the verb agrees with the nearer subject.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Tea or coffee?  — Either is fine, thanks.  — And cake?  — Neither, I'm full!

— Which twin is it?  — Both of them came, actually.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Neither answer isn't right.  ✓ Neither answer is right.
✗
both the two books  ✓ both books / both of the books.
✗
either of them are … (careful English)  ✓ either of them is …

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• both + plural; either/neither + singular noun & verb.
  • + of + the/us/them: both of us, either of them.
  • Pairs: both…and, either…or, neither…nor (verb agrees with the nearer subject).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Эти слова о двух: both = оба вместе; either = один или другой; neither = ни тот, ни другой. Both are good. Either is fine. Neither is right.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бұл сөздер екеу туралы: both = екеуі бірге; either = бірі не екіншісі; neither = екеуі де емес. Both are good. Either is fine. Neither is right.

ru.title: Формы и глагол
ru.html: both + множ.: Both hotels are full. either/neither + ед.: Either bus goes. + of + the/us: both of us. Пары: both…and / either…or / neither…nor.

kk.title: Формалар мен етістік
kk.html: both + көпше: Both hotels are full. either/neither + жекеше: Either bus goes. + of + the/us: both of us. Жұп: both…and / either…or / neither…nor.

ru.title: neither = отрицание; согласование
ru.html: neither уже значит 'not', поэтому глагол положительный: Neither answer is correct (не isn't). neither/either of + множ. → ед. глагол (формально). neither…nor/either…or — глагол с ближайшим субъектом.

kk.title: neither = теріс; үйлесім
kk.html: neither 'not' дегенді береді, сондықтан етістік оң: Neither answer is correct (isn't емес). neither/either of + көпше → жекеше етістік (ресми). neither…nor/either…or — етістік жақын субъектпен үйлеседі.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Tea or coffee? — Either is fine. — And cake? — Neither, I'm full! — Which twin? — Both of them came.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Tea or coffee? — Either is fine. — And cake? — Neither, I'm full! — Which twin? — Both of them came.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Neither answer isn't right. → Neither answer is right. both the two books → both books. either of them are → either of them is.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Neither answer isn't right. → Neither answer is right. both the two books → both books. either of them are → either of them is.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: both + множ.; either/neither + ед. + of + the/us/them. Пары: both…and, either…or, neither…nor (с ближайшим субъектом).

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: both + көпше; either/neither + жекеше. + of + the/us/them. Жұп: both…and, either…or, neither…nor (жақын субъектпен үйлеседі).

activities.q: "___ of my parents are doctors."

activities.why: neither is already negative → positive verb.

activities.after: restaurant is expensive, so let's find a cheaper one. (not this one, not that one → singular)
alts: neither
activities.why: not one and not the other → Neither + singular.

activities.after: of us can drive, so we'll take the train. (Not one → negative)
alts: neither

activities.answer: You can have either tea or coffee

activities.q: "Neither the manager nor the staff ___ aware of the change."
why: the verb agrees with the nearer subject.

activities.q: "'Do you want the red or the blue?' '___ — I'll take both!'"
why: Correct — Either = I don't mind which; then chooses both.
'both' wouldn't answer 'or' naturally as a single word here.

activities.why: neither is already negative → “neither … were good” (positive verb).

activities.prompt: She speaks French. She also speaks German.
activities.answer: she speaks both french and german
alts: she speaks both french and german.

activities.prompt: He didn't call. He didn't write.
alts: he neither called nor wrote.
activities.why: neither…nor links two negatives (positive verb forms).

steps.npc: The Grand or the Plaza for the meeting?

options.t: Either is fine — both have good rooms.
Either are fine — both has good rooms.
options.why: either + singular verb; both + plural verb.

options.t: Neither of us is free on Monday, unfortunately.
options.why: neither of us + singular positive verb.

options.t: Neither of us aren't free on Monday.
options.why: neither is already negative → positive verb.

activities.why: both + plural; either/neither + singular; add 'of' before the/us/them; and remember neither is already negative, so the verb stays positive.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
every/each take a singular noun and verb; all takes a plural or uncountable; whole means 'complete' (the whole = all of).

Every student has a book. · All students have books. · the whole class.

learn.title: The forms
learn.html:
every / each + singularEach room has a TV.
all + plural / uncountableAll rooms have a TV. · all day
the whole + singularthe whole story · the whole day
every + time wordsevery day / every two weeks

learn.title: each vs every; all vs whole
learn.html:
every = all together (3+ things, a general view). each = one by one (2+, seen separately): Each answer is marked separately.
