whole goes with singular countable nouns: the whole book (= all the book). With uncountables/plurals use all: all the money (not the whole money).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How often do you train?  — Every day, and I stretch each muscle carefully.

— Tired?  — I worked the whole weekend — all the reports are done.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Every students have a laptop.  ✓ Every student has a laptop.
✗
all the day  ✓ all day / the whole day.
✗
the whole people  ✓ all the people.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• every/each + singular noun & verb; all + plural/uncountable.
  • each = one by one; every = all together / general.
  • the whole + singular countable (the whole day); all + uncountable/plural.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: every/each — ед. существительное/глагол; all — множ./неисч.; whole = 'целый'. Every student has. All students have. the whole class.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: every/each — жекеше зат есім/етістік; all — көпше/саналмайтын; whole = 'тұтас'. Every student has. All students have. the whole class.

ru.title: Формы
ru.html: every/each + ед.: Each room has. all + множ./неисч.: All rooms have; all day. the whole + ед.: the whole story. every + время: every day / every two weeks.

kk.title: Формалары
kk.html: every/each + жекеше: Each room has. all + көпше/саналмайтын: All rooms have; all day. the whole + жекеше: the whole story. every + уақыт: every day / every two weeks.

ru.title: each vs every; all vs whole
ru.html: every = все вместе (3+, общий взгляд). each = по одному (2+, отдельно). whole — с ед. исчисляемым: the whole book. Неисч./множ. — all: all the money.

kk.title: each vs every; all vs whole
kk.html: every = бәрі бірге (3+, жалпы). each = бір-бірлеп (2+, бөлек). whole — жекеше саналатынмен: the whole book. Саналмайтын/көпше — all: all the money.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How often? — Every day, and I stretch each muscle. — Tired? — I worked the whole weekend — all the reports are done.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How often? — Every day, and I stretch each muscle. — Tired? — I worked the whole weekend — all the reports are done.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Every students have → Every student has. all the day → all day / the whole day. the whole people → all the people.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Every students have → Every student has. all the day → all day / the whole day. the whole people → all the people.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: every/each + ед.; all + множ./неисч. each = по одному; every = все вместе. the whole + ед. исчисляемое; all + неисч./множ.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: every/each + жекеше; all + көпше/саналмайтын. each = бір-бірлеп; every = бәрі бірге. the whole + жекеше саналатын; all + саналмайтын/көпше.

activities.q: "Every ___ in the team has a role."

activities.after: of the two players received a medal. (one by one, of two)
alts: each
activities.why: one by one, two → Each of the two.

activities.q: "The teacher gave ___ child a different task."
why: 'every' views them together — less precise for 'a different task'.
Correct — one by one, individually → each child.

activities.q: "He spent ___ his money on games."

activities.prompt: All the town was celebrating.
alts: the whole town was celebrating.

activities.prompt: All the students must wear a uniform.
activities.answer: every student must wear a uniform
alts: every student must wear a uniform.

options.t: Intense — I have meetings every day.
Intense — I have meetings every days.
I slept the whole weekend to recover.
I slept the whole weekends to recover.

activities.why: every/each take a singular noun and verb (each = individually, every = all together); all takes a plural or uncountable; the whole + singular countable equals 'all the'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Compounds with some-, any-, no-, every- + -one/-body, -thing, -where follow the same logic as some/any: some in positives, any in negatives & questions.

Someone called. · I didn't see anyone. · There's nothing here.

learn.title: The system
learn.html:
peoplethingsplaces
some-someone/somebodysomethingsomewhere
any-anyone/anybodyanythinganywhere
no-no one/nobodynothingnowhere
every-everyone/everybodyeverythingeverywhere

learn.title: Singular verb; no double negative
learn.html:
These words take a singular verb: Everyone is here. · Nothing works.

no- words are already negative — don't add another 'not': I saw nobody = I didn't see anybody (not didn't see nobody).

They're often followed by 'else' (someone else) and by adjectives after (something nice).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Is anybody home?  — No, there's nobody here.  — I need somewhere to sleep.

— Would you like something to eat?  — Anything, I'm starving!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I didn't see nobody.  ✓ I didn't see anybody / I saw nobody.
✗
Everyone are happy.  ✓ Everyone is happy.
✗
I want nothing else? (question)  ✓ Do you want anything else?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• some- in positives, any- in negatives/questions; no- = negative (positive verb).
  • All take a singular verb (everyone is, nothing works).
  • Avoid double negatives; adjectives go after (something new); add 'else' for 'in addition'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: some-, any-, no-, every- + -one/-body, -thing, -where по логике some/any: some — утверждения, any — отрицания/вопросы. Someone called. I didn't see anyone. There's nothing here.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: some-, any-, no-, every- + -one/-body, -thing, -where some/any логикасымен: some — болымды, any — болымсыз/сұрақ. Someone called. I didn't see anyone. There's nothing here.

ru.title: Система
ru.html: some-: someone/something/somewhere. any-: anyone/anything/anywhere. no-: no one/nothing/nowhere. every-: everyone/everything/everywhere.

kk.title: Жүйесі
kk.html: some-: someone/something/somewhere. any-: anyone/anything/anywhere. no-: no one/nothing/nowhere. every-: everyone/everything/everywhere.

ru.title: Ед. глагол; без двойного отрицания
ru.html: Ед. глагол: Everyone is. Nothing works. no- уже отрицательные — не добавляйте 'not': saw nobody = didn't see anybody. После — 'else' (someone else) и прилагательное (something nice).

kk.title: Жекеше етістік; қос теріс жоқ
kk.html: Жекеше етістік: Everyone is. Nothing works. no- сөздер теріс — 'not' қоспаңыз: saw nobody = didn't see anybody. Артынан 'else' (someone else) және сын есім (something nice).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Is anybody home? — There's nobody here. — I need somewhere to sleep. — Something to eat? — Anything!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Is anybody home? — There's nobody here. — I need somewhere to sleep. — Something to eat? — Anything!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: didn't see nobody → didn't see anybody / saw nobody. Everyone are happy → Everyone is. Вопрос: Do you want anything else?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: didn't see nobody → didn't see anybody / saw nobody. Everyone are happy → Everyone is. Сұрақ: Do you want anything else?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: some- утверждения, any- отрицания/вопросы; no- = отрицание (положит. глагол). Все ед. глагол. Без двойного отрицания; прилагательное после; 'else' — в дополнение.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: some- болымды, any- болымсыз/сұрақ; no- = теріс (оң етістік). Бәрі жекеше етістік. Қос теріс болмасын; сын есім артынан; 'else' — қосымша.

activities.q: "I didn't tell ___ about it."
why: 'didn't tell no one' is a double negative.

activities.why: avoid the double negative: didn't see anybody / saw nobody.

alts: no one

activities.after: for our holiday this year. (some + place + warm)

activities.why: some- in positives; any- in negatives/questions.

activities.q: "Everybody ___ ready to leave."
why: Correct — everybody takes a singular verb → is.
despite the plural meaning, the verb is singular.

activities.q: "I'm so hungry I'll eat ___."
why: Correct — positive 'anything' = it doesn't matter what.

activities.prompt: There isn't anything in the box.
alts: there is nothing in the box

activities.prompt: Someone phoned you. (ask about it)
alts: did anyone phone you
has anyone phoned me

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: looking for a lost item

steps.npc: Have you seen my keys anywhere?

options.t: No, I've looked everywhere but found nothing.
No, I've looked everywhere but didn't find nothing.
options.why: avoid double negative: didn't find anything / found nothing.

options.t: Yes, but nobody has seen them.
Yes, but nobody haven't seen them.
options.why: nobody is already negative → positive verb.

activities.why: Compounds with some-/any-/no-/every- follow the some/any rule, take a singular verb, avoid double negatives, and put adjectives after (something nice).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Use a reflexive pronoun (myself, yourself, himself…) when the subject and object are the same person.

She hurt herself. · I taught myself to cook. · Help yourself!

learn.title: The forms
learn.html:
I → myselfyou → yourself / yourselves
he → himselfshe → herself
it → itselfwe → ourselves
they → themselvesone → oneself

learn.title: by myself & each other
learn.html:
by myself/yourself… = alone / without help: I did it by myself. · She lives by herself.

each other / one another = a mutual action (not reflexive): They looked at each other (= she at him, he at her). Compare: They looked at themselves (in the mirror).

Some verbs don't take a reflexive in English: feel, relax, concentrate, meet — I feel better (not feel myself).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Did someone help you?  — No, I made it myself. I like doing things by myself.

— How do you two know each other?  — We met at university.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I feel myself tired.  ✓ I feel tired.
✗
They helped themselves each other.  ✓ They helped each other.
✗
She lives by she.  ✓ by herself.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Reflexive when subject = object: hurt myself, enjoy yourself.
  • by + myself = alone/without help.
  • each other = mutual (they + each other), ≠ themselves. Don't use reflexives with feel/relax/concentrate/meet.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Возвратное местоимение (myself, yourself, himself…) — когда субъект и объект одно лицо: She hurt herself. I taught myself. Help yourself!

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Өздік есімдік (myself, yourself, himself…) — субъект пен объект бір адам болғанда: She hurt herself. I taught myself. Help yourself!

ru.title: Формы
ru.html: I → myself, you → yourself/yourselves, he → himself, she → herself, it → itself, we → ourselves, they → themselves.

kk.title: Формалары
kk.html: I → myself, you → yourself/yourselves, he → himself, she → herself, it → itself, we → ourselves, they → themselves.

ru.title: by myself & each other
ru.html: by myself… = один/без помощи: did it by myself; lives by herself. each other/one another = друг друга: looked at each other (не возвратное). feel/relax/concentrate/meet — без возвратного: I feel better.

kk.title: by myself & each other
kk.html: by myself… = жалғыз/көмексіз: did it by myself; lives by herself. each other/one another = өзара: looked at each other (өздік емес). feel/relax/concentrate/meet — өздік алмайды: I feel better.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Did someone help you? — I made it myself. I like doing things by myself. — How do you know each other? — We met at university.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Did someone help you? — I made it myself. I like doing things by myself. — How do you know each other? — We met at university.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I feel myself tired. → I feel tired. helped themselves each other → helped each other. lives by she → by herself.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I feel myself tired. → I feel tired. helped themselves each other → helped each other. lives by she → by herself.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Субъект = объект → возвратное (hurt myself). by + myself = один. each other = друг друга, ≠ themselves. feel/relax/concentrate/meet — без возвратного.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Субъект = объект болса — өздік (hurt myself). by + myself = жалғыз. each other = өзара, ≠ themselves. feel/relax/concentrate/meet — өздіксіз.

activities.q: "Be careful with the knife — don't cut ___."

items.s: They enjoyed themselves at the party.

activities.why: 'feel' takes no reflexive: I feel happy.

alts: one another

activities.answer: She fixed the car by herself

activities.q: "The two boxers respect ___."
"Sit down and relax ___."
why: 'relax' doesn't take a reflexive in English.

activities.prompt: Ann helps Ben and Ben helps Ann.
activities.answer: ann and ben help each other
alts: they help each other

activities.prompt: He travelled alone, with no one helping him.
alts: he travelled by himself.

options.t: Great — I painted the whole room by myself.
Great — I painted the whole room by me.

steps.npc: And how are you and Sam getting on?

options.t: Well — we support each other a lot.
Well — we support ourselves each other.

activities.why: Use reflexive pronouns when subject and object are the same; 'by + reflexive' means alone/without help; 'each other' marks a mutual action; and feel/relax/concentrate/meet take no reflexive.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Three useful possessive patterns: a friend of mine (one of several), my own (belonging only to me), on my own (alone).

He's a colleague of mine. · I want my own room. · She travels on her own.

learn.title: The patterns
learn.html:
a/an/some + noun + of + mine/yours…a friend of mine · some books of hers
my/your… + own + nounmy own car · their own house
own = not shared, or done aloneI cook my own meals.
on my own / by myself = aloneliving on my own

learn.title: Details to watch
learn.html:
own cannot follow 'a/an': an own room ✗ → my own room. Add emphasis with 'very': my very own room.

of mine/yours/his/hers/ours/theirs — use the possessive pronoun form, not 'of me'.

on my own and by myself mean the same (alone).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Who's that?  — A cousin of mine.  — Do you share a flat?  — No, I've got my own place now.

— Do you go to the gym together?  — No, I prefer training on my own.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a friend of me  ✓ a friend of mine.
✗
I have an own car.  ✓ I have my own car.
✗
She lives on her own self.  ✓ on her own.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• a … of mine/yours = one of my/your … (double possessive).
  • my own + noun = belonging only to me (never 'an own').
  • on my own = by myself = alone / without help.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Три полезные модели: a friend of mine (один из), my own (только мой), on my own (один): a colleague of mine; my own room; on her own.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Үш пайдалы үлгі: a friend of mine (бірнешеудің бірі), my own (тек менікі), on my own (жалғыз): a colleague of mine; my own room; on her own.

ru.title: Модели
ru.html: a/an/some + noun + of + mine/yours: a friend of mine. my/your + own + noun: my own car. own = не общий/сам. on my own / by myself = один.

kk.title: Үлгілер
kk.html: a/an/some + noun + of + mine/yours: a friend of mine. my/your + own + noun: my own car. own = ортақ емес/өзі істеген. on my own / by myself = жалғыз.

ru.title: Обратите внимание
ru.html: own не идёт после 'a/an': an own room → my own room; с 'very': my very own. of mine/yours/hers… — притяжательное (не 'of me'). on my own = by myself.

kk.title: Назар аударыңыз
kk.html: own 'a/an'-нан кейін болмайды: an own room → my own room; 'very' арқылы: my very own. of mine/yours/hers… — тәуелдік есімдік ('of me' емес). on my own = by myself.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Who's that? — A cousin of mine. — Share a flat? — I've got my own place now. — I prefer training on my own.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Who's that? — A cousin of mine. — Share a flat? — I've got my own place now. — I prefer training on my own.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a friend of me → a friend of mine. an own car → my own car. on her own self → on her own.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a friend of me → a friend of mine. an own car → my own car. on her own self → on her own.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: a … of mine/yours = один из моих/твоих. my own + noun = только мой (не 'an own'). on my own = by myself = один.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: a … of mine/yours = менің/сенің …-ыңның бірі. my own + noun = тек менікі ('an own' емес). on my own = by myself = жалғыз.

activities.q: "He's an old friend ___."

items.s: A neighbour of ours moved away.

activities.before: I don't want to share — I want
activities.why: belonging only to me → my own room.

activities.answer: She did the project on her own

activities.q: "After years of renting, they finally bought ___ house."
why: Correct — belonging only to them → their own house.

activities.q: "'Did anyone help you?' 'No, I did it ___.'"

activities.prompt: One of my colleagues recommended it.
activities.answer: a colleague of mine recommended it
alts: a colleague of mine recommended it.
activities.why: one of my colleagues = a colleague of mine.

activities.prompt: He lives alone now.
activities.answer: he lives on his own now
alts: he lives on his own

activities.scene: Use of mine / my own / on my own.

options.t: Yes! I've got my own flat now.
options.why: belonging only to me → my own.

options.t: Yes! I've got an own flat now.
No, I live on my own — a friend of mine helped me move in.
options.why: on my own = alone; a friend of mine = double possessive.

options.t: No, I live on my own self — a friend of me helped.
options.why: on my own (no 'self'); a friend of mine.

activities.why: 'a … of mine/yours' names one of several; 'my own + noun' means belonging only to me (never 'an own'); 'on my own' = 'by myself' = alone or without help.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
there introduces that something exists (there is/are); it refers to a specific thing, or is an empty subject for time, weather, distance and situations.

There's a problem. · It's raining. · It's 8 o'clock.

learn.title: there vs it
learn.html:
there + be (existence)There is a café nearby. · There are two exits.
it (weather / time / distance)It's cold. · It's Monday. · It's 5 km to town.
it (a named thing)Where's my phone? — It's on the table.
it + adjective + to/thatIt's nice to see you. · It's true that…

learn.title: Agreement & tenses
learn.html:
there agrees with the following noun: There is a book / There are books. Other tenses: There was, There will be, There has been, There used to be.

Don't confuse with the 'place' word there (= in that place): The keys? They're over there.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Is there a bank near here?  — Yes, there's one on the corner.  — How far is it?  — It's two minutes away.

— What's the weather like?  — It's sunny.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It is a problem with the car.  ✓ There is a problem with the car.
✗
There is cold today.  ✓ It is cold today.
✗
There are a lot of traffic.  ✓ There is a lot of traffic.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• there + be = something exists (agrees with the noun: is/are).
  • it = weather, time, distance, situations, and named things.
  • it + adjective + to/that: It's important to…; It's clear that…

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: there вводит, что нечто существует (there is/are); it — конкретная вещь или пустое подлежащее для времени/погоды/расстояния/ситуации: There's a problem. It's raining. It's 8 o'clock.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: there — бір нәрсенің бар екенін енгізеді (there is/are); it — нақты затқа не уақыт/ауа райы/қашықтық/жағдай үшін бос бастауыш: There's a problem. It's raining. It's 8 o'clock.

ru.title: there vs it
ru.html: there + be (существование): There is/are. it (погода/время/расстояние): It's cold/Monday/5 km. it (названная вещь): It's on the table. it + adj + to/that: It's nice to…

kk.title: there vs it
kk.html: there + be (болмыс): There is/are. it (ауа райы/уақыт/қашықтық): It's cold/Monday/5 km. it (аталған зат): It's on the table. it + adj + to/that: It's nice to…

ru.title: Согласование и времена
ru.html: there согласуется со след. существительным: There is a book / There are books. Другие времена: There was/will be/has been/used to be. Не путайте с 'там': over there.

kk.title: Үйлесім мен шақтар
kk.html: there кейінгі зат есіммен үйлеседі: There is a book / There are books. Басқа шақтар: There was/will be/has been/used to be. 'Орын' there-мен шатастырмаңыз: over there.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Is there a bank near here? — There's one on the corner. — How far is it? — It's two minutes away. — It's sunny.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Is there a bank near here? — There's one on the corner. — How far is it? — It's two minutes away. — It's sunny.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: It is a problem with the car. → There is a problem. There is cold today. → It is cold. There are a lot of traffic. → There is a lot of traffic.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: It is a problem with the car. → There is a problem. There is cold today. → It is cold. There are a lot of traffic. → There is a lot of traffic.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: there + be = существование (is/are — с сущ.). it = погода, время, расстояние, ситуации, названная вещь. it + adj + to/that.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: there + be = бар болу (is/are — затпен). it = ауа райы, уақыт, қашықтық, жағдай, аталған зат. it + adj + to/that.

activities.q: "___ is a spider on the wall!"
why: 'it' introduces a known thing, not existence.
'That' doesn't fit as an existence subject.

items.s: There are three cinemas in town.
It is a mistake in your essay.

activities.after: 's a long way from here to London. (distance)
alts: it

activities.after: a lot of people waiting outside. (be)
alts: is
's

activities.why: there for existence; it for weather/time.

activities.q: "___ 's important to arrive on time."
why: Correct — it + adjective + to → It's important to…

activities.q: "When I was young, ___ a cinema in every town."
why: 'a cinema' is singular → was, not were.

activities.prompt: Many reasons explain the delay.
activities.answer: there are many reasons for the delay
alts: there are many reasons for the delay.

activities.prompt: Meeting you is a pleasure.
activities.answer: it's a pleasure to meet you
alts: it is a pleasure to meet you

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: asking about a town

steps.npc: Is there much to do in your town?

options.t: Yes, there's a theatre and it's quite lively at weekends.
Yes, it's a theatre and there's quite lively.
It's mild, though there are often clouds.
There's mild, though it are often clouds.

activities.why: Use there + be to say something exists (agreeing is/are with the noun) and it for weather, time, distance, situations, named things, and the it + adjective + to/that pattern.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
one/ones replace a countable noun to avoid repeating it. you/they can be 'people in general' (impersonal).

Which shirt? — The blue one. · These shoes are nice; I like the red ones.

learn.title: one / ones
learn.html:
one = a singular countableI need a pen. Have you got one?
the + adjective + one(s)the big one · the cheap ones
this/that/which + oneWhich one? · that one
NOT with uncountablesWant some coffee? — a one ✗ → some, please

learn.title: Impersonal you / they / it
learn.html:
you = people in general: You can't park here. (= one can't)

they = authorities / unnamed people: They say it'll rain. · They've built a new road.

Formal 'one' also = people in general: One should be polite. (quite formal)

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Which cup do you want?  — The big one, please. Those little ones are too small.

— Can you smoke here?  — No, you can't smoke indoors. They banned it.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Which shirt? The blue.  ✓ The blue one.
✗
I like the red ones shoes.  ✓ the red ones (no noun after).
✗
Do you want a coffee one?  ✓ (no 'one' with uncountables)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• one/ones replace a countable noun (the blue one, the small ones).
  • Don't use one/ones with uncountable nouns.
  • Impersonal you = people in general; they = authorities/unnamed people.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: one/ones заменяют исчисляемое существительное, чтобы не повторять. you/they — 'люди вообще' (безличное). The blue one; the red ones.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: one/ones — саналатын зат есімді қайталамау үшін. you/they — 'жалпы адамдар' (жалпылама). The blue one; the red ones.

ru.title: one / ones
ru.html: one = ед. исчисляемое: Have you got one? the + adj + one(s): the big one. this/that/which + one. С неисч. НЕ используется: some coffee (не a one).

kk.title: one / ones
kk.html: one = жекеше саналатын: Have you got one? the + adj + one(s): the big one. this/that/which + one. Саналмайтынмен ҚОЛДАНЫЛМАЙДЫ: some coffee (a one емес).

ru.title: Безличные you / they / it
ru.html: you = люди вообще: You can't park here. they = власти/неназванные: They say it'll rain. Формальное one = человек вообще: One should be polite.

kk.title: Жалпылама you / they / it
kk.html: you = жалпы адамдар: You can't park here. they = билік/белгісіз адамдар: They say it'll rain. Ресми one = жалпы адам: One should be polite.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Which cup? — The big one. Those little ones are too small. — Smoke here? — No, you can't. They banned it.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Which cup? — The big one. Those little ones are too small. — Smoke here? — No, you can't. They banned it.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: The blue. → The blue one. the red ones shoes → the red ones. С неисч. нет 'one'.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: The blue. → The blue one. the red ones shoes → the red ones. Саналмайтынмен 'one' жоқ.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: one/ones заменяют исчисляемое. С неисч. не используются. Безличное you = люди вообще; they = власти/неназванные.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: one/ones — саналатын зат есімді алмастырады. Саналмайтынмен қолданылмайды. Жалпылама you = жалпы адамдар; they = билік/белгісіз.

activities.q: "I don't like these cups. Show me those ___."
why: 'some' doesn't replace a countable noun like this.

items.s: Want some tea? A one, please.

activities.before: My shoes are old. I need some new

activities.after: drive on the left. (people in general)
alts: they

activities.q: "___ are building a new bridge over the river."
why: 'you' = people in general, not the builders.

activities.q: "'Which laptop is yours?' 'The ___ next to the window.'"

activities.why: 'one/ones' replaces the noun → “the big one” (and cake is singular → one).

activities.prompt: I've got a black bag and a brown bag.
activities.answer: i've got a black bag and a brown one
alts: i have got a black bag and a brown one

activities.prompt: People shouldn't judge others too quickly.
activities.answer: you shouldn't judge others too quickly
alts: you shouldn't judge people too quickly

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: shopping for a phone

steps.npc: Which phone are you thinking of?

options.t: The new one — the smaller ones feel too fiddly.
The new — the smaller feel too fiddly.
options.why: use one/ones after adjectives: the new one, the smaller ones.

options.t: Yes, they say prices go up next month.
Yes, it says prices go up next month.

activities.why: one/ones replace a countable noun (not uncountables); impersonal you means people in general, and they refers to authorities or unnamed people.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
another = one more / a different one (singular). other = different ones (+ noun). the other(s) = the remaining one(s).

another cup · other people · the other shoe · the others.

learn.title: The forms
learn.html:
another + singular nounHave another biscuit. (one more)
other + plural / uncountableother countries · other information
the other + singular (of two)one hand … the other hand
others / the others (pronoun)Some stayed; others left. · Where are the others?

learn.title: Key contrasts
learn.html:
another is one word and singular: an other cup ✗ → another cup. Before a number/'few' it can be plural in idea: another two days.

other before a noun has no -s (other people); as a pronoun it becomes others (no noun). the other(s) = the specific rest.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Would you like another coffee?  — Yes, and do you have any other cakes?

— Where are your friends?  — One's here; the others are outside.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
an other question  ✓ another question.
✗
other peoples think…  ✓ other people think… / others think…
✗
Where are the other?  ✓ Where are the others?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• another (one word, singular) = one more / a different one.
  • other + plural/uncountable noun; as a pronoun → others.
  • the other = the second of two; the others = the rest.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: another = ещё один / другой (ед.). other = другие (+ сущ.). the other(s) = остальные: another cup; other people; the other shoe; the others.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: another = тағы бір / басқа біреуі (жекеше). other = басқалары (+ зат есім). the other(s) = қалғаны: another cup; other people; the other shoe; the others.

ru.title: Формы
ru.html: another + ед.: another biscuit. other + множ./неисч.: other countries/information. the other + ед. (из двух): the other hand. others/the others (местоим.): others left; the others.

kk.title: Формалары
kk.html: another + жекеше: another biscuit. other + көпше/саналмайтын: other countries/information. the other + жекеше (екеудің): the other hand. others/the others (есімдік): others left; the others.

ru.title: Ключевые различия
ru.html: another — одно слово, ед.: an other cup → another cup; перед числом/'few': another two days. other перед сущ. без -s; как местоимение — others. the other(s) = конкретные остальные.

kk.title: Негізгі айырма
kk.html: another — бір сөз, жекеше: an other cup → another cup; сан/'few' алдында: another two days. other зат есім алдында -s жоқ; есімдік ретінде others. the other(s) = нақты қалғаны.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Another coffee? — Yes, and any other cakes? — Where are your friends? — One's here; the others are outside.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Another coffee? — Yes, and any other cakes? — Where are your friends? — One's here; the others are outside.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: an other question → another question. other peoples → other people / others. the other? → the others?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: an other question → another question. other peoples → other people / others. the other? → the others?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: another (одно слово, ед.) = ещё один/другой. other + множ./неисч.; местоим. → others. the other = второй из двух; the others = остальные.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: another (бір сөз, жекеше) = тағы бір/басқа. other + көпше/саналмайтын; есімдік → others. the other = екеудің екіншісі; the others = қалғаны.

activities.q: "This coffee's cold. Can I have ___?"

items.s: Some people like it; others don't.

activities.q: "We've seen three flats. Let's look at ___ before deciding."
"The project will take ___ two weeks."

activities.prompt: One twin is tall; ___ is short.
alts: the other twin
the other one
activities.why: the second of two → the other.

activities.prompt: Some people prefer tea; other people prefer coffee.
activities.answer: some people prefer tea; others prefer coffee
alts: some prefer tea; others prefer coffee

options.t: Yes, another bottle of water and some other snacks, please.
Yes, an other bottle and some others snacks.
options.why: another (one word); other + plural noun (no -s on 'other').

options.t: Two of us are; the others are parking the car.
Two of us are; the other are parking.

activities.why: another (one word, singular) = one more/a different one; other + plural/uncountable noun; others is the pronoun; the other(s) means the remaining one(s).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A relative clause gives essential information about a noun. Use who for people, which for things, that for either.

The woman who lives next door · a book which changed my life · the key that opens the door.

learn.title: who / which / that
learn.html:
people → who / thatthe man who/that called
things → which / thatthe car which/that broke down
as subject: keep the pronounthe girl who won (who = subject)
as object: can omitthe film (that) I saw

learn.title: Don't repeat the subject/object
learn.html:
The relative pronoun replaces the noun — don't add another pronoun:

the man who he called ✗ → the man who called. · the film that I saw it ✗ → the film (that) I saw.

When the relative pronoun is the object, you can leave it out: the book I read (= the book that I read).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Who's Emma?  — She's the friend who helped me move.  — And this?  — The phone (that) I bought yesterday.

— Do you know anyone who speaks French?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the people which live here  ✓ the people who live here.
✗
the car who I drive  ✓ the car which/that I drive.
✗
the man who he lives here  ✓ the man who lives here.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• who = people, which = things, that = both (defining clauses).
  • Never add an extra subject/object pronoun in the clause.
  • When the pronoun is the object, you can omit it (the book I read).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Относительное придаточное даёт важную информацию о существительном. Для людей — who, для вещей — which, для обоих — that: the woman who lives next door; a book which changed my life.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Қатыстық сөйлем зат есім туралы маңызды ақпарат береді. Адамға — who, затқа — which, екеуіне — that: the woman who lives next door; a book which changed my life.

ru.title: who / which / that
ru.html: Люди → who/that: the man who/that called. Вещи → which/that: the car which/that broke down. Как подлежащее — местоимение остаётся. Как дополнение — можно опустить.

kk.title: who / which / that
kk.html: Адам → who/that: the man who/that called. Зат → which/that: the car which/that broke down. Бастауыш ретінде — есімдік қалады. Толықтауыш ретінде — түсіруге болады.

ru.title: Не повторяйте подлежащее/дополнение
ru.html: Относительное местоимение заменяет существительное — не добавляйте ещё одно: the man who he called → the man who called. Если дополнение — можно опустить: the book I read.

kk.title: Бастауыш/толықтауышты қайталамаңыз
kk.html: Қатыстық есімдік зат есімді алмастырады — тағы есімдік қоспаңыз: the man who he called → the man who called. Толықтауыш болса, түсіруге болады: the book I read.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Who's Emma? — The friend who helped me move. — And this? — The phone (that) I bought. — Anyone who speaks French?

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Who's Emma? — The friend who helped me move. — And this? — The phone (that) I bought. — Anyone who speaks French?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the people which live → the people who live. the car who I drive → the car which/that. the man who he lives → the man who lives.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the people which live → the people who live. the car who I drive → the car which/that. the man who he lives → the man who lives.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: who = люди, which = вещи, that = оба. Не добавляйте лишнее местоимение. Дополнение можно опустить.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: who = адам, which = зат, that = екеуі. Артық есімдік қоспаңыз. Толықтауыш болса, түсіруге болады.

activities.q: "That's the man ___ fixed my car."

items.s: the man who he lives here

alts: that
that

activities.answer: The teacher who teaches us is kind

activities.q: "Which is fully correct AND natural (object relative)?"
why: Correct — object pronoun omitted → the film I watched.

activities.q: "I don't trust people ___ never say sorry."

activities.prompt: I met a woman. She works with my brother.
activities.answer: i met a woman who works with my brother
alts: i met a woman that works with my brother

activities.instruction: Join (omit the object pronoun if possible):
activities.prompt: This is the house. We bought it last year.
activities.answer: this is the house we bought last year
alts: this is the house that we bought last year
this is the house which we bought last year
activities.why: object relative → pronoun can be omitted.

options.t: The one who sits by the window.
The one which sits by the window.
The report I sent you this morning.
The report I sent you it this morning.

activities.why: Defining relative clauses use who (people), which (things) and that (both); never add an extra subject/object pronoun, and omit the pronoun when it's the object.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Beyond who/which, relative clauses also use whose (possession), where (place), when (time) and why (reason).

the man whose car was stolen · the town where I grew up · the day when we met.

learn.title: The forms
learn.html:
whose = possessivea writer whose books sell well
where = in/at a placethe hotel where we stayed
when = at a timethe year when I was born
why = the reasonthe reason why she left

learn.title: where vs which; dropping when/why
learn.html:
where = 'in/at which': the office where I work = the office which I work in. Use 'which' (not 'where') if there's no place-preposition idea: the office which I like.

when and why can often be dropped: the day (when) we met · the reason (why) I called.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Is that the café where you work?  — Yes, and that's my boss, whose daughter is in my class.

— Do you remember the day when we first met?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the man which car was stolen  ✓ the man whose car was stolen.
✗
the city where I visited  ✓ the city which/that I visited (no preposition idea) / the city where I lived.
✗
who's book (possession)  ✓ whose book.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• whose = possession (the boy whose bag…); where = place; when = time; why = reason.
  • where = in/at which; use which if there's no 'preposition + place' idea.
  • when/why can usually be dropped.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Кроме who/which: whose (принадлежность), where (место), when (время), why (причина): the man whose car; the town where I grew up; the day when we met.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: who/which-тен басқа: whose (иелік), where (орын), when (уақыт), why (себеп): the man whose car; the town where I grew up; the day when we met.

ru.title: Формы
ru.html: whose = принадлежность: a writer whose books sell well. where = место: the hotel where we stayed. when = время: the year when. why = причина: the reason why.

kk.title: Формалары
kk.html: whose = иелік: a writer whose books sell well. where = орын: the hotel where we stayed. when = уақыт: the year when. why = себеп: the reason why.

ru.title: where vs which; опущение when/why
ru.html: where = 'in/at which': the office where I work. Без идеи предлога → which: the office which I like. when, why часто опускаются: the day (when) we met.

kk.title: where vs which; when/why түсіру
kk.html: where = 'in/at which': the office where I work. Предлог идеясы жоқ болса which: the office which I like. when, why жиі түсіріледі: the day (when) we met.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Is that the café where you work? — Yes, and that's my boss, whose daughter is in my class. — Remember the day when we met?

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Is that the café where you work? — Yes, and that's my boss, whose daughter is in my class. — Remember the day when we met?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the man which car → the man whose car. the city where I visited → the city which/that I visited. who's book → whose book.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the man which car → the man whose car. the city where I visited → the city which/that I visited. who's book → whose book.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: whose = принадлежность; where = место; when = время; why = причина. where = in/at which; иначе which. when/why часто опускаются.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: whose = иелік; where = орын; when = уақыт; why = себеп. where = in/at which; идея жоқ болса which. when/why жиі түсіріледі.

activities.q: "That's the girl ___ brother plays for the team."
why: 'who' is the subject, not a possessive.

items.s: the house where I was born

activities.answer: I know a place where we can eat

activities.q: "The village ___ we visited was tiny."
why: 'where' = in/at which; but 'visited' takes no preposition.

activities.q: "Tell me the reason ___ you're upset."

activities.prompt: I have a friend. Her father is a pilot.
activities.answer: i have a friend whose father is a pilot
alts: i have a friend whose father is a pilot.

activities.prompt: That's the school. I studied there.
activities.answer: that's the school where i studied
alts: that is the school where i studied

options.t: That's the neighbour whose garden we loved.
That's the neighbour which garden we loved.
The beach where we spent every summer.
The beach when we spent every summer.

activities.why: Use whose for possession, where for place (= in/at which), when for time and why for reason; use which (not where) when there's no preposition-of-place idea, and drop when/why when natural.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A non-defining relative clause adds extra, non-essential information, set off by commas. Remove it and the sentence still makes sense.

My brother, who lives in Rome, is a chef. (I have one brother; the clause is extra.)

learn.title: Defining vs non-defining
learn.html:
defining (no commas)The man who called was angry. (which man)
non-defining (commas)Mr Lee, who called earlier, is here. (extra info)
no 'that' in non-definingParis, which I love, is busy. (not 'that')
can't omit the pronounMy car, which I bought last year, … (keep 'which')

learn.title: which can refer to a whole clause
learn.html:
In non-defining clauses, which can refer to the whole previous idea:

She passed her exam, which made everyone happy. (which = the fact that she passed).

Use commas here too; never use 'what' for this: …, what made everyone happy ✗.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How's the new job?  — Great — my manager, who's really supportive, gave me a project.

— We missed the train, which was annoying.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
My mother that is a nurse…  ✓ My mother, who is a nurse, …
✗
Tokyo which is huge is exciting (no commas).  ✓ Tokyo, which is huge, is exciting.
✗
…, what surprised me.  ✓ …, which surprised me.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Non-defining = extra info, marked by commas; the sentence works without it.
  • Use who/which/whose/where — never 'that', and never omit the pronoun.
  • which can refer to a whole clause (…, which was great).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Неопределяющее придаточное добавляет дополнительную, необязательную информацию, выделяется запятыми. Уберёшь — смысл сохранится: My brother, who lives in Rome, is a chef.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Айқындамайтын қатыстық сөйлем қосымша, міндетті емес ақпарат береді, үтірмен бөлінеді. Алып тастасаң, сөйлем мәнін жоймайды: My brother, who lives in Rome, is a chef.

ru.title: Определяющее vs неопределяющее
ru.html: Определяющее (без запятых): The man who called was angry. Неопределяющее (с запятыми): Mr Lee, who called earlier, is here. Здесь нет 'that'; местоимение опускать нельзя.

kk.title: Айқындайтын vs айқындамайтын
kk.html: Айқындайтын (үтірсіз): The man who called was angry. Айқындамайтын (үтірмен): Mr Lee, who called earlier, is here. Мұнда 'that' жоқ; есімдікті түсіруге болмайды.

ru.title: which указывает на всё предложение
ru.html: В неопределяющем which может указывать на всю предыдущую мысль: She passed her exam, which made everyone happy. Здесь тоже запятая; никогда 'what': …, what made… ✗.

kk.title: which бүкіл сөйлемге сілтейді
kk.html: Айқындамайтында which бүкіл алдыңғы ойға сілтей алады: She passed her exam, which made everyone happy. Мұнда да үтір; 'what' ешқашан: …, what made… ✗.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How's the job? — My manager, who's really supportive, gave me a project. — We missed the train, which was annoying.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How's the job? — My manager, who's really supportive, gave me a project. — We missed the train, which was annoying.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: My mother that is a nurse → My mother, who is a nurse, … Tokyo which is huge is exciting → Tokyo, which is huge, … , what surprised me → which.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: My mother that is a nurse → My mother, who is a nurse, … Tokyo which is huge is exciting → Tokyo, which is huge, … , what surprised me → which.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Неопределяющее = доп. информация, запятые. who/which/whose/where — никогда 'that', местоимение не опускаем. which может указывать на всю мысль.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Айқындамайтын = қосымша ақпарат, үтірмен. who/which/whose/where — 'that' ешқашан, есімдікті түсірмейміз. which бүкіл ойға сілтей алады.

activities.q: "My sister, ___ is a doctor, lives in Berlin."

items.s: Rome, which is ancient, is beautiful.
He passed, which pleased his parents.
My dad, that is retired, travels a lot.
The book which I read was long. (defining, no commas)

activities.after: is from Canada, is very kind. (person)
annoyed everyone. (refers to the whole fact)

items.t: The man who phoned was rude
Mr Ford, who phoned, was rude

activities.q: "The Amazon, ___ flows through Brazil, is huge."
"She didn't call, ___ worried me."
why: Correct — refers to the whole idea → which.

activities.prompt: Anna is coming to dinner. She's a vegetarian.
activities.answer: anna, who is a vegetarian, is coming to dinner
alts: anna, who's a vegetarian, is coming to dinner
activities.why: extra info → , who is a vegetarian,

activities.prompt: The flight was delayed. This ruined our plans.
activities.answer: the flight was delayed, which ruined our plans
alts: the flight was delayed, which ruined our plans.
activities.why: which refers to the whole event.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: catching up on news

steps.npc: Did you go to Tom's wedding?

options.t: Yes — his wife, who I'd never met, is lovely.
Yes — his wife who I'd never met is lovely.
The train broke down, which made us two hours late.
The train broke down, what made us late.
options.why: use 'which' for a whole clause, not 'what'.

activities.why: Non-defining clauses add extra information between commas, use who/which/whose/where (never 'that'), keep the pronoun, and 'which' can refer to a whole clause.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A relative clause can contain a preposition. In everyday English the preposition usually goes at the end; in formal English it goes before whom/which.

the man I spoke to (informal) = the man to whom I spoke (formal).

learn.title: Preposition position
learn.html:
informal: preposition at the endthe house (that) I live in
formal: preposition + whom/whichthe house in which I live
people → whom (formal object)the person to whom I wrote
'that' can't follow a prepositionin that ✗ → in which

learn.title: quantifier + of whom / of which
learn.html:
In formal/non-defining clauses: … , some of whom / many of which / all of which …

I have three sisters, two of whom are teachers. · He gave me some books, most of which I've read.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Who was that?  — The colleague I told you about. (informal, preposition at the end)

— This is the tool (that) I opened it with — I mean, with which I opened it.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the man to who I spoke  ✓ to whom I spoke.
✗
the house in that I live  ✓ in which I live / the house I live in.
✗
the tool with which I opened it with  ✓ with which I opened it. (one preposition)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Everyday: preposition at the end (the film I told you about).
  • Formal: preposition + whom/which (the film about which…).
  • 'that' can't follow a preposition; use one preposition, not two.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: В относительном придаточном может быть предлог. В разговорном он обычно в конце; в формальном — перед whom/which: the man I spoke to = the man to whom I spoke.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Қатыстық сөйлемде предлог болуы мүмкін. Күнделікті тілде предлог соңында; ресми тілде whom/which алдында: the man I spoke to = the man to whom I spoke.

ru.title: Позиция предлога
ru.html: Неформально: предлог в конце: the house (that) I live in. Формально: preposition + whom/which: the house in which I live. Люди → whom (формально). 'that' после предлога нельзя.

kk.title: Предлог орны
kk.html: Бейресми: предлог соңында: the house (that) I live in. Ресми: preposition + whom/which: the house in which I live. Адам → whom (ресми). 'that' предлогтан кейін болмайды.

ru.title: quantifier + of whom / of which
ru.html: В формальном/неопределяющем: some of whom / many of which / all of which: I have three sisters, two of whom are teachers. some books, most of which I've read.

kk.title: quantifier + of whom / of which
kk.html: Ресми/айқындамайтында: some of whom / many of which / all of which: I have three sisters, two of whom are teachers. some books, most of which I've read.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Who was that? — The colleague I told you about. — This is the tool I opened it with — with which I opened it.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Who was that? — The colleague I told you about. — This is the tool I opened it with — with which I opened it.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: to who I spoke → to whom. in that I live → in which / I live in. with which I opened it with → только один предлог.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: to who I spoke → to whom. in that I live → in which / I live in. with which I opened it with → бір предлог қана.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Разговорно: предлог в конце. Формально: preposition + whom/which. 'that' после предлога нельзя; предлог один.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Күнделікті: предлог соңында. Ресми: preposition + whom/which. 'that' предлогтан кейін болмайды; предлог біреу ғана.

activities.q: "That's the person ___."
why: correct but formal; the question asks for informal.
Correct — everyday English → the person I spoke to.

items.s: the man to whom I wrote
the house in that I live

activities.why: work for → the company for which I work.

activities.answer: This is the song I told you about

activities.q: "'The topic we talked about' becomes (formal): the topic ___ we talked."
"He made several promises, none ___ he kept."

activities.why: 'that' can't follow a preposition → “in which I slept” (or 'the bed I slept in').

activities.prompt: The pen with which I wrote is gone.
activities.answer: the pen i wrote with is gone
alts: the pen that i wrote with is gone
the pen which i wrote with is gone

activities.prompt: I have twenty students. Five of them failed.
activities.answer: i have twenty students, five of whom failed
alts: i have twenty students, five of whom failed.

options.t: The one I told you about last week.
options.why: casual chat → preposition at the end.

options.t: The one about whom I told you last week.
options.why: too formal for casual chat; the end-preposition form fits.

steps.npc: (writing a report) How many clients responded?

options.t: We contacted 50 clients, most of whom replied.
We contacted 50 clients, most of who replied.

activities.why: In everyday English put the preposition at the end; in formal English use preposition + whom/which (never 'that' after a preposition), and use quantifier + of whom/which in formal or non-defining clauses.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A participle clause can shorten a relative clause. -ing = active (the thing does it); -ed/past participle = passive (it's done to the thing).

the man sitting over there (= who is sitting) · the money stolen (= that was stolen).

learn.title: -ing vs -ed
learn.html:
-ing = active meaningthe boy playing football (= who is playing)
-ed/-en = passive meaningthe window broken in the storm (= that was broken)
replaces who/which + bepeople waiting outside (= who are waiting)
works in past toothe girl injured in the crash (= who was injured)

learn.title: When you can reduce
learn.html:
You can reduce a relative clause when the pronoun is the subject and the verb includes be (progressive or passive):

the train which is arriving → the train arriving. · the letters which were sent → the letters sent.

You can't reduce a simple-tense clause easily: the man who owns the shop (keep it).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Which one is your brother?  — The tall guy standing by the door.

— Did they recover the paintings stolen last year?  — Some of them.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the man standed there  ✓ the man standing there (active).
✗
the goods delivering yesterday (passive meaning)  ✓ the goods delivered yesterday.
✗
people who waiting  ✓ people waiting / people who are waiting.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• -ing clause = active (the child crying = who is crying).
  • -ed/past participle clause = passive (the car damaged = that was damaged).
  • Reduces subject relative clauses containing 'be'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Причастный оборот сокращает относительное придаточное. -ing = актив (предмет делает); -ed/причастие прош. = пассив (с предметом делают): the man sitting; the money stolen.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Есімше сөйлемі қатыстық сөйлемді қысқартады. -ing = актив (зат өзі істейді); -ed/өткен есімше = пассив (затқа істеледі): the man sitting; the money stolen.

ru.title: -ing vs -ed
ru.html: -ing = актив: the boy playing (= who is playing). -ed/-en = пассив: the window broken (= that was broken). Вместо who/which + be. И в прошлом: the girl injured.

kk.title: -ing vs -ed
kk.html: -ing = актив: the boy playing (= who is playing). -ed/-en = пассив: the window broken (= that was broken). who/which + be орнына. Өткенде де: the girl injured.

ru.title: Когда можно сократить
ru.html: Если местоимение — подлежащее и в глаголе есть be (продолж./пассив): the train which is arriving → arriving; the letters which were sent → sent. Простое время так легко не сокращается.

kk.title: Қашан қысқартуға болады
kk.html: Есімдік бастауыш әрі етістікте be болса (созылыңқы/пассив): the train which is arriving → arriving; the letters which were sent → sent. Жай шақты оңай қысқартпаймыз.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Which one is your brother? — The tall guy standing by the door. — The paintings stolen last year? — Some of them.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Which one is your brother? — The tall guy standing by the door. — The paintings stolen last year? — Some of them.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the man standed there → standing (актив). Пассив: the goods delivered yesterday. people who waiting → people waiting / who are waiting.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the man standed there → standing (актив). Пассив мағына: the goods delivered yesterday. people who waiting → people waiting / who are waiting.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: -ing = актив; -ed/причастие = пассив. Сокращает подлежащные относительные с 'be'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: -ing = актив; -ed/өткен есімше = пассив. 'be' бар бастауыш қатыстық сөйлемді қысқартады.

activities.q: "Do you know the woman ___ to the manager? (she is talking)"
why: need a participle, not the base verb.

items.s: the man sat there (= who is sitting)

activities.q: "The passengers ___ on flight 202 should board now. (they are waiting)"
why: past simple doesn't reduce a relative like this.
'were waited' is wrong (passive of an intransitive verb).

activities.q: "The information ___ in the report is confidential. (it was contained)"

activities.why: passive meaning → “the boy injured in the match” (= who was injured).

activities.prompt: The man who is standing by the window is my uncle.
activities.answer: the man standing by the window is my uncle
alts: the man standing by the window is my uncle.

activities.prompt: The parcel which was sent last week has arrived.
activities.answer: the parcel sent last week has arrived
alts: the parcel sent last week has arrived.

steps.npc: Which one is the new manager?

options.t: The woman wearing the red jacket.
The woman worn the red jacket.
The one reported in the news yesterday.
The one reporting in the news yesterday.

activities.why: Participle clauses shorten relative clauses: -ing for active meaning (who is doing) and -ed/past participle for passive meaning (that was done), replacing subject relatives containing 'be'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
what = 'the thing(s) that' — it has no separate noun before it. After all, everything, the only, superlatives, use that (not what).

What she said was true. (= the thing that…) · All that matters is health.

learn.title: what vs that
learn.html:
what = the thing(s) that (no noun)What I need is time.
after all/everything → thateverything that happened; all (that) I know
after superlative/the only → thatthe best film (that) I've seen; the only thing that works
never 'what' after a nounthe money that I earned (not 'what')

learn.title: what for emphasis (cleft sentences)
learn.html:
What can start a sentence for emphasis: What I love is the quiet. · What happened was (that) the car broke down.

Remember: if there's already a noun to describe, use who/which/that — not what: the book that I read (not the book what I read).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Tell me what you want.  — What I want is a holiday! It's the only thing that keeps me going.

— Is that everything that happened?  — Yes, that's all (that) I remember.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the thing what I need  ✓ the thing that I need / what I need.
✗
everything what he said  ✓ everything that he said.
✗
the best that ever what happened  ✓ the best thing that ever happened.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• what = 'the thing that' (no preceding noun).
  • After all, everything, the only, superlatives → use that, not what.
  • Never use 'what' when a noun is already there (the book that…).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: what = 'то, что' — перед ним нет существительного. После all, everything, the only, superlative — that (не what): What she said was true. All that matters is health.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: what = 'the thing(s) that' — алдында зат есім жоқ. all, everything, the only, superlative-тен кейін that (what емес): What she said was true. All that matters is health.

ru.title: what vs that
ru.html: what = без существительного: What I need is time. после all/everything → that: everything that happened. после superlative/the only → that. После существительного никогда 'what'.

kk.title: what vs that
kk.html: what = зат есімсіз: What I need is time. all/everything соңында → that: everything that happened. superlative/the only соңында → that. Зат есімнен кейін ешқашан 'what'.

ru.title: what — для акцента (cleft)
ru.html: What может начинать предложение для акцента: What I love is the quiet. What happened was the car broke down. Если есть существительное — who/which/that, не what.

kk.title: what — екпін үшін (cleft)
kk.html: What сөйлемді екпінмен бастай алады: What I love is the quiet. What happened was the car broke down. Зат есім бар болса — who/which/that, what емес.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Tell me what you want. — What I want is a holiday! The only thing that keeps me going. — Everything that happened? — That's all (that) I remember.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Tell me what you want. — What I want is a holiday! The only thing that keeps me going. — Everything that happened? — That's all (that) I remember.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the thing what I need → the thing that I need / what I need. everything what he said → everything that. the best what happened → the best thing that.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the thing what I need → the thing that I need / what I need. everything what he said → everything that. the best what happened → the best thing that.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: what = 'то, что' (без сущ. перед ним). После all, everything, the only, superlative → that. Если есть сущ., не 'what'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: what = 'the thing that' (алдында зат есім жоқ). all, everything, the only, superlative соңында → that. Зат есім бар болса, 'what' емес.

activities.q: "I didn't understand ___ he meant."
why: Correct — no noun → what (= the thing that).
'which' also needs a noun before it.

items.s: Everything that he owns is here.
The thing what I fear is failure.
It's the best book that I've read.

activities.why: after 'the thing' use that, not what.

activities.after: surprised me was his honesty. (the thing that…)
alts: what
activities.why: no noun before → What = the thing that.
what has no noun; after all/only/superlative use that.

activities.q: "That's the funniest joke ___ I've ever heard."
"___ we need is a clear plan."

activities.prompt: I really want some peace and quiet.
activities.answer: what i want is some peace and quiet
alts: what i really want is some peace and quiet

activities.prompt: This is the most important thing what I learned.
activities.answer: this is the most important thing that i learned
alts: this is the most important thing i learned

steps.npc: So, honest opinion on the show?

options.t: What I liked most was the ending.
That I liked most was the ending.
It's the only part that felt slow.
It's the only part what felt slow.
options.why: after 'the only' use that, not what.

activities.why: 'what' means 'the thing that' with no preceding noun; after all/everything/the only/superlatives use 'that', and never use 'what' when a noun is already present.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many adjectives have two forms. -ing describes what something is like (the cause); -ed describes how a person feels.

The film was boring. → I was bored.

learn.title: -ing (cause) vs -ed (feeling)
learn.html:
-ing = the thing/person causing itan interesting book · The news is surprising.
-ed = how you feelI'm interested. · We were surprised.
common pairstiring/tired · exciting/excited · confusing/confused
worrying/worried · amazing/amazeda worrying result → worried parents

learn.title: Watch the meaning!
learn.html:
The two aren't interchangeable: I am boring = I bore other people (a negative comment about you). I am bored = I feel no interest right now.

Ask: is this word describing the cause (-ing) or the feeling (-ed)?

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How was the lecture?  — So boring — I was completely bored.

— You look tired.  — Yeah, it was a tiring day.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'm boring in this class.  ✓ I'm bored.
✗
The trip was very tired.  ✓ The trip was tiring.
✗
We were very exciting.  ✓ We were excited.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• -ing = describes the cause (an exciting game).
  • -ed = describes the feeling (excited fans).
  • “I'm bored” (I feel it) ≠ “I'm boring” (I cause it in others).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: У многих прилагательных две формы. -ing — какой предмет (причина); -ed — что чувствует человек: The film was boring. → I was bored.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп сын есімнің екі түрі бар. -ing — зат қандай екенін (себеп); -ed — адам қалай сезінетінін: The film was boring. → I was bored.

ru.title: -ing (причина) vs -ed (чувство)
ru.html: -ing = причина (предмет/человек): an interesting book. -ed = чувство: I'm interested. Пары: tiring/tired, exciting/excited, confusing/confused, worrying/worried.

kk.title: -ing (себеп) vs -ed (сезім)
kk.html: -ing = себеп болатын зат/адам: an interesting book. -ed = сезім: I'm interested. Жұптар: tiring/tired, exciting/excited, confusing/confused, worrying/worried.

ru.title: Следите за смыслом!
ru.html: Не взаимозаменяемы: I am boring = я навожу скуку на других (о тебе негативно). I am bored = мне сейчас скучно. Спросите: слово о причине (-ing) или о чувстве (-ed)?

kk.title: Мағынасына абай!
kk.html: Ауыспайды: I am boring = мен басқаларды жалықтырамын (сен туралы теріс пікір). I am bored = қазір қызық емес. Сұраңыз: бұл сөз себепті (-ing) ме, сезімді (-ed) ме?

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How was the lecture? — So boring — I was bored. — You look tired. — It was a tiring day.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How was the lecture? — So boring — I was bored. — You look tired. — It was a tiring day.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I'm boring in this class. → I'm bored. The trip was very tired. → tiring. We were very exciting. → excited.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I'm boring in this class. → I'm bored. The trip was very tired. → tiring. We were very exciting. → excited.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: -ing = причина (an exciting game). -ed = чувство (excited fans). “I'm bored” ≠ “I'm boring”.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: -ing = себеп (an exciting game). -ed = сезім (excited fans). “I'm bored” ≠ “I'm boring”.

activities.q: "I'm really ___ in history."
why: 'interesting' describes the subject, not your feeling.

activities.why: -ing describes the thing; -ed the person's feeling.

activities.q: "The instructions were ___, so nobody knew what to do."
why: 'confused' is a feeling — instructions don't feel.

activities.q: "He never stops talking about himself. He's so ___."
why: 'bored' = he feels no interest — wrong here.
Correct — he causes boredom in others → boring.

activities.prompt: The news amazed everyone.
alts: everyone was amazed by the news

activities.prompt: Everyone was frightened by the storm.
alts: it was a frightening storm

options.t: Amazing! The rides were thrilling and we were exhausted by the end.
Amazing! The rides were thrilled and we were exhausting.
options.why: rides are thrilling; we were exhausted.

options.t: Definitely — I wasn't bored for a second.
Definitely — I wasn't boring for a second.

activities.why: Use -ing adjectives for the cause (an exciting film) and -ed adjectives for the feeling (excited viewers); 'I'm bored' (I feel it) differs from 'I'm boring' (I cause it).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
When several adjectives come before a noun, they follow a usual order: opinion before fact, and specific facts in a set sequence.

a lovely little old wooden table (opinion → size → age → material).

learn.title: The usual order
learn.html:
1 opinionnice, lovely, horrible
2 size / 3 age / 4 shapebig, old, round
5 colour / 6 originred, Italian
7 material / 8 purposewooden, cooking (a cooking pot)

learn.title: Adjectives after linking verbs
learn.html:
After be, seem, look, feel, taste, smell, sound, get, become, use an adjective (not an adverb) — it describes the subject:

The soup tastes delicious (not deliciously). · You look tired. · It's getting dark.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Look at this beautiful big blue vase!  — It looks expensive.

— How's the curry?  — It smells amazing and tastes great.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a red big car  ✓ a big red car (size before colour).
✗
It tastes deliciously.  ✓ It tastes delicious.
✗
She seems angrily.  ✓ She seems angry.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Order: opinion → size → age → shape → colour → origin → material → purpose.
  • Opinion adjectives come before fact adjectives.
  • After be/seem/look/taste/smell/feel use an adjective, not an adverb.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Несколько прилагательных перед существительным идут в определённом порядке: мнение раньше факта: a lovely little old wooden table (мнение → размер → возраст → материал).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Зат есім алдында бірнеше сын есім белгілі ретпен тұрады: пікір фактіден бұрын: a lovely little old wooden table (пікір → көлем → жас → материал).

ru.title: Обычный порядок
ru.html: 1 мнение: nice, lovely. 2 размер / 3 возраст / 4 форма: big, old, round. 5 цвет / 6 происхождение: red, Italian. 7 материал / 8 назначение: wooden, cooking.

kk.title: Әдеттегі рет
kk.html: 1 пікір: nice, lovely. 2 көлем / 3 жас / 4 пішін: big, old, round. 5 түс / 6 шығу тегі: red, Italian. 7 материал / 8 мақсат: wooden, cooking.

ru.title: После глаголов-связок
ru.html: После be, seem, look, feel, taste, smell, sound, get, become — прилагательное (не наречие) — описывает подлежащее: tastes delicious; look tired; getting dark.

kk.title: Байланыс етістіктерінен кейін
kk.html: be, seem, look, feel, taste, smell, sound, get, become-тан кейін сын есім (үстеу емес) — ол бастауышты сипаттайды: tastes delicious; look tired; getting dark.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — This beautiful big blue vase! — It looks expensive. — How's the curry? — It smells amazing and tastes great.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — This beautiful big blue vase! — It looks expensive. — How's the curry? — It smells amazing and tastes great.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a red big car → a big red car. tastes deliciously → tastes delicious. seems angrily → seems angry.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a red big car → a big red car. tastes deliciously → tastes delicious. seems angrily → seems angry.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Порядок: мнение → размер → возраст → форма → цвет → происхождение → материал → назначение. Мнение раньше факта. После be/seem/look/taste — прилагательное.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Рет: пікір → көлем → жас → пішін → түс → шығу тегі → материал → мақсат. Пікір фактіден бұрын. be/seem/look/taste-тан кейін сын есім.

activities.q: "She wore a ___ dress."
why: Correct — opinion → colour → material → beautiful red silk.

activities.why: size before colour; after 'seem' use an adjective.

activities.typeLabel: adjective or adverb after the verb?
activities.why: linking verbs (look/taste) take adjectives; action verbs take adverbs.

activities.q: "After the long walk, we all felt ___."
"He bought an ___ car."

activities.prompt: a (Chinese / lovely / old) vase
alts: a lovely old chinese vase.

activities.instruction: Correct the word after the verb:
activities.prompt: You look tiredly today.
alts: you look tired today.

activities.scene: Use adjective order and linking verbs.

steps.npc: What did you buy at the market?

options.t: A lovely little leather bag.
A leather little lovely bag.
options.why: opinion first, material last: lovely little leather.

options.t: It smells strong but tastes delicious.
It smells strongly but tastes deliciously.
options.why: linking verbs take adjectives: smells strong, tastes delicious.

activities.why: Adjectives follow the order opinion → size → age → shape → colour → origin → material → purpose; after linking verbs (be/seem/look/taste/smell/feel) use an adjective, not an adverb.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Adjectives describe nouns; adverbs describe verbs (how something is done). Most adverbs = adjective + -ly.

a quick car (adj) → he drives quickly (adv).

learn.title: Forming adverbs
learn.html:
+ -lyslow → slowly · careful → carefully
-y → -ilyhappy → happily · easy → easily
-le → -lyterrible → terribly · gentle → gently
-ic → -icallyautomatic → automatically

learn.title: What each one modifies
learn.html:
Adjective + noun / after be: a careful driver · She is careful.

Adverb + verb / adjective / adverb: She drives carefully · really good · very quickly.

Remember: after linking verbs (look, seem, taste) use the adjective — 'It looks good', not well (see Unit 99).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Was the test easy?  — Yes, I finished it easily.

— He speaks so quietly.  — I know — he's a very quiet person.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
He drives very careful.  ✓ He drives very carefully.
✗
She sings beautiful.  ✓ She sings beautifully.
✗
speak more clear  ✓ speak more clearly.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Adjective → noun / after be (a slow train, it's slow).
  • Adverb → verb / adjective / adverb (drive slowly, really good).
  • Most adverbs = adjective + -ly (spelling: happy→happily, terrible→terribly).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Прилагательное описывает существительное, наречие — глагол (как делается). Большинство наречий = прил. + -ly: a quick car → drives quickly.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Сын есім зат есімді, үстеу етістікті (қалай істелетінін) сипаттайды. Көп үстеу = сын есім + -ly: a quick car → drives quickly.

ru.title: Образование наречий
ru.html: + -ly: slow → slowly. -y → -ily: happy → happily. -le → -ly: terrible → terribly. -ic → -ically: automatic → automatically.

kk.title: Үстеу жасау
kk.html: + -ly: slow → slowly. -y → -ily: happy → happily. -le → -ly: terrible → terribly. -ic → -ically: automatic → automatically.

ru.title: Что описывает
ru.html: Прил. + сущ. / после be: a careful driver; She is careful. Наречие + глагол/прил./наречие: drives carefully; really good. После связок — прилагательное (looks good).

kk.title: Нені сипаттайды
kk.html: Сын есім + зат есім / be-дан кейін: a careful driver; She is careful. Үстеу + етістік/сын есім/үстеу: drives carefully; really good. Байланыс етістіктерінен кейін — сын есім (looks good).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Was the test easy? — I finished it easily. — He speaks so quietly. — He's a very quiet person.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Was the test easy? — I finished it easily. — He speaks so quietly. — He's a very quiet person.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: drives very careful → very carefully. sings beautiful → beautifully. speak more clear → more clearly.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: drives very careful → very carefully. sings beautiful → beautifully. speak more clear → more clearly.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Прил. → сущ. / после be. Наречие → глагол/прил./наречие. Большинство наречий = прил. + -ly.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Сын есім → зат есім / be-дан кейін. Үстеу → етістік/сын есім/үстеу. Көп үстеу = сын есім + -ly.

activities.q: "She sang the song ___."
why: 'beautiful' describes a noun, not the verb 'sang'.

activities.why: before a noun → adjective; with a verb → adverb.

activities.q: "The exam was hard, but she answered every question ___."
"The film was ___ boring."

activities.prompt: Her English is perfect. She speaks it ___.
alts: she speaks it perfectly

activities.prompt: He gave a careful answer.
alts: he answered carefully.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: giving feedback on a talk

options.t: You explained everything clearly and calmly.
You explained everything clear and calm.
Just speak a little more slowly next time.
Just speak a little more slow next time.

activities.why: Adjectives describe nouns (and follow be/seem/look); adverbs describe verbs, adjectives and other adverbs, and most are formed by adding -ly (with spelling changes like happy→happily).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some words are irregular: good→well, and some are the same as adjective and adverb (fast, hard, late, early). Watch out for hard/hardly and late/lately — different meanings!

a good singer → she sings well. · a fast car → he drives fast.

learn.title: Special cases
learn.html:
good (adj) → well (adv)good work → works well
same formfast, hard, late, early, straight
hard ≠ hardlywork hard (a lot) · hardly work (almost not)
late ≠ latelyarrive late · lately = recently

learn.title: well = 'in good health' too
learn.html:
well can also be an adjective meaning 'healthy': I don't feel well today.

hardly = almost not: I can hardly hear you. · hardly any/ever/anyone = almost none/never/no one.

good is never an adverb: She did it good ✗ → did it well.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How's your Spanish?  — Not great — I can hardly speak it, but I'm studying hard.

— You look well!  — Thanks, I've been exercising a lot lately.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She plays very good.  ✓ She plays very well.
✗
He drives fastly.  ✓ He drives fast.
✗
I hardly worked (meaning: a lot).  ✓ I worked hard.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• good (adj) → well (adv); well can also mean 'healthy'.
  • fast, hard, late, early = same adjective & adverb form (no -ly).
  • hardly = almost not; lately = recently — not the -ly of hard/late.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые слова неправильные: good→well; некоторые одинаковы как прил./наречие (fast, hard, late, early). hard/hardly, late/lately — разный смысл! a good singer → sings well.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір сөз бұрыс: good→well; кейбірі сын есім/үстеу бірдей (fast, hard, late, early). hard/hardly, late/lately — мағынасы бөлек! a good singer → sings well.

ru.title: Особые случаи
ru.html: good (прил) → well (нареч). Одинаковая форма: fast, hard, late, early, straight. hard ≠ hardly: work hard (много) / hardly work (почти нет). late ≠ lately (= недавно).

kk.title: Ерекше жағдайлар
kk.html: good (сын) → well (үстеу). Бірдей форма: fast, hard, late, early, straight. hard ≠ hardly: work hard (көп) / hardly work (дерлік жоқ). late ≠ lately (= жақында).

ru.title: well = 'здоров' тоже
ru.html: well как прилагательное = 'здоров': I don't feel well. hardly = почти не: I can hardly hear you; hardly any/ever/anyone. good никогда не наречие: did it well.

kk.title: well = 'дені сау' да
kk.html: well сын есім ретінде 'дені сау': I don't feel well. hardly = дерлік жоқ: I can hardly hear you; hardly any/ever/anyone. good ешқашан үстеу емес: did it well.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How's your Spanish? — I can hardly speak it, but I'm studying hard. — You look well! — I've been exercising a lot lately.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How's your Spanish? — I can hardly speak it, but I'm studying hard. — You look well! — I've been exercising a lot lately.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: plays very good → very well. drives fastly → fast. I hardly worked (=много) → worked hard.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: plays very good → very well. drives fastly → fast. I hardly worked (=көп) → worked hard.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: good → well; well = 'здоров' тоже. fast/hard/late/early — одинаковая форма. hardly = почти не; lately = недавно.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: good → well; well = 'дені сау' да. fast/hard/late/early — бірдей форма. hardly = дерлік жоқ; lately = жақында.

activities.q: "She plays the violin very ___."

activities.instruction: Does the adverb add -ly or stay the same?
activities.why: fast/hard = same; good→well; late→lately changes meaning.

activities.q: "There's ___ any milk left — just a drop."
"I'm not going to work today — I don't feel ___."
why: 'good' isn't used for health this way.
