Correct — 'well' = in good health → don't feel well.

activities.prompt: I almost never watch TV.
alts: i hardly ever watch tv.

activities.prompt: He's a fast runner and a good swimmer. He runs ___ and swims ___.
alts: he runs fast and swims well

options.t: Much better, thanks — I feel well now.
Much better, thanks — I feel good healthy now.
Tons — I've hardly touched my emails.
Tons — I've hard touched my emails.

activities.why: good→well (well also = healthy); fast/hard/late/early keep the same form as adverbs; and hardly (almost not) and lately (recently) differ in meaning from hard and late.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
so and such both add emphasis (= very), but the grammar differs: so + adjective/adverb; such + (a/an) + (adjective) + noun.

It's so cold! · It's such a cold day!

learn.title: so vs such
learn.html:
so + adjective / adverbso tired · so quickly
such + a/an + (adj) + singular nounsuch a nice person
such + (adj) + plural/uncountablesuch lovely flowers · such good news
so much / so many + nounso much money · so many people

learn.title: …that (result)
learn.html:
Both can be followed by (that) + result:

It was so hot that we stayed inside. · It was such a hot day that we stayed inside.

Use so much/many (not 'such') before a noun with a quantity idea: so much traffic that we were late.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Thanks for dinner — it was so delicious!  — You're such a kind guest.

— There were so many people that we couldn't get in.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's a so nice day.  ✓ It's such a nice day. / It's so nice.
✗
such tired  ✓ so tired.
✗
so a big house  ✓ such a big house.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• so + adjective/adverb; such + (a/an) + (adjective) + noun.
  • so much/many + noun (quantity).
  • Both take …(that) + result: so cold that…, such a day that…

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: so и such оба усиливают (= very), но грамматика разная: so + прил./наречие; such + (a/an) + (прил.) + сущ.: so cold! such a cold day!

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: so мен such — екеуі де күшейтеді (= very), бірақ грамматикасы бөлек: so + сын есім/үстеу; such + (a/an) + (сын есім) + зат есім: so cold! such a cold day!

ru.title: so vs such
ru.html: so + прил./наречие: so tired, so quickly. such + a/an + (прил) + ед. сущ.: such a nice person. such + (прил) + множ./неисч.: such lovely flowers. so much/many + сущ.

kk.title: so vs such
kk.html: so + сын есім/үстеу: so tired, so quickly. such + a/an + (сын) + жекеше зат: such a nice person. such + (сын) + көпше/саналмайтын: such lovely flowers. so much/many + зат.

ru.title: …that (результат)
ru.html: После обоих — (that) + результат: so hot that we stayed inside; such a hot day that… Перед сущ. с количеством — so much/many (не such): so much traffic that…

kk.title: …that (нәтиже)
kk.html: Екеуінен кейін (that) + нәтиже: so hot that we stayed inside; such a hot day that… Зат есім алдында мөлшер болса — so much/many (such емес): so much traffic that…

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — It was so delicious! — You're such a kind guest. — There were so many people that we couldn't get in.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — It was so delicious! — You're such a kind guest. — There were so many people that we couldn't get in.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a so nice day → such a nice day / so nice. such tired → so tired. so a big house → such a big house.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a so nice day → such a nice day / so nice. such tired → so tired. so a big house → such a big house.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: so + прил./наречие; such + (a/an) + (прил) + сущ. so much/many + сущ. Оба …(that) + результат.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: so + сын есім/үстеу; such + (a/an) + (сын) + зат. so much/many + зат. Екеуі …(that) + нәтиже.

activities.q: "It was ___ a great film!"
why: Correct — such + a + adjective + noun → such a great film.

activities.why: such a big house / so big.
so before adjectives/adverbs; such before nouns.

activities.q: "He told ___ funny jokes that everyone laughed."
why: 'so' can't go before 'funny jokes' (a noun phrase).
Correct — such + (adjective) + plural noun → such funny jokes.

activities.q: "I have ___ things to do today!"
why: 'such' would need an adjective + noun differently here.

activities.why: before 'a + adjective + noun' → “such a boring lecture”.

activities.prompt: The soup was so good.
alts: it was such good soup.
activities.why: such + (adjective) + uncountable noun → such good soup.

activities.prompt: The bag was very heavy. I couldn't lift it.
activities.answer: the bag was so heavy that i couldn't lift it
alts: the bag was so heavy i couldn't lift it

options.t: Incredible — such beautiful cities and so much history!
options.why: such + plural noun; so much + uncountable.

options.t: Incredible — so beautiful cities and such much history!
Absolutely — it's such a wonderful place.
Absolutely — it's so a wonderful place.
options.why: 'so' can't precede 'a place'; use such a.

activities.why: so goes before adjectives/adverbs (and so much/many before nouns); such goes before (a/an +) (adjective +) noun; both can lead to a …(that) result clause.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
too = more than you want (a problem); enough = the right amount / as much as needed. Position matters!

It's too hot. · It's not warm enough. · I don't have enough money.

learn.title: Position rules
learn.html:
too + adjective/adverbtoo expensive · too quickly
enough AFTER adjective/adverbbig enough · fast enough
enough BEFORE a nounenough time · enough chairs
too much/many + nountoo much salt · too many cars

learn.title: + to and + for
learn.html:
Add to + verb to say the effect: It's too heavy to lift. · She's old enough to drive.

Add for + person: This is too difficult for me. · Is there enough for everyone?

Combine: warm enough for us to swim / too cold for me to go out.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Can you reach it?  — No, I'm not tall enough. It's too high.

— Shall we start?  — Not yet — there aren't enough chairs for everyone.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's enough big.  ✓ It's big enough.
✗
too much hot  ✓ too hot.
✗
He's enough old to drive.  ✓ old enough to drive.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• too + adjective/adverb; too much/many + noun (excess).
  • enough comes AFTER an adjective/adverb but BEFORE a noun.
  • Add + to (effect) and + for + person: too hard for me to do.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: too = больше, чем нужно (проблема); enough = достаточно. Позиция важна! It's too hot. It's not warm enough. I don't have enough money.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: too = қалағаннан артық (мәселе); enough = жеткілікті мөлшер. Орны маңызды! It's too hot. It's not warm enough. I don't have enough money.

ru.title: Правила позиции
ru.html: too + прил./наречие: too expensive. enough — ПОСЛЕ прил./наречия: big enough. enough — ПЕРЕД сущ.: enough time. too much/many + сущ.: too much salt.

kk.title: Орын ережелері
kk.html: too + сын есім/үстеу: too expensive. enough — сын есім/үстеуден КЕЙІН: big enough. enough — зат есімнен БҰРЫН: enough time. too much/many + зат: too much salt.

ru.title: + to и + for
ru.html: Эффект: to + verb: too heavy to lift; old enough to drive. Для кого: for + person: too difficult for me. Вместе: warm enough for us to swim.

kk.title: + to және + for
kk.html: Әсерін: to + verb: too heavy to lift; old enough to drive. Кім үшін: for + person: too difficult for me. Бірге: warm enough for us to swim.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Can you reach it? — I'm not tall enough. It's too high. — Shall we start? — There aren't enough chairs for everyone.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Can you reach it? — I'm not tall enough. It's too high. — Shall we start? — There aren't enough chairs for everyone.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: enough big → big enough. too much hot → too hot. enough old to drive → old enough to drive.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: enough big → big enough. too much hot → too hot. enough old to drive → old enough to drive.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: too + прил./наречие; too much/many + сущ. enough — ПОСЛЕ прил., ПЕРЕД сущ. + to (эффект), + for + person.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: too + сын есім/үстеу; too much/many + зат. enough — сын есімнен КЕЙІН, зат есімнен БҰРЫН. + to (әсер), + for + person.

activities.q: "This coffee is ___ hot to drink."
why: 'enough hot' is wrong order and meaning.
Correct — more than wanted → too hot to drink.
'so' doesn't fit the 'to drink' pattern here.

activities.answer: He isn't strong enough to win

activities.q: "The box was ___ for one person to carry."
why: word order: too before the adjective.

activities.q: "We didn't have ___ to finish the project."
why: Correct — enough before a noun → enough time.

activities.why: enough after the adjective → “big enough for me”.

activities.prompt: The tea is very hot. I can't drink it.
activities.answer: the tea is too hot to drink
alts: the tea is too hot for me to drink

activities.prompt: He is strong. He can lift it.
activities.answer: he is strong enough to lift it
alts: he's strong enough to lift it

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: setting up an event

options.t: Almost — but we don't have enough chairs yet.
Almost — but we don't have chairs enough yet.
options.why: enough goes before the noun: enough chairs.

options.t: A bit too cold — turn the heating up.
A bit cold enough — turn the heating up.
options.why: 'cold enough' means sufficiently cold — contradicts 'turn it up'; use too cold.

activities.why: 'too' (excess) goes before an adjective/adverb and 'too much/many' before nouns; 'enough' goes after an adjective/adverb but before a noun; add + to (effect) and + for + person.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To compare two things, use the comparative: short adjectives add -er; longer ones use more. Follow with than.

cheaper than · more expensive than · better than.

learn.title: How to form it
learn.html:
1 syllable → -erold → older · cheap → cheaper
-e → -r; consonant doublingnice → nicer · big → bigger · hot → hotter
-y → -ierhappy → happier · easy → easier
2+ syllables → morecareful → more careful · modern → more modern

learn.title: Irregular & both forms
learn.html:
Irregular: good→better, bad→worse, far→further/farther. (much→more, little→less.)

Some 2-syllable adjectives allow both: clever→cleverer / more clever; quiet, simple, narrow.

Don't double up: more cheaper ✗ → cheaper.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Which phone should I get?  — This one's cheaper, but that one's more reliable.

— How's your new flat?  — Bigger and much better than the old one!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
more cheap  ✓ cheaper.
✗
gooder  ✓ better.
✗
cheaper that  ✓ cheaper than.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Short adjectives: + -er (older, bigger, happier); long: more + adjective.
  • Irregular: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
  • Follow with than; never use 'more' with an -er form.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Для сравнения двух — сравнительная степень: короткие + -er; длинные — more. После — than: cheaper than; more expensive than; better than.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Екі затты салыстыру үшін салыстырмалы шырай: қысқа сын есім + -er; ұзын — more. Артынан than: cheaper than; more expensive than; better than.

ru.title: Как образуется
ru.html: 1 слог → -er: old → older. -e → -r; удвоение согласной: nice → nicer, big → bigger. -y → -ier: happy → happier. 2+ слога → more: more careful.

kk.title: Қалай жасалады
kk.html: 1 буын → -er: old → older. -e → -r; дауыссыз қосарлану: nice → nicer, big → bigger. -y → -ier: happy → happier. 2+ буын → more: more careful.

ru.title: Неправильные & две формы
ru.html: Неправ.: good→better, bad→worse, far→further/farther. Некоторые 2-сложные: clever→cleverer/more clever. Не удваивайте: more cheaper → cheaper.

kk.title: Бұрыс & екі түрі
kk.html: Бұрыс: good→better, bad→worse, far→further/farther. Кейбір 2 буынды: clever→cleverer/more clever. Қосарламаңыз: more cheaper → cheaper.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Which phone? — This one's cheaper, but that one's more reliable. — New flat? — Bigger and much better than the old one!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Which phone? — This one's cheaper, but that one's more reliable. — New flat? — Bigger and much better than the old one!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: more cheap → cheaper. gooder → better. cheaper that → cheaper than.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: more cheap → cheaper. gooder → better. cheaper that → cheaper than.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Короткие: + -er; длинные: more + прил. Неправ.: good→better, bad→worse. После — than; с -er нет 'more'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Қысқа: + -er; ұзын: more + сын есім. Бұрыс: good→better, bad→worse. Артынан than; -er-мен 'more' болмайды.

activities.q: "Today is ___ than yesterday."
"The film was ___ than I expected — I nearly fell asleep."
why: 'bored' is a feeling, not a comparative.

activities.q: "The traffic today is even ___ than usual."

activities.prompt: The Nile is long. The Amazon is longer.
activities.answer: the amazon is longer than the nile
alts: the amazon is longer than the nile.

activities.prompt: Gold costs more than silver.
activities.answer: gold is more expensive than silver
alts: gold is more expensive than silver.

options.t: The train's cheaper, but the plane's faster.
The train's more cheap, but the plane's more fast.
Yes, and it's more relaxing than flying.
Yes, and it's relaxinger than flying.

activities.why: Form comparatives with -er for short adjectives (with spelling changes) and more for longer ones; learn irregulars (good→better, bad→worse); follow with than and never combine more with an -er form.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Ways to make comparatives more precise: strengthen with much/far/a lot/a bit, show change with more and more, and link two changes with the … the ….

much better · a bit cheaper · more and more expensive · the more you practise, the better you get.

learn.title: The patterns
learn.html:
big differencemuch/far/a lot + comparative: far older
small differencea bit / a little / slightly + comparative
continuing change-er and -er / more and more: colder and colder
two linked changesthe + comparative … the + comparative

learn.title: any/no + comparative; and more
learn.html:
any / no + comparative: Is he any better? · It's no bigger than a phone.

Continuing change with long adjectives: more and more difficult; with -er adjectives: warmer and warmer.

Don't use 'very' with comparatives: very better ✗ → much better.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How's the patient?  — Much better, thanks — a bit stronger every day.

— City life?  — It's getting more and more expensive. The longer I stay, the poorer I get!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
very cheaper  ✓ much cheaper.
✗
more and more cheap  ✓ cheaper and cheaper.
✗
the more you eat, more you want  ✓ the more you eat, the more you want.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Strengthen: much/far/a lot + comparative; soften: a bit/slightly.
  • Continuing change: -er and -er / more and more.
  • the + comparative … the + comparative links two changes. Use 'much', not 'very'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Уточнение сравнения: усиление much/far/a lot/a bit, изменение more and more, связь двух изменений the … the …: much better; the more you practise, the better you get.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Салыстырманы дәлдеу: much/far/a lot/a bit-пен күшейту, more and more-мен өзгеріс, the … the …-мен екі өзгерісті байланыстыру: much better; the more you practise, the better you get.

ru.title: Модели
ru.html: Большая разница: much/far/a lot + comparative. Малая: a bit/a little/slightly. Продолж. изменение: -er and -er / more and more. Два изменения: the + comparative … the + comparative.

kk.title: Үлгілер
kk.html: Үлкен айырма: much/far/a lot + comparative. Кіші: a bit/a little/slightly. Жалғасқан өзгеріс: -er and -er / more and more. Екі өзгеріс: the + comparative … the + comparative.

ru.title: any/no + comparative
ru.html: any/no + comparative: Is he any better? It's no bigger than a phone. Продолж.: more and more difficult / warmer and warmer. 'very' нельзя: much better.

kk.title: any/no + comparative
kk.html: any/no + comparative: Is he any better? It's no bigger than a phone. Жалғасқан: more and more difficult / warmer and warmer. 'very' болмайды: much better.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How's the patient? — Much better — a bit stronger every day. — It's getting more and more expensive. The longer I stay, the poorer I get!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How's the patient? — Much better — a bit stronger every day. — It's getting more and more expensive. The longer I stay, the poorer I get!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: very cheaper → much cheaper. more and more cheap → cheaper and cheaper. the more you eat, more you want → …, the more you want.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: very cheaper → much cheaper. more and more cheap → cheaper and cheaper. the more you eat, more you want → …, the more you want.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Усиление: much/far/a lot; смягчение: a bit/slightly. Продолж.: -er and -er / more and more. the … the …. 'much', не 'very'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Күшейту: much/far/a lot; жұмсарту: a bit/slightly. Жалғасқан: -er and -er / more and more. the … the …. 'much', 'very' емес.

activities.q: "This car is ___ faster than my old one."

items.s: Prices are getting higher and higher.
The more I read, the more I learn.

activities.before: The queue is getting longer and

activities.answer: This is far more difficult than before

activities.q: "'How's your headache?' 'No ___, unfortunately.'"
"Learning a language becomes ___ interesting over time."

activities.prompt: If you sleep more, you feel better.
activities.answer: the more you sleep, the better you feel
alts: the more you sleep the better you feel

activities.prompt: This exam is harder than the last.
activities.answer: this exam is much harder than the last
alts: this exam is much harder than the last one

options.t: Much better — I'm getting faster and faster.
Very better — I'm getting more fast and fast.
The more you train, the easier it gets.
More you train, easier it gets.

activities.why: Strengthen comparatives with much/far/a lot (not 'very') and soften with a bit/slightly; show continuing change with -er and -er / more and more; and link two changes with the + comparative … the + comparative.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
as … as says two things are equal. not as … as says one is less. The middle word is the plain adjective/adverb (no -er).

She's as tall as her brother. · It's not as cold as yesterday.

learn.title: The patterns
learn.html:
equalas + adjective/adverb + as: as fast as
not equal (less)not as/so … as: not as expensive as
as much/many … asas much money as · as many people as
the same asMy bag is the same as yours.

learn.title: twice as … as; than vs as
learn.html:
Multiples: twice as expensive as, three times as big as, half as long as.

Use than only with comparatives (bigger than); use as with the equal form (as big as). Don't mix: as big than ✗.

After 'as', a subject pronoun is more correct: as tall as I am (informally 'as tall as me').

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Is the new café good?  — Not as good as the old one, honestly.

— They look alike!  — Yes, she's just as clever as her sister — and the same age as her too.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She's as taller as him.  ✓ as tall as him.
✗
not as big than  ✓ not as big as.
✗
the same like yours  ✓ the same as yours.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• as + adjective/adverb + as = equal; not as … as = less.
  • as much/many … as; twice/three times as … as; the same as.
  • Use than with -er/more forms, as with the plain form.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: as … as — два предмета равны. not as … as — один меньше. В середине обычное прил./наречие (без -er): as tall as; not as cold as.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: as … as — екі зат тең. not as … as — біреуі кем. Ортасында жай сын есім/үстеу (-er жоқ): as tall as; not as cold as.

ru.title: Модели
ru.html: Равно: as + adj/adv + as: as fast as. Не равно (меньше): not as/so … as. as much/many … as. the same as: the same as yours.

kk.title: Үлгілер
kk.html: Тең: as + adj/adv + as: as fast as. Тең емес (кем): not as/so … as. as much/many … as. the same as: the same as yours.

ru.title: twice as … as; than vs as
ru.html: Кратность: twice/three times/half as … as. than только с comparative (bigger than); as с равной формой. Не смешивайте: as big than ✗. После 'as' правильнее: as tall as I am.

kk.title: twice as … as; than vs as
kk.html: Еселер: twice/three times/half as … as. than тек comparative-пен (bigger than); as тең формамен. Араластырмаңыз: as big than ✗. 'as'-тан кейін дұрысы: as tall as I am.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — New café good? — Not as good as the old one. — She's just as clever as her sister — and the same age as her.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — New café good? — Not as good as the old one. — She's just as clever as her sister — and the same age as her.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: as taller as → as tall as. not as big than → not as big as. the same like yours → the same as yours.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: as taller as → as tall as. not as big than → not as big as. the same like yours → the same as yours.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: as + adj/adv + as = равно; not as … as = меньше. as much/many … as; twice as … as; the same as. than — с -er/more; as — с обычной формой.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: as + adj/adv + as = тең; not as … as = кем. as much/many … as; twice as … as; the same as. than — -er/more-пен; as — жай формамен.

activities.q: "He runs as fast ___ his brother."

items.s: It's not as cold as yesterday.
My phone is the same as yours.

activities.why: the middle word stays plain: as old as.

activities.after: difficult as the last one. (not as … as)

activities.answer: This one is twice as expensive as that

activities.q: "The film was better ___ I expected."
"My salary is ___ as it was five years ago — no rise at all."

activities.why: equal form uses 'as' → “as beautifully as a professional”.

activities.prompt: Today is warmer than yesterday.
activities.answer: yesterday wasn't as warm as today
alts: yesterday was not as warm as today

activities.prompt: My case weighs 20kg. Yours weighs 20kg too.
activities.answer: my case is as heavy as yours
alts: my case weighs as much as yours

activities.scene: Use as … as / not as … as.

options.t: It's not as big as the first, but it's just as bright.
options.why: not as … as; just as … as.

options.t: It's not as bigger than the first, but as bright than.
About the same as the other one.
About the same than the other one.

activities.why: as + adjective/adverb + as marks equality (not as … as = less); use the plain form in the middle, use than only with -er/more comparatives, and note as much/many … as, twice as … as, and the same as.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
The superlative compares one thing with a whole group (the most/least). Short adjectives add -est; longer ones use most. Usually with the.

the tallest building · the most expensive car · the best day.

learn.title: How to form it
learn.html:
short → the …-estold → the oldest · big → the biggest
-y → -iesthappy → the happiest
long → the most …the most beautiful
irregulargood→the best · bad→the worst · far→the furthest

learn.title: in/of + group; present perfect
learn.html:
Use in with places/groups: the tallest in the world / in the class. Use of with a period or number: the best day of my life · the youngest of three.

Common with present perfect + ever: the best film I've ever seen.

Don't say the most tallest — one superlative marker only.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How was the holiday?  — The best we've ever had! Venice is the most beautiful city in Italy.

— Who's the tallest in your family?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the most tall  ✓ the tallest.
✗
the most best  ✓ the best.
✗
the tallest of the world  ✓ the tallest in the world.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Short: the …-est; long: the most …; irregular: the best/worst/furthest.
  • in + place/group, of + period/number.
  • Often with present perfect + ever; never double the marker.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Превосходная степень сравнивает один предмет со всей группой. Короткие + -est; длинные — most. Обычно с the: the tallest; the most expensive; the best.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Күшейтпелі шырай бір затты бүкіл топпен салыстырады. Қысқа + -est; ұзын — most. Әдетте the-мен: the tallest; the most expensive; the best.

ru.title: Как образуется
ru.html: Короткие → the …-est: the oldest, the biggest. -y → -iest: the happiest. Длинные → the most: the most beautiful. Неправ.: the best/worst/furthest.

kk.title: Қалай жасалады
kk.html: Қысқа → the …-est: the oldest, the biggest. -y → -iest: the happiest. Ұзын → the most: the most beautiful. Бұрыс: the best/worst/furthest.

ru.title: in/of + группа; present perfect
ru.html: in — с местом/группой: the tallest in the world. of — с периодом/числом: the best day of my life. present perfect + ever: the best I've ever seen. Не удваивайте: the most tallest.

kk.title: in/of + топ; present perfect
kk.html: in — орын/топпен: the tallest in the world. of — кезең/санмен: the best day of my life. present perfect + ever: the best I've ever seen. Қосарламаңыз: the most tallest.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How was the holiday? — The best we've ever had! Venice is the most beautiful city in Italy. — Who's the tallest in your family?

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How was the holiday? — The best we've ever had! Venice is the most beautiful city in Italy. — Who's the tallest in your family?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the most tall → the tallest. the most best → the best. the tallest of the world → the tallest in the world.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the most tall → the tallest. the most best → the best. the tallest of the world → the tallest in the world.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Короткие: the …-est; длинные: the most …; неправ.: the best/worst. in + место/группа, of + период/число. Часто present perfect + ever.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Қысқа: the …-est; ұзын: the most …; бұрыс: the best/worst. in + орын/топ, of + кезең/сан. Жиі present perfect + ever.

items.s: She's the youngest in the team.
That was the best meal of my life.

activities.q: "It was the happiest day ___ my life."
"That's the funniest thing I've ___ heard."

activities.answer: the nile is the longest river
alts: the nile is the longest river in the world

activities.answer: it's the best holiday i've ever had
alts: this is the best holiday i've ever had

options.t: Kyoto — the most peaceful place I've ever visited.
options.why: long adjective → the most peaceful + ever.

options.t: Kyoto — the most peacefulest place I've visited.
options.why: one marker only: the most peaceful.

options.t: The best ramen in Japan, no question.
The best ramen of Japan, no question.

activities.why: Form superlatives with the …-est (short) or the most … (long), learn irregulars (best/worst/furthest), use in with places/groups and of with periods/numbers, and never double the superlative marker.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Adverbs of frequency and certain other adverbs go in a mid-position: after 'be', but before other verbs (and after the first auxiliary).

She is always late. · I always drink coffee. · I have never been there.

learn.title: Mid-position rules
learn.html:
after 'be'He is often tired.
before a main verbThey usually walk home.
after the first auxiliary/modalI can never remember. · She has already left.
frequency & othersalways, usually, often, sometimes, never; also, still, just, already, probably

learn.title: Manner/place/time adverbs at the end
learn.html:
Adverbs of manner (how), place (where), time (when) usually go at the end, in that order: She sang beautifully (how) at the concert (where) last night (when).

never/rarely/seldom are negative — don't add another 'not': I never eat meat (not don't never).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Do you cook much?  — I sometimes cook at weekends, but I'm usually too busy.

— Have you seen it?  — I've never watched it, actually.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I drink always coffee.  ✓ I always drink coffee.
✗
She always is late.  ✓ She is always late.
✗
I don't never smoke.  ✓ I never smoke.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Frequency/mid-adverbs: after 'be', before main verbs, after the first auxiliary.
  • Manner → place → time adverbs go at the end.
  • never/rarely are already negative — no extra 'not'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Наречия частотности в средней позиции: после 'be', но перед другими глаголами (и после первого вспомогательного): She is always late. I always drink coffee. I have never been there.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Жиілік үстеулері орта позицияда: 'be'-дан кейін, бірақ басқа етістіктен бұрын (және бірінші көмекшіден кейін): She is always late. I always drink coffee. I have never been there.

ru.title: Правила средней позиции
ru.html: После 'be': He is often tired. Перед главным глаголом: They usually walk. После первого вспом./модального: I can never remember; She has already left. always, usually, often, never; also, still, just, already.

kk.title: Орта позиция ережелері
kk.html: 'be'-дан кейін: He is often tired. Негізгі етістіктен бұрын: They usually walk. Бірінші көмекші/модальдан кейін: I can never remember; She has already left. always, usually, often, never; also, still, just, already.

ru.title: Manner/place/time — в конце
ru.html: Наречия manner (как), place (где), time (когда) обычно в конце, в этом порядке: sang beautifully (как) at the concert (где) last night (когда). never/rarely отрицательны — без 'not': I never eat meat.

kk.title: Manner/place/time — соңында
kk.html: Manner (қалай), place (қайда), time (қашан) үстеулері әдетте соңында, осы ретпен: sang beautifully (қалай) at the concert (қайда) last night (қашан). never/rarely теріс — 'not' қоспаңыз: I never eat meat.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Do you cook much? — I sometimes cook at weekends, but I'm usually too busy. — Seen it? — I've never watched it.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Do you cook much? — I sometimes cook at weekends, but I'm usually too busy. — Seen it? — I've never watched it.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I drink always coffee. → I always drink coffee. She always is late. → She is always late. I don't never smoke. → I never smoke.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I drink always coffee. → I always drink coffee. She always is late. → She is always late. I don't never smoke. → I never smoke.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Наречия частотности: после 'be', перед главным глаголом, после первого вспом. Manner → place → time в конце. never/rarely — без 'not'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Жиілік үстеулері: 'be'-дан кейін, негізгі етістіктен бұрын, бірінші көмекшіден кейін. Manner → place → time соңында. never/rarely — 'not' жоқ.

activities.q: "Which is correct?"
why: Correct — before the main verb → I always take.
mid-position, not the start, is normal here.

activities.why: before main verb (usually go); after be (is always).

activities.after: forgets his keys. (often — before the verb)
activities.why: before the main verb → he often forgets.

activities.answer: I have never been to Japan
activities.why: after the first auxiliary → have never been.

activities.answer: She danced gracefully on stage yesterday

activities.q: "My brother ___ tired after work."
"Choose the correct sentence."

activities.why: frequency adverb before the main verb → “We usually eat dinner…”.

activities.prompt: She is happy to help.
activities.answer: she is always happy to help
alts: she's always happy to help

activities.prompt: I have eaten octopus.
alts: i've never eaten octopus
activities.why: after the first auxiliary → have never eaten.

options.t: I usually go running, and I'm always careful about my diet.
I go usually running, and I always am careful.
I've never really enjoyed gyms, to be honest.
options.why: after the first auxiliary → have never.

options.t: I've enjoyed never really gyms.
options.why: 'never' goes right after the auxiliary: have never enjoyed.

activities.why: Frequency and mid-adverbs go after 'be', before main verbs, and after the first auxiliary; manner/place/time adverbs go at the end in that order; and never/rarely already carry the negative.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
These adverbs describe how a situation relates to time and expectation. still = continuing; yet = up to now (not happened); already = sooner than expected; no longer / not … any more = it has stopped.

He's still asleep. · Have you finished yet? · She's already left.

learn.title: The differences
learn.html:
still = continues (surprise it hasn't stopped)I still live here.
yet = questions/negatives, up to nowNot yet. · Has it started yet?
already = before expected (positive)They've already gone.
no longer / not any more = stoppedI no longer work there / not any more.

learn.title: Position
learn.html:
still = mid-position (before the main verb, after 'be'): She still works / is still here. yet and any more = end of the clause: I haven't finished yet. · I don't smoke any more.

no longer = mid-position: I no longer believe him.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Are you still at work?  — No, I've already left.  — Have you eaten yet?  — Not yet.

— Do you play tennis?  — Not any more — my knee's bad.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I have yet finished.  ✓ I haven't finished yet.
✗
She works still here.  ✓ She still works here.
✗
I don't work there no more.  ✓ not any more / no longer work there.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• still = continuing (mid-position); yet = up to now, in questions/negatives (end).
  • already = sooner than expected (positive, mid-position).
  • no longer (mid) / not … any more (end) = a situation has stopped.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Эти наречия показывают связь ситуации со временем и ожиданием: still = продолжается; yet = до сих пор (не произошло); already = раньше ожидаемого; no longer / not … any more = прекратилось.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бұл үстеулер жағдайдың уақыт пен күтуге қатысын білдіреді: still = жалғасуда; yet = әзірге (болмаған); already = күткеннен ерте; no longer / not … any more = тоқтады.

ru.title: Различия
ru.html: still = продолжается: I still live here. yet = вопрос/отрицание: Not yet. already = рано (утвержд.): already gone. no longer / not any more = прекратилось.

kk.title: Айырмашылықтары
kk.html: still = жалғасады: I still live here. yet = сұрақ/болымсыз: Not yet. already = ерте (болымды): already gone. no longer / not any more = тоқтады.

ru.title: Позиция
ru.html: still = средняя позиция: She still works / is still here. yet, any more = в конце: haven't finished yet; don't smoke any more. no longer = средняя позиция.

kk.title: Орны
kk.html: still = орта позиция: She still works / is still here. yet, any more = сөйлем соңында: haven't finished yet; don't smoke any more. no longer = орта позиция.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Are you still at work? — I've already left. — Eaten yet? — Not yet. — Play tennis? — Not any more.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Are you still at work? — I've already left. — Eaten yet? — Not yet. — Play tennis? — Not any more.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I have yet finished. → haven't finished yet. works still here → still works. no more → not any more / no longer.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I have yet finished. → haven't finished yet. works still here → still works. no more → not any more / no longer.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: still = продолжение (средн.); yet = до сих пор, вопрос/отриц. (конец). already = рано (утвержд., средн.). no longer/not … any more = прекратилось.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: still = жалғасу (орта); yet = әзірге, сұрақ/болымсыз (соңы). already = ерте (болымды, орта). no longer/not … any more = тоқтады.

activities.q: "Is dinner ready ___?"
why: 'still' = continuing, wrong in a question about completion.
'already' expects it's done sooner than expected.

items.s: I still live with my parents.

activities.why: 'yet' goes at the end: hasn't arrived yet.

alts: anymore

activities.why: still/already = continuing/early; yet/no longer = up to now/stopped.

activities.q: "I thought the shop opened at nine, but it's ___ open — great!"
"Which is correct?"

activities.why: a neutral question uses 'yet' at the end → “Have you bought the tickets yet?” (don't also use 'already').

activities.prompt: He doesn't work here any more.
alts: he no longer works here.

activities.prompt: 'You should call Mum.' (You did it earlier.)
alts: i already called her
i have already called her

steps.npc: Are you still writing that report?

options.t: No, I've already sent it.
No, I've yet sent it.
options.why: 'yet' is for questions/negatives at the end.

steps.npc: Do you still use the old software?

options.t: Not any more — we switched last month.
Not yet more — we switched last month.
options.why: stopped → not any more / no longer.

activities.why: still = a continuing situation (mid-position); yet = up to now in questions/negatives (end); already = sooner than expected (positive, mid); no longer / not … any more = the situation has stopped.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
even = a surprising extreme ('more than you'd expect'). even + comparative = still more. even if / even though / even when introduce clauses.

Even a child could do it. · It's even colder today. · Even though he's rich, he's unhappy.

learn.title: Uses of even
learn.html:
even + surprising wordEveryone came, even Grandma.
even + comparative (= still)It got even worse.
even though = although (a fact)Even though it rained, we went.
even if = whether or not (imagined)I'll go even if it rains.

learn.title: even though vs even if
learn.html:
even though = a real fact, stronger 'although': Even though I studied, I failed (I really did study).

even if = a possibility that won't change the result: I won't lend it to you, even if you beg (whether you beg or not).

Position: even goes just before the word it emphasises. Not even a child not.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Was the exam hard?  — Even the teacher couldn't answer question 5!

— Will you come if it's cold?  — Even if it snows, I'll be there.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Even if he's rich, he's mean (he really is rich).  ✓ Even though he's rich…
✗
It's more even cold.  ✓ It's even colder.
✗
Even is difficult for adults.  ✓ It's difficult even for adults.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• even = surprising extreme; goes before the emphasised word.
  • even + comparative = still more (even better).
  • even though = a real fact (strong although); even if = a possibility that won't change things.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: even = удивительная крайность ('больше, чем ждёшь'). even + comparative = ещё более. even if / even though / even when вводят придаточные: Even a child could do it. Even though he's rich, he's unhappy.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: even = таңқаларлық шектен тыс ('күткеннен көп'). even + comparative = одан да. even if / even though / even when — сөйлем бастайды: Even a child could do it. Even though he's rich, he's unhappy.

ru.title: Использование even
ru.html: even + неожид. слово: Everyone came, even Grandma. even + comparative: got even worse. even though = although (факт). even if = даже если (гипотеза).

kk.title: even қолданысы
kk.html: even + таңқаларлық сөз: Everyone came, even Grandma. even + comparative: got even worse. even though = although (факт). even if = болса да (қиял).

ru.title: even though vs even if
ru.html: even though = реальный факт, сильное 'although': Even though I studied, I failed (я правда учился). even if = возможность, не меняющая результат: I won't lend it, even if you beg. even — перед выделяемым словом.

kk.title: even though vs even if
kk.html: even though = нақты факт, күшті 'although': Even though I studied, I failed (шынымен оқыдым). even if = нәтижені өзгертпейтін мүмкіндік: I won't lend it, even if you beg. even — екпінделетін сөздің алдында.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Was the exam hard? — Even the teacher couldn't answer question 5! — Come if it's cold? — Even if it snows, I'll be there.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Was the exam hard? — Even the teacher couldn't answer question 5! — Come if it's cold? — Even if it snows, I'll be there.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Even if he's rich (реально) → Even though he's rich. more even cold → even colder. Even is difficult → difficult even for adults.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Even if he's rich (шын) → Even though he's rich. more even cold → even colder. Even is difficult → difficult even for adults.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: even = удивит. крайность; перед выделяемым словом. even + comparative = ещё более. even though = факт; even if = не меняющая возможность.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: even = таңқаларлық шек; екпінделетін сөз алдында. even + comparative = одан да. even though = факт; even if = өзгертпейтін мүмкіндік.

activities.q: "___ I don't like coffee, I'll drink it to stay awake. (I really don't like it)"
why: 'even if' = a possibility; but this is a real fact.

items.s: Even a beginner can use it.
Even though it might rain, take a coat. (uncertain)
I'll help even if I'm busy.

activities.after: you apologise, I won't forgive you. (a possibility)

activities.q: "He remembered everyone's name — ___ mine, which we'd only just met."

activities.q: "___ she was tired, she finished the marathon."
why: 'even' alone doesn't introduce a clause here.
'even if' = a possibility, but she really was tired.

activities.prompt: He had a map. He still got lost.
activities.answer: even though he had a map, he got lost
alts: even though he had a map he got lost

activities.prompt: Maybe it'll be expensive. I'll buy it anyway.
activities.answer: i'll buy it even if it's expensive
alts: even if it's expensive, i'll buy it
activities.why: a possibility that won't change the result → even if.

activities.scene: Use even / even if / even though.

steps.npc: The hike is really long, you know.

options.t: I'll do it even if it takes all day.
options.why: a possibility that won't stop you → even if.

options.t: I'll do it even though it takes all day. (not yet a fact)
options.why: it's a possibility, not a known fact → even if.

options.t: Even though I'm tired, I want to keep going.
options.why: a real present fact → even though.

options.t: Even if I'm tired, I want to keep going. (you clearly are)
options.why: it's a real fact now → even though.

activities.why: 'even' marks a surprising extreme (before the emphasised word); 'even + comparative' means still more; 'even though' introduces a real fact (strong 'although'), while 'even if' introduces a possibility that won't change the outcome.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
All show contrast ('but'). The difference is grammar: although/though + a clause (subject + verb); in spite of / despite + a noun/-ing.

Although it was late, we went. = Despite the late hour, we went.

learn.title: Clause vs noun
learn.html:
although / (even) though + clauseAlthough he tried, he failed.
in spite of / despite + nounDespite the rain, …
in spite of / despite + -ingIn spite of feeling ill, she worked.
despite/in spite of + the fact that + clauseDespite the fact that he tried, …

learn.title: Key details
learn.html:
despite has no 'of': despite of the rain ✗ → despite the rain. in spite of keeps 'of'.

though can also go at the end (informal) = however: It's expensive. I'll buy it, though.

Don't confuse with because (of) — that shows reason, not contrast.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— You look happy!  — Despite the bad weather, we had a great trip. Although it rained, we swam every day.

— It's pricey.  — I like it, though.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Despite it was raining…  ✓ Although it was raining… / Despite the rain…
✗
despite of the cost  ✓ despite the cost / in spite of the cost.
✗
Although of the traffic…  ✓ Despite the traffic… / Although there was traffic…

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• although/though + clause (subject + verb).
  • despite / in spite of + noun or -ing (despite has no 'of').
  • All mean 'but'; end-position though = however.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Все показывают контраст ('but'). Разница в грамматике: although/though + предложение; in spite of / despite + существительное/-ing: Although it was late, we went. = Despite the late hour, …

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бәрі қарама-қайшылық ('but'). Айырма — грамматика: although/though + сөйлем; in spite of / despite + зат есім/-ing: Although it was late, we went. = Despite the late hour, …

ru.title: Предложение vs существительное
ru.html: although/(even) though + предложение: Although he tried. in spite of/despite + сущ.: Despite the rain. + -ing: In spite of feeling ill. + the fact that + предложение.

kk.title: Сөйлем vs зат есім
kk.html: although/(even) though + сөйлем: Although he tried. in spite of/despite + зат есім: Despite the rain. + -ing: In spite of feeling ill. + the fact that + сөйлем.

ru.title: Важные детали
ru.html: despite — без 'of': despite of the rain → despite the rain. in spite of — с 'of'. though в конце (неформ.) = however. because (of) — причина, не контраст.

kk.title: Маңызды бөлшектер
kk.html: despite — 'of' жоқ: despite of the rain → despite the rain. in spite of — 'of' сақталады. though соңында да (бейресми) = however. because (of) — себеп, қайшылық емес.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Despite the bad weather, we had a great trip. Although it rained, we swam. — It's pricey. — I like it, though.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Despite the bad weather, we had a great trip. Although it rained, we swam. — It's pricey. — I like it, though.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Despite it was raining → Although it was raining / Despite the rain. despite of the cost → despite/in spite of the cost. Although of the traffic → Despite the traffic.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Despite it was raining → Although it was raining / Despite the rain. despite of the cost → despite/in spite of the cost. Although of the traffic → Despite the traffic.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: although/though + предложение. despite / in spite of + сущ./-ing (despite — без 'of'). Все = 'but'; though в конце = however.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: although/though + сөйлем. despite / in spite of + зат есім/-ing (despite — 'of' жоқ). Бәрі 'but'; соңындағы though = however.

activities.q: "___ the traffic, we arrived on time."
why: 'although' needs a subject + verb after it.

items.s: Although it was cold, we went out.
Despite the cold, we went out.
Despite it was cold, we went out.
In spite of the rain, they played.

activities.after: working hard, she didn't get the job. (+ -ing)
alts: In spite of
despite
in spite of

activities.after: he was rich, he lived simply. (+ clause)
alts: although
Even though
even though

activities.q: "___ the high price, the phone sold out in hours."
why: Correct — + noun phrase → Despite the high price.

activities.q: "They won ___ having ten players."

activities.why: a clause follows → “Although it was late…” (or 'Despite the late hour').

activities.prompt: Although he was ill, he came to work.
activities.answer: despite being ill, he came to work
alts: despite his illness, he came to work

activities.prompt: In spite of the noise, the baby slept.
activities.answer: although it was noisy, the baby slept
alts: although there was noise, the baby slept
activities.why: in spite of + noun → although + clause.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: reviewing a tough week

options.t: Despite a few technical problems, it went well.
Despite there were a few problems, it went well.
Although I was terrified, I stayed calm on stage.
Despite I was terrified, I stayed calm.
options.why: despite + noun/-ing, not a clause: 'Although I was…' or 'Despite my fear…'.

activities.why: although/though take a clause (subject + verb); despite/in spite of take a noun or -ing (despite has no 'of'); all express contrast, and end-position 'though' means 'however'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
in case = as a precaution, because something might happen. You do the action first, in case the problem comes later.

Take an umbrella in case it rains. (= because it might rain — so be ready).

learn.title: Form & tenses
learn.html:
present action + in case + presentI'll write it down in case I forget.
past action + in case + pastI wrote it down in case I forgot.
in case of + noun (notices)In case of fire, use the stairs.
NOT will after in casein case it will rain → in case it rains

learn.title: in case ≠ if
learn.html:
if = do the action only when the condition happens (afterwards). in case = do it before, as a precaution.

I'll call you if I need help. (= only when I need it) · I'll take my phone in case I need help. (= ready in advance).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Why are you taking a coat?  — In case it gets cold later.

— Save my number in case you get lost.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Take money in case you will need it.  ✓ in case you need it.
✗
I'll take an umbrella if it rains later (precaution meaning).  ✓ in case it rains.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• in case = as a precaution (do it in advance, because it might happen).
  • No will after in case: in case it rains (present), in case it rained (past).
  • in case of + noun for signs/notices; don't confuse in case with if.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: in case = на всякий случай, потому что что-то может случиться. Действие делаешь заранее: Take an umbrella in case it rains (вдруг дождь — будь готов).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: in case = сақтық үшін, бірдеңе болуы мүмкін болғандықтан. Әрекетті алдын ала жасайсың: Take an umbrella in case it rains (жаңбыр болуы мүмкін — дайын бол).

ru.title: Форма и времена
ru.html: наст. действие + in case + наст.: I'll write it down in case I forget. прош. + in case + прош. in case of + сущ. (объявления): In case of fire… после in case нет will.

kk.title: Форма мен шақтар
kk.html: осы шақ әрекет + in case + осы шақ: I'll write it down in case I forget. өткен + in case + өткен. in case of + зат есім (хабарландыру): In case of fire… in case-тан кейін will жоқ.

ru.title: in case ≠ if
ru.html: if = делаешь только когда условие наступит (потом). in case = заранее, на всякий случай: I'll call you if I need help vs I'll take my phone in case I need help.

kk.title: in case ≠ if
kk.html: if = шарт болғанда ғана істейсің (кейін). in case = алдын ала, сақтық үшін: I'll call you if I need help vs I'll take my phone in case I need help.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Why a coat? — In case it gets cold later. — Save my number in case you get lost.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Why a coat? — In case it gets cold later. — Save my number in case you get lost.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: in case you will need it → in case you need it. В значении предосторожности: in case it rains (не if).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: in case you will need it → in case you need it. Сақтық мағынасында: in case it rains (if емес).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: in case = предосторожность (заранее). После in case нет will. in case of + сущ. — таблички; не путайте in case с if.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: in case = сақтық (алдын ала). in case-тан кейін will жоқ. in case of + зат есім — белгілер; in case-ті if-пен шатастырмаңыз.

activities.q: "Take some water in case you ___ thirsty."
why: past doesn't fit this present situation.

items.s: I'll bring a map in case we get lost.
Take a coat in case it will snow.
I took cash in case the card didn't work.

activities.before: Write down the address in case you

activities.after: fire, do not use the lift. (In case … + noun)
alts: in case of
activities.why: before a noun on a notice → In case of.

activities.answer: I'll take an umbrella in case it rains

activities.q: "I'll leave now, and I'll phone you ___ I'm going to be late."
why: Correct — the action depends on the condition happening → if.

activities.q: "Keep the receipt ___ you want to return it."
why: Correct — a precaution now for a possible future → in case.
'if' would mean 'only when you want to return it', losing the precaution meaning.

activities.why: no 'will' after in case → “in case I am out”.

activities.prompt: Bring a jacket. It might be cold.
activities.answer: bring a jacket in case it's cold
alts: bring a jacket in case it is cold

activities.instruction: Rewrite the past version with 'in case':
activities.prompt: I saved the file. I was worried the computer might crash.
activities.answer: i saved the file in case the computer crashed
alts: i saved the file in case the computer crashed.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: preparing for a trip

options.t: I packed extra clothes in case it gets cold.
I packed extra clothes in case it will get cold.
Yes, in case my phone dies on the way.
Yes, if my phone will die on the way.

activities.why: 'in case' introduces a precaution taken in advance because something might happen; use a present (or past) verb after it — never 'will' — and don't confuse it with 'if', which acts only when the condition occurs.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
These are conditional connectors. unless = 'if not'; as long as / provided (that) / providing (that) = 'only if / on condition that'.

I won't go unless you come. · You can stay as long as you're quiet.

learn.title: The connectors
learn.html:
unless = if … notYou'll fail unless you study. (= if you don't study)
as long as = only ifI'll help as long as you ask nicely.
provided/providing (that)You can borrow it provided (that) you return it.
present tense (like if)unless you will → unless you do

learn.title: unless = if not (careful!)
learn.html:
unless already contains 'not', so the verb is positive: Unless you hurry, we'll miss it (= if you don't hurry).

Like other conditionals, use the present tense after these words for future meaning — never 'will'.

as long as can also mean 'for the whole time that': Stay as long as you like.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Can I use your car?  — Sure, as long as you fill it up. But not unless you have a licence!

— We'll get there on time provided there's no traffic.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Unless you don't hurry…  ✓ Unless you hurry… (unless = if not).
✗
as long as you will pay  ✓ as long as you pay.
✗
provided of good weather  ✓ provided (that) the weather is good.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• unless = if … not (use a positive verb after it).
  • as long as / provided (that) / providing (that) = only if / on condition that.
  • Present tense for future meaning — never 'will' after these words.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Это условные союзы. unless = 'if not'; as long as / provided (that) / providing (that) = 'только если / при условии'. I won't go unless you come. You can stay as long as you're quiet.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бұлар — шартты жалғаулықтар. unless = 'if not'; as long as / provided (that) / providing (that) = 'тек ... болса'. I won't go unless you come. You can stay as long as you're quiet.

ru.title: Союзы
ru.html: unless = if … not: You'll fail unless you study. as long as = только если. provided/providing (that): borrow it provided (that) you return it. Наст. время (как if).

kk.title: Жалғаулықтар
kk.html: unless = if … not: You'll fail unless you study. as long as = тек ... болса. provided/providing (that): borrow it provided (that) you return it. Осы шақ (if сияқты).

ru.title: unless = if not (осторожно!)
ru.html: В unless уже есть 'not', поэтому глагол положительный: Unless you hurry (= if you don't hurry). Для будущего — наст. время, без 'will'. as long as = 'всё время, пока' тоже.

kk.title: unless = if not (абай!)
kk.html: unless ішінде 'not' бар, сондықтан етістік оң: Unless you hurry (= if you don't hurry). Болашақ мағынасында осы шақ — 'will' жоқ. as long as = 'бүкіл уақыт бойы' да.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Can I use your car? — As long as you fill it up. But not unless you have a licence! — We'll get there provided there's no traffic.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Can I use your car? — As long as you fill it up. But not unless you have a licence! — We'll get there provided there's no traffic.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Unless you don't hurry → Unless you hurry. as long as you will pay → as long as you pay. provided of good weather → provided (that) the weather is good.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Unless you don't hurry → Unless you hurry. as long as you will pay → as long as you pay. provided of good weather → provided (that) the weather is good.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: unless = if … not (положит. глагол). as long as / provided (that) = только если. Для будущего наст. время — без 'will'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: unless = if … not (оң етістік). as long as / provided (that) = тек ... болса. Болашаққа осы шақ — 'will' жоқ.

activities.q: "You can't enter ___ you have a ticket."
why: 'if you have a ticket' reverses the meaning.
Correct — 'if … not' → unless you have a ticket.
'as long as' would allow entry, the opposite.

items.s: You can go as long as you're back by ten.
Unless you don't call, I'll worry.

activities.why: unless already means 'if not' → unless you call.
if we don't leave → unless we leave.

activities.before: You can borrow my notes as long as you

cats: as long as / provided (= only if)
activities.why: unless = if not; the others = only if / on condition.

activities.q: "I'll lend you the money ___ you pay it back next week."
"'Call me unless you're busy' means:"
options: Call me if you're not busy
why: Correct — unless = if not → call if you're not busy.

activities.why: unless already = if not → “Unless you study, you'll fail.”

activities.prompt: If you don't hurry, you'll miss the bus.
activities.answer: unless you hurry, you'll miss the bus
alts: you'll miss the bus unless you hurry

activities.prompt: You can play outside, but only if you wear a coat.
activities.answer: you can play outside as long as you wear a coat
alts: you can play outside as long as you wear a coat.

activities.scene: Use unless / as long as / provided.

options.t: Sure, as long as you're careful with it.
options.why: on condition → as long as + present.

options.t: Sure, as long as you'll be careful with it.
Then you replace it — unless it's clearly a fault.
Then you replace it — unless it isn't clearly a fault.
options.why: unless already means 'if not': 'unless it's a fault'.

activities.why: unless means 'if … not' (use a positive verb after it); as long as / provided (that) / providing (that) mean 'only if / on condition that'; and all take a present tense for future meaning — never 'will'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
as has several jobs: reason (= because), time (= while/when), and manner/role. like = 'similar to' (+ noun).

As it was late, we left. · She works as a nurse. · He sings like a professional.

learn.title: as vs like
learn.html:
as = becauseAs you're tired, rest.
as = while / at the same timeI saw her as I was leaving.
as = in the role/function ofHe works as a teacher. (he really is one)
like = similar to (+ noun)She looks like her mother. (not identical)

learn.title: as + clause vs like + noun
learn.html:
Use as before a subject + verb: Do as I say. In careful English, don't use 'like' + clause here.

Use like before a noun/pronoun: Do it like this. · a house like mine.

Contrast role: work as a waiter (that's your job) vs treat me like a child (I'm not one).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html: — Why the early start?  — As we've got a long drive.  — You cook like a chef!  — Thanks — I trained as one.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She works like a doctor (she is one).  ✓ works as a doctor.
✗
Do like I do. (careful English)  ✓ Do as I do.
✗
He looks as his brother.  ✓ looks like his brother.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• as = because / while / in the role of (real job or function).
  • like = similar to (+ noun/pronoun): looks like, tastes like.
  • as + clause (as I said); like + noun (like me). 'as a teacher' = you are one.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: У as несколько ролей: причина (= because), время (= while/when), роль/образ. like = 'похоже на' (+ сущ.): As it was late, we left. works as a nurse. sings like a pro.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: as-тың бірнеше қызметі: себеп (= because), уақыт (= while/when), рөл/тәсіл. like = 'ұқсас' (+ зат есім): As it was late, we left. works as a nurse. sings like a pro.

ru.title: as vs like
ru.html: as = because: As you're tired, rest. as = while: saw her as I was leaving. as = в роли: works as a teacher (реально). like = похоже: looks like her mother.

kk.title: as vs like
kk.html: as = because: As you're tired, rest. as = while: saw her as I was leaving. as = рөлде: works as a teacher (шынымен). like = ұқсас: looks like her mother.

ru.title: as + предложение vs like + сущ.
ru.html: as — перед subject + verb: Do as I say. like — перед сущ./местоим.: Do it like this; a house like mine. work as a waiter (работа) vs treat me like a child (я не он).

kk.title: as + сөйлем vs like + зат есім
kk.html: as — subject + verb алдында: Do as I say. like — зат есім/есімдік алдында: Do it like this; a house like mine. work as a waiter (жұмысың) vs treat me like a child (мен ондай емеспін).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Why the early start? — As we've got a long drive. — You cook like a chef! — I trained as one.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Why the early start? — As we've got a long drive. — You cook like a chef! — I trained as one.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: works like a doctor (реально) → works as a doctor. Do like I do → Do as I do. looks as his brother → looks like his brother.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: works like a doctor (шын) → works as a doctor. Do like I do → Do as I do. looks as his brother → looks like his brother.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: as = because / while / в роли. like = похоже (+ сущ.). as + предложение; like + сущ. 'as a teacher' = ты учитель.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: as = because / while / рөлде. like = ұқсас (+ зат есім). as + сөйлем; like + зат есім. 'as a teacher' = сен мұғалімсің.

activities.q: "He works ___ a firefighter."
why: 'like a firefighter' = similar to (not really one).
Correct — his real job → as a firefighter.

items.s: As it was raining, we stayed in.
Do it like I told you. (careful English)

activities.why: before a clause use 'as': do it as I told you.

activities.after: I was walking home, I met an old friend. (time)
alts: as

activities.q: "___ I don't have your number, I couldn't call you."
"My brother treats me ___ a child, even though I'm 25."
why: 'as a child' would suggest I actually am one.
Correct — I'm not really a child → treats me like a child.

activities.why: a knife isn't a screwdriver, just similar in use → “like a screwdriver” (though 'as' is used for real function — here 'like' fits the comparison).

activities.prompt: It was getting dark. We decided to head back.
activities.answer: as it was getting dark, we decided to head back
alts: as it was getting dark we decided to head back
activities.why: reason → As it was getting dark…

activities.prompt: He's a real professional. He plays ___ a professional and is employed ___ a coach.
activities.answer: like a professional and as a coach
alts: he plays like a professional and is employed as a coach

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: talking about a friend

options.t: She works as a translator now.
She works like a translator now.
options.why: her actual job → as a translator.

options.t: Brilliant — she speaks French like a native.
Brilliant — she speaks French as a native.
options.why: she isn't literally a native → like a native.

activities.why: 'as' introduces a reason (because), a time (while/when) or a real role/function; 'like' means 'similar to' before a noun/pronoun — use as + clause and like + noun.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
To give a reason use because + clause or because of / due to + noun. To give a result use so (informal) or therefore (formal).

We stayed in because it rained = because of the rain. It rained, so we stayed in.

learn.title: Reason vs result
learn.html:
because + subject + verb… because I was tired.
because of / due to + noun… because of the noise.
so + result (informal)I was tired, so I left.
therefore / as a result (formal)Sales fell; therefore, we cut costs.

learn.title: Word order & punctuation
learn.html:
because can start the sentence (with a comma): Because it was late, we left. But 'so' cannot start like this.

due to is a bit more formal than 'because of'; both take a noun. therefore often follows a semicolon or starts a new sentence: … . Therefore, …

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Why are you late?  — Because of the traffic. There was an accident, so everything stopped.

— The flight was cancelled due to fog.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
because of it was late  ✓ because it was late / because of the late hour.
✗
Because I was tired. (alone)  ✓ I went to bed because I was tired. (join the clauses)
✗
due to the train was late  ✓ due to the train's delay.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• because + clause; because of / due to + noun (reason).
  • so (informal) / therefore, as a result (formal) give the result.
  • because can start a sentence; so cannot.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Причина: because + предложение или because of / due to + сущ. Результат: so (неформ.) или therefore (форм.): because it rained = because of the rain. It rained, so we stayed in.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Себеп үшін: because + сөйлем немесе because of / due to + зат есім. Нәтиже үшін: so (бейресми) немесе therefore (ресми): because it rained = because of the rain. It rained, so we stayed in.

ru.title: Причина vs результат
ru.html: because + subject + verb. because of / due to + сущ. so + результат (неформ.). therefore / as a result (форм.).

kk.title: Себеп vs нәтиже
kk.html: because + subject + verb. because of / due to + зат есім. so + нәтиже (бейресми). therefore / as a result (ресми).

ru.title: Порядок и пунктуация
ru.html: because может начинать предложение (с запятой): Because it was late, we left. Но 'so' так не начинает. due to — формальнее 'because of'. therefore — после точки с запятой или новое предложение.

kk.title: Сөз реті мен тыныс белгі
kk.html: because сөйлемді бастай алады (үтірмен): Because it was late, we left. Бірақ 'so' олай бастамайды. due to — 'because of'-тан ресмиірек. therefore — нүктелі үтірден кейін не жаңа сөйлем.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Why late? — Because of the traffic. There was an accident, so everything stopped. — The flight was cancelled due to fog.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Why late? — Because of the traffic. There was an accident, so everything stopped. — The flight was cancelled due to fog.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: because of it was late → because it was late / because of the late hour. Because I was tired. (отдельно) → соедините. due to the train was late → due to the delay.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: because of it was late → because it was late / because of the late hour. Because I was tired. (жеке) → сөйлемдерді біріктір. due to the train was late → due to the delay.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: because + предложение; because of / due to + сущ. so (неформ.) / therefore (форм.) — результат. because может начинать; so — нет.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: because + сөйлем; because of / due to + зат есім. so (бейресми) / therefore (ресми) — нәтиже. because бастай алады; so — жоқ.

activities.q: "The match was cancelled ___ the storm."
why: 'so' gives a result, not a reason.

items.s: I stayed home because I was ill.
We left early due to the traffic.
Because of it was raining, we stopped.
He was tired, so he went to bed.

activities.why: because + clause, or because of + noun (not both).

alts: due to

activities.q: "Profits doubled this year. ___, staff received a bonus."
why: 'because' introduces a reason, not a result sentence.

activities.q: "The delay was ___ a technical fault."
why: Correct — + noun → due to a technical fault.

activities.why: a clause follows → “because the bus was delayed” (drop 'of').

activities.prompt: The game stopped because it was snowing.
activities.answer: the game stopped because of the snow
alts: the game stopped because of the snow.

activities.prompt: Because the shops were shut, we went home.
activities.answer: the shops were shut, so we went home
alts: the shops were shut so we went home

options.t: Because of the noise — we couldn't sleep, so we moved.
Because of the noise was terrible, so we moved.
options.why: because of + noun, or 'because the noise was terrible'.

options.t: Much quieter; therefore we slept well.
Much quieter; because we slept well.

activities.why: Give reasons with because + clause or because of / due to + noun; give results with so (informal) or therefore / as a result (formal); because can begin a sentence, so cannot.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Time words (when, while, as, as soon as, until, before, after, by the time) introduce a time clause. For future time, use the present tense in the time clause — not 'will'.

I'll call you when I arrive. (not when I will arrive).

learn.title: when / while / as
learn.html:
when + shorter action / pointWhen the phone rang, I answered.
while / as + longer backgroundWhile I was cooking, he called.
as = two things changing togetherAs I got older, I understood.
as soon as = immediately whenI'll leave as soon as I'm ready.

learn.title: present for future; by the time
learn.html:
Never use 'will' after these words for the future: I'll wait until you come (not will come).

by the time + present/past = 'not later than the moment when': By the time we arrived, the film had started.

Use the past perfect for what happened first (after 'by the time … arrived').

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Text me when you get home.  — I will, as soon as I'm in.

— I fell asleep while I was watching the film. By the time it ended, I'd missed it all!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'll phone you when I will arrive.  ✓ when I arrive.
✗
While I cooked, suddenly the alarm (background needs continuous).  ✓ While I was cooking, the alarm went off.
✗
by the time you'll come  ✓ by the time you come.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• After when/while/as/until/before/after/as soon as: present for future (no 'will').
  • while/as + background action (often continuous); when + point/shorter action.
  • by the time = 'not later than'; use past perfect for the earlier event.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Слова времени (when, while, as, as soon as, until, before, after, by the time) вводят придаточное времени. Для будущего — наст. время (не will): I'll call you when I arrive.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Уақыт сөздері (when, while, as, as soon as, until, before, after, by the time) уақыт сөйлемін бастайды. Болашақта — осы шақ (will емес): I'll call you when I arrive.

ru.title: when / while / as
ru.html: when + короткое/момент: When the phone rang. while/as + длинный фон: While I was cooking. as = меняются вместе: As I got older. as soon as = сразу.

kk.title: when / while / as
kk.html: when + қысқа әрекет/сәт: When the phone rang. while/as + ұзақ фон: While I was cooking. as = бірге өзгеру: As I got older. as soon as = дереу.

ru.title: наст. для будущего; by the time
ru.html: После этих слов нет 'will': I'll wait until you come. by the time + наст./прош. = 'не позже момента, когда': By the time we arrived, the film had started. Для раннего — past perfect.

kk.title: болашаққа осы шақ; by the time
kk.html: Бұл сөздерден кейін 'will' жоқ: I'll wait until you come. by the time + осы/өткен = 'сол сәттен кешікпей': By the time we arrived, the film had started. Бұрын болғанға past perfect.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Text me when you get home. — As soon as I'm in. — I fell asleep while I was watching the film. By the time it ended, I'd missed it all!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Text me when you get home. — As soon as I'm in. — I fell asleep while I was watching the film. By the time it ended, I'd missed it all!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: when I will arrive → when I arrive. While I cooked, suddenly… → While I was cooking… by the time you'll come → by the time you come.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: when I will arrive → when I arrive. While I cooked, suddenly… → While I was cooking… by the time you'll come → by the time you come.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: После when/while/as/until/before/after/as soon as для будущего — наст. время. while/as + фон; when + момент. by the time = 'не позже'; для раннего — past perfect.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: when/while/as/until/before/after/as soon as кейін болашаққа осы шақ. while/as + фон; when + сәт. by the time = 'кешікпей'; бұрынғыға past perfect.

activities.q: "I'll start cooking when the guests ___."
why: no 'will' in a future time clause.
past doesn't fit a future plan.

items.s: Call me as soon as you land.
While I was reading, the lights went out.
I'll wait until you will finish.
When I see her, I'll tell her.

activities.after: I was walking to work, it started to rain. (longer background)
alts: while
As
as

activities.before: We'll leave as soon as the taxi

activities.answer: By the time we arrived, the show had started
activities.why: by the time + past; earlier event in past perfect.

activities.q: "___ she grew older, she became more confident."
"By the time you read this, I ___ already left."
why: Correct — future perfect for what will be done → will have left.
present doesn't show completion before that point.
'had' is for a past reference point.

activities.prompt: I was having lunch. My phone rang.
activities.answer: while i was having lunch, my phone rang
alts: my phone rang while i was having lunch

activities.prompt: First I'll finish this, then I'll help you.
activities.answer: i'll help you as soon as i finish this
alts: as soon as i finish this, i'll help you
activities.why: immediately after → as soon as + present.

options.t: I'll text you as soon as I leave the office.
options.why: as soon as + present for future.

options.t: I'll text you as soon as I'll leave the office.
By the time you arrive, it'll be on the table.
options.why: by the time + present; main clause future.

options.t: By the time you'll arrive, it's on the table.
options.why: by the time + present (arrive), not 'will arrive'.

activities.why: Time clauses (when/while/as/until/before/after/as soon as) take a present tense for future meaning — never 'will'; use while/as for background actions and by the time (+ past perfect for the earlier event).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
as if / as though describe how something seems. Use them before a clause. In informal English, like is often used the same way.

She looks as if she's tired. · It sounds as though it's raining. · You look like you've seen a ghost.

learn.title: as if / as though
learn.html:
seems true → present/perfectHe looks as if he has been running. (probably true)
not true / imagined → pastShe talks as if she knew everything. (she doesn't)
with be → were (formal)He acts as if he were the boss.
like (informal) = as ifIt looks like it's going to rain.

learn.title: Real vs unreal
learn.html:
Use a present tense when it may be true: It looks as if it's going to snow (it might).

Use a past tense (an 'unreal' past) when it's clearly not true: He behaves as if he owned the place (he doesn't). · She spends money as if she were rich.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— You look as if you didn't sleep!  — I didn't — I feel like I could sleep for a week.

— He talks as though he'd been there, but he hasn't.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
as if to be tired  ✓ as if she is/were tired (+ clause).
✗
He acts as if he is the king (clearly not).  ✓ as if he were the king.
✗
as like  ✓ as if / like (not both).

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• as if / as though + clause = how something seems.
  • Present tense = possibly true; past tense (unreal) = clearly not true.
  • like is the informal alternative; with 'be' unreal, use 'were'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: as if / as though описывают, как что-то кажется, перед предложением. В неформальном like так же: She looks as if she's tired. You look like you've seen a ghost.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: as if / as though — бірдеңенің қалай көрінетінін білдіреді, сөйлем алдында. Бейресми тілде like да солай: She looks as if she's tired. You look like you've seen a ghost.

ru.title: as if / as though
ru.html: Похоже на правду → наст./perfect: looks as if he has been running. Не правда → прош.: talks as if she knew everything. с be → were (формально): as if he were the boss. like (неформ.) = as if.

kk.title: as if / as though
kk.html: Шындыққа ұқсас → осы/perfect: looks as if he has been running. Шын емес → өткен: talks as if she knew everything. be-мен → were (ресми): as if he were the boss. like (бейресми) = as if.

ru.title: Реально vs нереально
ru.html: Может быть правдой → наст.: as if it's going to snow. Явно не правда → прош. ('нереальное'): as if he owned the place; as if she were rich.

kk.title: Шын vs жалған
kk.html: Шын болуы мүмкін → осы шақ: as if it's going to snow. Анық шын емес → өткен ('жалған' өткен): as if he owned the place; as if she were rich.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — You look as if you didn't sleep! — I feel like I could sleep for a week. — He talks as though he'd been there, but he hasn't.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — You look as if you didn't sleep! — I feel like I could sleep for a week. — He talks as though he'd been there, but he hasn't.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: as if to be tired → as if she is/were tired. as if he is the king (не правда) → as if he were. as like → as if / like.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
