kk.html: as if to be tired → as if she is/were tired. as if he is the king (шын емес) → as if he were. as like → as if / like.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: as if / as though + предложение = как кажется. Наст. = возможно правда; прош. (нереальное) = явно не правда. like — неформ.; be нереальное — 'were'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: as if / as though + сөйлем = қалай көрінеді. Осы шақ = мүмкін шын; өткен (жалған) = анық шын емес. like — бейресми; be жалған — 'were'.

activities.q: "She looks as if she ___ crying. (it seems true)"
why: Correct — probably true → present perfect has been crying.

items.s: It looks as if it's going to rain.
He talks as if he knew everything (he doesn't).
You look like you've seen a ghost.
She acts as if she is the queen (clearly not).

activities.why: clearly untrue → as if she were the queen.

activities.before: He orders us around as if he
alts: was
activities.why: clearly untrue → unreal past were (was in informal).

activities.before: It sounds as if the party
alts: 's

items.t: acts as if he owned it
talks as if she were rich

activities.q: "He spends money as if he ___ a millionaire — but he's broke."
"(informal) You look ___ you need a holiday!"
why: Correct — informal → like you need a holiday.

activities.why: clearly untrue → “as if I were a child”.

activities.prompt: Maybe he has just woken up — he looks it.
activities.answer: he looks as if he has just woken up
alts: he looks as if he's just woken up
activities.why: possibly true → as if + present perfect.

activities.prompt: He isn't an expert, but he talks like one.
activities.answer: he talks as though he were an expert
alts: he talks as though he was an expert

activities.scene: Use as if / as though / like.

steps.npc: How does the new manager seem?

options.t: He acts as if he owned the company, honestly.
He acts as if he owns the company (you mean he clearly doesn't).
options.why: to stress it's untrue, use the unreal past: as if he owned.

options.t: It feels as if everyone's on edge.
It feels as if everyone were on edge (you think it really is).
options.why: if it seems genuinely true, use present: as if everyone's on edge.

activities.why: as if / as though (and informal 'like') describe how things seem; use a present tense when it may be true and an unreal past (were) when it clearly isn't.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
All relate to time, but differ grammatically. for + a length of time; during + a noun (when); while + a clause (subject + verb).

for two hours · during the film · while I was watching.

learn.title: The three words
learn.html:
for + period (how long)for three days, for a while
during + noun (when)during the meeting, during summer
while + subject + verbwhile we were talking
during vs while (same idea)during the film = while the film was on

learn.title: for = duration; during = within a period
learn.html:
for answers 'how long?': I studied for two hours. during answers 'when?' (inside a period): I fell asleep during the lesson.

Both 'during' and 'while' can introduce the time of an event, but the grammar differs: during + noun, while + clause.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How long were you away?  — For a week. My phone broke during the trip, while we were hiking.

— I'll wait here for ten minutes.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
during two hours  ✓ for two hours.
✗
while the holidays  ✓ during the holidays / while we were on holiday.
✗
for I was sleeping  ✓ while I was sleeping.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• for + a length of time (how long): for an hour.
  • during + a noun (when, within a period): during the night.
  • while + a clause (subject + verb): while I slept.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Все о времени, но грамматика разная: for + длительность; during + сущ. (когда); while + предложение: for two hours · during the film · while I was watching.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бәрі уақытқа қатысты, бірақ грамматикасы бөлек: for + ұзақтық; during + зат есім (қашан); while + сөйлем: for two hours · during the film · while I was watching.

ru.title: Три слова
ru.html: for + период (сколько): for three days. during + сущ. (когда): during the meeting. while + subject + verb: while we were talking. during the film = while the film was on.

kk.title: Үш сөз
kk.html: for + кезең (қанша уақыт): for three days. during + зат есім (қашан): during the meeting. while + subject + verb: while we were talking. during the film = while the film was on.

ru.title: for = длительность; during = внутри
ru.html: for 'сколько?': studied for two hours. during 'когда?' (внутри периода): fell asleep during the lesson. Оба дают время события, но during + сущ., while + предложение.

kk.title: for = ұзақтық; during = ішінде
kk.html: for 'қанша уақыт?': studied for two hours. during 'қашан?' (кезең ішінде): fell asleep during the lesson. Екеуі де оқиға уақытын береді, бірақ during + зат есім, while + сөйлем.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How long away? — For a week. My phone broke during the trip, while we were hiking. — I'll wait for ten minutes.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How long away? — For a week. My phone broke during the trip, while we were hiking. — I'll wait for ten minutes.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: during two hours → for two hours. while the holidays → during the holidays / while we were on holiday. for I was sleeping → while I was sleeping.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: during two hours → for two hours. while the holidays → during the holidays / while we were on holiday. for I was sleeping → while I was sleeping.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: for + длительность. during + сущ. while + предложение.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: for + ұзақтық. during + зат есім. while + сөйлем.

activities.q: "We talked ___ three hours."
why: 'during' needs a noun/event, not a length.

items.s: While we were eating, the phone rang.

activities.why: a length of time → for two days.
for + length; during + noun; while + clause.

activities.q: "I lost my wallet ___ the festival."
why: 'for' is for a length of time.
Correct — within an event (+ noun) → during the festival.

activities.q: "Please don't use your phone ___ the exam."
why: 'for' + a length of time doesn't fit.
Correct — within the event (+ noun) → during the exam.

activities.why: a length of time → “waited for two hours”.

activities.prompt: I hurt my knee during the match.
activities.answer: i hurt my knee while i was playing
alts: i hurt my knee while i was playing the match

activities.prompt: The lights went out while the show was on.
activities.answer: the lights went out during the show
alts: the lights went out during the show.

steps.npc: How long were you in Italy?

options.t: For two weeks — and it rained during the first few days.
During two weeks — and it rained for the first few days.
options.why: for + period; during + noun (the first few days is a period → for).

options.t: We got lost while we were exploring the old town.
We got lost during we were exploring.

activities.why: Use for + a length of time (how long), during + a noun (when, within a period), and while + a clause (subject + verb).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
until = up to a point in time (a continuing situation). by = not later than a point (a deadline). They are not the same!

I'll be here until 6. (I stay the whole time) · Finish it by 6. (at 6 or before).

learn.title: until vs by
learn.html:
until = up to that time (continuous)Wait until Monday. · open until late
by = at or before (deadline)Pay by Friday. · Be back by ten.
until + clauseStay until I come back.
by the time + clauseBy the time we arrived, it had closed.

learn.title: How to choose
learn.html:
Ask: is it a continuing action up to that time (→ until) or a single completion no later than then (→ by)?

I'll wait until 5 (continuous waiting). · I'll finish by 5 (done at some point before 5).

by the time introduces a clause; by is used with a noun/time.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— When's it due?  — By Friday. So I'll work on it until then.

— Will you be up?  — Yes, I'm awake until midnight, so call by then.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Finish it until Friday.  ✓ Finish it by Friday.
✗
Wait by I come back.  ✓ Wait until I come back.
✗
by the time Friday  ✓ by Friday / by the time Friday comes.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• until = a continuing action up to that time (wait until 6).
  • by = a deadline; something happens at or before then (finish by 6).
  • until + clause; by the time + clause; by + noun/time.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: until = до какого-то момента (продолжающееся). by = не позже момента (крайний срок). Это не одно и то же! I'll be here until 6. Finish it by 6.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: until = белгілі уақытқа дейін (жалғасатын жағдай). by = сол уақыттан кешікпей (мерзім). Екеуі бірдей емес! I'll be here until 6. Finish it by 6.

ru.title: until vs by
ru.html: until = до того времени (продолж.): Wait until Monday. by = к или раньше (срок): Pay by Friday. until + предложение: Stay until I come back. by the time + предложение.

kk.title: until vs by
kk.html: until = сол уақытқа дейін (созылу): Wait until Monday. by = сонда не одан бұрын (мерзім): Pay by Friday. until + сөйлем: Stay until I come back. by the time + сөйлем.

ru.title: Как выбрать
ru.html: Спросите: продолжающееся действие до того времени (→ until) или однократное завершение к тому моменту (→ by)? wait until 5; finish by 5. by the time — с предложением; by — с сущ./временем.

kk.title: Қалай таңдау керек
kk.html: Сұраңыз: сол уақытқа дейін жалғасатын әрекет пе (→ until) немесе сонда/бұрын бір рет аяқталу ма (→ by)? wait until 5; finish by 5. by the time — сөйлеммен; by — зат есім/уақытпен.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — When's it due? — By Friday. I'll work on it until then. — Will you be up? — I'm awake until midnight, so call by then.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — When's it due? — By Friday. I'll work on it until then. — Will you be up? — I'm awake until midnight, so call by then.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Finish it until Friday. → by Friday. Wait by I come back. → until I come back. by the time Friday → by Friday / by the time Friday comes.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Finish it until Friday. → by Friday. Wait by I come back. → until I come back. by the time Friday → by Friday / by the time Friday comes.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: until = продолжение до того времени. by = срок; к или раньше. until + предложение; by the time + предложение; by + сущ./время.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: until = сол уақытқа дейін жалғасу. by = мерзім; сонда/бұрын. until + сөйлем; by the time + сөйлем; by + зат есім/уақыт.

activities.q: "Please submit the form ___ Monday."
why: 'until Monday' = keep submitting the whole time — wrong.

activities.q: "I won't know the results ___ next week, so let's meet then."
why: 'by next week' = at some point before — but the state continues.
Correct — the not-knowing continues up to then → until next week.

activities.q: "___ we got to the cinema, the film had already started."
why: Correct — + clause → By the time we got there.

activities.prompt: I need it done no later than 5. So keep working ___ then, and have it ready ___ 5.
activities.answer: until then, and have it ready by 5
alts: keep working until then and have it ready by 5

activities.prompt: We arrived. The shop was already closed.
activities.answer: by the time we arrived, the shop had closed
alts: by the time we arrived, the shop was closed
activities.why: by the time + clause; earlier event in past perfect.

steps.npc: When do you need the designs?

options.t: By Thursday, if possible.
Until Thursday, if possible.

steps.npc: Can I work on them over the week?

options.t: Sure — take your time until Thursday.
Sure — take your time by Thursday.
options.why: a continuing action up to then → until.

activities.why: until marks a continuing action up to a time (wait until 6); by marks a deadline — something happens at or before that time (finish by 6); use by the time + clause and by + noun/time.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Linking adverbs connect ideas across sentences. however (contrast), therefore (result), whereas / while (direct comparison), on the other hand (a different view).

It's expensive. However, it's worth it. · He's tall, whereas she's short.

learn.title: How they work
learn.html:
however (contrast, new sentence)… . However, …
on the other hand (other side)On the one hand … on the other hand …
whereas / while (direct contrast, one sentence)I like tea, whereas he prefers coffee.
therefore / as a result (result)… ; therefore, …

learn.title: Punctuation & difference from 'but'
learn.html:
however/therefore usually start a new sentence (or follow a semicolon) and are followed by a comma: … . However, … .

but/although join clauses within one sentence: It's cold, but sunny.

whereas/while directly contrast two facts in one sentence (no comma needed before them is common in short clauses).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Should we drive or fly?  — Flying's faster. On the other hand, driving is cheaper.

— The plan is good. However, we need more time.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's cold, however I'll go out.  ✓ It's cold. However, I'll go out. / It's cold, but I'll go out.
✗
He works hard, therefore lazy brother.  ✓ He works hard, whereas his brother is lazy.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• however (contrast) & therefore (result) link separate sentences (+ comma).
  • whereas / while directly contrast two facts within one sentence.
  • but/although join clauses; on the other hand introduces a different view.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Связующие наречия соединяют идеи между предложениями: however (контраст), therefore (результат), whereas / while (прямое сравнение), on the other hand (другая сторона).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Байланыстырушы үстеулер ойларды сөйлемдер арасында жалғайды: however (қайшылық), therefore (нәтиже), whereas / while (тікелей салыстыру), on the other hand (басқа көзқарас).

ru.title: Как работают
ru.html: however (контраст, новое предложение). on the other hand (другая сторона). whereas/while (прямой контраст, в одном предложении). therefore/as a result (результат).

kk.title: Қалай жұмыс істейді
kk.html: however (қайшылық, жаңа сөйлем). on the other hand (басқа жағы). whereas/while (тікелей қарсылық, бір сөйлемде). therefore/as a result (нәтиже).

ru.title: Пунктуация; отличие от 'but'
ru.html: however/therefore — новое предложение (или после точки с запятой) + запятая. but/although — соединяют клаузы в одном предложении. whereas/while — прямо противопоставляют два факта.

kk.title: Тыныс белгі; 'but'-тан айырма
kk.html: however/therefore — жаңа сөйлем (не нүктелі үтірден кейін) + үтір. but/although — бір сөйлемде сөйлемшелерді жалғайды. whereas/while — екі фактіні тікелей қарсы қояды.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Drive or fly? — Flying's faster. On the other hand, driving is cheaper. — The plan is good. However, we need more time.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Drive or fly? — Flying's faster. On the other hand, driving is cheaper. — The plan is good. However, we need more time.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: It's cold, however I'll go out. → . However, … / , but … He works hard, therefore lazy brother. → , whereas his brother is lazy.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: It's cold, however I'll go out. → . However, … / , but … He works hard, therefore lazy brother. → , whereas his brother is lazy.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: however (контраст), therefore (результат) — отдельные предложения (+ запятая). whereas / while — прямой контраст в одном предложении. but/although — соединяют клаузы.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: however (қайшылық), therefore (нәтиже) — бөлек сөйлемдер (+ үтір). whereas / while — бір сөйлемде тікелей қарсылық. but/although — сөйлемшелерді жалғайды.

activities.q: "The hotel was cheap. ___, the service was excellent."

items.s: It rained. However, we had fun.
It rained, however we had fun.

activities.why: 'however' starts a new sentence (or after ;), not a comma splice.

activities.after: I prefer reading. (direct contrast, one sentence)
alts: while
As a result

activities.why: however/whereas = contrast; therefore/as a result = result.

activities.q: "Electric cars are clean. ___, they can be expensive to buy."
why: 'therefore' shows a result, not another angle.
Correct — presenting a different side → On the other hand.
'whereas' compares two subjects within one sentence.

activities.q: "Which is punctuated correctly?"
options: The plan is risky. However, it could work.
The plan is risky, however it could work.
The plan is risky however, it could work.
why: 'however' needs a full stop or semicolon before it.

activities.why: the idea is contrast, not result → “, but I kept working” / “. However, …”.

activities.prompt: Tom is careful. His brother is reckless.
activities.answer: tom is careful, whereas his brother is reckless
alts: tom is careful whereas his brother is reckless

activities.prompt: The offer is good, but I need to think about it.
activities.answer: the offer is good. however, i need to think about it
alts: the offer is good. however, i need to think about it.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: weighing up a job offer

steps.npc: So, will you take the job?

options.t: The salary is great. However, the commute is long.
The salary is great, however the commute is long.
options.why: 'however' needs a full stop/semicolon before it, comma after.

options.t: The old job was dull, whereas this one is exciting.
options.why: direct contrast in one sentence → whereas.

options.t: The old job was dull, therefore this one is exciting.

activities.why: however (contrast) and therefore (result) link separate sentences with a comma; whereas/while directly contrast two facts in one sentence; but/although join clauses, and 'on the other hand' introduces a different view.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Three prepositions for time: at for clock times & points, on for days & dates, in for longer periods.

at 6 o'clock · on Monday · in July.

learn.title: The system
learn.html:
at — clock time, pointsat 5, at noon, at night, at the weekend
on — days & dateson Friday, on 3rd May, on my birthday
in — months, years, seasons, parts of dayin May, in 2020, in winter, in the morning
in = after a period (future)I'll be back in ten minutes.

learn.title: No preposition with these
learn.html:
Use no preposition before: last, next, this, every, tomorrow, yesterday: See you next week (not in next week). · I'll call you tomorrow.

Note: at night but in the morning/afternoon/evening; at the weekend (BrE) / on the weekend (AmE).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— When's the meeting?  — On Tuesday at 10, in the morning.

— See you next week!  — Yes, I'll be back in a few days.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
in Monday  ✓ on Monday.
✗
at the morning  ✓ in the morning.
✗
I'll see you in next week.  ✓ see you next week.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• at + clock times/points; on + days/dates; in + longer periods.
  • in + period = 'after that time' (in an hour).
  • No preposition before last/next/this/every/tomorrow.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Три предлога времени: at — часы/моменты, on — дни/даты, in — долгие периоды: at 6 o'clock · on Monday · in July.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Уақыттың үш предлогы: at — сағат/сәт, on — күн/күндер, in — ұзақ кезең: at 6 o'clock · on Monday · in July.

ru.title: Система
ru.html: at — час, момент: at 5, at noon, at night, at the weekend. on — дни/даты: on Friday, on my birthday. in — месяц/год/сезон/часть дня: in May, in 2020, in the morning. in = через (будущее): in ten minutes.

kk.title: Жүйесі
kk.html: at — сағат, сәт: at 5, at noon, at night, at the weekend. on — күн/күндер: on Friday, on my birthday. in — ай/жыл/маусым/күн бөлігі: in May, in 2020, in the morning. in = кейін (болашақ): in ten minutes.

ru.title: С этими предлога нет
ru.html: Без предлога: last, next, this, every, tomorrow, yesterday: See you next week; I'll call you tomorrow. at night, но in the morning; at the weekend (BrE) / on the weekend (AmE).

kk.title: Бұлармен предлог жоқ
kk.html: Предлогсыз: last, next, this, every, tomorrow, yesterday: See you next week; I'll call you tomorrow. at night бірақ in the morning; at the weekend (BrE) / on the weekend (AmE).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — When's the meeting? — On Tuesday at 10, in the morning. — See you next week! — I'll be back in a few days.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — When's the meeting? — On Tuesday at 10, in the morning. — See you next week! — I'll be back in a few days.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: in Monday → on Monday. at the morning → in the morning. in next week → next week.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: in Monday → on Monday. at the morning → in the morning. in next week → next week.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: at + час/момент; on + дни/даты; in + долгий период. in + период = 'через это время'. Перед last/next/this/every/tomorrow предлога нет.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: at + сағат/сәт; on + күн/күндер; in + ұзақ кезең. in + кезең = 'сол уақыттан кейін'. last/next/this/every/tomorrow алдында предлог жоқ.

activities.q: "The train leaves ___ 9:15."

activities.why: at times; on days; in periods.

activities.q: "I'm meeting them ___ next Friday."
"We usually go skiing ___ winter."

activities.prompt: I'll see you _ Monday _ 8pm.
activities.answer: i'll see you on monday at 8pm
alts: i'll see you on monday at 8pm.

activities.prompt: She was born _ 1998, _ the summer.
activities.answer: she was born in 1998, in the summer
alts: she was born in 1998 in the summer

steps.npc: When shall we have the call?

options.t: On Wednesday at 3, in the afternoon works for me.
options.why: on day; at time; in the afternoon.

options.t: In Wednesday on 3, at the afternoon.
options.why: on Wednesday; at 3; in the afternoon.

steps.npc: Can you send the file before then?

options.t: Sure — I'll email it in an hour.
Sure — I'll email it on an hour.
options.why: after a period → in an hour.

activities.why: Use at for clock times and points, on for days and dates, and in for longer periods (and 'in' + a period for 'after that time'); no preposition before last/next/this/every/tomorrow.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Time words for duration and points: for + a length; since + a starting point; ago = back from now.

for two years · since 2020 · two years ago.

learn.title: for / since / ago
learn.html:
for + period (how long)I've lived here for five years.
since + point (from when)I've lived here since 2019.
ago + past time (from now back)I moved here five years ago.
from … to / untilfrom Monday to Friday · open until 6

learn.title: Which tense?
learn.html:
since and for (duration to now) usually go with the present perfect: I've known her for ages / since school.

ago goes with the past simple: I met her three years ago.

Word order: ago comes after the time (two days ago), while for/since come before it.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html: — How long have you been here?  — For six months — since January.  — And your last job?  — I left a year ago.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
since two years  ✓ for two years / since 2022.
✗
I have seen him ago three days.  ✓ I saw him three days ago.
✗
ago two years  ✓ two years ago.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• for + a length (present perfect); since + a start point (present perfect).
  • ago + past simple; it goes after the time period (two years ago).
  • from … to/until for a start and end; until = up to a point.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Длительность и точка: for + период; since + начальная точка; ago = назад от сейчас: for two years · since 2020 · two years ago.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ұзақтық пен нүкте: for + кезең; since + бастау нүктесі; ago = қазірден кейінге: for two years · since 2020 · two years ago.

ru.title: for / since / ago
ru.html: for + период: lived here for five years. since + точка: since 2019. ago + прошлое время: five years ago. from … to / until: from Mon to Fri; until 6.

kk.title: for / since / ago
kk.html: for + кезең: lived here for five years. since + нүкте: since 2019. ago + өткен уақыт: five years ago. from … to / until: from Mon to Fri; until 6.

ru.title: Какое время?
ru.html: since, for (длительность до сейчас) — present perfect: I've known her for ages / since school. ago — past simple: met her three years ago. ago — после времени; for/since — перед.

kk.title: Қай шақ?
kk.html: since, for (қазірге дейінгі ұзақтық) — present perfect: I've known her for ages / since school. ago — past simple: met her three years ago. ago — уақыттан кейін; for/since — алдында.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How long have you been here? — For six months — since January. — Your last job? — I left a year ago.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How long have you been here? — For six months — since January. — Your last job? — I left a year ago.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: since two years → for two years / since 2022. have seen him ago three days → saw him three days ago. ago two years → two years ago.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: since two years → for two years / since 2022. have seen him ago three days → saw him three days ago. ago two years → two years ago.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: for + период; since + точка (present perfect). ago + past simple; после времени. from … to/until — начало и конец.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: for + кезең; since + нүкте (present perfect). ago + past simple; уақыттан кейін. from … to/until — бастау мен аяқ.

activities.q: "I've worked here ___ 2018."

items.s: I've lived here for ten years.
We've been friends since two years.

activities.why: a length → for two years / since a point.

activities.before: I finished my degree three years

activities.why: for + period; since + point; ago after the period.

activities.q: "I ___ this book two weeks ago."
why: 'ago' doesn't go with the present perfect.
present perfect continuous doesn't fit with 'ago'.

activities.q: "The shop is open ___ 9 ___ 5."

activities.why: a length of time → “for five years” (since needs a point).

activities.prompt: I last saw her in 2020.
activities.answer: i last saw her years ago
alts: i saw her years ago
i last saw her four years ago
activities.why: back from now → … years ago (past simple).

activities.prompt: I started this job in March, and I still do it.
activities.answer: i've done this job since march
alts: i have done this job since march
i've had this job since march
activities.why: a start point + present perfect → since March.

steps.npc: How long have you played guitar?

options.t: For about ten years — since I was a teenager.
Since about ten years — for I was a teenager.
I played one just a month ago.
options.why: ago + past simple, after the period.

options.t: I've played one ago a month.
options.why: ago + past simple, after the time: a month ago.

activities.why: for + a length and since + a start point go with the present perfect; ago goes with the past simple and follows the time period; use from … to / until for a start and end.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
For position: in = inside/within an area; on = on a surface/line; at = a point or specific place.

in the box · on the table · at the door.

learn.title: The system
learn.html:
in — enclosed space / areain a room, in a car, in London, in the water
on — surface / lineon the wall, on the floor, on the bus, on the river
at — a point / place / eventat the bus stop, at home, at work, at a party
at + address / on + streetat 10 Downing Street · on Main Street

learn.title: Tricky pairs
learn.html:
in a car/taxi but on a bus/train/plane (public transport). at the corner (point) vs in the corner (of a room). in the picture / on the screen.

at for buildings as places you go to (at the station, at school); in when you mean inside (in the station building).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where are you?  — At the station, waiting on platform 2.  — And your bag?  — In the locker.

— Meet me at the entrance.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
in the bus  ✓ on the bus.
✗
on the airport  ✓ at the airport.
✗
in home  ✓ at home.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• in = inside an area/space; on = on a surface/line; at = a point/place.
  • on public transport (bus/train), in a car/taxi.
  • at home / at work / at school (places you're at).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Для положения: in = внутри/в области; on = на поверхности/линии; at = точка/конкретное место: in the box · on the table · at the door.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Орын үшін: in = ішінде/аумақта; on = бетте/сызықта; at = нүкте/нақты орын: in the box · on the table · at the door.

ru.title: Система
ru.html: in — замкнутое пространство/область: in a room, in London, in the water. on — поверхность/линия: on the wall, on the bus. at — точка/место/событие: at the bus stop, at home, at a party. at + адрес / on + улица.

kk.title: Жүйесі
kk.html: in — жабық кеңістік/аумақ: in a room, in London, in the water. on — бет/сызық: on the wall, on the bus. at — нүкте/орын/іс-шара: at the bus stop, at home, at a party. at + мекенжай / on + көше.

ru.title: Сложные пары
ru.html: in a car/taxi, но on a bus/train/plane. at the corner (точка) vs in the corner (внутри комнаты). Здание — место назначения: at the station; внутри: in the station building.

kk.title: Қиын жұптар
kk.html: in a car/taxi, бірақ on a bus/train/plane. at the corner (нүкте) vs in the corner (бөлме ішінде). Ғимарат — бару орны: at the station; ішінде: in the station building.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Where are you? — At the station, on platform 2. — Your bag? — In the locker. — Meet me at the entrance.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Where are you? — At the station, on platform 2. — Your bag? — In the locker. — Meet me at the entrance.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: in the bus → on the bus. on the airport → at the airport. in home → at home.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: in the bus → on the bus. on the airport → at the airport. in home → at home.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: in = внутри; on = на поверхности; at = точка/место. on обществ. транспорт, in a car/taxi. at home/work/school.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: in = ішінде; on = бетте; at = нүкте/орын. on қоғамдық көлік, in a car/taxi. at home/work/school.

activities.q: "There's a beautiful painting ___ the wall."
why: 'at' is a point, not a surface.

items.s: The keys are on the table.

activities.why: in water; on ceiling/train; at airport.

activities.q: "He's not here — he's ___ work until six."
"The lamp is ___ the corner of the room."
why: 'at the corner' = an outside point (e.g. of a street).
Correct — inside a room → in the corner.
'on the corner' is for street corners.

activities.prompt: I live _ a small flat _ the third floor.
activities.answer: i live in a small flat on the third floor
alts: i live in a small flat on the third floor.
activities.why: in a flat; on a floor.

activities.prompt: She's waiting _ the reception _ the hotel.
activities.answer: she's waiting at the reception in the hotel
alts: she's waiting at reception in the hotel

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: meeting up in town

options.t: At the café on the corner of King Street.
In the café in the corner of King Street.
options.why: at the café; on the corner (of a street).

options.t: I'm on the train now, arriving in ten minutes.
I'm in the train now.

activities.why: Use in for enclosed spaces/areas, on for surfaces/lines and public transport, and at for points, places and events (at home, at work); a car/taxi takes 'in', a bus/train takes 'on'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
More position words describe relationships between things: above/below, over/under, in front of/behind, between/among, next to, opposite.

The lamp is above the desk. · The cat is under the table. · The bank is opposite the park.

learn.title: The pairs
learn.html:
above / below (higher/lower, not touching)a shelf above the sink
over / under (directly above/below, cover)a bridge over the river · under the bed
in front of / behinda car in front of the house
between (two) / among (many)between A and B · among friends

learn.title: More useful words
learn.html:
next to / beside = at the side of; near = close to; opposite = facing; by = right next to.

over/under can also mean 'more/less than': over 100 people, children under five.

Don't confuse beside (next to) with besides (= in addition to).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Where's the remote?  — Between the cushions, or maybe behind the sofa.

— The chemist?  — Opposite the station, next to the bank.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
between the three of them  ✓ among the three of them (more than two).
✗
in front the house  ✓ in front of the house.
✗
beside doing that (= in addition)  ✓ besides doing that.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• above/below (higher/lower); over/under (directly, or more/less than).
  • in front of / behind; next to/beside; opposite (facing).
  • between (two) vs among (many); beside ≠ besides.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Ещё слова места описывают отношения между предметами: above/below, over/under, in front of/behind, between/among, next to, opposite: lamp is above the desk; cat is under the table; bank is opposite the park.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Тағы орын сөздері заттар арасындағы қатынасты сипаттайды: above/below, over/under, in front of/behind, between/among, next to, opposite: lamp is above the desk; cat is under the table; bank is opposite the park.

ru.title: Пары
ru.html: above / below (выше/ниже, не касаясь): shelf above the sink. over / under (прямо над/под): bridge over the river. in front of / behind. between (два) / among (много).

kk.title: Жұптар
kk.html: above / below (жоғары/төмен, тимей): shelf above the sink. over / under (тікелей үсті/асты): bridge over the river. in front of / behind. between (екеу) / among (көп).

ru.title: Ещё полезные слова
ru.html: next to / beside = рядом; near = близко; opposite = напротив; by = прямо рядом. over/under = 'больше/меньше': over 100 people. beside (рядом) ≠ besides (=кроме того).

kk.title: Тағы пайдалы сөздер
kk.html: next to / beside = қасында; near = жақын; opposite = қарсы; by = дәл қасында. over/under = 'артық/кем': over 100 people. beside (қасында) ≠ besides (=оған қоса).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Where's the remote? — Between the cushions, or behind the sofa. — The chemist? — Opposite the station, next to the bank.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Where's the remote? — Between the cushions, or behind the sofa. — The chemist? — Opposite the station, next to the bank.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: between the three → among the three. in front the house → in front of the house. beside doing that (=кроме) → besides doing that.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: between the three → among the three. in front the house → in front of the house. beside doing that (=қоса) → besides doing that.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: above/below; over/under (прямо, или 'больше/меньше'). in front of/behind; next to; opposite. between (два) vs among (много); beside ≠ besides.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: above/below; over/under (тікелей, не 'артық/кем'). in front of/behind; next to; opposite. between (екеу) vs among (көп); beside ≠ besides.

activities.q: "The dog is hiding ___ the table."

items.s: The bank is opposite the park.
There's a plane above the clouds.

activities.why: more than two → among (or 'from' the five).
at the front → in front of the house.

activities.q: "The film is suitable for children ___ 12."
"___ football, he also plays tennis."

activities.why: more than two → “among all the tall buildings”.

activities.prompt: The car is behind the house.
activities.answer: the car is in front of the house
alts: the car is in front of the house.

activities.prompt: There's a bridge _ the river and a tunnel _ it.
activities.answer: there's a bridge over the river and a tunnel under it
alts: there's a bridge over the river and a tunnel under it.

options.t: Opposite the library, next to the town hall.
In front the library, beside to the town hall.
options.why: in front of; next to / beside (no 'to').

options.t: Yes, there's a car park behind it, under the shopping centre.
Yes, there's a car park among it, over the shopping centre.

activities.why: above/below (higher/lower), over/under (directly, or more/less than a number), in front of/behind, next to/beside, opposite (facing); between links two, among links many, and beside ≠ besides.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Movement prepositions show direction. to = towards a destination; into / out of = in/out; onto / off = on/off; plus across, through, along, over, past, up, down.

go to school · walk into the room · jump off the wall.

learn.title: Direction words
learn.html:
to (destination) / from (origin)from London to Paris
into / out ofget into the car · get out of bed
across (surface) / through (inside)walk across the bridge · through the tunnel
along / past / over / roundwalk along the road · drive past the shop

learn.title: Special cases
learn.html:
No 'to' with home: go home (not to home). Also 'get in/out' for cars (get in the car / get out), but 'get on/off' for buses, trains, planes and bikes.

across = over a flat surface; through = within something 3D (a forest, a crowd).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How do I get there?  — Go along this street, across the square, and into the big building.

— Quick, get on the train!  — And get off at the third stop.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
go to home  ✓ go home.
✗
get off the car  ✓ get out of the car.
✗
walk through the bridge  ✓ walk across the bridge.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• to (destination), into/out of, onto/off; go home (no 'to').
  • across = over a surface; through = inside/within something.
  • get in/out of a car; get on/off a bus/train/plane/bike.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Предлоги движения показывают направление: to = к цели; into / out of; onto / off; и across, through, along, over, past: go to school · walk into the room.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Қозғалыс предлогтары бағытты көрсетеді: to = мақсатқа; into / out of; onto / off; және across, through, along, over, past: go to school · walk into the room.

ru.title: Слова направления
ru.html: to (цель) / from (начало). into / out of: get into the car / out of bed. across (поверхность) / through (внутри): across the bridge / through the tunnel. along / past / over / round.

kk.title: Бағыт сөздері
kk.html: to (мақсат) / from (бастау). into / out of: get into the car / out of bed. across (бет) / through (ішінде): across the bridge / through the tunnel. along / past / over / round.

ru.title: Особые случаи
ru.html: С home нет 'to': go home. Машина: get in/out a car; bus/train/plane/bike: get on/off. across = плоская поверхность; through = внутри 3D (лес, толпа).

kk.title: Ерекше жағдайлар
kk.html: home-мен 'to' жоқ: go home. Көлік: get in/out a car; bus/train/plane/bike: get on/off. across = тегіс бет; through = 3D нәрсе ішінде (орман, топ).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How do I get there? — Go along this street, across the square, and into the building. — Get on the train! — Get off at the third stop.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How do I get there? — Go along this street, across the square, and into the building. — Get on the train! — Get off at the third stop.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: go to home → go home. get off the car → get out of the car. through the bridge → across the bridge.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: go to home → go home. get off the car → get out of the car. through the bridge → across the bridge.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: to, into/out of, onto/off; go home (без 'to'). across = поверхность; through = внутри. get in/out of a car; get on/off a bus/train.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: to, into/out of, onto/off; go home ('to' жоқ). across = бет; through = ішінде. get in/out of a car; get on/off a bus/train.

activities.q: "She walked ___ the room and sat down."
why: Correct — entering an enclosed space → into the room.

items.s: The train went through the tunnel.

activities.why: cars → get out of the car.
in/out for cars/taxis; on/off for planes/buses; across/through for paths.

activities.q: "We drove ___ the mountains to reach the coast."
why: Correct — winding within them → through the mountains.

activities.q: "It's late — let's go ___."

activities.why: passing inside an opening → “rolled through the hole”.

activities.prompt: She jumped _ the horse and rode away.
activities.answer: she jumped onto the horse and rode away
alts: she jumped onto the horse and rode away.

activities.prompt: Tell someone to leave the taxi and enter the shop.
activities.answer: get out of the taxi and go into the shop
alts: get out of the taxi and go into the shop.
activities.why: out of a taxi; into a shop.

steps.npc: How do I reach the castle?

options.t: Walk across the bridge, then through the old gate.
Walk through the bridge, then across the old gate.
options.why: across the bridge; through the gate.

options.t: Yes, up the hill and into the courtyard.
Yes, onto the hill and to the courtyard inside.
options.why: up the hill; into the courtyard.

activities.why: Movement prepositions show direction: to (destination), into/out of, onto/off, across (over a surface) vs through (inside something); go home takes no 'to', and you get in/out of a car but on/off a bus or train.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
by has many uses: method (by car, by email), the agent in the passive (written by…), position (next to), and deadline (by Friday, Unit 119).

travel by train · a book by Orwell · sit by the window.

learn.title: Uses of by
learn.html:
method of travel/communicationby car / bus / plane · by phone / email
agent (passive)painted by Picasso
how something is done (+ -ing)She learned by watching.
position / deadlinestand by the door · finish by 5

learn.title: by vs with; on foot
learn.html:
by = the method in general (by hand); with = the specific tool/instrument: cut it with a knife (not by a knife).

Travel is by car/bus/train, BUT on foot (not by foot). Also by chance, by mistake, by accident, by myself (alone).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How did you get here?  — By bus, then the rest on foot.

— How did you pay?  — By card. I paid by mistake twice, actually!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
by foot  ✓ on foot.
✗
I opened it by a key.  ✓ opened it with a key.
✗
by my car (my specific car)  ✓ in my car / by car (in general).

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• by + method (by train, by email), agent (by Shakespeare), by + -ing (by studying).
  • with + a specific tool (cut with scissors); on foot, not 'by foot'.
  • Fixed: by chance/mistake/accident/myself.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: У by много ролей: способ (by car, by email), деятель в пассиве (written by…), положение (рядом), срок (by Friday): travel by train · a book by Orwell · sit by the window.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: by-дың көп қызметі: тәсіл (by car, by email), пассивтегі орындаушы (written by…), орын (қасында), мерзім (by Friday): travel by train · a book by Orwell · sit by the window.

ru.title: Использование by
ru.html: Способ передвижения/связи: by car/bus/plane · by phone/email. Деятель (пассив): painted by Picasso. Как (+ -ing): learned by watching. Положение/срок: by the door; finish by 5.

kk.title: by қолданысы
kk.html: Жол жүру/байланыс тәсілі: by car/bus/plane · by phone/email. Орындаушы (пассив): painted by Picasso. Қалай (+ -ing): learned by watching. Орын/мерзім: by the door; finish by 5.

ru.title: by vs with; on foot
ru.html: by = способ в целом (by hand); with = конкретный инструмент: cut it with a knife. Путь: by car/bus, НО on foot. Устойчивое: by chance/mistake/accident/myself.

kk.title: by vs with; on foot
kk.html: by = жалпы тәсіл (by hand); with = нақты құрал: cut it with a knife. Жол: by car/bus, БІРАҚ on foot. Тұрақты: by chance/mistake/accident/myself.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How did you get here? — By bus, then the rest on foot. — How did you pay? — By card.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How did you get here? — By bus, then the rest on foot. — How did you pay? — By card.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: by foot → on foot. opened it by a key → with a key. by my car → in my car / by car.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: by foot → on foot. opened it by a key → with a key. by my car → in my car / by car.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: by + способ, деятель, by + -ing. with + конкретный инструмент; on foot. Устойчивое: by chance/mistake/myself.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: by + тәсіл, орындаушы, by + -ing. with + нақты құрал; on foot. Тұрақты: by chance/mistake/myself.

activities.q: "He cut the rope ___ a knife."
why: 'by' = a general method, not a specific tool.
Correct — a specific instrument → with a knife.

items.s: The play was written by Shakespeare.

activities.why: by + method; with + a specific instrument.

activities.q: "I met an old friend completely ___ chance yesterday."
"This bridge was designed ___ a famous engineer."
why: Correct — the agent in a passive → by a famous engineer.

activities.prompt: To save money, she cooked at home.
activities.answer: she saved money by cooking at home
alts: she saved money by cooking at home.
activities.why: how it was done → by cooking.

activities.prompt: We opened the door by a card.
activities.answer: we opened the door with a card
alts: we opened the door with a card.
activities.why: a specific tool → with a card.

options.t: Fine — by train, then five minutes on foot.
Fine — with train, then five minutes by foot.
Yes, I paid by card and got the ticket by email.
Yes, I paid with card and got the ticket with email.

activities.why: by marks a method (by bus, by email), the agent in the passive (by Dickens) and by + -ing (how); use with for a specific tool, say 'on foot' not 'by foot', and note fixed phrases like by chance/mistake.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many nouns are followed by a fixed preposition. Learn the noun + preposition as a pair.

a reason for · an increase in · a solution to · the cause of.

learn.title: Common noun + preposition
learn.html:
fora reason for · a need for · demand for
inan increase/rise in · interest in · a change in
ofthe cause of · an advantage of · a photo of
to / with / betweena solution to · a relationship with · a difference between

learn.title: Watch these
learn.html:
a reason for (not of). an increase/rise in prices (not of). a solution/answer/key to a problem. the advantage of doing (+ -ing).

a relationship/contact with someone; a difference/gap between two things.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— What's the reason for the delay?  — A sudden increase in traffic.

— Any solution to the problem?  — Not yet — we're still looking for the cause of it.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the reason of the problem  ✓ the reason for the problem.
✗
an increase of prices  ✓ an increase in prices.
✗
a solution of the issue  ✓ a solution to the issue.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Learn noun + preposition together: reason for, increase in, cause of.
  • solution/answer/key to a problem; difference between.
  • Meaning matters: a rise in prices, not 'of'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После многих существительных идёт фиксированный предлог. Учите существительное + предлог вместе: a reason for · an increase in · a solution to · the cause of.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп зат есімнен кейін тұрақты предлог тұрады. Зат есім + предлогты бірге жаттаңыз: a reason for · an increase in · a solution to · the cause of.

ru.title: Частые сущ. + предлог
ru.html: for: reason/need/demand for. in: increase/rise in, interest in. of: cause of, advantage of, photo of. to/with/between: solution to, relationship with, difference between.

kk.title: Жиі зат есім + предлог
kk.html: for: reason/need/demand for. in: increase/rise in, interest in. of: cause of, advantage of, photo of. to/with/between: solution to, relationship with, difference between.

ru.title: Обратите внимание
ru.html: a reason for (не of). an increase/rise in prices. a solution/answer/key to a problem. the advantage of doing. relationship with; difference between.

kk.title: Мыналарға назар
kk.html: a reason for (of емес). an increase/rise in prices. a solution/answer/key to a problem. the advantage of doing. relationship with; difference between.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — What's the reason for the delay? — A sudden increase in traffic. — Any solution to the problem? — We're still looking for the cause of it.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — What's the reason for the delay? — A sudden increase in traffic. — Any solution to the problem? — We're still looking for the cause of it.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: reason of → reason for. increase of prices → increase in prices. solution of → solution to.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: reason of → reason for. increase of prices → increase in prices. solution of → solution to.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Сущ. + предлог вместе: reason for, increase in, cause of. solution/answer/key to; difference between.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Зат есім + предлогты бірге: reason for, increase in, cause of. solution/answer/key to; difference between.

activities.q: "What's the reason ___ your decision?"
why: 'reason to' + verb is different (a reason to go).

activities.q: "There's a big difference ___ the two plans."
"She has a good relationship ___ her colleagues."

activities.prompt: There's been a sharp increase _ demand _ electric cars.
activities.answer: an increase in demand for electric cars
alts: there's been a sharp increase in demand for electric cars

activities.prompt: Nobody knows the cause _ the delay or the reason _ the change.
activities.answer: the cause of the delay or the reason for the change
alts: nobody knows the cause of the delay or the reason for the change

options.t: Mainly an increase in raw material prices.
Mainly an increase of raw material prices.
We're working on a solution to the problem.
We're working on a solution of the problem.

activities.why: Many nouns take a fixed preposition — reason for, increase/rise in, cause/advantage of, solution/answer/key to, difference between, relationship with — so learn each noun together with its preposition.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many adjectives are followed by a fixed preposition. After the preposition, a verb takes -ing.

good at maths · afraid of dogs · interested in art · keen on running.

learn.title: Common combinations
learn.html:
atgood/bad at, brilliant at
ofafraid/scared of, proud of, full of, tired of
ininterested in, involved in
with / about / topleased with, worried about, kind to, similar to

learn.title: Preposition forces -ing
learn.html:
A verb after the preposition takes -ing: good at cooking, afraid of flying, tired of waiting.

Some adjectives take different prepositions with different meanings: good at (skill) vs good for (beneficial) vs good to (kind to).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Are you good at chess?  — Terrible! But I'm really keen on learning.

— She's afraid of spiders and worried about the exam.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
good in maths  ✓ good at maths.
✗
afraid from dogs  ✓ afraid of dogs.
✗
interested in to learn  ✓ interested in learning.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• good/bad at; afraid/proud/tired of; interested in; keen on.
  • pleased with, worried about, kind/similar to.
  • A verb after the preposition takes -ing.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После многих прилагательных идёт фиксированный предлог. Глагол после него — -ing: good at maths · afraid of dogs · interested in art · keen on running.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп сын есімнен кейін тұрақты предлог тұрады. Предлогтан кейінгі етістік -ing: good at maths · afraid of dogs · interested in art · keen on running.

ru.title: Частые сочетания
ru.html: at: good/bad at. of: afraid/scared of, proud of, full of, tired of. in: interested in. with/about/to: pleased with, worried about, kind to, similar to.

kk.title: Жиі тіркестер
kk.html: at: good/bad at. of: afraid/scared of, proud of, full of, tired of. in: interested in. with/about/to: pleased with, worried about, kind to, similar to.

ru.title: Предлог требует -ing
ru.html: Глагол после предлога — -ing: good at cooking, afraid of flying. Некоторые по смыслу: good at (умение) vs good for (полезно) vs good to (добр).

kk.title: Предлог -ing тудырады
kk.html: Предлогтан кейінгі етістік -ing: good at cooking, afraid of flying. Кейбіреуі мағынаға қарай: good at (шеберлік) vs good for (пайдалы) vs good to (мейірімді).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Good at chess? — Terrible! But keen on learning. — She's afraid of spiders and worried about the exam.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Good at chess? — Terrible! But keen on learning. — She's afraid of spiders and worried about the exam.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: good in maths → good at maths. afraid from dogs → afraid of dogs. interested in to learn → interested in learning.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: good in maths → good at maths. afraid from dogs → afraid of dogs. interested in to learn → interested in learning.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: good/bad at; afraid/proud/tired of; interested in; keen on. pleased with, worried about, kind/similar to. После предлога -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: good/bad at; afraid/proud/tired of; interested in; keen on. pleased with, worried about, kind/similar to. Предлогтан кейін -ing.

activities.q: "She's very good ___ drawing."
why: 'good of' is a different structure (kind of you).

activities.q: "He was very pleased ___ his exam results."
why: 'pleased about' is possible for news, but here…

activities.q: "Exercise is ___ your health."

activities.prompt: He's not good _ (get) up early.
activities.answer: he's not good at getting up early
alts: he's not good at getting up early.
activities.why: good at + -ing → good at getting up.

activities.prompt: They're worried _ their son and proud _ his effort.
activities.answer: worried about their son and proud of his effort
alts: they're worried about their son and proud of his effort

steps.npc: Are you nervous about the interview?

options.t: A bit, but I'm confident about my experience.
A bit, but I'm confident of my experience with... on...
options.why: confident about/of — keep it consistent; 'confident about my experience' is natural.

options.t: I'm good at solving problems and keen on learning.
I'm good in solving problems and keen of learning.

activities.why: Adjectives take fixed prepositions (good at, afraid/proud/tired of, interested in, keen on, worried about, kind/similar to); a verb after the preposition takes -ing, and some adjectives change preposition with meaning (good at/for/to).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
More adjective + preposition pairs, grouped so you can remember them. Again, a following verb takes -ing.

responsible for · famous for · different from · married to.

learn.title: More combinations
learn.html:
forresponsible/famous/known for, sorry for
fromdifferent from, absent from, safe from
tomarried/engaged to, similar to, used to, rude to
about / withexcited/nervous/serious about, bored/fed up with

learn.title: Tricky ones
learn.html:
married to someone (not with). different from (BrE; 'different to/than' also heard). sorry for/about something (past); sorry to + verb (now).

fed up with / bored with + -ing: I'm fed up with waiting.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Who's responsible for the project?  — Sara — she's famous for getting things done.

— This town is so different from mine.  — And I'm fed up with the rain!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
married with her  ✓ married to her.
✗
responsible of the team  ✓ responsible for the team.
✗
different than mine (BrE)  ✓ different from mine.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• responsible/famous/known for; different from; married/similar to.
  • excited/nervous about; bored/fed up with.
  • A following verb takes -ing (fed up with waiting).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Ещё пары прилаг. + предлог, сгруппированы для запоминания. Глагол после — -ing: responsible for · famous for · different from · married to.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Тағы сын есім + предлог жұптары, есте сақтауға топталған. Кейінгі етістік -ing: responsible for · famous for · different from · married to.

ru.title: Ещё сочетания
ru.html: for: responsible/famous/known for, sorry for. from: different from, absent from. to: married/engaged to, similar to, rude to. about/with: excited/nervous about, bored/fed up with.

kk.title: Тағы тіркестер
kk.html: for: responsible/famous/known for, sorry for. from: different from, absent from. to: married/engaged to, similar to, rude to. about/with: excited/nervous about, bored/fed up with.

ru.title: Сложные
ru.html: married to (не with). different from (BrE). sorry for/about (прошлое); sorry to + verb (сейчас). fed up with / bored with + -ing: fed up with waiting.

kk.title: Қиындары
kk.html: married to (with емес). different from (BrE). sorry for/about (өткен); sorry to + verb (қазір). fed up with / bored with + -ing: fed up with waiting.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Who's responsible for the project? — Sara — famous for getting things done. — This town is different from mine. — I'm fed up with the rain!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Who's responsible for the project? — Sara — famous for getting things done. — This town is different from mine. — I'm fed up with the rain!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: married with her → married to her. responsible of → responsible for. different than mine (BrE) → different from.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: married with her → married to her. responsible of → responsible for. different than mine (BrE) → different from.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: responsible/famous for; different from; married/similar to. excited/nervous about; bored/fed up with. Глагол после — -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: responsible/famous for; different from; married/similar to. excited/nervous about; bored/fed up with. Кейінгі етістік -ing.

activities.q: "He's responsible ___ the whole department."
"Your phone is completely different ___ mine."
"I'm sorry ___ interrupt, but there's a call for you."
why: 'sorry for' + -ing is for a past action.

activities.prompt: I'm bored _ (do) the same job.
activities.answer: i'm bored with doing the same job
alts: i'm bored of doing the same job

activities.prompt: He's known _ his kindness and famous _ his novels.
activities.answer: known for his kindness and famous for his novels
alts: he's known for his kindness and famous for his novels

options.t: Dr Lee — she's famous for her research on sleep.
Dr Lee — she's famous of her research.

steps.npc: Is she married to that author?

options.t: Yes, married to him for ten years.
Yes, married with him for ten years.

activities.why: More fixed pairs: responsible/famous/known for, different from, married/similar to, excited/nervous about, bored/fed up with — with -ing on any following verb.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many verbs need a fixed preposition before their object. These are not phrasal verbs — the preposition just links the verb to its object.

listen to music · wait for the bus · depend on the weather.

learn.title: Common verb + preposition
learn.html:
tolisten to, belong to, talk/speak to, happen to
forwait for, look for, ask for, pay for, search for
atlook at, laugh at, shout at, stare at
on / aboutdepend/rely on, concentrate on; think/worry about

learn.title: No preposition needed with some verbs
learn.html:
Some verbs take a direct object (no preposition), unlike other languages: discuss something, enter a room, marry someone, phone/call someone.

Not listen music — always listen to music. Not discuss about — just discuss it.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— What are you looking for?  — My keys. I can't rely on my memory!

— Are you listening to me?  — Sorry, I was thinking about something else.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
listen music  ✓ listen to music.
✗
wait the bus  ✓ wait for the bus.
✗
discuss about it  ✓ discuss it (no preposition).

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• listen to, wait for, look at, depend on, think about.
  • Learn the verb + preposition as a unit.
  • Some verbs take a direct object: discuss/enter/marry/phone (no preposition).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Многие глаголы требуют фиксированный предлог перед дополнением. Это не фразовые глаголы — предлог связывает глагол с дополнением: listen to music · wait for the bus · depend on the weather.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп етістік объектінің алдында тұрақты предлог қажет етеді. Бұл — phrasal verb емес; предлог етістікті объектімен байланыстырады: listen to music · wait for the bus · depend on the weather.

ru.title: Частые глагол + предлог
ru.html: to: listen to, belong to, talk to. for: wait for, look for, ask for, pay for. at: look at, laugh at, shout at. on/about: depend/rely on; think/worry about.

kk.title: Жиі етістік + предлог
kk.html: to: listen to, belong to, talk to. for: wait for, look for, ask for, pay for. at: look at, laugh at, shout at. on/about: depend/rely on; think/worry about.

ru.title: Некоторым предлог не нужен
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы берут прямое дополнение (без предлога): discuss something, enter a room, marry someone, phone someone. Не listen music — listen to. Не discuss about — discuss it.

kk.title: Кейбіреуіне предлог керек емес
kk.html: Кейбір етістік тура толықтауыш алады (предлогсыз): discuss something, enter a room, marry someone, phone someone. listen music емес — listen to. discuss about емес — discuss it.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — What are you looking for? — My keys. I can't rely on my memory! — Are you listening to me? — I was thinking about something else.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — What are you looking for? — My keys. I can't rely on my memory! — Are you listening to me? — I was thinking about something else.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: listen music → listen to music. wait the bus → wait for the bus. discuss about it → discuss it.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: listen music → listen to music. wait the bus → wait for the bus. discuss about it → discuss it.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: listen to, wait for, look at, depend on, think about. Учите глагол + предлог как набор. discuss/enter/marry/phone — без предлога.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: listen to, wait for, look at, depend on, think about. Етістік + предлогты жиынтық ретінде жаттаңыз. discuss/enter/marry/phone — предлогсыз.

activities.q: "I'm waiting ___ my friend."
"We need to ___ the problem at the meeting."
why: Correct — discuss takes a direct object → discuss the problem.

activities.q: "Stop shouting ___ the children!"
why: 'shout to' = call out to (neutral); here it's anger.

activities.prompt: She's looking _ a new job and thinking _ moving city.
activities.answer: looking for a new job and thinking about moving city
alts: she's looking for a new job and thinking about moving city

activities.instruction: Correct if needed (some need no preposition):
activities.prompt: They entered into the building and phoned to the manager.
activities.answer: they entered the building and phoned the manager
alts: they entered the building and phoned the manager.
activities.why: enter / phone take a direct object (no preposition).

steps.npc: Can we count on your team?

options.t: Yes, you can rely on us completely.
Yes, you can rely to us completely.

steps.npc: Shall we talk it through now?

options.t: Sure, let's discuss the timeline.
Sure, let's discuss about the timeline.

activities.why: Many verbs need a fixed preposition before their object (listen to, wait for, look at, depend on, think about); learn each pair, and remember discuss/enter/marry/phone take a direct object with no preposition.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
More verb + preposition pairs. After the preposition, a verb takes -ing.

apologise for being late · apply for a job · care about the result · succeed in passing.

learn.title: More combinations
learn.html:
forapologise/apply/care/vote/hope for
inbelieve in, succeed in, specialise in, result in
about / ofcare/complain/dream about; approve/consist/dream of
with / fromagree/deal/cope with; suffer from, benefit from

learn.title: Same verb, different preposition
learn.html:
Some verbs change meaning with the preposition: care about (be concerned) vs care for (look after/like). dream of/about (both used). agree with a person vs agree to a plan.

hear about (news) vs hear from (get a message) vs hear of (know exists).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Did you apply for the job?  — Yes, and I'm hoping for good news. I really believe in this company.

— Have you heard from Tom?  — No, but I heard about his promotion.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
apologise about being late  ✓ apologise for being late.
✗
apply to a job  ✓ apply for a job.
✗
suffer of a cold  ✓ suffer from a cold.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• apologise/apply/hope for; believe/succeed in; agree/deal/cope with; suffer/benefit from.
  • Meaning shifts: care about ≠ care for; agree with ≠ agree to.
  • A verb after the preposition takes -ing.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Ещё глагол + предлог. Глагол после предлога — -ing: apologise for being late · apply for a job · care about · succeed in passing.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Тағы етістік + предлог. Предлогтан кейінгі етістік -ing: apologise for being late · apply for a job · care about · succeed in passing.

ru.title: Ещё сочетания
ru.html: for: apologise/apply/care/vote/hope for. in: believe/succeed/specialise/result in. about/of: care/complain about; approve/consist of. with/from: agree/deal/cope with; suffer/benefit from.

kk.title: Тағы тіркестер
kk.html: for: apologise/apply/care/vote/hope for. in: believe/succeed/specialise/result in. about/of: care/complain about; approve/consist of. with/from: agree/deal/cope with; suffer/benefit from.

ru.title: Один глагол, разные предлоги
ru.html: care about (беспокоиться) vs care for (заботиться/нравиться). agree with человеком vs agree to планом. hear about (новость) vs hear from (весть) vs hear of (знать о существовании).

kk.title: Бір етістік, әр предлог
kk.html: care about (алаңдау) vs care for (қарау/ұнату). agree with адаммен vs agree to жоспармен. hear about (жаңалық) vs hear from (хабар) vs hear of (бар екенін білу).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Did you apply for the job? — I'm hoping for good news. I believe in this company. — Heard from Tom? — No, but I heard about his promotion.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Did you apply for the job? — I'm hoping for good news. I believe in this company. — Heard from Tom? — No, but I heard about his promotion.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: apologise about → apologise for. apply to a job → apply for a job. suffer of a cold → suffer from a cold.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: apologise about → apologise for. apply to a job → apply for a job. suffer of a cold → suffer from a cold.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: apologise/apply/hope for; believe/succeed in; agree/deal/cope with; suffer/benefit from. Смысл меняется: care about ≠ care for. Глагол после — -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: apologise/apply/hope for; believe/succeed in; agree/deal/cope with; suffer/benefit from. Мағына ауысады: care about ≠ care for. Кейінгі етістік -ing.

activities.q: "She apologised ___ being late."
"She ___ the environment and campaigns to protect it."
"I completely ___ you on this issue."

activities.why: you apply to a university (institution) but apply for a job/place — here 'apply to three universities' is actually correct; the common error is 'apply for a university'. Keep 'apply to' for institutions.

activities.prompt: They complained _ (wait) so long.
activities.answer: they complained about waiting so long
alts: they complained about waiting so long.

activities.prompt: I don't approve _ smoking, but I benefit _ the fresh air here.
activities.answer: i don't approve of smoking, but i benefit from the fresh air
alts: i don't approve of smoking but i benefit from the fresh air here

options.t: I've applied for two positions and I'm hoping for interviews.
I've applied to two positions and I'm hoping to interviews.
options.why: apply for a position; hope for + noun.

options.t: I'm dealing with a lot of rejection, but I'm not giving up.
I'm dealing from a lot of rejection.

activities.why: More fixed pairs — apologise/apply/hope for, believe/succeed in, agree/deal/cope with, suffer/benefit from — with -ing on a following verb; watch meaning shifts (care about vs care for, agree with vs agree to).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some verbs take an object + a preposition before the next part. Learn the whole frame: verb + somebody + preposition + something.

thank somebody for · remind somebody of · blame somebody for · spend money on.

learn.title: Common frames
learn.html:
forthank/blame/forgive/punish sb for
ofremind/accuse/warn/inform sb of
onspend (money/time) on, congratulate sb on
to / from / withexplain/describe sth to sb; borrow from; provide sb with

learn.title: remind of vs remind to
learn.html:
remind sb of sth = it makes you think of it: This song reminds me of summer. remind sb to do = tell them not to forget: Remind me to call her.

explain/describe/suggest something to somebody (not explain me): Explain the rule to me.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Thanks for helping!  — No problem. It reminds me of when we were students.

— Don't blame me for the mistake.  — Sorry — can you explain it to me again?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
thank you the gift  ✓ thank you for the gift.
✗
explain me the plan  ✓ explain the plan to me.
✗
spend money for clothes  ✓ spend money on clothes.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• thank/blame sb for; remind/accuse/warn sb of; spend/congratulate on.
  • explain/describe/suggest sth to sb (not 'explain me').
  • remind sb of sth (memory) vs remind sb to do (a task).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы берут дополнение + предлог перед следующей частью. Учите всю конструкцию: thank sb for · remind sb of · blame sb for · spend money on.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір етістік келесі бөліктің алдында object + предлог алады. Тұтас құрылымды жаттаңыз: thank sb for · remind sb of · blame sb for · spend money on.

ru.title: Частые конструкции
ru.html: for: thank/blame/forgive/punish sb for. of: remind/accuse/warn/inform sb of. on: spend on, congratulate sb on. to/from/with: explain sth to sb; borrow from; provide sb with.

kk.title: Жиі құрылымдар
kk.html: for: thank/blame/forgive/punish sb for. of: remind/accuse/warn/inform sb of. on: spend on, congratulate sb on. to/from/with: explain sth to sb; borrow from; provide sb with.

ru.title: remind of vs remind to
ru.html: remind sb of sth = напоминает: reminds me of summer. remind sb to do = не забыть: Remind me to call her. explain/describe sth to sb (не explain me): Explain the rule to me.

kk.title: remind of vs remind to
kk.html: remind sb of sth = еске салады: reminds me of summer. remind sb to do = ұмытпауды айту: Remind me to call her. explain/describe sth to sb (explain me емес): Explain the rule to me.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Thanks for helping! — It reminds me of when we were students. — Don't blame me for the mistake. — Can you explain it to me again?

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Thanks for helping! — It reminds me of when we were students. — Don't blame me for the mistake. — Can you explain it to me again?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: thank you the gift → thank you for the gift. explain me the plan → explain the plan to me. spend money for clothes → spend money on clothes.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: thank you the gift → thank you for the gift. explain me the plan → explain the plan to me. spend money for clothes → spend money on clothes.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: thank/blame sb for; remind/accuse/warn sb of; spend/congratulate on. explain/describe sth to sb. remind sb of (напоминает) vs remind sb to do.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: thank/blame sb for; remind/accuse/warn sb of; spend/congratulate on. explain/describe sth to sb. remind sb of (еске салу) vs remind sb to do.

activities.q: "Thank you ___ your help."

items.s: He reminded me of my brother.
She spent a fortune on shoes.

activities.why: explain sth to sb → explain the answer to me.

activities.q: "Can you ___ post the letter tomorrow?"
why: 'remind me of' = it makes me think of something.
Correct — a task not to forget → remind me to post.

activities.q: "Don't blame me ___ the delay — it wasn't my fault."
why: 'blame on' needs a different structure (blame sth on sb).

activities.why: spend money on → “spends too much money on clothes”.

activities.prompt: When I see the sea, I think of my childhood.
activities.answer: the sea reminds me of my childhood
alts: the sea reminds me of my childhood.

activities.prompt: Can you explain me this word?
activities.answer: can you explain this word to me
alts: can you explain this word to me?

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: sorting out a mix-up

steps.npc: Why is he upset with me?

options.t: He blames you for missing the deadline.
He blames you of missing the deadline.
Sure — I'll explain the situation to him.
Sure — I'll explain him the situation.

activities.why: Learn the whole frame verb + object + preposition: thank/blame sb for, remind/accuse/warn sb of, spend/congratulate on, explain/describe sth to sb — and note remind sb of sth (memory) vs remind sb to do (a task).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many fixed preposition + noun phrases have set meanings — and some pairs look alike but differ. Learn them as chunks.

on time (punctual) vs in time (early enough); at the end (point) vs in the end (finally).

learn.title: Confusing pairs
learn.html:
on time = punctualThe train left on time (at 9:00 exactly).
in time (for) = early enoughWe arrived in time to catch it.
at the end (of) = at the final pointat the end of the film
in the end = finally, after allIn the end, we stayed home.

learn.title: More fixed phrases
learn.html:
on: on holiday, on business, on purpose, on the phone, on fire, on the whole.

in: in trouble, in a hurry, in love, in danger, in general, in fact, in cash.

at: at first, at last, at least, at the moment, at once.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Did you finish?  — Only just in time! But I got there on time for once.

— Was it hard?  — At first, yes. But in the end it went well.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
arrive in time (meaning punctual)  ✓ arrive on time.
✗
at the end we decided (meaning finally)  ✓ in the end we decided.
✗
in holiday  ✓ on holiday.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• on time = punctual; in time (for) = early enough.
  • at the end (of) = final point; in the end = finally.
  • Learn set phrases: on holiday/purpose, in fact/trouble, at first/last.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Многие устойчивые предлог + сущ. имеют фиксированный смысл — некоторые пары похожи, но различны: on time (вовремя) vs in time (успеть); at the end vs in the end (в конце концов).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп тұрақты предлог + зат есім тіркесінің тиянақты мағынасы бар — кейбір жұп ұқсас, бірақ бөлек: on time (уақтылы) vs in time (үлгеріп); at the end vs in the end (ақыры).

ru.title: Путающие пары
ru.html: on time = вовремя: left on time. in time (for) = успеть: arrived in time. at the end (of) = финальная точка: at the end of the film. in the end = в итоге: In the end, we stayed home.

kk.title: Шатастыратын жұптар
kk.html: on time = уақтылы: left on time. in time (for) = үлгеріп: arrived in time. at the end (of) = соңғы нүкте: at the end of the film. in the end = ақыры: In the end, we stayed home.

ru.title: Ещё устойчивые фразы
ru.html: on: on holiday, on business, on purpose, on the phone, on fire. in: in trouble, in a hurry, in love, in general, in fact, in cash. at: at first, at last, at least, at once.

kk.title: Тағы тұрақты тіркестер
kk.html: on: on holiday, on business, on purpose, on the phone, on fire. in: in trouble, in a hurry, in love, in general, in fact, in cash. at: at first, at last, at least, at once.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Did you finish? — Only just in time! But I got there on time. — Hard? — At first, yes. But in the end it went well.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Did you finish? — Only just in time! But I got there on time. — Hard? — At first, yes. But in the end it went well.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: arrive in time (вовремя) → arrive on time. at the end we decided (в итоге) → in the end. in holiday → on holiday.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: arrive in time (уақтылы) → arrive on time. at the end we decided (ақыры) → in the end. in holiday → on holiday.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: on time = вовремя; in time (for) = успеть. at the end (of) = точка; in the end = в итоге. Устойчивое: on holiday/purpose, in fact, at first/last.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: on time = уақтылы; in time (for) = үлгеріп. at the end (of) = нүкте; in the end = ақыры. Тұрақты: on holiday/purpose, in fact, at first/last.

activities.q: "The meeting started exactly ___ at 9."

Correct — the final point of something → at the end of the book.

activities.q: "I didn't mean to break it — it wasn't ___."

activities.why: 'in time to board' = early enough — this is actually correct! The common slip is using 'on time' here; keep 'in time' when it means 'early enough (to do something)'.

activities.prompt: The doctor is very punctual; appointments always start ___.

activities.prompt: We considered many options, but ___ we chose the simplest.
