Just on time — I ran to the gate!
options.why: 'in time' fits 'only just made it'; 'on time' = punctual schedule.

options.t: At first I doubted it, but in the end it was great.
In first I doubted it, but at the end it was great.

activities.why: Fixed preposition + noun phrases carry set meanings: on time (punctual) vs in time (early enough), at the end (of) (final point) vs in the end (finally), plus on holiday/purpose, in fact/trouble, at first/last.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
English has many idiomatic prepositional phrases used to organise or comment on what we say. Learn them as whole units.

in general, on the whole, by chance, in fact, as a result.

learn.title: Useful phrases by function
learn.html:
generalisingin general, on the whole, as a rule
adding/correctingin fact, actually, in addition
chance / intentionby chance/accident, on purpose
manner / memoryby heart, by hand, at a glance

learn.title: More to know
learn.html:
in my opinion, to be honest, as far as I know = ways to give a view. for example / for instance = to illustrate. at least = not less than / a positive point.

These phrases are fixed — don't change the preposition: by heart (not in heart).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How was the course?  — On the whole, very good. In fact, better than I expected.

— Did you plan to meet?  — No, completely by chance!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
in the general  ✓ in general.
✗
learn by the heart  ✓ learn by heart.
✗
for the example  ✓ for example.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Generalise: in general, on the whole, as a rule.
  • Add/correct: in fact, in addition, for example.
  • These are fixed phrases — keep the exact preposition and no extra 'the'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: В английском много идиоматических предложных фраз для организации речи. Учите целиком: in general, on the whole, by chance, in fact, as a result.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ағылшында ойды ұйымдастыратын көп идиомалық предлог тіркесі бар. Тұтас жаттаңыз: in general, on the whole, by chance, in fact, as a result.

ru.title: По функции
ru.html: Обобщение: in general, on the whole, as a rule. Добавление/поправка: in fact, actually, in addition. Случайность/намерение: by chance/accident, on purpose. Способ/память: by heart, by hand.

kk.title: Қызметі бойынша
kk.html: Жалпылау: in general, on the whole, as a rule. Қосу/түзету: in fact, actually, in addition. Кездейсоқтық/ниет: by chance/accident, on purpose. Тәсіл/жад: by heart, by hand.

ru.title: Ещё знать
ru.html: in my opinion, to be honest, as far as I know — мнение. for example / for instance — пример. at least — по крайней мере. Не меняйте предлог: by heart.

kk.title: Тағы білу керек
kk.html: in my opinion, to be honest, as far as I know — пікір беру. for example / for instance — мысал. at least — кемінде / оң жағы. Предлогты өзгертпеңіз: by heart.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How was the course? — On the whole, very good. In fact, better than I expected. — Plan to meet? — Completely by chance!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How was the course? — On the whole, very good. In fact, better than I expected. — Plan to meet? — Completely by chance!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: in the general → in general. by the heart → by heart. for the example → for example.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: in the general → in general. by the heart → by heart. for the example → for example.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Обобщение: in general, on the whole. Добавление/поправка: in fact, for example. Устойчивые фразы — точный предлог, без лишнего 'the'.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Жалпылау: in general, on the whole. Қосу/түзету: in fact, for example. Тұрақты тіркестер — дәл предлог, артық 'the' жоқ.

"It was a hard week, but ___ I finished the project."

activities.answer: many fruits are healthy — apples, for example
alts: many fruits are healthy; for example, apples

alts: i bumped into her by chance
i met her by accident

steps.npc: So, honest verdict on the hotel?

options.t: On the whole it was great — in fact, the best we've stayed in.
On whole it was great — in the fact, the best.
Not really — at least, nothing serious.
Not really — by least, nothing serious.

activities.why: Idiomatic prepositional phrases organise and comment on speech — in general/on the whole (generalise), in fact/for example (correct or illustrate), by chance/on purpose, by heart — and are fixed, so keep the exact preposition.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
A phrasal verb = verb + particle (a preposition or adverb) that together often make a new meaning. They are very common in everyday English.

get up (rise) · turn on (start a machine) · give up (stop trying).

learn.title: Literal vs idiomatic
learn.html:
literal (easy to guess)sit down, come in, go out
idiomatic (new meaning)give up = quit · look after = care for
one verb, many meaningstake off (plane/clothes/success)
often replaceable by one verbfind out = discover · put off = postpone

learn.title: How to learn them
learn.html:
Learn a phrasal verb like new vocabulary: verb + particle + meaning + example. Group by particle (up, off, out) or by topic (get up, wake up, grow up).

Some are formal-neutral (discover), while the phrasal verb (find out) is more everyday.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— What time do you get up?  — Early. Then I turn on the radio and look through the news.

— I've decided to give up coffee.  — Good luck working out how!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
get up the bus (= board)  ✓ get on the bus.
✗
I gave up to smoke.  ✓ gave up smoking (particle + -ing).
✗
look after to the kids  ✓ look after the kids (no 'to').

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Phrasal verb = verb + particle, often with a new (idiomatic) meaning.
  • Learn them as vocabulary: meaning + example; a verb after them takes -ing.
  • Many everyday phrasal verbs replace a single formal verb (find out = discover).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Фразовый глагол = глагол + частица (предлог/наречие), вместе часто новое значение. Очень частотны: get up (вставать) · turn on (включать) · give up (бросать).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Phrasal verb = етістік + бөлшек (предлог/үстеу), бірге жиі жаңа мағына береді. Ағылшын тілінде өте жиі: get up (тұру) · turn on (қосу) · give up (тастау).

ru.title: Буквальные vs идиоматические
ru.html: Буквальные (легко угадать): sit down, come in. Идиоматические (новый смысл): give up = бросить, look after = заботиться. Один глагол — много смыслов: take off. Заменяет один глагол: find out = discover.

kk.title: Тура vs идиомалық
kk.html: Тура (болжауға оңай): sit down, come in. Идиомалық (жаңа мағына): give up = тастау, look after = қарау. Бір етістік, көп мағына: take off. Бір етістікпен ауысады: find out = discover.

ru.title: Как учить
ru.html: Как новое слово: глагол + частица + смысл + пример. Группируйте по частице (up, off, out) или теме. Некоторые формальны (discover), фразовый (find out) — обиходный.

kk.title: Қалай үйрену керек
kk.html: Жаңа сөз ретінде: етістік + бөлшек + мағына + мысал. Бөлшек (up, off, out) немесе тақырып бойынша топтаңыз. Кейбірі ресми (discover), phrasal verb (find out) күнделікті.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — What time do you get up? — Early. Then I turn on the radio and look through the news. — I'll give up coffee. — Good luck working out how!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — What time do you get up? — Early. Then I turn on the radio and look through the news. — I'll give up coffee. — Good luck working out how!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: get up the bus → get on the bus. gave up to smoke → gave up smoking. look after to the kids → look after the kids.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: get up the bus → get on the bus. gave up to smoke → gave up smoking. look after to the kids → look after the kids.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Фразовый глагол = глагол + частица, часто новый смысл. Учите как слово; глагол после — -ing. Многие заменяют один формальный глагол.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Phrasal verb = етістік + бөлшек, жиі жаңа мағына. Сөз ретінде жаттаңыз; кейінгі етістік -ing. Көбі бір ресми етістікті ауыстырады.

activities.q: "'Give up' means:"

activities.after: the light — it's dark. (start it → turn …)
alts: switch on

activities.why: Each phrasal verb ≈ one formal verb.

activities.q: "We had to ___ the meeting until next week."
"Can you ___ my dog while I'm away?"

activities.instruction: Replace the formal verb with a phrasal verb:
activities.prompt: I need to discover the truth.
activities.answer: i need to find out the truth
alts: i need to find out the truth.

activities.instruction: Replace the formal verb with a phrasal verb:
activities.prompt: They decided to postpone the wedding.
activities.answer: they decided to put off the wedding
alts: they decided to put the wedding off

options.t: Yes, I got up at six and turned on the coffee machine.
Yes, I got up the coffee machine at six.
options.why: turn on the machine; get up = rise from bed.

options.t: I'm trying to give up scrolling my phone in the mornings.
I'm trying to give up to scroll my phone.

activities.why: A phrasal verb is a verb + particle that often takes on a new, idiomatic meaning; learn them as vocabulary with meaning and example, and use -ing on a verb that follows one.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some phrasal verbs are separable (the object can go in the middle), others inseparable (it can't). And a pronoun object almost always goes in the middle.

turn on the TV / turn the TV on / turn it on. · look after them (inseparable).

learn.title: The rules
learn.html:
separable + noun (either order)switch off the light / switch the light off
separable + pronoun (middle only)switch it off (NOT switch off it)
inseparable (no splitting)look after them · get over it
3-part verbs (always together)put up with it · look forward to it

learn.title: The pronoun rule
learn.html:
The most important rule: with a separable phrasal verb, a pronoun (it/them/him) must go between the verb and the particle: pick it up (not pick up it).

With inseparable verbs, everything stays after: I'll look after it (not look it after). A dictionary tells you which type each is.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Can you turn the music down?  — Sure, I'll turn it down.

— Who looks after the baby?  — My mum looks after her on Fridays.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
turn off it  ✓ turn it off.
✗
look them after  ✓ look after them (inseparable).
✗
put up it with  ✓ put up with it.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Separable: verb + object + particle OR verb + particle + object (noun).
  • A pronoun object goes in the middle of a separable verb (turn it on).
  • Inseparable & 3-part verbs never split (look after them, put up with it).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые фразовые глаголы разделяемые (дополнение в середину), другие неразделяемые. Местоимение почти всегда в середине: turn on the TV / turn the TV on / turn it on. look after them (неразделяемый).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір phrasal verb бөлінеді (объект ортаға), кейбірі бөлінбейді. Есімдік объект әрдайым дерлік ортада: turn on the TV / turn the TV on / turn it on. look after them (бөлінбейді).

ru.title: Правила
ru.html: Разделяемый + сущ. (любой порядок): switch off the light / switch the light off. Разделяемый + местоим. (только середина): switch it off. Неразделяемый: look after them. Из 3 частей: put up with it.

kk.title: Ережелер
kk.html: Бөлінетін + зат есім (кез келген рет): switch off the light / switch the light off. Бөлінетін + есімдік (тек орта): switch it off. Бөлінбейтін: look after them. 3 бөлікті: put up with it.

ru.title: Правило местоимения
ru.html: Главное правило: у разделяемого глагола местоимение (it/them/him) идёт между глаголом и частицей: pick it up (не pick up it). У неразделяемого всё после: look after it. Словарь укажет тип.

kk.title: Есімдік ережесі
kk.html: Ең маңызды ереже: бөлінетін phrasal verb-те есімдік (it/them/him) етістік пен бөлшек арасында: pick it up (pick up it емес). Бөлінбейтінде бәрі артында: look after it. Сөздік түрін көрсетеді.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Can you turn the music down? — I'll turn it down. — Who looks after the baby? — My mum looks after her on Fridays.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Can you turn the music down? — I'll turn it down. — Who looks after the baby? — My mum looks after her on Fridays.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: turn off it → turn it off. look them after → look after them. put up it with → put up with it.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: turn off it → turn it off. look them after → look after them. put up it with → put up with it.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Разделяемый: глагол + дополнение + частица ИЛИ глагол + частица + сущ. Местоимение — в середине. Неразделяемые и из 3 частей не разделяются.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Бөлінетін: етістік + объект + бөлшек НЕМЕСЕ етістік + бөлшек + зат есім. Есімдік объект — ортада. Бөлінбейтін және 3 бөлікті ешқашан бөлінбейді.

activities.q: "The radio's too loud. Can you turn ___?"
why: a pronoun can't go after the particle.
Correct — pronoun in the middle → turn it off.

activities.why: pronoun goes in the middle of a separable verb.

activities.before: Here are your keys — don't forget to pick

activities.instruction: Can the object go in the middle?
activities.why: turn off/pick up split; look after/get over don't.

activities.q: "I can't stand this noise. How do you ___?"
why: Correct — 3-part verb stays together → put up with it.
the pronoun can't go inside it.

activities.q: "Grandma is ill, so we're ___ this week."
why: 'look after' is inseparable — don't split it.

activities.instruction: Replace the noun with a pronoun:

activities.instruction: Replace the noun with a pronoun:
activities.prompt: She's looking after the children.
alts: she is looking after them

steps.npc: What shall I do with these toys?

options.t: Just pick them up and put them away.
Just pick up them and put away them.
options.why: pronoun goes in the middle: pick them up, put them away.

options.t: Turn it off, please — I can't put up with the noise.
options.why: turn it off (separable); put up with (3-part, together).

options.t: Turn off it, please — I can't put up it with the noise.

activities.why: Separable phrasal verbs allow the object before or after the particle, but a pronoun object must go in the middle (turn it on); inseparable and three-part verbs never split (look after them, put up with it).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Phrasal verbs with in and out. Often in = entering/including; out = leaving/excluding, or 'completely / to the end'.

fill in a form · check in · go out · find out · run out of milk.

learn.title: Common verbs
learn.html:
infill in (complete), check in, drop in (visit), give in (surrender)
out (leave/finish)go out, eat out, sort out, work out, sell out
out (discover/produce)find out, point out, hand out, turn out
run out (of)We've run out of bread.

learn.title: Meaning notes
learn.html:
work out = (1) exercise, (2) solve/calculate, (3) end well: It worked out fine. turn out = happen in the end: It turned out to be true.

fill in (BrE) / fill out (AmE) a form — both used. give in = surrender; give out = distribute / stop working.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Shall we eat out tonight?  — We can't — I need to sort out some work first.

— We've run out of coffee.  — I'll find out if the shop's open.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
fill the form  ✓ fill in the form.
✗
We run out milk.  ✓ We've run out of milk.
✗
find out it  ✓ find it out.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• in: fill in, check in, give in (surrender), drop in (visit).
  • out: go/eat out, find out, sort out, work out, run out (of).
  • run out of + noun; put a pronoun in the middle (find it out).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Фразовые глаголы с in и out. Часто in = вход/включение; out = выход/исключение или 'полностью/до конца': fill in a form · find out · run out of milk.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: in және out-пен phrasal verb-тер. Жиі in = кіру/қосу; out = шығу/шығару немесе 'толық/аяғына дейін': fill in a form · find out · run out of milk.

ru.title: Частые глаголы
ru.html: in: fill in (заполнить), check in, drop in (зайти), give in (сдаться). out (выход/финиш): go out, eat out, sort out, work out. out (узнать/выдать): find out, point out, hand out. run out (of).

kk.title: Жиі етістіктер
kk.html: in: fill in (толтыру), check in, drop in (қыдырып кіру), give in (беріліп қою). out (шығу/аяқтау): go out, eat out, sort out, work out. out (табу/шығару): find out, point out, hand out. run out (of).

ru.title: Заметки о смысле
ru.html: work out = (1) тренироваться, (2) решить/подсчитать, (3) хорошо закончиться. turn out = оказаться. fill in (BrE) / fill out (AmE). give in = сдаться; give out = раздать / перестать работать.

kk.title: Мағына ескертпелері
kk.html: work out = (1) жаттығу, (2) шешу/есептеу, (3) жақсы біту. turn out = ақыры болу. fill in (BrE) / fill out (AmE). give in = берілу; give out = тарату / істен шығу.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Shall we eat out? — I need to sort out some work first. — We've run out of coffee. — I'll find out if the shop's open.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Shall we eat out? — I need to sort out some work first. — We've run out of coffee. — I'll find out if the shop's open.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: fill the form → fill in the form. run out milk → run out of milk. find out it → find it out.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: fill the form → fill in the form. run out milk → run out of milk. find out it → find it out.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: in: fill in, check in, give in. out: go/eat out, find out, sort out, run out (of). run out of + сущ.; местоимение в середине.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: in: fill in, check in, give in. out: go/eat out, find out, sort out, run out (of). run out of + зат есім; есімдік ортада.

alts: i want to find out what happened.

activities.prompt: We have no more paper.
alts: we have run out of paper

options.t: Let's eat out — we've run out of food anyway.
Let's eat out — we've run out food anyway.
I'll find out if they have a table.
I'll find it out if they have a table? no...
options.why: before a clause use 'find out' (find out if…), not 'find it out'.

activities.why: Phrasal verbs with in (fill in, check in, give in) and out (find out, sort out, eat out, run out of); use 'of' after run out, and put a pronoun object in the middle (find it out).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Phrasal verbs with on and off. Often on = continue/start/wear; off = stop/remove/leave/cancel.

carry on · put on a coat · turn off the TV · take off your shoes · call off a match.

learn.title: Common verbs
learn.html:
on (continue/start/wear)carry on, go on, put on, get on (a bus)
off (stop/turn off)turn off, switch off, put off (postpone)
off (leave/remove)take off, set off, get off, drop off
off (cancel)call off, put off

learn.title: Meaning notes
learn.html:
take off = (1) remove (clothes), (2) a plane leaves the ground, (3) become successful. get on = (1) board, (2) progress, (3) have a good relationship (get on with someone).

put off = postpone or discourage; set off = start a journey; drop off = (1) take someone there, (2) fall asleep.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— What time do we set off?  — Early. Put on warm clothes and don't forget to turn off the heating.

— The concert was called off.  — Oh no, they put it off again!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
put on it (the coat)  ✓ put it on.
✗
get off from the bus  ✓ get off the bus.
✗
get on well him  ✓ get on well with him.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• on: carry/go on, put on (wear), get on (board/progress).
  • off: turn/switch off, take off, set off, call/put off (cancel/postpone).
  • get on with someone (relationship); pronoun in the middle (put it on).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Фразовые с on и off. Часто on = продолжать/начать/надеть; off = выключить/снять/уехать/отменить: carry on · put on a coat · turn off · take off · call off.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: on және off-пен phrasal verb. Жиі on = жалғастыру/бастау/кию; off = тоқтату/алу/кету/болдырмау: carry on · put on a coat · turn off · take off · call off.

ru.title: Частые глаголы
ru.html: on: carry on, go on, put on, get on (сесть). off: turn off, switch off, put off (отложить). off (уехать/снять): take off, set off, get off, drop off. off (отменить): call off.

kk.title: Жиі етістіктер
kk.html: on: carry on, go on, put on, get on (міну). off: turn off, switch off, put off (кейінге қалдыру). off (кету/алу): take off, set off, get off, drop off. off (болдырмау): call off.

ru.title: Заметки о смысле
ru.html: take off = (1) снять, (2) взлёт самолёта, (3) стать успешным. get on = (1) сесть, (2) продвигаться, (3) ладить (get on with sb). put off = отложить/оттолкнуть; set off = отправиться; drop off = подвезти/уснуть.

kk.title: Мағына ескертпелері
kk.html: take off = (1) шешу, (2) ұшақ көтерілу, (3) табысқа жету. get on = (1) міну, (2) ілгерілеу, (3) тіл табысу (get on with sb). put off = кейінге қалдыру/жиіркендіру; set off = жолға шығу; drop off = апарып тастау/ұйықтап кету.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — What time do we set off? — Put on warm clothes and turn off the heating. — The concert was called off. — They put it off again!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — What time do we set off? — Put on warm clothes and turn off the heating. — The concert was called off. — They put it off again!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: put on it → put it on. get off from the bus → get off the bus. get on well him → get on well with him.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: put on it → put it on. get off from the bus → get off the bus. get on well him → get on well with him.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: on: carry/go on, put on, get on. off: turn/switch off, take off, set off, call/put off. get on with sb; местоимение в середине.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: on: carry/go on, put on, get on. off: turn/switch off, take off, set off, call/put off. get on with sb; есімдік ортада.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
up is the most common particle. It often adds the idea of completely / finishing, increasing, or appearing.

eat up (finish) · grow up · give up · turn up (arrive) · speak up (louder).

learn.title: Common verbs
learn.html:
completely / finisheat up, drink up, use up, wash up, finish up
increase / improvespeak up, hurry up, cheer up, do up
appear / happenturn up, show up, come up (arise), make up (invent)
create / arrangeset up, put up, bring up (a child/topic), fill up

learn.title: Multi-meaning ones
learn.html: make up = (1) invent a story, (2) become friends again, (3) form/compose, (4) apply cosmetics. bring up = (1) raise a child, (2) mention a topic. give up = quit; take up = start a hobby; pick up = collect / learn / improve.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Hurry up, we're late!  — OK, I'll just wash up these plates.

— Did he turn up to the party?  — Yes, and he brought up the old argument again!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
grow up in a house (= be raised) needs care  ✓ I grew up in London. (correct)
✗
give up to smoke  ✓ give up smoking.
✗
pick up it  ✓ pick it up.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• up often = completely/finish (eat up), increase (hurry up), appear (turn up).
  • Multi-meaning: make up, bring up, pick up, take up.
  • give up + -ing; pronoun in the middle (pick it up).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: up — самая частая частица. Часто добавляет идею полностью/до конца, увеличение или появление: eat up · grow up · give up · turn up · speak up.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: up — ең жиі бөлшек. Жиі толық/аяқтау, арту немесе пайда болу идеясын қосады: eat up · grow up · give up · turn up · speak up.

ru.title: Частые глаголы
ru.html: полностью/финиш: eat up, drink up, use up, wash up. увеличить/улучшить: speak up, hurry up, cheer up. появиться: turn up, show up, come up, make up. создать/организовать: set up, put up, bring up, fill up.

kk.title: Жиі етістіктер
kk.html: толық/аяқтау: eat up, drink up, use up, wash up. арту/жақсарту: speak up, hurry up, cheer up. пайда болу: turn up, show up, come up, make up. жасау/ұйымдастыру: set up, put up, bring up, fill up.

ru.title: Многозначные
ru.html: make up = (1) выдумать, (2) помириться, (3) составлять, (4) краситься. bring up = (1) растить ребёнка, (2) поднять тему. give up = бросить; take up = заняться хобби; pick up = забрать/выучить/улучшиться.

kk.title: Көп мағыналылар
kk.html: make up = (1) ойдан шығару, (2) қайта табысу, (3) құрау, (4) макияж жасау. bring up = (1) бала тәрбиелеу, (2) тақырып көтеру. give up = тастау; take up = хобби бастау; pick up = алу/үйрену/жақсару.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Hurry up, we're late! — I'll just wash up these plates. — Did he turn up? — Yes, and he brought up the old argument again!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Hurry up, we're late! — I'll just wash up these plates. — Did he turn up? — Yes, and he brought up the old argument again!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I grew up in London (верно). give up to smoke → give up smoking. pick up it → pick it up.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I grew up in London (дұрыс). give up to smoke → give up smoking. pick up it → pick it up.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: up часто = полностью/финиш (eat up), увеличение (hurry up), появление (turn up). Многозначные: make up, bring up, pick up, take up. give up + -ing; местоимение в середине.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: up жиі = толық/аяқтау (eat up), арту (hurry up), пайда болу (turn up). Көп мағына: make up, bring up, pick up, take up. give up + -ing; есімдік ортада.

activities.why: 'up' often means completely/finish (eat up), increase (hurry up), appear (turn up) or create (set up); many are multi-meaning (make up, bring up, pick up), give up takes -ing, and a pronoun object goes in the middle.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
down often adds the idea of reducing, stopping/failing, writing, or lowering.

turn down (reduce / refuse) · break down · write down · calm down · slow down.

learn.title: Common verbs
learn.html:
reduce / lowerturn down, slow down, cut down (on), calm down
stop working / failbreak down, let (sb) down, close down, shut down
write / recordwrite down, note down, take down, put down
otherturn down (refuse), settle down, break down (cry)

learn.title: Multi-meaning ones
learn.html: turn down = (1) lower the volume, (2) reject an offer. break down = (1) a machine stops, (2) start crying, (3) analyse into parts. let down = disappoint; cut down (on) = reduce (cut down on sugar).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Can you turn down the music?  — Sure. And please write down the address.

— The car broke down again!  — Calm down — we'll call for help.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
cut down sugar  ✓ cut down on sugar.
✗
write down it  ✓ write it down.
✗
He let down me.  ✓ He let me down.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• down often = reduce (turn/slow down), fail (break down), write (write down).
  • cut down on + noun; let somebody down = disappoint.
  • Multi-meaning: turn down, break down; pronoun in the middle (write it down).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: down часто добавляет идею уменьшения, остановки/провала, записи или понижения: turn down · break down · write down · calm down · slow down.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: down жиі азайту, тоқтау/сәтсіздік, жазу немесе төмендету идеясын қосады: turn down · break down · write down · calm down · slow down.

ru.title: Частые глаголы
ru.html: уменьшить/понизить: turn down, slow down, cut down (on), calm down. сломаться/провал: break down, let (sb) down, close down. записать: write down, note down, take down. другое: turn down (отказать), settle down.

kk.title: Жиі етістіктер
kk.html: азайту/төмендету: turn down, slow down, cut down (on), calm down. істен шығу/сәтсіздік: break down, let (sb) down, close down. жазу: write down, note down, take down. басқа: turn down (бас тарту), settle down.

ru.title: Многозначные
ru.html: turn down = (1) убавить громкость, (2) отклонить предложение. break down = (1) машина ломается, (2) расплакаться, (3) разобрать на части. let down = разочаровать; cut down (on) = сократить.

kk.title: Көп мағыналылар
kk.html: turn down = (1) дыбысты азайту, (2) ұсыныстан бас тарту. break down = (1) машина тоқтау, (2) жылап жіберу, (3) бөлшектеп талдау. let down = көңілін қалдыру; cut down (on) = азайту.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Can you turn down the music? — And write down the address. — The car broke down again! — Calm down — we'll call for help.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Can you turn down the music? — And write down the address. — The car broke down again! — Calm down — we'll call for help.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: cut down sugar → cut down on sugar. write down it → write it down. let down me → let me down.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: cut down sugar → cut down on sugar. write down it → write it down. let down me → let me down.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: down часто = уменьшить, сломаться, записать. cut down on + сущ.; let sb down. Многозначные: turn down, break down; местоимение в середине.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: down жиі = азайту, істен шығу, жазу. cut down on + зат есім; let sb down. Көп мағына: turn down, break down; есімдік ортада.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
away often = to a distance / disappearing / continuously; back often = returning / in return.

throw away · run away · give back · call back · pay back.

learn.title: Common verbs
learn.html:
away (remove/disappear)throw away, put away, give away, run away, go away
away (continuously)work away, chat away
back (return)go back, come back, get back, call back
back (in return / previous state)give back, pay back, put back, take back

learn.title: Meaning notes
learn.html:
put away = tidy things into their place; give away = give free / reveal a secret; take back = (1) return an item, (2) withdraw what you said.

get back to someone = reply later; look back (on) = remember the past.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Don't throw that away — I need it!  — Sorry, I'll put it back.

— Can you call me back later?  — Sure, I'll get back to you this afternoon.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
throw away it  ✓ throw it away.
✗
give back me the book  ✓ give me the book back / give it back.
✗
I'll get back you  ✓ get back to you.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• away: throw/put/give away, run away, go away (remove/disappear).
  • back: go/come/get back (return), give/pay back (in return).
  • get back to someone; pronoun in the middle (throw it away).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: away часто = вдаль/исчезновение/непрерывно; back часто = возврат/в ответ: throw away · run away · give back · call back · pay back.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: away жиі = алысқа/жоғалу/үздіксіз; back жиі = қайту/қайтарым: throw away · run away · give back · call back · pay back.

ru.title: Частые глаголы
ru.html: away (убрать/исчезнуть): throw away, put away, give away, run away, go away. away (непрерывно): work away, chat away. back (вернуться): go back, come back, get back, call back. back (в ответ): give back, pay back, put back, take back.

kk.title: Жиі етістіктер
kk.html: away (алу/жоғалу): throw away, put away, give away, run away, go away. away (үздіксіз): work away, chat away. back (қайту): go back, come back, get back, call back. back (қайтарым): give back, pay back, put back, take back.

ru.title: Заметки о смысле
ru.html: put away = убрать на место; give away = отдать бесплатно / выдать секрет; take back = (1) вернуть вещь, (2) взять слова обратно. get back to sb = ответить позже; look back (on) = вспоминать прошлое.

kk.title: Мағына ескертпелері
kk.html: put away = орнына жинау; give away = тегін беру / құпияны ашу; take back = (1) затты қайтару, (2) айтқаныңнан бас тарту. get back to sb = кейін жауап беру; look back (on) = өткенді еске алу.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Don't throw that away — I need it! — I'll put it back. — Can you call me back? — I'll get back to you this afternoon.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Don't throw that away — I need it! — I'll put it back. — Can you call me back? — I'll get back to you this afternoon.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: throw away it → throw it away. give back me the book → give it back / give me the book back. get back you → get back to you.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: throw away it → throw it away. give back me the book → give it back / give me the book back. get back you → get back to you.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: away: throw/put/give away, run away, go away. back: go/come/get back, give/pay back. get back to sb; местоимение в середине.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: away: throw/put/give away, run away, go away. back: go/come/get back, give/pay back. get back to sb; есімдік ортада.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Phrasal verbs with over (recover / consider / cross), through (finish / connect / experience) and round / around (turn / persuade / be present).

get over an illness · go through a list · come round (visit / recover).

learn.title: Common verbs
learn.html:
overget over (recover), think over (consider), take over (control), go over (review)
throughgo through (examine/experience), get through (finish/contact), look through
round / aroundcome round (visit/regain consciousness), turn round, show round, get round to

learn.title: Meaning notes
learn.html:
get over = recover from (an illness, a shock). get through = (1) finish, (2) contact by phone, (3) pass an exam. go through = (1) examine carefully, (2) experience a difficult time.

take over = take control of a company/job; come round/around = visit informally, or regain consciousness, or change your mind.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— Have you got over your cold?  — Almost. Let's go over the plan again.

— Why not come round tonight?  — I can't get through all this work first!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
get over from an illness  ✓ get over an illness.
✗
I couldn't get through to nobody.  ✓ couldn't get through to anybody.
✗
think it over about  ✓ think it over.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• over: get over (recover), think over (consider), take over, go over (review).
  • through: get through (finish/contact), go through (examine/experience).
  • round/around: come round (visit/recover), get round to (finally do).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: over (выздороветь/обдумать/пересечь), through (закончить/связаться/пережить), round/around (повернуть/убедить/зайти): get over an illness · go through a list · come round.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: over (айығу/ойлану/өту), through (аяқтау/байланыс/бастан кешу), round/around (бұрылу/көндіру/қыдырып келу): get over an illness · go through a list · come round.

ru.title: Частые глаголы
ru.html: over: get over (выздороветь), think over (обдумать), take over (взять управление), go over (пересмотреть). through: go through (проверить/пережить), get through (закончить/дозвониться), look through. round: come round (зайти/очнуться), get round to.

kk.title: Жиі етістіктер
kk.html: over: get over (айығу), think over (ойлану), take over (басқаруды алу), go over (қайта қарау). through: go through (тексеру/кешу), get through (аяқтау/байланысу), look through. round: come round (келу/есін жию), get round to.

ru.title: Заметки о смысле
ru.html: get over = оправиться (болезнь, шок). get through = (1) закончить, (2) дозвониться, (3) сдать экзамен. go through = (1) тщательно проверить, (2) пережить трудное. take over = взять управление; come round = зайти/очнуться/передумать.

kk.title: Мағына ескертпелері
kk.html: get over = айығу (ауру, күйзеліс). get through = (1) аяқтау, (2) телефонмен байланысу, (3) емтихан тапсыру. go through = (1) мұқият тексеру, (2) қиын кезеңді бастан кешу. take over = басқаруды алу; come round = келу/есін жию/пікірін өзгерту.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — Have you got over your cold? — Let's go over the plan again. — Why not come round tonight? — I can't get through all this work first!

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — Have you got over your cold? — Let's go over the plan again. — Why not come round tonight? — I can't get through all this work first!

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: get over from an illness → get over an illness. get through to nobody → to anybody. think it over about → think it over.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: get over from an illness → get over an illness. get through to nobody → to anybody. think it over about → think it over.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: over: get over, think over, take over, go over. through: get through, go through. round: come round, get round to.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: over: get over, think over, take over, go over. through: get through, go through. round: come round, get round to.

activities.answer: i need to think over your offer
alts: i need to think your offer over

activities.answer: the police went through all the evidence
alts: the police went through all the evidence.

options.t: Slowly getting over it, thanks.
Slowly getting over from it, thanks.
Yes — come round later and we'll go over everything.
Yes — come round later and we'll get over everything.
options.why: review → go over; get over = recover.

activities.why: 'over' (get over = recover, think over = consider, take over, go over = review), 'through' (get through = finish/contact, go through = examine/experience) and 'round' (come round = visit/recover, get round to = finally do).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some phrasal verbs have three parts: verb + particle + preposition. They never separate, and the object always comes at the end.

look forward to · put up with · get on with · run out of · catch up with.

learn.title: Common three-part verbs
learn.html:
look forward to (+ -ing)I look forward to seeing you.
put up with = tolerateI can't put up with the noise.
get on with = have a good relationship / continueget on with colleagues
run out of / catch up with / keep up withrun out of time · catch up with friends

learn.title: Never split; -ing after 'to'
learn.html:
These stay together and take the object at the end — even a pronoun: put up with it (not put it up with).

After look forward to, the 'to' is a preposition → use -ing: looking forward to meeting you (not to meet).

More: come up with (an idea), get away with (do wrong unpunished), drop out of (leave), stand up for (defend).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How's the new team?  — Great — I get on with everyone. But I can't put up with the early starts.

— I'm looking forward to the weekend!  — Same — if I can catch up with my emails first.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
look forward to meet you  ✓ look forward to meeting you.
✗
put up it with  ✓ put up with it.
✗
get on my boss  ✓ get on with my boss.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Three-part verbs never split; the object (even a pronoun) goes at the end.
  • look forward to + -ing; put up with, get on with, run out of.
  • Also: come up with, get away with, drop out of, stand up for.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые фразовые глаголы из трёх частей: глагол + частица + предлог. Не разделяются, дополнение в конце: look forward to · put up with · get on with · run out of.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір phrasal verb үш бөліктен тұрады: етістік + бөлшек + предлог. Ешқашан бөлінбейді, объект соңында: look forward to · put up with · get on with · run out of.

ru.title: Частые из трёх частей
ru.html: look forward to (+ -ing): look forward to seeing you. put up with = терпеть. get on with = ладить/продолжать. run out of / catch up with / keep up with.

kk.title: Жиі үш бөлікті етістіктер
kk.html: look forward to (+ -ing): look forward to seeing you. put up with = шыдау. get on with = тіл табысу/жалғастыру. run out of / catch up with / keep up with.

ru.title: Не разделяются; -ing после 'to'
ru.html: Вместе, дополнение в конце — даже местоимение: put up with it (не put it up with). После look forward to 'to' — предлог → -ing: looking forward to meeting. Ещё: come up with, get away with, drop out of, stand up for.

kk.title: Бөлінбейді; 'to'-дан кейін -ing
kk.html: Бірге тұрады, объект соңында — есімдік те: put up with it (put it up with емес). look forward to-дан кейін 'to' — предлог → -ing: looking forward to meeting. Тағы: come up with, get away with, drop out of, stand up for.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How's the team? — I get on with everyone. But I can't put up with the early starts. — I'm looking forward to the weekend! — If I can catch up with my emails first.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How's the team? — I get on with everyone. But I can't put up with the early starts. — I'm looking forward to the weekend! — If I can catch up with my emails first.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: look forward to meet you → to meeting you. put up it with → put up with it. get on my boss → get on with my boss.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: look forward to meet you → to meeting you. put up it with → put up with it. get on my boss → get on with my boss.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Из трёх частей не разделяются; дополнение (и местоимение) в конце. look forward to + -ing; put up with, get on with, run out of. Ещё: come up with, stand up for.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Үш бөлікті ешқашан бөлінбейді; объект (есімдік те) соңында. look forward to + -ing; put up with, get on with, run out of. Тағы: come up with, stand up for.

activities.q: "I can't ___ his constant complaining."

items.s: I look forward to hearing from you.
She looks forward to meet you.
I get on with my sister.

activities.before: The music is loud, but I'll just put up
activities.why: 3-part verb never splits → put up with it.

activities.q: "The team ___ a brilliant idea for the campaign."
"He cheated in the test but somehow ___ it."
why: Correct — did wrong unpunished → got away with.
'caught up with' = reached the same level.

activities.prompt: I won't tolerate this behaviour.
activities.answer: i won't put up with this behaviour
alts: i won't put up with this behavior

activities.prompt: Do you have a good relationship with your neighbours?
activities.answer: do you get on with your neighbours
alts: do you get on with your neighbors

options.t: Good — we get on with each other really well.
Good — we get on each other really well.
I have to put up with his loud music sometimes.
I have to put his loud music up with sometimes.
options.why: three-part verbs don't split: put up with it.

activities.why: Three-part phrasal verbs (look forward to, put up with, get on with, run out of, come up with) never split — the object goes at the end — and after 'look forward to' the verb takes -ing.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Grouping phrasal verbs by topic helps you remember and use them naturally. Here are useful sets for daily life, study and relationships.

Work: take on, lay off, carry out. Study: hand in, catch up, look up. Relationships: fall out, make up, split up.

learn.title: By topic
learn.html:
work / jobstake on (hire), lay off (make redundant), carry out (do), fill in for
studyhand in (submit), catch up (on), look up (check), go over (revise)
relationshipsget on (with), fall out (with), make up, split up (with), ask out
daily lifeget up, dress up, eat out, drop by, tidy up

learn.title: Watch the prepositions
learn.html:
Some need a preposition before the person: fall out with someone, split up with someone, catch up on work / with a friend.

ask (somebody) out = invite on a date; settle down = start a stable life; bring up = raise a child / mention.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— How's work?  — Busy — they took on three new people and I'm carrying out the training.

— Did you and Sam make up?  — Yes, we'd fallen out over nothing.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
hand the essay (submit)  ✓ hand in the essay.
✗
fall out my friend  ✓ fall out with my friend.
✗
ask out her (pronoun)  ✓ ask her out.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Learn phrasal verbs in topic sets (work, study, relationships, daily life).
  • Mind the preposition: fall out with, catch up on/with.
  • Separable ones take a pronoun in the middle (ask her out, hand it in).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Группировка фразовых глаголов по теме помогает запоминать. Работа: take on, lay off, carry out. Учёба: hand in, catch up, look up. Отношения: fall out, make up, split up.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Phrasal verb-терді тақырып бойынша топтау есте сақтауға көмектеседі. Жұмыс: take on, lay off, carry out. Оқу: hand in, catch up, look up. Қарым-қатынас: fall out, make up, split up.

ru.title: По теме
ru.html: работа: take on (нанять), lay off (сократить), carry out (выполнить). учёба: hand in (сдать), catch up (on), look up, go over. отношения: get on (with), fall out (with), make up, split up, ask out. быт: get up, dress up, eat out, drop by.

kk.title: Тақырып бойынша
kk.html: жұмыс: take on (жалдау), lay off (қысқарту), carry out (орындау). оқу: hand in (тапсыру), catch up (on), look up, go over. қарым-қатынас: get on (with), fall out (with), make up, split up, ask out. күнделікті: get up, dress up, eat out, drop by.

ru.title: Следите за предлогами
ru.html: Некоторым нужен предлог перед человеком: fall out with, split up with, catch up on work / with a friend. ask sb out = позвать на свидание; settle down = осесть; bring up = растить/поднять тему.

kk.title: Предлогтарға назар
kk.html: Кейбіреуіне адам алдында предлог: fall out with, split up with, catch up on work / with a friend. ask sb out = кездесуге шақыру; settle down = тұрақты өмір бастау; bring up = бала тәрбиелеу/тақырып көтеру.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — How's work? — They took on three new people and I'm carrying out the training. — Did you and Sam make up? — We'd fallen out over nothing.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — How's work? — They took on three new people and I'm carrying out the training. — Did you and Sam make up? — We'd fallen out over nothing.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: hand the essay → hand in the essay. fall out my friend → fall out with my friend. ask out her → ask her out.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: hand the essay → hand in the essay. fall out my friend → fall out with my friend. ask out her → ask her out.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Учите тематическими наборами. Внимание к предлогу: fall out with, catch up on/with. Разделяемые берут местоимение в середину (ask her out).

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Тақырып топтарымен жаттаңыз. Предлогқа абай: fall out with, catch up on/with. Бөлінетіндер есімдікті ортаға алады (ask her out).

steps.npc: And how are you and Jo?

We fell out us, but we've made up now.

activities.why: Grouping phrasal verbs by topic (work: take on/lay off; study: hand in/catch up; relationships: fall out/make up) aids recall — mind the preposition (fall out with) and put a pronoun in the middle of separable ones (ask her out).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
This unit brings everything together: choosing the right phrasal verb from context, getting the particle and word order right, and using them fluently in longer texts.

The key skills: meaning, particle, separability, and the preposition in three-part verbs.

learn.title: The four checks
learn.html:
1 meaningDoes the phrasal verb fit the context? (give up = quit)
2 particleRight particle? (turn on vs turn off vs turn down)
3 word orderPronoun in the middle of a separable verb (turn it on)
4 three-partKeep together + end preposition (put up with it)

learn.title: Register & style
learn.html:
Phrasal verbs are common in everyday and spoken English; a single-word verb (postpone, tolerate, discover) is often more formal. Choose to match the situation: an email to a friend vs an official report.

When in doubt, learn each phrasal verb with an example sentence — that fixes meaning, particle and grammar together.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
— I've taken up running, cut down on sugar, and given up late nights.  — You've really turned your life around!

— I'm looking forward to the weekend, if work doesn't pile up.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
turn on it / put off it  ✓ turn it on / put it off.
✗
look forward to see you  ✓ look forward to seeing you.
✗
run out milk / cut down sugar  ✓ run out of milk / cut down on sugar.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Check meaning → particle → word order → (three-part) preposition.
  • Pronoun objects go in the middle of separable verbs; three-part verbs never split.
  • Phrasal verbs are everyday; single verbs are often more formal.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Этот раздел объединяет всё: выбор верного фразового глагола по контексту, правильная частица и порядок слов, свободное употребление. Ключевое: смысл, частица, разделяемость, предлог в трёхчастных.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бұл бөлім бәрін біріктіреді: контекстен дұрыс phrasal verb таңдау, бөлшек пен сөз ретін дұрыс қою, мәтінде еркін қолдану. Негізгі: мағына, бөлшек, бөліну, үш бөлікті предлог.

ru.title: Четыре проверки
ru.html: 1 смысл: подходит ли к контексту? 2 частица: turn on/off/down? 3 порядок слов: местоимение в середине (turn it on). 4 трёхчастные: вместе + предлог в конце (put up with it).

kk.title: Төрт тексеру
kk.html: 1 мағына: контекстке сай ма? 2 бөлшек: turn on/off/down? 3 сөз реті: есімдік ортада (turn it on). 4 үш бөлікті: бірге + соңында предлог (put up with it).

ru.title: Стиль
ru.html: Фразовые глаголы частотны в обиходной/устной речи; однословный глагол (postpone, tolerate, discover) обычно формальнее. Выбирайте по ситуации. Если сомневаетесь — учите каждый с примером.

kk.title: Стиль
kk.html: Phrasal verb — күнделікті/ауызша тілде жиі; бір сөзді етістік (postpone, tolerate, discover) көбіне ресми. Жағдайға сай таңдаңыз. Күмән болса, әр phrasal verb-ті мысал сөйлеммен жаттаңыз.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: — I've taken up running, cut down on sugar, and given up late nights. — You've really turned your life around! — I'm looking forward to the weekend, if work doesn't pile up.

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: — I've taken up running, cut down on sugar, and given up late nights. — You've really turned your life around! — I'm looking forward to the weekend, if work doesn't pile up.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: turn on it → turn it on. look forward to see → to seeing. run out milk / cut down sugar → run out of / cut down on.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: turn on it → turn it on. look forward to see → to seeing. run out milk / cut down sugar → run out of / cut down on.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Смысл → частица → порядок слов → (трёхчастный) предлог. Местоимение в середине разделяемого; трёхчастные не разделяются. Фразовые — обиходны; однословные — формальнее.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Мағына → бөлшек → сөз реті → (үш бөлікті) предлог. Есімдік бөлінетіннің ортасында; үш бөлікті бөлінбейді. Phrasal verb — күнделікті; бір сөзді — ресмиірек.
